HTML output uses order, placing reader guidance report-summary tables full Markdown text. report_index shows major evidence areas, status, readiness label, review-signal count, primary/template tables inspect next; claim_readiness report_gaps show claims ready, need caveats, require specific include preset helper. report_index also carries EvidenceRoute, TemplateRoute, PlotRoute, ExportRoute, IncludePreset columns report route points next table, figure, export, mfrm_results(include = ...) call without turning routes new evidence. template_index stacks reporting-template rows across fit, precision, bias, misfit/pathway, linking/anchor areas unsupported caveated wording can reviewed opening full template text. Detailed tables remain available report$tables; use report$report_index$PrimaryTable, report$report_index$TemplateTable, report$report_index$PlotRoute choose next table figure rather opening every report table default. fit claims, also inspect fit_criteria, zstd_conventions, fit_decision_policy, plus result-specific fit_evidence_summary, fit_threshold_sensitivity, fit_reporting_templates, fit_df_sensitivity_summary tables. Also inspect precision_evidence_summary, precision_basis, precision_reporting_templates writing separation, reliability, strata claims. tables keep selected MnSq band, observed fit-status counts, alternative published threshold profiles, engine-vs-FACETS-style ZSTD standardization, Rasch/FACETS-style precision indices visible. reporting templates turn counts cautious APA/QC/validation/reviewer wording scaffolds without turning result single pass/fail sentence. reporting-template table also carries EvidenceTable, EvidenceRoute, BoundaryType, ClaimStrength, RecommendedUse, wording can traced back evidence source claim boundary pasted manuscript, QC memo, reviewer response, appendix. FACETS-style ZSTD review uses fourth-moment df convention can retain positive df 1 capped ZSTD values; report standardization convention rather different MnSq fit signal. ZSTD flag changes df convention changes, treat row review prompt return MnSq size, facet role, response context writing substantive fit claim. separation reliability high, still report precision evidence rather inter-rater agreement standalone validity evidence. res built include = "bias", bias_evidence_summary bias_reporting_templates add guardrails bias, DFF, fairness language: facet-level bias rows screening prompts, interaction-bias contrasts must chosen explicitly, DFF claims require documented group, method, linking/anchor support, threshold policy. res built include = "misfit_review", misfit_evidence_summary misfit_reporting_templates extend boundary unexpected responses, displacement, pathway maps: local misfit rows case-review prompts, automatic exclusion, fairness, validity decisions. res built include = "linking", linking_evidence_summary linking_reporting_templates extend boundary anchor readiness, drift review, equating-chain wording: anchor evidence supports scale-maintenance review, drift equating claims still require explicit multi-fit wave/form comparisons. Inside summary(res), start triage reading every table. orders unavailable, review, informational, OK signals across diagnostics, plots, tables, precision/reliability, reporting, model scope, network review surfaces. mfrm_results() accepts purpose presets include, common workflows can stay readable: want shortest possible recommendation: Final estimation: prefer method = "MML" Fast exploratory pass : use method = "JML" Preferred RSM / PCM fit screen: diagnose_mfrm(..., diagnostic_mode = "") First visual screen: plot_qc_dashboard(..., preset = "publication") First reporting screen: reporting_checklist() First case-review screen: build_misfit_casebook() inspect casebook$group_view_index First weighting-policy screen: build_weighting_review() First operational linking screen: mfrm_results(fit, include = "linking") anchor readiness one fit, detect_anchor_drift() / build_equating_chain() explicit lists fitted waves forms; build_linking_review() synthesis layer. bounded GPCM, use caveated exploratory index direct anchor, drift, chain support treat operational linking decision.
code: fit_bin <- fit_mfrm(   data = binary_df,   person = "Person",   facets = "Item",   score = "Score",   model = "RSM" )  fit_bin$summary[, c("Model", "Facets", "Categories", "Converged")] mfrmr_output_guide("binary") res_bin <- mfrm_results(fit_bin) summary(res_bin)$triage res <- mfrm_results(fit, include = c("publication", "bias", "misfit_review")) mfrmr_output_guide("viewer")[, c("Question", "MainFunction")]  if (interactive() && requireNamespace("shiny", quietly = TRUE)) {   launch_mfrmr_viewer(res) } download <- export_mfrm_results(   res,   output_dir = "mfrmr-results",   prefix = "analysis01",   include = c("default", "report"),   overwrite = TRUE ) download$written_files report <- mfrm_report(res, style = "qc") summary(report) report$first_screen report$report_index report$template_index names(report$tables) mfrm_report(res, style = "validation", output = "html") mfrm_results(fit, include = "standard")     # first screen mfrm_results(fit, include = "publication")  # add APA assembly mfrm_results(fit, include = "validation")   # add FACETS-fit review mfrm_results(fit, include = "bias")         # add bias-screen guidance mfrm_results(fit, include = "misfit_review")# add unexpected/displacement/pathway review mfrm_results(fit, include = "linking")      # add anchor-readiness/linking review mfrm_results(fit, include = "network")      # add connectivity review mfrm_results(                              # add descriptive timing QC   fit,   include = "response_time",   response_time = "ResponseTime",   response_time_data = original_data ) mfrm_results(fit, include = "gpcm_review")  # standard route with GPCM caveats

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: mfrmr expects long-format rating data: one row per observed rating. one person column one ordered score column one non-person facet columns supplied facets = c(...) scores ordered integer category codes 0/1, 1/2, 1:5 binary two-category scores supported ordered-response interface fractional scores rejected; recode explicitly fitting non-numeric score labels dropped warning coercion fails keep_original = FALSE, unused intermediate categories collapsed contiguous internal scale recorded fit$prep$score_map intended scale unused boundary categories, 1-5 scale 2-5 observed, set rating_min = 1, rating_max = 5 zero-count boundary category remains fitted support intended scale unused intermediate categories, 1, 3, 5 observed 1-5 scale, also set keep_original = TRUE summary(describe_mfrm_data(...)) reports retained zero-count categories Notes, printed Caveats block, $caveats; summary(fit) carries full structured rows printed Caveats appendix/export role analysis_caveats, Key warnings short triage subset Subset disconnected-form linking work Weight weighted analyses Group downstream fairness DFF workflows need grouping metadata ResponseTime (similar) descriptive timing review response_time_review(); timing metadata part fitted MFRM likelihood Response-time metadata can screened separate quality-control layer: Use outputs locate rapid/slow response-time patterns person, facet, score category. describe joint speed-accuracy model parameters automatic exclusion rules. Minimal pattern: exploratory use, mfrm_results(df) can start standard long-format data frame Person Score unambiguous column names; remaining columns treated facets. ambiguous files, keep reproducible route explicit fit_mfrm(...). want column-selection prompts interactive R session, use opt-wizard:
code: rt <- response_time_review(   dat,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   time = "ResponseTime" )  summary(rt) plot_response_time_review(rt, type = "distribution") plot_response_time_review(rt, type = "person")  res_rt <- mfrm_results(   fit,   include = c("standard", "response_time"),   response_time = "ResponseTime",   response_time_data = dat ) summary(res_rt)$next_actions plot(res_rt, type = "response_time", draw = FALSE) names(df) # [1] "Person" "Rater" "Criterion" "Score"  fit <- fit_mfrm(   data = df,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   model = "RSM" ) if (interactive()) {   res <- mfrm_results_interactive(df) }

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: mfrmr now includes first-version latent-regression branch inside fit_mfrm(). Activate method = "MML", population_formula = ~ ..., one-row-per-person person_data. Current supported boundary: ordered-response RSM / PCM one latent dimension conditional-normal person population model person covariates supplied explicit person table expanded stats::model.matrix(), including numeric/logical predictors factor/character categorical predictors posterior scoring plausible-value draws condition fitted population model inspect fitting: summary(fit)$population_overview shows posterior basis, residual variance, omitted-person counts. summary(fit)$population_coefficients shows latent-regression coefficients. summary(fit)$population_coding shows categorical covariates coded. summary(fit)$key_warnings summary(fit)$caveats flag issues reviewed reporting exporting results. Introductory workflow: Use population_policy = "omit" complete-case removal intended, report omitted-person omitted-row counts. Coefficients population_coefficients conditional-normal population-model parameters, post hoc regression EAP/MLE scores. manuscript reporting, keep population-model tables together: population_overview posterior basis omission review, population_coefficients fitted population-model parameters, population_coding contrasts encoded columns, caveats complete-case score-support warnings. branch currently provide Wald-test p-value reporting, multidimensional latent regression, posterior predictive checking; use transparent one-dimensional MML population-model route rather general latent-regression framework. Reference checks branch: ConQuest preparation case checks package-side preparation. run ConQuest. actual ConQuest output tables available documented overlap case, use external-table comparison helpers: Treat scoped comparison, full ConQuest numerical equivalence. ConQuest must run separately extracted tables must reviewed. Current non-goals branch: JML latent regression bounded GPCM latent regression multidimensional population models arbitrary imported design specifications full ConQuest plausible-values workflow described first-version overlap ConQuest latent-regression framework, ConQuest numerical equivalence. predict_mfrm_population() remains simulation-based scenario-forecasting helper. described latent-regression estimator .
code: # response data: one row per rating event # person data: one row per person, with the same Person IDs person_tbl <- unique(dat[c("Person", "Grade", "Group")])  fit_pop <- fit_mfrm(   data = dat,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   model = "RSM",   population_formula = ~ Grade + Group,   person_data = person_tbl,   population_policy = "error" )  s_pop <- summary(fit_pop) s_pop$population_overview      # posterior basis, residual variance, omissions s_pop$population_coefficients  # latent-regression coefficients s_pop$population_coding        # categorical levels / contrasts / encoded columns s_pop$caveats                  # complete-case and category-support warnings bench_pop <- reference_case_benchmark(   cases = c("synthetic_latent_regression_omit", "synthetic_conquest_overlap_dry_run"),   method = "MML",   model = "RSM",   quad_points = 5,   maxit = 30 )  summary(bench_pop) bench_pop$population_policy_checks  # complete-case omission check bench_pop$conquest_overlap_checks   # package-side ConQuest preparation check bundle <- build_conquest_overlap_bundle(fit_overlap, output_dir = "conquest_overlap") normalized <- normalize_conquest_overlap_files(   population_file = "conquest_population.csv",   item_file = "conquest_items.csv",   case_file = "conquest_cases.csv" ) review <- review_conquest_overlap(bundle, normalized) summary(review)$summary review$attention_items

Use gpcm_score_side_contract() inspect specific score-side estimand, native uncertainty, score-side delta SE, reduction-test, schema, FACETS-compatible uncertainty requirements separate current caveated scorefile route full FACETS-style score-side review. installed bounded-GPCM scope notes keep unsupported areas explicit: release review, optional script system.file("validation", "recovery-validation.R", package = "mfrmr") defines core RSM / PCM / bounded-GPCM recovery cases, extended latent-regression case, extended high-dispersion/sparse-category bounded-GPCM case, structured release-review steps, CSV/RDS/Markdown summaries. intentionally separate routine tests useful settings long-running Monte Carlo checks. summary separates recovery metric status uncertainty status, generator-condition status, diagnostic-fit/separation status, unavailable coverage columns, sparse generated categories, fit/separation flags look like failed parameter recovery . Printing validation object calling summary(validation) shows release-level status first. direct recovery checks, plot(evaluate_mfrm_recovery(...), ...) shows recovery summaries, row-level errors, truth-estimate scatter, replication status. assess_mfrm_recovery(), use recovery_review$condition_reporting_notes recovery_review$condition_review confirm bounded-GPCM slope-regime generator condition generated score-category support, recovery_review$diagnostic_reporting_notes recovery_review$diagnostic_review recovery run retained diagnostic fit/separation operating characteristics, plot(recovery_review, type = "status") checklist status counts plot(recovery_review, type = "metrics", metric = "rmse") parameter-group metric review. recommended reading order : summary(recovery_review), condition notes/review, diagnostic notes/review available, status plot, metric plot, row-level recovery table parameter groups need follow-. summary(recovery_review)$reading_order records order directly; draw = FALSE plot data also include reading_order guidance fields plotting handoff. compact bounded-GPCM recovery smoke check looks like . one replication setting checking workflow reading handoff tables; increase reps using result release evidence. Read validation outputs order: topline_release_decision: release-level recovery conclusion. ReleaseRecoveryStatus uses core validation cases release basis reports extended sensitivity cases separately via ExtendedSensitivityStatus. Recovery metrics, convergence, Monte Carlo precision remain primary evidence release status. release_decision_table: decision validation case, short interpretation uncertainty limitation. condition_reporting_notes: reporter-facing generator-condition caveats, high-dispersion slope stress sparse generated score support. condition_summary: generator-condition table separates bounded-GPCM slope-regime stress generated score-category support. diagnostic_reporting_notes: reporter-facing fit/separation caveats, zero separation/reliability df-sensitive ZSTD flags, kept release gate. domain_decision_table: diagnostic split among recovery metrics, uncertainty, Monte Carlo precision, score support, broader overall status. appendix handoff, pass validation summary build_summary_table_bundle(summary(validation)). bundle includes top-line decision, case decisions, case summary, condition summary, domain decision tables, plus condition reporting notes, diagnostic reporting notes, raw diagnostic summaries recovery-validation appendix roles. local smoke-read packaged validation protocol : particular, treat OverallStatus = "review" release-level recovery failure . validation bundle, UncertaintyStatus = "review" can mean SE/coverage evidence intentionally reported separate limitation recovery metrics remain acceptable. source-grounded release review plan, read packaged evidence map structured checklist. 0.2.2 files cover current public workflow, bounded-GPCM recovery-review refinements, sparse linked designs, peer-review design review, release-engineering gates; external common-data recovery summary remains 0.2.0 artifact separate workflow refreshed. links release checks ordered-response model literature, FACETS/Winsteps fit conventions, ADEMP-style simulation-study reporting, package’s current implementation boundaries. checklist classifies item required release evidence, caveat-managed evidence, future-scope evidence. external parameter-recovery summary records separate common-data simulation workflow. supports distinction recovery checks, cross-engine agreement, design endorsement: sparse stress designs can converge agree across engines still showing recovery, coverage, precision, role-bias risk. large generated datasets engine outputs bundled package; code: file.show(system.file(   "validation", "release-evidence-map-0.2.2.md",   package = "mfrmr" )) file.show(system.file(   "validation", "gpcm-post-0.2.2-roadmap.md",   package = "mfrmr" )) gpcm_spec <- build_mfrm_sim_spec(   n_person = 14,   n_rater = 2,   n_criterion = 2,   raters_per_person = 2,   model = "GPCM",   step_facet = "Criterion",   slope_facet = "Criterion",   slopes = c(0.85, 1.15),   assignment = "crossed" )  gpcm_rec <- evaluate_mfrm_recovery(   sim_spec = gpcm_spec,   reps = 1,   fit_method = "MML",   quad_points = 5,   maxit = 12,   include_person = FALSE,   include_diagnostics = TRUE,   diagnostic_fit_df_method = "both",   seed = 456 )  gpcm_review <- assess_mfrm_recovery(   gpcm_rec,   min_reps = 1,   max_rmse = c(slope = 2),   max_abs_bias = c(slope = 1),   min_se_available = NULL,   max_mcse_rmse_ratio = NULL )  gpcm_review$condition_reporting_notes[, c(   "ConditionArea", "ReportingAttention", "ConditionFinding" )] gpcm_review$condition_review[, c(   "Model", "GPCMSlopeRegime", "StressLevel", "ScoreSupportStatus" )] gpcm_review$diagnostic_reporting_notes[, c(   "Facet", "ReportingAttention", "DiagnosticFinding" )] gpcm_review$diagnostic_review[, c(   "Facet", "MeanSeparation", "MeanReliability", "ValidationUse" )] summary(gpcm_review)$reading_order plot(gpcm_review, type = "status") plot(gpcm_review, type = "metrics", metric = "rmse") source(system.file("validation", "recovery-validation.R", package = "mfrmr"))  validation <- mfrmr_run_recovery_validation(   case_ids = c("gpcm_slope_profile", "gpcm_high_dispersion_sparse"),   quick = TRUE,   seed = 20260525,   verbose = FALSE )  s_validation <- summary(validation) s_validation$reading_order s_validation$topline_release_decision s_validation$condition_reporting_notes[, c(   "CaseID", "ConditionArea", "ReportingAttention", "ConditionFinding" )] s_validation$condition_summary[, c(   "CaseID", "GPCMSlopeRegime", "ScoreSupportStatus" )] s_validation$diagnostic_reporting_notes[, c(   "CaseID", "Facet", "ReportingAttention", "DiagnosticFinding" )] s_validation$diagnostic_oc_summary[, c(   "CaseID", "Facet", "MeanSeparation", "MeanReliability",   "DiagnosticAvailability", "ValidationUse" )]  validation_bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(s_validation) validation_bundle$tables$reading_order validation_bundle$tables$condition_reporting_notes validation_bundle$tables$diagnostic_reporting_notes validation_bundle$tables$domain_decision_table  validation_appendix <- export_summary_appendix(   list(validation = s_validation),   output_dir = tempdir(),   prefix = "mfrmr_validation_appendix",   preset = "recommended",   include_html = FALSE,   overwrite = TRUE ) validation_appendix$selection_catalog  # The same validation summary can be supplied to # export_mfrm_bundle(..., summary_tables = list(validation = s_validation)) # when you want release-review what: Equal weighting and when to prefer the Rasch-family route
title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: mfrmr treats RSM / PCM package’s equal-weighting reference models. Rasch-family route, category discrimination fixed, operational scoring contract let psychometric model reweight item-facet combinations heavily others. Bounded GPCM serves different purpose. allows estimated slopes, observed design cells become influential others discrimination-based reweighting. often improves fit, better-fitting GPCM automatically make preferred operational model. package therefore recommends: prefer RSM / PCM equal contributions items raters part substantive scoring argument use bounded GPCM explicitly want inspect allow discrimination-based reweighting can defend choice validity grounds read RSM / PCM versus GPCM model-choice sensitivity question, contest fit alone decides winner One distinction matters. weight = argument fit_mfrm() observation-weight column. different equal-weighting question discussed . Observation weights adjust rating events enter estimation summaries; turn Rasch-family fit discrimination-based model.

what: Model selection guide and report wording
title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Use model argument match score interpretation first, use fit statistics diagnostics checks interpretation. Avoid shortcuts: describe whole package surface “Rasch-MFRM” now bounded GPCM implemented write bounded GPCM better operational scoring solely AIC, BIC, log-likelihood improves use FACETS-style score-side, operational scoring, calibrated design-forecasting, operational linking-synthesis language bounded GPCM; use APA/QC/export, design/screening, scorefile, linking helpers gpcm_capability_matrix() marks row supported_with_caveat manuscript, defensible model-choice sentence : treated RSM/PCM equal-weighting operational reference used bounded GPCM inspect whether allowing discrimination-based reweighting changed substantive conclusions. fitting candidate models, use build_model_choice_review() keep guidance attached actual fit objects:
code: review <- build_model_choice_review(RSM = fit_rsm, GPCM = fit_gpcm) summary(review)  # Add the detailed reweighting review when an RSM/PCM reference and bounded # GPCM sensitivity fit were estimated on the same response data. review <- build_model_choice_review(RSM = fit_rsm, GPCM = fit_gpcm,                                     run_weighting_review = TRUE)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: manuscript reviewer handoff, keep candidate-model comparison three separate layers: Use cmp inspect whether log-likelihood, AIC, BIC comparable basis. Use review$model_roles review$report_templates explain fit equal-weighting operational reference fit slope-aware sensitivity model. Use model_choice_tables APA table handoff, appendix export, inclusion HTML bundle. report bounded GPCM operational winner solely fit improved. defensible report needs comparison basis, score interpretation, weighting/sensitivity review, bounded-GPCM capability boundary visible output.
code: cmp <- compare_mfrm(RSM = fit_rsm, PCM = fit_pcm, GPCM = fit_gpcm) review <- build_model_choice_review(   RSM = fit_rsm,   PCM = fit_pcm,   GPCM = fit_gpcm,   run_weighting_review = TRUE ) model_choice_tables <- build_summary_table_bundle(   review,   appendix_preset = "recommended" )

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Fit model: fit_mfrm() Diagnostics: diagnose_mfrm() Optional residual-structure screen: analyze_residual_pca() Optional interaction bias: estimate_bias() Optional model-estimated facet interactions: interaction_effect_table() Differential-functioning analysis: analyze_dff(), dif_report() Model comparison: compare_mfrm() Reporting: apa_table(), build_apa_outputs(), build_visual_summaries() Quality control: run_qc_pipeline() Anchoring & linking: anchor_to_baseline(), detect_anchor_drift(), build_equating_chain() FACETS output-contract review needed: facets_output_contract_review(); checks package output contracts, external FACETS numerical equivalence Reproducible inspection: summary() plot(..., draw = FALSE) Dimensionality wording deliberately conservative. Residual PCA Q3-style local-dependence screens exploratory follow-evidence, standalone proofs unidimensionality established implementations DIMTEST/UNIDIM. MFRM manuscripts, combine global residual fit, element fit, residual PCA, local-dependence checks, use limited wording “evidence consistent essential unidimensionality specified facet structure.”
code: fit_mfrm() --> diagnose_mfrm() --> reporting / advanced analysis                     |                     +--> analyze_residual_pca()                    +--> estimate_bias()                     +--> interaction_effect_table()                     +--> analyze_dff()                     +--> compare_mfrm()                     +--> run_qc_pipeline()                     +--> anchor_to_baseline() / detect_anchor_drift()

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: facet 3-10 levels, fixed-effects many-facet model retains wide per-level SEs. Empirical-Bayes partial pooling (Efron & Morris, 1973) dominates MLE squared-error loss whenever K >= 3.
code: # Integrated path: shrinkage applied as part of the fit. fit_eb <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                    method = "MML", quad_points = 15,                    facet_shrinkage = "empirical_bayes") shrinkage_report(fit_eb) plot(fit_eb, type = "shrinkage", show_ci = TRUE)  # Post-hoc path: apply to an existing fit. fit_post <- apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(fit) head(fit_post$facets$others[, c("Facet", "Level", "Estimate",                                  "ShrunkEstimate", "ShrinkageFactor")])

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models

Replacement counts kept fit$prep$missing_recoding surfaced build_mfrm_manifest()$missing_recoding. default (missing_codes = NULL) strictly backward-compatible. standalone recode_missing_codes() helper exported users prefer recode calling fit_mfrm().

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models

Downstream precision summaries distinguish model_based, hybrid, exploratory tiers. Use precision_review_report() need decide strongly phrase SE, CI, reliability claims. Typical pattern:
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core")  fit_final <- fit_mfrm(   toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 15 )  diag_final <- diagnose_mfrm(   fit_final,   diagnostic_mode = "both",   residual_pca = "none" )  precision_review_report(fit_final, diagnostics = diag_final)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Full marginal-likelihood strict-marginal derivations, along literature positioning (Bock & Aitkin, 1981; Linacre, 1989; Eckes, 2005; Orlando & Thissen, 2000; Haberman & Sinharay, 2013; Sinharay & Monroe, 2025), collected dedicated vignette:
code: vignette("mfrmr-mml-and-marginal-fit", package = "mfrmr")

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: load_mfrmr_data("example_core"): compact, approximately unidimensional example fitting, diagnostics, plots, reports. load_mfrmr_data("example_bias"): compact example known Group x Criterion differential-functioning Rater x Criterion interaction signals bias-focused help pages. load_mfrmr_data("study1") / load_mfrmr_data("study2"): larger Eckes/Jin-inspired synthetic studies realistic end--end analyses. Direct dataset access also works data("mfrmr_example_core", package = "mfrmr") data("mfrmr_example_bias", package = "mfrmr").

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
code: library(mfrmr)  data("mfrmr_example_core", package = "mfrmr") df <- mfrmr_example_core  # Fit fit <- fit_mfrm(   data = df,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   model = "RSM",   quad_points = 7 ) summary(fit)  # Fast diagnostics first diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") summary(diag)  # APA outputs apa <- build_apa_outputs(fit, diag) cat(apa$report_text)  # QC pipeline reuses the same diagnostics object qc <- run_qc_pipeline(fit, diagnostics = diag) summary(qc)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: package workflows reuse small set objects rather recomputing everything scratch. canonical list kept date summary(fit) “Next actions”; items short orientation pointer. fit: fitted model object returned fit_mfrm() diag: diagnostic summaries returned diagnose_mfrm() chk: reporting manuscript-draft checks returned reporting_checklist() apa: structured APA/report draft outputs returned build_apa_outputs() sc: connectivity linking summaries returned subset_connectivity_report() bias / dff: interaction screening differential-functioning results returned estimate_bias() analyze_dff() Typical reuse pattern:
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core")  fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 7) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) apa <- build_apa_outputs(fit, diag) sc <- subset_connectivity_report(fit, diagnostics = diag)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: endpoint manuscript internal report, use package-native reporting contract rather composing text hand. question-based map reporting API, see help("mfrmr_reporting_and_apa", package = "mfrmr").
code: diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none")  # Add `bias_results = ...` to either helper when bias screening should # appear in the checklist or draft text. chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) chk$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady", "Priority", "NextAction")]  apa <- build_apa_outputs(   fit,   diag,   context = list(     assessment = "Writing assessment",     setting = "Local scoring study",     scale_desc = "0-4 rubric scale",     rater_facet = "Rater"   ) )  cat(apa$report_text) apa$section_map[, c("SectionId", "Available", "Heading")]  tbl_fit <- apa_table(fit, which = "summary") tbl_reliability <- apa_table(fit, which = "reliability", diagnostics = diag)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: task-oriented index plotting surface lives help("mfrmr_visual_diagnostics", package = "mfrmr"), worked publication examples collected vignette("mfrmr-visual-diagnostics", package = "mfrmr"). common starter patterns : interval-aware figures tables, start : interval guide separates Wald, delta-method, profile-like, plotting overlay routes 95% CI displays read precision screening evidence, automatic fit, fairness, validity decisions. second-wave teaching / drift / agreement layer ships follow-inspection; default reporting figure set:
code: plot(fit, type = "wright", preset = "publication", show_ci = TRUE) plot(fit, type = "pathway", preset = "publication") plot(fit, type = "ccc", preset = "publication") plot_qc_dashboard(fit, diagnostics = diag, preset = "publication") mfrmr_interval_guide("visual")[, c("Route", "PrimaryHelper", "Basis")] plot_fair_average(fit, show_ci = TRUE, ci_level = 0.95) plot_bias_interaction(bias, plot = "ranked", show_ci = TRUE, ci_level = 0.95) plot_rater_severity_profile(fit, ci_level = 0.95) plot_apa_figure_one(fit, ci_level = 0.95, draw = FALSE) fm <- fit_measures_table(fit, ci_level = 0.95) plot(fm, type = "measure_ci") plot_guttman_scalogram(fit, diagnostics = diag)       # teaching ordering view plot_residual_qq(fit, diagnostics = diag)             # residual tail follow-up plot_rater_agreement_heatmap(fit, diagnostics = diag) # compact pairwise agreement plot_rater_trajectory(list(T1 = fit_a, T2 = fit_b))   # requires anchor-linked waves

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Use route design spans forms, waves, subgroup comparisons. linking-specific guidance, see help("mfrmr_linking_and_dff", package = "mfrmr").
code: data("mfrmr_example_bias", package = "mfrmr") df_bias <- mfrmr_example_bias fit_bias <- fit_mfrm(df_bias, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                      method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 7) diag_bias <- diagnose_mfrm(fit_bias, residual_pca = "none")  # Connectivity and design coverage sc <- subset_connectivity_report(fit_bias, diagnostics = diag_bias) summary(sc) plot(sc, type = "design_matrix", preset = "publication")  # Anchor export from a baseline fit anchors <- make_anchor_table(fit_bias, facets = "Criterion") head(anchors)  # Differential facet functioning dff <- analyze_dff(   fit_bias,   diag_bias,   facet = "Criterion",   group = "Group",   data = df_bias,   method = "residual" ) dff$summary plot_dif_heatmap(dff) plot_dif_summary(dff)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Interpretation rules: residual DFF screening route. refit DFF can support logit-scale contrasts subgroup linking adequate. Residual-method classifications screening labels, ETS /B/C severity categories. Check ScaleLinkStatus, ContrastComparable, reported classification system treating contrast strong interpretive claim.
code: data("mfrmr_example_bias", package = "mfrmr") df_bias <- mfrmr_example_bias fit_bias <- fit_mfrm(df_bias, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                      method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 7) diag_bias <- diagnose_mfrm(fit_bias, residual_pca = "none")  dff <- analyze_dff(fit_bias, diag_bias, facet = "Criterion",                    group = "Group", data = df_bias, method = "residual") dff$dif_table dff$summary  # Cell-level interaction table dit <- dif_interaction_table(fit_bias, diag_bias, facet = "Criterion",                              group = "Group", data = df_bias)  # Visual, narrative, and bias reports plot_dif_heatmap(dff) plot_dif_summary(dff)  # Optional display controls for review meetings or appendices plot_dif_heatmap(dff, metric = "t", flag_threshold = 2,                  show_values = FALSE, scale_limit = 3) plot_dif_summary(dff, ci_level = 0.90,                  effect_thresholds = c(screen = 0.5)) dr <- dif_report(dff) cat(dr$narrative)  # Refit-based contrasts can support ETS labels only when subgroup linking is adequate dff_refit <- analyze_dff(fit_bias, diag_bias, facet = "Criterion",                          group = "Group", data = df_bias, method = "refit") dff_refit$summary  bias <- estimate_bias(fit_bias, diag_bias, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion") summary(bias)  # App-style batch bias estimation across all modeled facet pairs bias_all <- estimate_all_bias(fit_bias, diag_bias) bias_all$summary

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: confirmatory interaction hypotheses, fit_mfrm() can estimate explicit two-way non-person facet interactions model likelihood. Rules interpretation: Name facet pair explicitly. package add possible interactions automatically. current scope two-way interactions non-person facets RSM PCM; GPCM, person-involving, higher-order, random-effect interaction terms deferred. interaction matrix uses zero marginal sums, estimate deviation additive main-effects MFRM. Positive values indicate higher--expected scores facet-level combination; negative values indicate lower--expected scores. interaction_effect_table() reports model-estimated fixed effects. estimate_bias() estimate_all_bias() remain residual screening tools exploratory bias review. Sparse cells matter. Use min_obs_per_interaction inspect Sparse column reporting substantive interaction claims.
code: fit_add <- fit_mfrm(df, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                     method = "MML", model = "RSM")  fit_rxcrit <- fit_mfrm(df, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                        method = "MML", model = "RSM",                        facet_interactions = "Rater:Criterion")  interaction_effect_table(fit_rxcrit) compare_mfrm(Additive = fit_add, RaterCriterion = fit_rxcrit, nested = TRUE)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
code: fit_rsm <- fit_mfrm(df, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                      method = "MML", model = "RSM") fit_pcm <- fit_mfrm(df, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                      method = "MML", model = "PCM", step_facet = "Criterion") cmp <- compare_mfrm(RSM = fit_rsm, PCM = fit_pcm) cmp$table  # Request nested tests only when models are truly nested and fit on the same basis cmp_nested <- compare_mfrm(RSM = fit_rsm, PCM = fit_pcm, nested = TRUE) cmp_nested$comparison_basis  # RSM design-weighted precision curves info <- compute_information(fit_rsm) plot_information(info)

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Notes: Use build_mfrm_sim_spec() want one explicit, reusable data-generating mechanism. Use extract_mfrm_sim_spec(fit) want fit-derived starting point later design study. Use extract_mfrm_sim_spec(fit, latent_distribution = "empirical", assignment = "resampled") want semi-parametric design study reuses empirical fitted spreads observed rater-assignment profiles. Use extract_mfrm_sim_spec(fit, latent_distribution = "empirical", assignment = "skeleton") want plasmode-style study preserves observed person--facet design skeleton resimulates responses. summary(sim_eval)$ademp records simulation-study contract: aims, DGM, estimands, methods, performance measures. evaluate_mfrm_design() Monte Carlo design-evaluation helper. can show separation, reliability, strata, RMSE, fit-screen rates change facet counts vary; use mfrm_generalizability() plus mfrm_d_study() observed G-study components analytic D-study projections. analytic G/D-study projections, read IdentificationStatus status columns using numeric bands. Boundary singular mixed-model fits kept design-identification warnings, large G Phi automatically become high-stakes-ready evidence.
code: spec <- build_mfrm_sim_spec(   n_person = 50,   n_rater = 4,   n_criterion = 4,   raters_per_person = 2,   assignment = "rotating",   model = "RSM" )  sim_eval <- evaluate_mfrm_design(   n_person = c(30, 50, 80),   n_rater = 4,   n_criterion = 4,   raters_per_person = 2,   reps = 2,   maxit = 30,   sim_spec = spec,   seed = 123 )  s_sim <- summary(sim_eval) s_sim$design_summary s_sim$ademp  rec <- recommend_mfrm_design(sim_eval) rec$recommended  plot(sim_eval, facet = "Rater", metric = "separation", x_var = "n_person") plot(sim_eval, facet = "Criterion", metric = "severityrmse", x_var = "n_person") if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   gt <- mfrm_generalizability(fit)   gt$coefficients[, c("G", "Phi", "GStatus", "PhiStatus",                       "IdentificationStatus")]    ds <- mfrm_d_study(     gt,     data.frame(Rater = c(2, 3, 4), Criterion = 4),     residual_scaling = "sensitivity"   )   ds[, c("n_Rater", "n_Criterion", "ResidualScaling",          "G", "Phi", "GStatus", "PhiStatus", "IdentificationStatus")] }

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Use assignment = "sparse_linked" design contain planned missingness: persons receive small rater subset, linking set receives larger rater set preserve common-person links among raters. true data-generating simulation route, unlike observed-data resampling . sparse-design metadata reports design density, planned missing rate, rater coverage, rater-pair common-person counts users can inspect whether generated rating network enough linking study intend run. table bundle keeps sparse diagnostics separate appendix-ready table, rather mixing performance metrics. LinkReviewStatus column flags zero common-person rater pairs requested-link target shortfalls design-review items; parameter-recovery model-fit decision.
code: sparse_spec <- build_mfrm_sim_spec(   n_person = 80,   n_rater = 6,   n_criterion = 4,   raters_per_person = 2,   assignment = "sparse_linked",   sparse_controls = list(     link_fraction = 0.10,     link_raters_per_person = 6,     min_common_persons_per_rater_pair = 4   ) )  sparse_sim <- simulate_mfrm_data(sim_spec = sparse_spec, seed = 20260526) sparse_design <- attr(sparse_sim, "mfrm_sparse_design") sparse_design$overview sparse_design$rater_pair_links  sparse_eval <- evaluate_mfrm_design(   n_person = c(40, 80),   n_rater = 6,   n_criterion = 4,   raters_per_person = 2,   assignment = "sparse_linked",   sparse_controls = list(     link_fraction = 0.10,     link_raters_per_person = 6,     min_common_persons_per_rater_pair = 4   ),   reps = 2,   maxit = 30,   seed = 20260526,   progress = FALSE ) summary(sparse_eval)$design_summary[   ,   c("Facet", "n_person", "MeanDesignDensity",     "MeanPlannedMissingRate", "MeanMinCommonPersonsPerRaterPair") ] summary(sparse_eval)$sparse_review  plot(   sparse_eval,   facet = "Rater",   metric = "plannedmissingrate",   x_var = "n_person",   draw = FALSE )  sparse_bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(summary(sparse_eval)) sparse_bundle$tables$sparse_review sparse_bundle$tables$sparse_design

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Use build_peer_review_sim_spec() submissions reviewers drawn participant pool, peer-assessment peer-review scoring studies. helper builds fixed skeleton self-review can excluded design, ordinary submissions can receive small peer set, smaller anchor set can reviewed many eligible peers common-link support. peer-review metadata reports assignment density, self-review counts, reviewer load, reciprocal review pairs, common submissions per reviewer pair. design diagnostics. establish peer fairness, reviewer quality, fit, separation, parameter recovery. metadata can carried build_mfrm_network_review() model fit, peer-review assignment checks appear alongside graph connectedness bridge/articulation diagnostics.
code: peer_spec <- build_peer_review_sim_spec(   n_submission = 30,   n_criterion = 4,   reviewers_per_submission = 3,   anchor_fraction = 0.10,   avoid_self_review = TRUE )  peer_sim <- simulate_mfrm_data(sim_spec = peer_spec, seed = 20260526) peer_review <- build_peer_review_design_review(peer_sim) summary(peer_review)$overview[   ,   c("Submissions", "Reviewers", "ReviewPairs", "SelfReviews",     "MinCommonSubmissionsPerReviewerPair", "ZeroCommonReviewerPairs") ]  peer_bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(peer_review) peer_bundle$tables$low_common_pairs if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   peer_fit <- fit_mfrm(     peer_sim,     person = "Person",     facets = c("Reviewer", "Criterion"),     score = "Score",     method = "JML",     maxit = 30   )    peer_net <- build_mfrm_network_review(     peer_fit,     peer_review_design = peer_sim,     top_n = 8   )   summary(peer_net)$peer_review }

title: Estimation and Diagnostics for Many-Facet Measurement Models
text: Use build_mfrm_network_review() question whether observed person--facet design well linked enough support common-scale interpretation. helper wraps mfrm_network_analysis() keeps graph diagnostics separate MFRM fit, separation, recovery, rater-quality claims. sparse simulations, pass generated sparse-design metadata review can show observed network vulnerability planned-missingness link diagnostics. route follows linking-set sparse-design literature treating connected components, articulation points, bridge edges, common-person rater links design evidence. turn network centrality person measure, rater severity estimate, fit statistic, recovery gate.
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "JML",   maxit = 30 )  if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   net_review <- build_mfrm_network_review(fit, top_n = 8)   summary(net_review)$overview   summary(net_review)$top_cut_nodes   summary(net_review)$top_bridge_edges    net_bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(net_review)   net_bundle$tables$overview   net_bundle$tables$facet_summary } if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   net_review <- build_mfrm_network_review(     fit,     sparse_design = sparse_design,     top_n = 8   )   summary(net_review)$sparse_review }

code: toy_pred <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy_pred,   "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7 )  raters <- unique(toy_pred$Rater)[1:2] criteria <- unique(toy_pred$Criterion)[1:2]  new_units <- data.frame(   Person = c("NEW01", "NEW01", "NEW02", "NEW02"),   Rater = c(raters[1], raters[2], raters[1], raters[2]),   Criterion = c(criteria[1], criteria[2], criteria[1], criteria[2]),   Score = c(2, 3, 2, 4) )  pred_units <- predict_mfrm_units(toy_fit, new_units, n_draws = 0) summary(pred_units)$estimates[, c("Person", "Estimate", "Lower", "Upper")]  pv_units <- sample_mfrm_plausible_values(   toy_fit,   new_units,   n_draws = 3,   seed = 123 ) summary(pv_units)$draw_summary[, c("Person", "Draws", "MeanValue")]

code: d1 <- load_mfrmr_data("study1") d2 <- load_mfrmr_data("study2") fit1 <- fit_mfrm(d1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 25) fit2 <- fit_mfrm(d2, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 25)  # Anchored calibration res <- anchor_to_baseline(d2, fit1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score") summary(res) res$drift  # Drift detection drift <- detect_anchor_drift(list(Wave1 = fit1, Wave2 = fit2)) summary(drift) plot_anchor_drift(drift, type = "drift")  # Screened linking chain chain <- build_equating_chain(list(Form1 = fit1, Form2 = fit2)) summary(chain) plot_anchor_drift(chain, type = "chain")

NULL (default), mfrmr tries recover data fit$prep$data. slot holds columns fit_mfrm() actually modelled; grouping column among (common DIF screening), pass original data frame via data = <df> explicitly. applies fit object serialized without prep slot. focal Optional character vector group levels treat focal. NULL (default), pairwise group comparisons performed. method Analysis method: "residual" (default) uses fitted model's residuals without re-estimation; "refit" re-estimates model within group subset. residual method faster avoids convergence issues small subsets. min_obs Minimum number observations per cell (facet-level x group). Cells threshold flagged sparse statistics set NA. Default 10. p_adjust Method multiple-comparison adjustment, passed stats::p.adjust(). Default "holm". ... Passed directly analyze_dff().

title: Differential facet functioning analysis — analyze_dff
text: Differential facet functioning (DFF) occurs difficulty severity facet element differs across subgroups population, controlling overall ability.  MFRM context generalises classical DIF (applies items) facet: raters, criteria, tasks, etc. Differential functioning threat measurement fairness: Criterion 1 harder Group Group B ability level, measurement scale longer group-invariant. Two methods available: Residual method (method = "residual"): Uses existing fitted model's observation-level residuals.  facet-level \(\times\) group cell, observed expected score sums aggregated standardized residual computed : $$z = \frac{\sum (X_{obs} - E_{exp})}{\sqrt{\sum \mathrm{Var}}}$$ Pairwise contrasts groups compare mean observed-minus-expected difference facet level, uncertainty summarized Welch/Satterthwaite approximation. method fast, stable small subsets, require re-estimation. resulting contrast logit-scale parameter difference, residual method treated screening procedure rather ETS-style classifier. Refit method (method = "refit"): Subsets data group, refits MFRM model within subset, anchors non-target facets back baseline calibration possible, compares resulting facet-level estimates using Welch t-statistic: $$t = \frac{\hat{\delta}_1 - \hat{\delta}_2}                {\sqrt{SE_1^2 + SE_2^2}}$$ provides group-specific parameter estimates common scale linking anchors available, slower may encounter convergence issues small subsets.  ETS categories reported contrasts whose subgroup calibrations retained enough linking anchors support common-scale interpretation whose subgroup precision remained package's model-based MML path. facet refers item-like facet (example Criterion), recovers familiar DIF case. facet refers raters prompts/tasks, machinery supports DRF/DPF-style analyses. refit method , effect size classified following ETS (Educational Testing Service) DIF guidelines subgroup calibrations linked eligible model-based inference: (Negligible): \(|\Delta| <\) 0.43 logits B (Moderate): 0.43 \(\le |\Delta| <\) 0.64 logits C (Large): \(|\Delta| \ge\) 0.64 logits Multiple comparisons adjusted using Holm's step-procedure default, controls family-wise error rate without assuming independence.  Alternative methods (e.g., "BH" false discovery rate) can specified via p_adjust.

Use method = "refit" specifically want group-specific parameter estimates can tolerate extra computation.  methods yield similar conclusions sample sizes adequate (\(N \ge 100\) per group useful guideline stable differential-functioning detection).

title: Differential facet functioning analysis — analyze_dff

Includes Method, N_Group1, N_Group2, EffectMetric, ClassificationSystem, ContrastBasis, SEBasis, StatisticLabel, ProbabilityMetric, DFBasis, ReportingUse, PrimaryReportingEligible, sparse columns. $cell_table: (residual method ) per-cell detail N, ObsScore, ExpScore, ObsExpAvg, StdResidual. $summary: counts screening result (method = "residual") ETS category plus linked-screening insufficient-linking rows (method = "refit"). $group_fits: (refit method ) list per-group facet estimates subgroup linking diagnostics. $gpcm_boundary: bounded GPCM fits, capability-boundary table marking DFF/DIF output caveated screening evidence.

title: Differential facet functioning analysis — analyze_dff
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias")  fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 7, maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both") dff <- analyze_dff(fit, diag, facet = "Rater", group = "Group", data = toy) dff$summary # Look for: a small `FlaggedPairs` count relative to `Pairs`. Under #   method = "residual", `ClassificationSystem` is "screening", not #   ETS. "Screen positive" rows are prompts for substantive review. head(dff$dif_table[, c("Level", "Group1", "Group2", "Contrast",                        "Classification", "ClassificationSystem")]) # The residual contrast is an observed-minus-expected average contrast # between groups. It is useful for screening, but it is not an ETS # A/B/C logit-delta classification. dff_refit <- analyze_dff(fit, diag, facet = "Rater", group = "Group",                          data = toy, method = "refit") unique(dff_refit$dif_table$ClassificationSystem) # Look for: "ETS" only when subgroup calibration, linking, and precision #   checks all support a common-scale model-based contrast. sc <- subset_connectivity_report(fit, diagnostics = diag) plot(sc, type = "design_matrix", draw = FALSE) if ("ScaleLinkStatus" %in% names(dff_refit$dif_table)) {   unique(dff_refit$dif_table$ScaleLinkStatus) } # Look for: "linked" in `ScaleLinkStatus` confirms the focal and #   reference groups share enough common elements for a comparable #   contrast; "demoted_*" rows lose linking under the refit branch #   and should be read as exploratory. } # }

text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). NULL, diagnostics computed residual_pca = "none". facet Character scalar naming non-person facet evaluate. Choose equivalence_bound based smallest difference practically meaningful assessment context (commonly 0.3 0.5 logits rater-mediated designs) check $summary per-level SE magnitude drawing conclusions. ci_level Confidence level used forest-style interval view. title: Analyze practical equivalence within a facet — analyze_facet_equivalence
text: named list class mfrm_facet_equivalence.

title: Analyze practical equivalence within a facet — analyze_facet_equivalence
text: function tests whether facet elements (e.g., raters) similar enough treated practically interchangeable, rather merely testing whether differ significantly.  key distinction standard chi-square heterogeneity test: absence evidence difference evidence equivalence. function uses existing facet estimates standard errors diagnostics$measures; re-estimation performed. bundle combines four complementary views: Fixed chi-square test: tests \(H_0\): element measures equal.  non-significant result necessary sufficient interchangeability. reported context, direct evidence equivalence. Pairwise TOST (Two One-Sided Tests): pair elements, tests whether difference falls within \(\pm\)equivalence_bound.  TOST procedure (Schuirmann, 1987) rejects null hypothesis non-equivalence one-sided tests significant level \(\alpha\).  pair declared "Equivalent" TOST p-value < 0.05. BIC-based Bayes-factor heuristic: approximate screening tool (full Bayesian inference) compares evidence common-facet model (elements equal) heterogeneity model (elements differ) via \(\mathrm{BF}_{01} \approx \exp((\mathrm{BIC}_{H_1} -    \mathrm{BIC}_{H_0}) / 2)\) (Kass & Raftery, 1995).  Values > 3 favour common-facet model; < 1/3 favour heterogeneity. ROPE-style grand-mean proximity: proportion element's normal-approximation confidence distribution falls within \(\pm\)equivalence_bound weighted grand mean. descriptive proximity summary, Bayesian ROPE decision rule around prespecified null value. Choosing equivalence_bound: default 0.5 logits moderate criterion.  high-stakes certification, 0.3 logits may appropriate; exploratory low-stakes contexts, 1.0 logits may suffice.  bound reflect smallest difference practically meaningful application.

title: Analyze practical equivalence within a facet — analyze_facet_equivalence
text: non-significant chi-square result evidence equivalence. Forest/ROPE displays descriptive replace pairwise TOST decision rule. BIC-based Bayes-factor summary heuristic screen, full Bayesian equivalence analysis.

title: Analyze practical equivalence within a facet — analyze_facet_equivalence
text: Start summary$Decision, conservative summary pairwise TOST results. use remaining tables context: chi_square: broad heterogeneity facet? pairwise: specific pairs meet practical-equivalence bound? rope / forest: close level facet grand mean? Smaller equivalence_bound values make criterion stricter. decision "partial_pairwise_equivalence", means pairwise contrasts satisfy practical-equivalence bound .

title: Analyze practical equivalence within a facet — analyze_facet_equivalence
text: final Decision pairwise TOST summary rather global equivalence proof. pairwise contrasts satisfy practical- equivalence bound, facet labeled "all_pairs_equivalent". least one, , pairwise contrasts equivalent, facet labeled "partial_pairwise_equivalence". pairwise contrasts meet practical-equivalence bound, facet labeled "no_pairwise_equivalence_established". chi-square, Bayes-factor, grand-mean proximity summaries reported descriptive context.

title: Analyze the hierarchical structure of a rating design — analyze_hierarchical_structure

Plot via plot(x, type = "crosstab", pair = "FacetA__FacetB"). icc: per-facet variance shares. See compute_facet_icc() two-scale interpretation. design_effect: Kish (1965) Deff EffectiveN. connectivity: number bipartite components linking Person x facet levels. single component required common measurement scale; multiple components indicate disconnected design.

code: analyze_residual_pca(   diagnostics,   mode = c("overall", "facet", "both"),   facets = NULL,   pca_max_factors = 10L,   parallel = FALSE,   parallel_reps = 200L,   parallel_quantile = 0.95,   parallel_method = c("residual_permutation"),   seed = NULL )

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: diagnostics Output diagnose_mfrm() fit_mfrm(). mode "overall", "facet", "". facets Optional subset facets facet-specific PCA. pca_max_factors Maximum number retained components. parallel Logical; TRUE, add residual-permutation parallel analysis PCA tables. parallel_reps Number residual permutations used parallel = TRUE. parallel_quantile Upper null quantile used exploratory comparison cutoff. default (0.95) follows common parallel analysis convention. parallel_method Parallel-analysis null method. Currently "residual_permutation" implemented: standardized residuals permuted within residual column, preserving column's residual distribution missingness pattern breaking residual association. seed Optional integer seed reproducible residual permutations.

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: named list : mode: resolved mode used computation facet_names: facets analyzed overall: overall PCA bundle (NULL) by_facet: named list facet PCA bundles overall_table: variance table overall PCA by_facet_table: stacked variance table across facets parallel_settings, parallel_overall_table, parallel_by_facet_table, parallel_status: returned every call; parallel tables populated parallel = TRUE errors: named list per-facet PCA errors caught turned NA_real_ rows variance tables (e.g., psych::principal() failure near-singular residual matrix). list empty every facet PCA succeeded. warnings: named list non-fatal PCA warnings captured underlying PCA engine. indicate exploratory boundary conditions, confirmatory evidence.

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: function works standardized residual structures derived diagnose_mfrm(). fitted object fit_mfrm() supplied, diagnostics computed internally. Conceptually, follows Rasch residual-PCA tradition examining structure model residuals primary Rasch dimension extracted. mfrmr, however, implementation exploratory many-facet adaptation: works standardized residual matrices built person x combined-facet person x facet-level layouts, rather reproducing FACETS/Winsteps residual-contrast tables one--one. Residual PCA therefore reported residual-structure evidence, formal proof unidimensionality. also described DIMTEST UNIDIM: essential-unidimensionality tests require separate item-response-layer definition uniquely determined many-facet long data set. applied MFRM reporting, residual PCA best triangulated global residual fit, element fit, Q3-style local-dependence screens. Output tables use: Component: principal-component index (1, 2, ...) Eigenvalue: eigenvalue component Proportion: component variance proportion Cumulative: cumulative variance proportion parallel = TRUE, variance tables additionally include data-driven null summaries: ParallelMean: mean permuted-residual eigenvalue ParallelCutoff: parallel_quantile cutoff permuted eigenvalues ExcessOverParallelCutoff: observed eigenvalue minus cutoff ExceedsParallelCutoff: whether observed eigenvalue exceeds permutation cutoff default parallel_reps = 200 intended practical review setting. stable final reporting 95% cutoff, use larger value residual matrix size makes computationally reasonable. mode = "facet" "", by_facet_table additionally includes Facet column. summary(pca) supported summary(). plot(pca) dispatched plot() class mfrm_residual_pca. Available types include "overall_scree", "facet_scree", "overall_parallel_scree", "facet_parallel_scree", "overall_parallel_excess", "facet_parallel_excess", "overall_loadings", "facet_loadings".

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: Use overall_table first: early components noticeably larger eigenvalues proportions suggest stronger residual structure may deserve follow-. Small early components can described evidence consistent specified one-dimensional facet structure fit local-dependence screens tell story. inspect by_facet_table: helps localize facet contributes residual structure. Finally, inspect loadings via plot_residual_pca() identify variables/elements drive component.

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: residual-PCA idea follows Rasch residual-structure literature, especially Linacre's discussions principal components Rasch residuals. current mfrmr implementation interpreted exploratory extension many-facet workflows rather direct reproduction single FACETS/Winsteps output table. optional parallel analysis follows Horn's data-driven eigenvalue comparison logic later recommendations compare observed eigenvalues high quantiles empirical null distribution. mfrmr applies standardized Rasch-family residual matrices, null distribution generated within-column residual permutation rather simulating raw item scores. Horn, J. L. (1965). rationale test number factors factor analysis. Psychometrika, 30, 179-185. Glorfeld, L. W. (1995). improvement Horn's parallel analysis methodology selecting correct number factors retain. Educational Psychological Measurement, 55, 377-393. Hayton, J. C., Allen, D. G., & Scarpello, V. (2004). Factor retention decisions exploratory factor analysis: tutorial parallel analysis. Organizational Research Methods, 7, 191-205. Timmerman, M. E., & Lorenzo-Seva, U. (2011). Dimensionality assessment ordered polytomous items parallel analysis. Psychological Methods, 16, 209-220. Linacre, J. M. (1998). Structure Rasch residuals: principal components analysis (PCA)? Rasch Measurement Transactions, 12(2), 636. Linacre, J. M. (1998). Detecting multidimensionality: residual data-type works best? Journal Outcome Measurement, 2(3), 266-283. Eckes, T. (2005). Examining rater effects TestDaF writing speaking performance assessments: many-facet Rasch analysis. Language Assessment Quarterly, 2(3), 197-221. Yamashita, T. (2024). application many-facet Rasch measurement evaluate automated essay scoring: case ChatGPT-4.0. Research Methods Applied Linguistics, 3(3), 100133. Uto, M. (2021). multidimensional generalized many-facet Rasch model rubric-based performance assessment. Behaviormetrika, 48(2), 425-457. Aryadoust, V., Ng, L. Y., & Sayama, H. (2021). comprehensive review Rasch measurement language assessment: Recommendations guidelines research. Language Testing, 38(1), 6-40. Tseng, W.-T. (2016). Measuring English vocabulary size via computerized adaptive testing. Computers & Education, 97, 69-85.

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
text: Fit model run diagnose_mfrm() residual_pca = "none" "". Call analyze_residual_pca(..., mode = ""). Review summary(pca), plot scree/loadings. Cross-check fit/misfit diagnostics conclusions.

title: Run exploratory residual PCA summaries — analyze_residual_pca
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "both") pca <- analyze_residual_pca(diag, mode = "both") pca2 <- analyze_residual_pca(fit, mode = "both") summary(pca) p <- plot_residual_pca(pca, mode = "overall", plot_type = "scree", draw = FALSE) p$data$plot head(p$data) pca_pa <- analyze_residual_pca(diag, mode = "overall", parallel = TRUE, parallel_reps = 10) head(pca_pa$overall_table) head(pca$overall_table) } # }

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: Re-estimates fitted many-facet model new data holding selected facet parameters fixed values previous (baseline) calibration. standard workflow placing new data onto existing scale, linking test forms, carrying baseline calibration across administration windows. bounded GPCM, treat direct exploratory anchor/drift support rather package's formal linking-synthesis route.

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
code: anchor_to_baseline(   new_data,   baseline_fit,   person,   facets,   score,   anchor_facets = NULL,   include_person = FALSE,   weight = NULL,   model = NULL,   method = NULL,   anchor_policy = "warn",   ... )  # S3 method for class 'mfrm_anchored_fit' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'mfrm_anchored_fit' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.mfrm_anchored_fit' print(x, ...)

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: new_data Data frame long format (one row per rating). baseline_fit mfrm_fit object previous calibration. person Character column name person/examinee. facets Character vector facet column names. score Character column name rating score. anchor_facets Character vector facets anchor (default: non-Person facets). include_person TRUE, also anchor person estimates. weight Optional character column name observation weights. model Scale model override; defaults baseline model. method Estimation method override; defaults baseline method. anchor_policy handle anchor issues: "warn", "error", "silent". ... Ignored. x mfrm_anchored_fit object. object mfrm_anchored_fit object (summary).

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: Object class mfrm_anchored_fit components: fit anchored mfrm_fit object. diagnostics Output diagnose_mfrm() anchored fit. baseline_anchors Anchor table extracted baseline. drift Tibble element-level drift statistics.

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: function automates baseline-anchored calibration workflow: Extracts anchor values baseline fit using make_anchor_table(). Re-estimates model new_data anchors fixed via fit_mfrm(..., anchors = anchor_table). Runs diagnose_mfrm() anchored fit. Computes element-level differences (new estimate minus baseline estimate) every common element. model method arguments default baseline fit's settings calibration framework remains consistent.  Elements present anchor table absent new data handled according anchor_policy: "warn" (default) emits message, "error" stops execution, "silent" ignores silently. returned drift table best interpreted anchored consistency check. facet fixed anchor_facets, anchored levels constrained new run, reported differences independent drift analysis. genuine cross-wave drift monitoring, fit waves separately use detect_anchor_drift() resulting fits. Element-level differences calculated every element appears baseline new calibration: $$\Delta_e = \hat{\delta}_{e,\text{new}} - \hat{\delta}_{e,\text{base}}$$ element flagged \(|\Delta_e| > 0.5\) logits \(|\Delta_e / SE_{\Delta_e}| > 2.0\), \(SE_{\Delta_e} = \sqrt{SE_{\mathrm{base}}^2 + SE_{\mathrm{new}}^2}\).

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: Use anchor_to_baseline() one new dataset want place directly baseline scale. Use detect_anchor_drift() already multiple fitted waves want compare stability. Use build_equating_chain() need cumulative offsets across ordered series waves.

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: $drift: one row per common element columns Facet, Level, Baseline, New, Drift, SE_Baseline, SE_New, SE_Diff, Drift_SE_Ratio, Flag. Read anchored consistency table. Small absolute differences indicate anchored re-fit stayed close baseline scale. Flagged rows warrant review, substitute separate drift study unanchored common elements. $fit: full anchored mfrm_fit object, usable diagnose_mfrm(), measurable_summary_table(), etc. $diagnostics: pre-computed diagnostics anchored calibration. $baseline_anchors: anchor table fed fit_mfrm(), useful reviewing elements constrained.

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
text: Fit baseline model: fit1 <- fit_mfrm(...). Collect new data (e.g., later administration). Call res <- anchor_to_baseline(new_data, fit1, ...). Inspect summary(res) confirm anchored run remains close baseline scale. multi-wave drift monitoring, fit waves separately pass fits detect_anchor_drift() build_equating_chain().

title: Fit new data anchored to a baseline calibration — anchor_to_baseline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ d1 <- load_mfrmr_data("study1") keep1 <- unique(d1$Person)[1:15] d1 <- d1[d1$Person %in% keep1, , drop = FALSE] fit1 <- fit_mfrm(d1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) d2 <- load_mfrmr_data("study2") keep2 <- unique(d2$Person)[1:15] d2 <- d2[d2$Person %in% keep2, , drop = FALSE] res <- anchor_to_baseline(d2, fit1, "Person",                           c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                           anchor_facets = "Criterion") summary(res) head(res$drift[, c("Facet", "Level", "Drift", "Flag")]) res$baseline_anchors[1:3, ] } # }

title: Build APA-style table output using base R structures — apa_table
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) tbl <- apa_table(fit, which = "summary", caption = "Model summary", note = "Toy example") tbl_facets <- apa_table(fit, which = "summary", branch = "facets") fit_bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(summary(fit)) tbl_from_summary <- apa_table(fit_bundle, which = "facet_overview") summary(tbl) #> APA Table Summary #>  Branch Style   Which Rows Columns NumericColumns MissingValues #>     apa   apa summary    1      42             24             7 #>  #> Caption #>  - Model summary #>  #> Note #>  - Toy example #>  #> Numeric profile #>            Column N    Mean SD     Min     Max #>               AIC 1 1753.90 NA 1753.90 1753.90 #>               BIC 1 2013.95 NA 2013.95 2013.95 #>        Categories 1    4.00 NA    4.00    4.00 #>   ConvergenceCode 1    0.00 NA    0.00    0.00 #>      EMIterations 0      NA NA      NA      NA #>  EMRelativeChange 0      NA NA      NA      NA #>      ExtremeHighN 1    0.00 NA    0.00    0.00 #>       ExtremeLowN 1    0.00 NA    0.00    0.00 p <- plot(tbl, draw = FALSE) p_facets <- plot(tbl_facets, type = "numeric_profile", draw = FALSE) p$data$plot #> [1] "numeric_profile" p_facets$data$plot #> [1] "numeric_profile" if (interactive()) {   plot(     tbl,     type = "numeric_profile",     main = "APA Table Numeric Profile (Customized)",     palette = c(numeric_profile = "#2b8cbe", grid = "#d9d9d9"),     label_angle = 45   ) } tbl$note #> [1] "Toy example"

what: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
text: Post-hoc shrinkage helper augments mfrm_fit James-Stein / empirical-Bayes shrunk estimates non-person facet. shrinkage variance \(\hat{\tau}^2\) estimated method moments facet-level point estimates standard errors: $$\hat{\tau}^2 = \max\!\left(0,   \frac{1}{K}\sum_{j=1}^{K}\hat{\delta}_j^{2} -   \overline{\mathrm{SE}^2}\right),$$ first term population variance facet point estimates around known mean zero (mfrmr sum--zero identification pins facet mean exactly 0, degree freedom consumed mean estimation). shrinkage factor \(B_j = \mathrm{SE}_j^2 / (\hat{\tau}^2 + \mathrm{SE}_j^2)\), shrunk point / standard error \(\hat{\delta}_j^{EB} = (1 - B_j)\hat{\delta}_j\) \(\mathrm{SE}_j^{EB} = \sqrt{(1 - B_j)\mathrm{SE}_j^2}\). posterior SE form treats \(\hat{\tau}^2\) known; omits Morris (1983, eqs. 4.1-4.2, p. 51) confidence-interval correction \(v \cdot \hat{\delta}_j^{2}\) \(v = 2 B_j^2 / (K - r - 2)\), \(r\) number regression coefficients used model prior mean (mfrmr's sum--zero pinning, \(r = 0\), divisor \(K - 2\)). correction adds variance proportional squared deviation \(\hat{\delta}_j^{2}\), accounting uncertainty \(\hat{\tau}^2\). equal-variance assumption \(\hat{\delta}_j^{2} \approx \hat{\tau}^2\), omitted variance order \(2 / (K - 2)\) times reported posterior variance \(V(1 - B_j)\), true SE approximately \(\sqrt{1 + 2/(K - 2)}\) times reported ShrunkSE. Magnitudes: SE understated ~73\ \(K = 8\), ~7\ ShrunkSE lower bound rather calibrated posterior SE.

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
code: apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(   fit,   facet_prior_sd = NULL,   shrink_person = FALSE )

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
text: fit mfrm_fit fit_mfrm() non-empty facets$others table. facet_prior_sd Optional numeric scalar. supplied, shrinkage variance fixed facet_prior_sd^2 instead estimated data. Useful prior elicited expert knowledge previous fit. shrink_person Logical. TRUE, empirical-Bayes shrinkage also applied fit$facets$person. Default FALSE, since MML person estimates already reflect N(0, sigma^2) prior.

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
text: fit$facets$others gains ShrunkEstimate, ShrunkSE, ShrinkageFactor columns, fit$shrinkage_report records per-facet \(\hat{\tau}^2\), mean shrinkage, effective degrees freedom (\(\mathrm{EffectiveDF}_f = \sum_j (1 - B_j)\), matches "effective number parameters" defined Efron & Morris, 1973). original Estimate / SE columns preserved.

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
text: Fit model usual fit_mfrm(). Call apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(fit) small-N facets present (see facet_small_sample_review()). Report original shrunk estimates manuscript, citing Efron & Morris (1973). build_apa_outputs() add sentence automatically fit$config$facet_shrinkage set.

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
text: Efron, B., & Morris, C. (1973). Combining possibly related estimation problems. Journal Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 35(3), 379-402. Efron, B. (2021). Empirical Bayes: Concepts methods (Technical report). Department Statistics, Stanford University. https://efron.ckirby.su.domains/papers/2021EB-concepts-methods.pdf Morris, C. N. (1983). Parametric empirical Bayes inference: Theory applications. Journal American Statistical Association, 78(381), 47-55.

title: Apply empirical-Bayes shrinkage to fitted non-person facet estimates — apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit_eb <- apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(fit) fit_eb$shrinkage_report # Look for: # - `Tau2` is the estimated between-level prior variance per facet. #   `Tau2 = 0` means the data did not justify any pooling and the #   shrunken estimates equal the raw estimates (`MeanShrinkage = 0`). # - `MeanShrinkage` near 0 = little movement, near 1 = heavy pooling #   toward 0. Small-N facets typically pull values further than #   well-identified ones. # - `EffectiveDF` is the implied "effective number of parameters" #   (Efron & Morris 1973); EffectiveDF much smaller than the row #   count of the facet means most levels were pooled together. head(fit_eb$facets$others[, c("Facet", "Level", "Estimate",                                "ShrunkEstimate", "ShrinkageFactor")]) # Look for: rows where `ShrinkageFactor` is large (close to 1) had #   their estimates pulled most strongly toward the facet mean (0). }
