what: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
text: Converts numerical output evaluate_mfrm_recovery() reviewer-facing adequacy checklist. goal impose one universal pass/fail rule; make main user questions explicit: runs finish? fitted models converge? uncertainty summaries available? coverage Monte Carlo precision plausible? practical RMSE bias limits supplied, parameter groups need follow-? bounded GPCM, slope-regime generator condition frames recovery evidence?

title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
code: assess_mfrm_recovery(   x,   min_reps = 30,   min_success_rate = 0.95,   min_convergence_rate = 0.95,   min_se_available = 0.8,   coverage_target = 0.95,   coverage_tolerance = 0.05,   max_mcse_rmse_ratio = 0.25,   max_rmse = NULL,   max_abs_bias = NULL,   top_n = 6,   digits = 3,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'mfrm_recovery_assessment' plot(   x,   y = NULL,   type = c("status", "metrics"),   metric = c("rmse", "bias", "coverage", "se_available", "mcse_rmse"),   draw = TRUE,   ... )

title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
text: x assess_mfrm_recovery(), output evaluate_mfrm_recovery(). plot.mfrm_recovery_assessment(), output assess_mfrm_recovery(). min_reps Minimum replication count expected treating simulation smoke check. min_success_rate Minimum acceptable proportion replications generated data produced fitted model. min_convergence_rate Minimum acceptable proportion replications whose fitted model reported convergence. min_se_available Minimum acceptable proportion recovery rows standard errors parameter group. Set NULL skip check. coverage_target Nominal coverage target, usually 0.95. coverage_tolerance Absolute tolerance around coverage_target. max_mcse_rmse_ratio Maximum acceptable Monte Carlo SE RMSE divided RMSE. Set NULL skip precision check. max_rmse Optional practical RMSE limit. Use scalar parameter groups named vector/list names "facet", "step", "slope", "Rater", "facet:Rater:logit". max_abs_bias Optional practical absolute-bias limit. Naming follows max_rmse. top_n Number next-action lines retained compact output. digits Digits used print method. ... Reserved future extensions. y Reserved S3 generic compatibility. type Assessment plot route. "status" summarizes checklist status counts; "metrics" plots parameter-group assessment metric colored status. metric Metric used type = "metrics". Supported values "rmse", "bias", "coverage", "se_available", "mcse_rmse". draw TRUE, draw base graphics. FALSE, return mfrm_plot_data object reusable plot tables metadata.

title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
text: object class mfrm_recovery_assessment : overview: compact run-level status. checklist: reviewer-facing adequacy checks. condition_review: generator-condition metadata, including bounded GPCM slope-regime interpretation generated score-category support available. condition_reporting_notes: reporter-facing generator-condition caveats separated recovery metrics release-gate decisions. diagnostic_reporting_notes: reporter-facing fit/separation caveats retained diagnostic context rather recovery gates. diagnostic_review: optional fit/separation operating-characteristic context retained evaluate_mfrm_recovery(). metric_review: parameter-group metric checks. uncertainty_review: compact coverage / SE availability interpretation. reading_order: recommended first-read order summary, condition, plot, row-level recovery outputs. next_actions: short action list sorted severity. thresholds: thresholds used assessment.

title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
text: RMSE bias adequacy depends substantive scale use case, function mark failed unless user supplies max_rmse max_abs_bias. Without limits, corresponding rows marked not_assessed next action asks user set practical thresholds decision depends metric. condition_review table generator metadata interpreting recovery run. bounded GPCM, GPCMSlopeRegime, StressLevel, generated score-category support describe data-generating condition; model-fit tests literature-derived adequacy cut points. condition_reporting_notes turns generator conditions reporter-facing caveats, high-dispersion slope stress sparse generated score support. optional diagnostic_review table available evaluate_mfrm_recovery() called include_diagnostics = TRUE. summarizes fit separation operating characteristics diagnostic context . availability fields mean fit separation values adequate, rows enter recovery adequacy status. diagnostic_reporting_notes read first drafting fit/separation language separates zero separation/reliability, absolute fit-ZSTD flags, df-sensitive ZSTD flags recovery gates. plot.mfrm_recovery_assessment() user-facing review aid. Use type = "status" first see checklist attention needed, type = "metrics" inspect parameter groups behind RMSE, bias, coverage, standard-error availability, Monte Carlo precision statuses. intended reading order summary(recovery_review), condition_reporting_notes condition_review, diagnostic_reporting_notes diagnostic_review, status plot, metric plot, row-level recovery table parameter groups need follow-. draw = FALSE, plot data also include reading_order, guidance, condition/diagnostic handoff tables, user-facing plot tables section_status status plots metric_review metric plots.

title: Assess whether recovery-simulation results are ready to use — assess_mfrm_recovery
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec <- evaluate_mfrm_recovery(   n_person = 12,   n_rater = 2,   n_criterion = 2,   reps = 1,   maxit = 30,   seed = 123 ) assess_mfrm_recovery(rec, min_reps = 1, max_rmse = 1)  # Read the bounded-GPCM generator condition separately from recovery adequacy. gpcm_spec <- build_mfrm_sim_spec(   n_person = 14,   n_rater = 2,   n_criterion = 2,   raters_per_person = 2,   model = "GPCM",   step_facet = "Criterion",   slope_facet = "Criterion",   slopes = c(0.85, 1.15),   assignment = "crossed" ) gpcm_rec <- suppressWarnings(evaluate_mfrm_recovery(   sim_spec = gpcm_spec,   reps = 1,   fit_method = "MML",   quad_points = 5,   maxit = 12,   include_diagnostics = TRUE,   include_person = FALSE,   seed = 456 )) gpcm_review <- assess_mfrm_recovery(   gpcm_rec,   min_reps = 1,   max_rmse = c(slope = 2),   max_abs_bias = c(slope = 1),   min_se_available = NULL,   max_mcse_rmse_ratio = NULL ) gpcm_review$condition_reporting_notes[, c(   "ConditionArea", "ReportingAttention", "ConditionFinding" )] gpcm_review$condition_review[, c(   "Model", "GPCMSlopeRegime", "StressLevel", "ScoreSupportStatus" )] gpcm_review$diagnostic_reporting_notes[, c(   "Facet", "ReportingAttention", "DiagnosticFinding" )] summary(gpcm_review)$reading_order } # }

title: Build a bias-cell count report — bias_count_table
text: plot-selection guide longer walkthrough, see mfrmr_visual_diagnostics vignette("mfrmr-visual-diagnostics", package = "mfrmr").

title: Build a bias-cell count report — bias_count_table
text: table data.frame contains, legacy-compatible branch: FacetA, FacetB Interaction facet level identifiers; generic names two interaction facets. Sq Sequential row number. Observd Count Number observations cell. Obs-Exp Average Observed minus expected average cell. Model S.E. Standard error bias estimate. Infit, Outfit Fit statistics cell. LowCountFlag Logical; TRUE count < min_count_warn. summary data.frame contains: InteractionFacets Names interaction facets. Cells, TotalCount Number cells total observations. LowCountCells, LowCountPercent Number share low-count cells.

title: Build a bias-cell count report — bias_count_table
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) t11 <- bias_count_table(bias) t11_facets <- bias_count_table(bias, branch = "facets", fit = fit) summary(t11) p <- plot(t11, draw = FALSE) p2 <- plot(t11, type = "lowcount_by_facet", draw = FALSE) if (interactive()) {   plot(     t11,     type = "cell_counts",     draw = TRUE,     main = "Bias Cell Counts (Customized)",     palette = c(count = "#2b8cbe", low = "#cb181d"),     label_angle = 45   ) } }

title: Build a bias-interaction plot-data bundle (FACETS Table 13: ranked bias list) — bias_interaction_report

title: Build a bias-interaction plot-data bundle (FACETS Table 13: ranked bias list) — bias_interaction_report
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) out <- bias_interaction_report(bias, top_n = 10) summary(out) p_bi <- plot(out, draw = FALSE) p_bi$data$plot }

text: x Output estimate_bias() fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm() (used x fit). facet_a First facet name (required x fit interaction_facets supplied). facet_b Second facet name (required x fit interaction_facets supplied). interaction_facets Character vector two facets. max_abs Bound absolute bias size estimating fit. omit_extreme Omit extreme-elements estimating fit. max_iter Iteration cap bias estimation x fit. tol Convergence tolerance bias estimation x fit. target_facet Facet whose local contrasts compared across paired context facet. Defaults first interaction facet. context_facet Optional facet condition . Defaults facet 2-way interaction. top_n Maximum number ranked rows keep. p_max Flagging cutoff pairwise p-values. sort_by Ranking key: "abs_t", "abs_bias", "prob".

text: named list : table: pairwise contrast rows summary: one-row contrast summary orientation_review: interaction-facet sign review settings: resolved reporting options direction_note: one-line interpretive note describing dominant pairwise-contrast direction (carried underlying bias estimator; empty string applicable) recommended_action: one-line recommended-action label (e.g. routing user follow-review largest flagged pairs); empty string underlying estimator emit one

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
text: helper exposes pairwise contrast table previously reachable fixed-width output generation. available 2-way interactions. pairwise contrast statistic uses Welch/Satterthwaite approximation labeled Rasch-Welch comparison output metadata.

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
text: table: one row per ordered (target_level_1, target_level_2) pair, Bias_diff, SE_diff, t_diff, df_diff, p_diff, underlying per-level bias rows. Rows sorted largest-magnitude |t_diff| rises top. summary: one-row screening summary MaxAbsBiasDiff, MaxAbsT, Significant (count flagged pairs p_max), BonferroniSignificant, HolmSignificant. orientation_review carries facet-orientation sign review parent estimate_bias() run. SE caveat applies: read Significant / BonferroniSignificant screening triage, formal inferential tests.

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
text: Fit diagnose model. Run estimate_bias() get underlying interaction effects. Pass result bias_pairwise_report() rater-pair contrast table. Use summary()$MaxAbsT top rows $table flag rater-pair systematic differences follow-review. ranked flagged-cells view ((rater, item) pairs largest local bias), use bias_interaction_report() estimate_bias() output.

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
text: contrast standard error computed SE(b_i - b_j) = sqrt(SE_i^2 + SE_j^2) – independence approximation. -facet bias values share sum--zero identification, Cov(b_i, b_j) < 0, true contrast variance SE_i^2 + SE_j^2 - 2 * Cov(b_i, b_j), smaller reported value. reported t-statistics p-values therefore conservative -facet contrasts (true significance higher reported). across-facet contrasts covariance term approximately zero approximation appropriate. Use report screening / triage table; inferential claims hinge marginally-significant -facet contrast, follow contrast uses full parameter covariance.

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
text: Linacre, J. M. (1989). Many-Facet Rasch Measurement. MESA Press. Eckes, T. (2005). Examining rater effects TestDaF writing speaking performance assessments: many-facet Rasch analysis. Language Assessment Quarterly, 2(3), 197-221. Myford, C. M., & Wolfe, E. W. (2003). Detecting measuring rater effects using many-facet Rasch measurement: Part . Journal Applied Measurement, 4(4), 386-422. Myford, C. M., & Wolfe, E. W. (2004). Detecting measuring rater effects using many-facet Rasch measurement: Part II. Journal Applied Measurement, 5(2), 189-227.

title: Build a bias pairwise-contrast report (FACETS Table 14: pairwise contrasts) — bias_pairwise_report
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") out <- bias_pairwise_report(fit, diagnostics = diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion") s <- summary(out) s$summary # Look for: `MaxAbsBiasDiff` < ~0.5 logits and `Significant = 0` mean #   no rater pair contrasts above the screen. The `BonferroniSignificant` #   / `HolmSignificant` columns count pairs that survive multiple- #   testing correction; both being 0 is a stronger "no rater-pair #   inconsistency" signal than the raw screen-positive count alone. head(out$table) # Look for: top rows with `|t_diff|` > 2 and |Bias_diff| > 0.5 logits #   warrant content-review of the two raters' scoring conventions on #   the conditioning context facet (e.g. compare their item-level #   marks for systematic strictness/leniency patterns). }

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
text: object class mfrm_apa_outputs : report_text: APA-style Method/Results draft prose table_figure_notes: consolidated draft notes tables/visuals table_figure_captions: draft caption candidates without figure numbering section_map: package-native section table manuscript assembly contract: structured APA reporting contract used downstream checks

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
text: context optional named list narrative customization. Frequently used fields include: assessment, setting, scale_desc rater_training, raters_per_response rater_facet (used targeted reliability note text) line_width (optional text wrapping width report_text; default = 92) Output text includes residual-PCA screening commentary PCA diagnostics available diagnostics. bounded GPCM, helper returns caveated partial reporting bundle supported diagnostics, direct tables, plots. also includes gpcm_boundary table. Treat output slope-aware sensitivity-reporting text, FACETS score-side equivalence, automatic operational scoring, design-forecasting evidence. default, report_text includes: model/data design summary (N, facet counts, scale range) optimization/convergence metrics (Converged, Iterations, LogLik, AIC, BIC) anchor/constraint summary (noncenter_facet, anchored levels, group anchors, dummy facets) latent-regression population-model wording fit active population_formula category/threshold diagnostics (including disordered-step details present) overall fit, misfit count, top misfit levels facet reliability/separation, residual PCA summary, bias-screen counts

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
text: report_text: manuscript-draft narrative covering Method (model specification, estimation, convergence) Results (global fit, facet separation/reliability, misfit triage, category diagnostics, residual-PCA screening, bias screening).  Written third-person past tense following APA 7th edition conventions, still intended human review. table_figure_notes: reusable draft note blocks table/figure appendices. table_figure_captions: draft caption candidates aligned generated outputs. active latent-regression fits add population-model section Table 5 notes/captions distinguish conditional-normal coefficient reporting post hoc regression EAP/MLE scores. bias results PCA diagnostics supplied, sections omitted narrative rather producing placeholder text.

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
text: Build diagnostics (optional bias results). RSM / PCM reporting runs, prefer MML fit diagnose_mfrm(..., diagnostic_mode = ""). Run build_apa_outputs(...). Check summary(apa) completeness. Insert apa$report_text note/caption fields manuscript drafts checking listed cautions.

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
text: minimal context list can include fields : assessment: name assessment task setting: administration context scale_desc: short description score scale rater_facet: rater facet label used narrative reliability text

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs

title: Build APA text outputs from model results — build_apa_outputs
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Fast smoke run: a JML fit and a legacy diagnostic let us build the # APA bundle and confirm `report_text` is non-empty in well under # a second. toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit_quick <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "JML", maxit = 30 ) diag_quick <- diagnose_mfrm(fit_quick,   residual_pca = "none",   diagnostic_mode = "legacy" ) apa_quick <- build_apa_outputs(fit_quick, diag_quick) nchar(apa_quick$report_text) > 0  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "MML", quad_points = 7, maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "both", diagnostic_mode = "both") apa <- build_apa_outputs(   fit,   diag,   context = list(     assessment = "Toy writing task",     setting = "Demonstration dataset",     scale_desc = "0-2 rating scale",     rater_facet = "Rater"   ) ) s_apa <- summary(apa) s_apa$overview # Look for: `SentenceCount` non-zero in every section that the run #   should support (Method / Results / fit / reliability / bias). #   Zero counts mean that section's prose is empty and the #   manuscript will need to fill it manually. chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) head(chk$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady", "NextAction")]) # Look for: rows with `DraftReady = "yes"` are ready to paste into #   the manuscript. `"no"` rows tell you which helper / setting #   needs to run before that paragraph can be drafted, via #   `NextAction`. Aim for every Visual Displays / Reliability / #   Diagnostics row to be `"yes"` before submitting. cat(apa$report_text) apa$section_map[, c("SectionId", "Available")] } # } }

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: fits Named list mfrm_fit objects chain order. anchor_facets Character vector facets use linking elements. include_person Include person estimates linking. drift_threshold Threshold flagging large residuals links. x mfrm_equating_chain object. ... Ignored. y Unused (S3 plot signature requirement). type One "graph" (bipartite Wave x anchor-element graph; requires igraph package), "common_anchors" (default; bar chart common-anchor counts per wave pair), "chain". preset Visual preset. draw TRUE, draw plot base graphics. object mfrm_equating_chain object (summary).

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: Object class mfrm_equating_chain components: links Tibble link-level statistics (offset, SD, etc.). cumulative Tibble cumulative offsets per wave. element_detail Tibble element-level linking details. common_by_facet Tibble retained common-element counts facet. config List analysis configuration.

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: screened linking chain uses screened link-offset method.  pair adjacent waves \((, B)\), function: Identifies common linking elements (facet levels present fits). Computes per-element differences: $$d_e = \hat{\delta}_{e,B} - \hat{\delta}_{e,}$$ Computes preliminary link offset using inverse-variance weighted mean differences standard errors available (otherwise unweighted mean). Screens elements whose residual preliminary offset exceeds drift_threshold, recomputes final offset retained set. Records Offset_SD (standard deviation retained residuals) Max_Residual (maximum absolute deviation mean) indicators link quality. Flags links fewer 5 retained common elements linking facet thin support. Cumulative offsets computed chaining link offsets Wave 1 forward, placing waves onto metric first wave. Elements whose per-link residual exceeds drift_threshold flagged $element_detail$Flag.  high Offset_SD, many flagged elements, thin retained anchor set signals unstable link may compromise resulting scale placement.

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: Use anchor_to_baseline() single new wave anchored known baseline. Use detect_anchor_drift() want direct comparison one reference wave. Use build_equating_chain() single wave dominate want ordered, adjacent links across series.

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: $links: one row per adjacent pair , , N_Common, N_Retained, Offset_Prelim, Offset, Offset_SD, Max_Residual. Small Offset_SD relative offset indicates consistent shift across elements. LinkSupportAdequate = FALSE means least one linking facet retained fewer 5 common elements screening. $cumulative: one row per wave cumulative offset Wave 1. Wave 1 always offset 0. $element_detail: per-element linking statistics (estimate wave, difference, residual mean offset, flag status). Flagged elements may indicate DIF rater re-training effects. $common_by_facet: retained common-element counts linking facet adjacent link. $config: records wave names analysis parameters. Read links cumulative: weak adjacent links can make later cumulative offsets less trustworthy.

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
text: Fit administration wave separately: fit_a <- fit_mfrm(...). Combine ordered named list: fits <- list(Spring23 = fit_s, Fall23 = fit_f, Spring24 = fit_s2). Call chain <- build_equating_chain(fits). Review summary(chain) link quality. Visualize plot_anchor_drift(chain, type = "chain"). problematic links, investigate flagged elements chain$element_detail consider removing anchor set.

title: Build a screened linking chain across ordered calibrations — build_equating_chain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") people <- unique(toy$Person) d1 <- toy[toy$Person %in% people[1:12], , drop = FALSE] d2 <- toy[toy$Person %in% people[13:24], , drop = FALSE] fit1 <- fit_mfrm(d1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit2 <- fit_mfrm(d2, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) chain <- build_equating_chain(list(Form1 = fit1, Form2 = fit2)) summary(chain) chain$cumulative } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) fixed <- build_fixed_reports(bias) fixed_original <- build_fixed_reports(bias, branch = "original") summary(fixed) p <- plot(fixed, draw = FALSE) p2 <- plot(fixed, type = "pvalue", draw = FALSE) if (interactive()) {   plot(     fixed,     type = "contrast",     draw = TRUE,     main = "Pairwise Contrasts (Customized)",     palette = c(pos = "#1b9e77", neg = "#d95f02"),     label_angle = 45   ) } } # }

title: Build an observed-data resampling specification — build_mfrm_resampling_spec
text: data long-format observed MFRM data set. person Person/respondent identifier column. facets Non-person facet columns used target MFRM fit. score Ordered score column. strata Optional person-level stratification columns, example Region L1 group column. person must one unique stratum combination. preserve_facets Optional facet columns whose level coverage reviewed , possible, topped stratified person draw. common choice rater facet. design Resampling design. "stratified_subsample" samples persons without replacement inside stratum. "stratified_bootstrap" samples persons replacement inside stratum re-keys duplicate person instances returned data. reps Number resampling replicates draw. sample_fraction Fraction persons draw within stratum sample_n = NULL. sample_n Optional target number persons draw per stratum. Supply either one scalar used every stratum, named numeric vector whose names match computed stratum labels. replace Optional logical override replacement. default, replacement FALSE "stratified_subsample" TRUE "stratified_bootstrap". seed Optional seed used draw_mfrm_resamples(). min_per_stratum Minimum target persons per represented stratum. topup_preserve_facets Logical; TRUE, add extra person clusters possible recover missing levels preserve_facets.

title: Build an observed-data resampling specification — build_mfrm_resampling_spec
code: toy <- simulate_mfrm_data(n_person = 12, n_rater = 3, n_criterion = 2,                           raters_per_person = 2, seed = 11) region_map <- setNames(rep(c("A", "B", "C"),                            length.out = length(unique(toy$Person))),                        unique(toy$Person)) toy$Region <- unname(region_map[toy$Person]) spec <- build_mfrm_resampling_spec(   toy, person = "Person", facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score", strata = "Region", preserve_facets = "Rater",   reps = 2, sample_fraction = 0.5, seed = 99 ) draws <- draw_mfrm_resamples(spec) summary(draws)$overview #>                 Design Reps SamplesReturned Replace #> 1 stratified_subsample    2               2   FALSE #>   PreserveCoverageCompleteRate TopupReps GapOrFallbackReps #> 1                            1         0                 0

title: Build an explicit simulation specification for MFRM design studies — build_mfrm_sim_spec
text: n_person Number persons/respondents generate. n_rater Number rater facet levels generate. n_criterion Number criterion/item facet levels generate. raters_per_person Number raters assigned person. design Optional named design override supplied named list, named vector, one-row data frame. Names may use canonical variables (n_person, n_rater, n_criterion, raters_per_person), current public aliases implied facet_names (example n_judge, n_task, judge_per_person), role keywords (person, rater, criterion, assignment). schema-future branch input design$facets = c(person = ..., judge = ..., task = ...) also accepted currently exposed facet keys. specify variable design scalar count arguments. score_levels Number ordered score categories. theta_sd Standard deviation simulated person measures. rater_sd Standard deviation simulated rater severities. criterion_sd Standard deviation simulated criterion difficulties. noise_sd Optional observation-level noise added linear predictor. step_span Spread used generate equally spaced thresholds thresholds = NULL. thresholds Optional threshold specification. Use numeric vector common thresholds; named list list(C01 = c(-1, 0, 1)); numeric matrix one row per StepFacet one column per step; long data frame columns StepFacet, Step/StepIndex, Estimate. model Measurement model recorded simulation specification. step_facet Step facet used model = "PCM" threshold values vary across levels. slope_facet Slope facet used model = "GPCM". current bounded GPCM branch requires slope_facet == step_facet. slopes Optional slope specification model = "GPCM". Use either numeric vector aligned generated slope-facet levels data frame columns SlopeFacet Estimate. Supplied slopes treated relative discriminations normalized package's geometric-mean- one identification convention log scale. omitted, slopes default 1 every slope-facet level, giving exact PCM reduction. GPCM model form follows Muraki's generalized partial credit model; package's slope-regime labels validation stress labels, published psychometric cut points. facet_names Optional public names two simulated non-person facet columns. Supply either unnamed character vector length 2 rater-like / criterion-like order, named vector names c("rater", "criterion"). assignment Assignment design. "crossed" means every person sees every rater; "rotating" uses balanced rotating subset; "sparse_linked" uses incomplete rating design optional linking persons; "resampled" reuses empirical person-level rater-assignment profiles; "skeleton" reuses observed person--facet design skeleton. latent_distribution Latent-value generator. "normal" samples centered normal distributions using supplied standard deviations. "empirical" resamples centered support values empirical_person/empirical_rater/empirical_criterion. empirical_person Optional numeric support values used latent_distribution = "empirical". empirical_rater Optional numeric support values used latent_distribution = "empirical". empirical_criterion Optional numeric support values used latent_distribution = "empirical". assignment_profiles Optional data frame columns TemplatePerson public rater-like facet column (optionally Group) describing empirical person-level rater-assignment profiles used assignment = "resampled". canonical name Rater also accepted. design_skeleton Optional data frame columns TemplatePerson, public rater-like facet column, public criterion-like facet column (optionally Group, Weight, TemplatePersonReuse) describing observed response skeleton used assignment = "skeleton". canonical names Rater Criterion also accepted. TemplatePersonReuse = TRUE asks simulate_mfrm_data() keep template-person order instead resampling templates, used build_peer_review_sim_spec(). sparse_controls Optional named list used assignment = "sparse_linked". Supported entries link_fraction (default 0.1), link_persons (overrides link_fraction), link_raters_per_person (default n_rater), assignment_mode ("balanced" "random"), min_common_persons_per_rater_pair (diagnostic target, default 1). group_levels Optional character vector group labels. dif_effects Optional data frame true group-linked DIF effects. interaction_effects Optional data frame true interaction effects. population_formula Optional one-sided formula describing person-level latent-regression population model used generating person measures, example ~ X + G. supplied, person measures generated X %*% beta + e rather N(0, theta_sd^2). population_coefficients Optional numeric vector latent-regression coefficients corresponding design matrix implied population_formula. population_sigma2 Optional residual variance latent-regression person distribution. population_covariates Optional template data frame containing one row per template person background variables referenced population_formula. Numeric/logical categorical factor/character variables expanded stats::model.matrix() contract used latent-regression fitting. simulation, template rows resampled requested n_person.

text: build_mfrm_sim_spec() creates explicit, portable simulation specification can passed simulate_mfrm_data(). goal make data-generating mechanism inspectable reusable rather relying ad hoc scalar arguments. resulting object records: design counts (n_person, n_rater, n_criterion, raters_per_person) latent spread assumptions (theta_sd, rater_sd, criterion_sd) optional empirical latent support values semi-parametric simulation threshold structure (threshold_table) optional discrimination structure bounded GPCM (slope_table) identified log-slope spread label (slope_regime) assignment design (assignment) optional sparse linked-design controls (sparse_controls) assignment = "sparse_linked" optional empirical assignment profiles (assignment_profiles) optional person-level Group labels optional observed response skeleton (design_skeleton) optional person-level Group labels observation-level Weight values optional person-level latent-regression population metadata including population_formula, population_coefficients, population_sigma2, reusable template person-level covariates, including model-matrix xlevel/contrast provenance categorical covariates planning_scope, explicit record current planning/forecasting helpers target role-based person x rater-like x criterion-like design contract rather fully arbitrary-facet planner planning_constraints, explicit record design variables can currently changed specification without rebuilding planning_schema, combined schema contract bundling role descriptor, scope boundary, current mutability map, facet_manifest, schema-future_facet_table, matching future_design_template, plus nested future_branch_schema scaffold future arbitrary-facet planning branch current design$facets(...) parser now normalizes nested facet-count input bundled future_branch_schema, whose nested design_schema now authoritative schema-branch object optional signal tables DIF interaction bias current generator targets package's standard person x rater x criterion workflow, public output names two facet roles can now customized facet_names. naming layer improves public ergonomics; yet turn generator fully arbitrary-facet simulator. Internally, helper objects keep canonical role mappings planning functions can treat first non-person facet rater-like second criterion-like. threshold values provided StepFacet, supported step facets generated levels chosen public rater-like criterion-like column. convenience, step-facet-specific thresholds can supplied named list numeric matrix whose row names StepFacet labels. model = "GPCM", public facet naming rules apply slope table; current bounded branch keeps slope_facet equal step_facet. slope_regime field summarizes centered log-slope spread recovery simulations can read intended generator stress level. labels (unit_slopes, near_flat, moderate, high_dispersion) package validation labels; model-fit decisions interpreted literature-derived adequacy thresholds. GPCM data-generating form follows Muraki (1992, doi:10.1177/014662169201600206), information-function interpretation follows Muraki (1993, doi:10.1177/014662169301700403). explicit simulation-specification metadata intended support ADEMP-style simulation reporting described Morris, White, Crowther (2019, doi:10.1002/sim.8086). assignment = "sparse_linked" branch follows sparse rater-mediated assessment work treating incomplete rater assignment planned missingness rather incidental nonresponse. link_persons link_raters_per_person controls emulate common linking set users can inspect rater-pair common-person counts using generated data recovery design studies. design generator diagnostic metadata layer; impose universal minimum-linking cutoff. Sparse-design motivation follows Wind, Jones, Grajeda (2023, doi:10.1177/01466216231182148), Wind Jones (2018, doi:10.1177/0013164417703733), DeMars, Shapovalov, Hathcoat (2023). population_formula supplied, simulation specification carries first-version person-level latent-regression generator. affects person distribution. current implementation keeps non-person facets existing many-facet Rasch generator resamples rows population_covariates requested design size computing \(\theta_n = x_n^\top \beta + \varepsilon_n\) \(\varepsilon_n \sim N(0, \sigma^2)\).

text: build_model_choice_review() user-facing synthesis helper. estimate new models. bundles: compare_mfrm() AIC/BIC/log-likelihood comparison; model-role guidance RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM; downstream-route availability APA output, score-side export, linking, recovery, fair averages, bias screening, summary-appendix handoff; report wording templates avoid treating better bounded-GPCM fit automatic operational-scoring decision; gpcm_capability_matrix() bounded GPCM present; optionally, build_weighting_review() first Rasch-family reference versus bounded-GPCM pair. word "bounded" intentional: package implements bounded GPCM route, every possible generalized partial-credit many-facet extension. current route uses positive slopes, requires slope_facet == step_facet, identifies slopes log scale geometric mean 1, keeps several downstream score-side/reporting helpers outside validated boundary.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "MML", model = "RSM", quad_points = 7, maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "both", diagnostic_mode = "both") vis <- build_visual_summaries(fit, diag, threshold_profile = "strict") vis2 <- build_visual_summaries(   fit,   diag,   threshold_profile = "standard",   thresholds = c(misfit_ratio_warn = 0.20, pca_first_eigen_warn = 2.0),   summary_options = list(detail = "detailed", top_misfit_n = 5) ) vis_facets <- build_visual_summaries(fit, diag, branch = "facets") vis_facets$branch summary(vis) p <- plot(vis, type = "comparison", draw = FALSE) p2 <- plot(vis, type = "warning_counts", draw = FALSE) vis$plot_payloads$comparison$data$plot vis$public_plot_routes[, c("Visual", "PlotHelper", "DrawFreeRoute")] if (interactive()) {   plot(     vis,     type = "comparison",     draw = TRUE,     main = "Warning vs Summary Counts (Customized)",     palette = c(warning = "#cb181d", summary = "#3182bd"),     label_angle = 45   ) } } # }

text: named list category-curve components. Class: mfrm_category_curves.

title: Build a category curve export bundle (preferred alias) — category_curves_report
text: Preferred high-level API category-probability curve exports. Returns tidy curve coordinates summary metadata quick plotting/report integration without calling low-level helpers directly. expected-score table also carries per-curve score variance information function. GPCM, information column follows Muraki/Samejima identity \(^2 \mathrm{Var}(X \mid \theta)\); RSM / PCM, reduces usual score variance discrimination fixed one. category_information table decomposes total category-level contributions, \(^2 P_k(\theta)(k - E[X \mid \theta])^2\), whose sum equals reported information theta value. cumulative_probabilities table follows FACETS / Winsteps graph convention accumulating modeled probabilities across ordered categories (P(X <= k) default, P(X >= k) also returned flipped curves). cumulative_boundaries reports approximate theta values P(X <= k) = .5, BoundaryStatus CrossingCount avoid -interpreting boundaries outside requested theta range multiple crossings.

title: Build a category curve export bundle (preferred alias) — category_curves_report
text: Use report inspect: category highest probability across theta cumulative category probabilities cross .5 whether adjacent categories cross expected order whether probability bands look compressed (often sparse categories) Recommended read order: summary() compact diagnostics. $probabilities, $expected_ogive, $category_information custom graphics. plot() default visual check, plot(, type = "cumulative") inspect cumulative probabilities. plot(, type = "information") inspect curve-level information. Use plot(, type = "category_information") category-level contributions needed.

title: Build a category curve export bundle (preferred alias) — category_curves_report
text: Category response curves follow Andrich's rating-scale formulation, Masters' partial-credit model, Muraki's generalized partial-credit model. Information column bounded GPCM uses Muraki's item-information result obtained Samejima's general polytomous information formula. Andrich, D. (1978). rating formulation ordered response categories. Psychometrika, 43(4), 561-573. Masters, G. N. (1982). Rasch model partial credit scoring. Psychometrika, 47(2), 149-174. Muraki, E. (1992). generalized partial credit model: Application EM algorithm. Applied Psychological Measurement, 16(2), 159-176. doi:10.1177/014662169201600206 Muraki, E. (1993). Information functions generalized partial credit model. Applied Psychological Measurement, 17(4), 351-363. doi:10.1177/014662169301700403

title: Build a category curve export bundle (preferred alias) — category_curves_report

Use preset = "monochrome" grayscale/line-type output boundary_status = "none" cumulative .5 boundary lines suppressed. plot(, type = "category_probability") plot(, type = "conditional_probability") explicit aliases category-probability curves type = "ccc". Use plot_data(, component = "plot_long") rebuilding curves ggplot2, plotly, another R graphics system.

title: Build a category curve export bundle (preferred alias) — category_curves_report
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) out <- category_curves_report(fit, theta_points = 101) summary(out) head(out$probabilities[, c("CurveGroup", "Theta", "Category", "Probability")]) p_overview <- plot(out, draw = FALSE) p_overview$data$plot p_cum <- plot(out, type = "cumulative", draw = FALSE) head(p_cum$data$cumulative_boundaries) p_info <- plot(out, type = "category_information", draw = FALSE) head(p_info$data$category_information) curve_long <- plot_data(out, component = "plot_long") head(curve_long[, c("PlotType", "Theta", "Series", "Value")]) }

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: ... Two mfrm_fit objects compare. labels Optional character vector labels model. NULL, labels generated model/method combinations. warn_constraints Logical. TRUE (default), emit warning models use different centering constraints (noncenter_facet dummy_facets), can make information-criterion comparisons misleading. nested Logical. Set TRUE supplied models known nested fitted likelihood basis observations. default FALSE, case likelihood-ratio test reported. TRUE, function still runs conservative structural nesting review computes LRT supported nesting patterns.

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: object class mfrm_comparison (named list) : table: data.frame model-level statistics (LogLik, AIC, BIC, Delta_AIC, AkaikeWeight, Delta_BIC, BICWeight, npar, nobs, WeightedN, ICSampleSize, ICSampleSizeBasis, Model, Method, Converged, ICComparable). lrt: data.frame likelihood-ratio test result (two models supplied nested = TRUE). Contains ChiSq, df, p_value. evidence_ratios: data.frame pairwise Akaike-weight ratios (Model1, Model2, EvidenceRatio). NULL weights computed. preferred: named list preferred model label criterion. comparison_basis: list describing whether IC LRT comparisons considered comparable. Includes conservative nesting_review plus lrt_status / lrt_reason withheld LRTs explicit rather silently absent.

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: Models fit data (rows, person/facet columns) comparison meaningful. function checks observation counts match warns otherwise. Information-criterion ranking reported candidate models use package's MML estimation path, analyze observations, converge successfully. Raw AIC BIC values still shown model, Delta_*, weights, preferred-model summaries suppressed likelihood basis comparable enough primary reporting. comparison table records row count (nobs) sample-size basis used BIC penalty (ICSampleSize, ICSampleSizeBasis); weighted fits sum weights rather number rows. Nesting: Two models nested one special case obtained imposing equality constraints.  common nesting MFRM RSM (shared thresholds) inside PCM (item-specific thresholds).  Models differ estimation method (MML vs JML) specification nested usual sense—use information criteria rather LRT comparison. current mfrmr model space, automatic nesting review intentionally conservative. currently supports two fixed-effect restrictions shared data shared constraints: RSM nested inside PCM PCM fit explicit step_facet; -family additive-vs-interaction comparisons smaller fit's facet_interactions set subset larger fit's set. Cross-method comparisons, comparisons change anchors/dummying/centering, -family comparisons add fixed interaction terms automatically promoted LRT claims. likelihood-ratio test (LRT) reported exactly two models supplied, nested = TRUE, structural nesting review passes, difference number parameters positive: $$\Lambda = -2 (\ell_{\mathrm{restricted}} - \ell_{\mathrm{full}})   \sim \chi^2_{\Delta p}$$ LRT asymptotically valid models nested data independent.  small samples boundary conditions (e.g., variance components near zero), treat p-values approximate.

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: addition raw AIC BIC values, function computes: Delta_AIC / Delta_BIC: difference best (minimum) value. Delta < 2 typically considered negligible; 4–7 suggests moderate evidence; > 10 indicates strong evidence higher-scoring model (Burnham & Anderson, 2002). AkaikeWeight / BICWeight: model probabilities derived exp(-0.5 * Delta), normalised across candidate set.  Akaike weight 0.90 means model 90\ best candidate set. Evidence ratios: pairwise ratios Akaike weights, quantifying relative evidence one model another (e.g., evidence ratio 5 means preferred model 5 times likely). AIC penalises complexity less BIC; disagree, AIC favours complex model BIC simpler one.

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: compare_mfrm() -basis model-comparison helper. strongest claims apply models fit response data, compatible likelihood basis, compatible constraint structure.

title: Compare two or more fitted MFRM models — compare_mfrm
text: Lower AIC/BIC values indicate better parsimony-accuracy trade-table$ICComparable TRUE. significant LRT p-value suggests complex model provides meaningfully better fit nesting assumption truly holds. preferred indicates model preferred criterion. evidence_ratios gives pairwise Akaike-weight ratios (returned Akaike weights can computed least two models). comparing two models, interpret evidence ratios cautiously—adjust multiple comparisons.

Inspect comparison_basis$nesting_review$relation reason reading LRT output. lrt: nested-model test summary, present requested reviewed conditions met. preferred: candidate preferred criterion summaries available.

what: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect
title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect
text: Combines per-facet average cluster size ICC estimates return Kish (1965) design effect Deff = 1 + (m - 1) * rho, m average number observations per facet element rho ICC.

title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect
text: data Data frame long format. facets Character vector facet column names. icc_table Output compute_facet_icc() (optional; computed fly NULL). score Score column name; required icc_table NULL. person Person column; passed compute_facet_icc().

title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect
text: Deff = 1: facet behaves like simple random sampling; clustering-induced variance inflation. Deff > 1: variance mean estimate inflated factor Deff relative SRS. EffectiveN = N / Deff sample size one need SRS achieve precision. rater-mediated designs, Deff well 1 Rater facet means rater-level clustering noticeable; consider whether rater generalisation warranted. Reported ICC pulled icc_table$ICC (variance share); interpretation compute_facet_icc().

title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect

title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect

title: Compute Kish design effects for each facet — compute_facet_design_effect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   icc <- compute_facet_icc(toy, facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),                            score = "Score", person = "Person")   deff <- compute_facet_design_effect(toy,                                       facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),                                       icc_table = icc)   print(deff)   # Large DesignEffect -> modest EffectiveN relative to raw N. } } # }

what: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc

text: Fits random-effects variance-components model Score ~ 1 + (1 | Person) + (1 | Facet1) + (1 | Facet2) + ... using lme4::lmer (Suggests) returns proportion observed score variance attributable facet. descriptive summary complementary Rasch-metric rater separation/reliability reported elsewhere.

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
code: compute_facet_icc(   data,   facets,   score,   person = NULL,   reml = TRUE,   ci_method = c("none", "profile", "boot"),   ci_level = 0.95,   ci_boot_reps = 1000L,   ci_boot_seed = NULL,   ci_boot_parallel = c("no", "multicore", "snow"),   ci_boot_ncpus = 1L )

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
text: data Data frame long format. facets Character vector facet column names. score Name score column. person Optional person column. supplied added separate random intercept Person-level variance partitioned . reml Logical; whether fit REML. Default TRUE. ci_method Confidence-interval method ICC column. One "none" (default, point estimate ), "profile" (first-order approximation: marginal likelihood-profile bounds variance-component SD via lme4::confint.merMod() method = "profile", squared variances, plugged ICC ratio holding components point estimate; fast deterministic, default recommendation reporting), "boot" (parametric bootstrap via lme4::bootMer(); slower robust non-normal ICC sampling distributions bootstrap replicate resamples full variance decomposition jointly). ci_level Confidence level ci_method != "none"; default 0.95. Koo & Li (2016) recommend banding CI rather point estimate classifying reliability Poor / Moderate / Good / Excellent. ci_boot_reps Number bootstrap replicates used ci_method = "boot". Default 1000. ci_boot_seed Optional integer seed bootstrap path (NULL leaves RNG state untouched). ci_boot_parallel Parallelisation strategy parametric-bootstrap CI path, passed lme4::bootMer(): "" (default), "multicore" (POSIX mclapply), "snow" (PSOCK cluster). "multicore" nothing Windows falls back serial; case use "snow" parallel::makeCluster() scope. ci_boot_ncpus Number CPUs use parallel bootstrap path (ignored ci_boot_parallel = ""). per-replicate progress bar suppressed parallel execution worker processes push updates parent's cli console.

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
text: data.frame class mfrm_facet_icc one row per variance component (including "Residual" row) columns: Facet: grouping factor name ("Residual"). Variance: REML variance estimate. ICC: variance share (Variance / sum(Variance)), [0, 1]. Interpretation: band label according facet's scale. InterpretationScale: "Koo-Li reliability" person facet, "Variance share" others. ICC_CI_Lower / ICC_CI_Upper / ICC_CI_Level / ICC_CI_Method: CI bounds, level, method (populated ci_method != "none"; NA_real_ otherwise). ICC_CI_NReps: bootstrap replicate count ci_method = "boot" (absent otherwise).

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
text: Interpretation column uses two scales numeric ICC reads correctly facet role: person facet, higher ICC = better. Koo & Li (2016, p. 161) bands applied: < 0.5 Poor, [0.5, 0.75] Moderate, (0.75, 0.9] Good, > 0.9 Excellent. strict > boundary 0.9 follows Koo & Li's wording "values greater 0.90 indicate excellent reliability" (ICC exactly 0.9 reads Good). non-person facets (Rater, Criterion, Task, Region, ...) numeric value variance share: much total observed score variance sits facet. bands used different (Trivial share < 0.05, Small share < 0.15, Moderate share < 0.30, Large share >= 0.30), large rater share generally bad news (raters disagree averages), good news. InterpretationScale column explicitly records scale applies row, downstream reporting confuse two. FACETS (Linacre, 2026) reports rater separation/reliability Rasch metric instead ICC; mfrmr surfaces , Rasch-metric version diagnostics$reliability variance-share view . Note: Koo & Li (2016) recommend applying reliability bands 95% confidence interval ICC rather point estimate alone. Set ci_method = "profile" (default "none") obtain likelihood-profile CI bounds alongside point estimate, ci_method = "boot" parametric bootstrap ci_boot_reps replicates. returned data frame gains ICC_CI_Lower / ICC_CI_Upper columns downstream reporting can apply band CI rather point estimate. Interpretation column still uses point estimate callers want CI-aware banding can implement externally supplied bounds.

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
text: Fit MFRM model fit_mfrm() Rasch-metric separation/reliability. Call compute_facet_icc(data, facets, score, person) get complementary variance-share summary. Feed compute_facet_design_effect() convert ICCs average cluster sizes Kish (1965) design effects.

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
text: Koo, T. K., & Li, M. Y. (2016). guideline selecting reporting intraclass correlation coefficients reliability research. Journal Chiropractic Medicine, 15(2), 155-163. Bates, D., Maechler, M., Bolker, B., & Walker, S. (2015). Fitting linear mixed-effects models using lme4. Journal Statistical Software, 67(1), 1-48.

title: Compute intra-class correlations for each facet — compute_facet_icc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   icc <- compute_facet_icc(toy, facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),                            score = "Score", person = "Person")   print(icc)   # Look for:   # - Person ICC reads as Koo & Li (2016) reliability: < 0.5 poor,   #   0.5-0.75 moderate, 0.75-0.9 good, > 0.9 excellent.   # - Rater / Criterion ICC reads as variance share, NOT reliability;   #   here SMALL values are desirable (raters / items agree), and   #   shares > 0.10 hint at meaningful systematic facet differences.   # - `Interpretation` summarises the variance-share band the helper   #   has assigned to each row. } } # }

title: Person fit indices: lz and Snijders-corrected lz* — compute_person_fit_indices
text: data frame class mfrm_person_fit_indices one row per Person columns: Person Person ID. N Number contributing response opportunities. LogLik Sum log P(X = x | theta) fitted model. Computed per-observation category probability PrObserved (model probability observed category), Gaussian residual approximation. lz Drasgow et al. (1985) standardized log-likelihood, proper polytomous form. lz_star Snijders-corrected lz* source fit used JML/fixed-effect person estimates, conditioning fitted non-person calibration, diagnostics include required derivative terms; otherwise NA. lz_star_status Status string lz_star, "computed_jml_conditional_calibration", "fit_required", "not_applicable_eap", "insufficient_information". lz_star_c Estimated Snijders projection coefficient c_n person, available. lz_star_variance Corrected variance denominator used lz_star, available. lz_flag_5pct, lz_flag_1pct Logical flags practical two-sided lz thresholds |z| > 1.96 |z| > 2.58. lz_star_flag_5pct, lz_star_flag_1pct flags lz_star, returned FALSE lz_star unavailable. ReportIndex, ReportValue, ReportFlagLevel, ReportFlag, ReviewStatus, ReviewReason, ReportCaveat Compact reporting columns. ReportIndex prefers lz_star Snijders correction computed; otherwise falls back lz explicit caveat. conditional-independence assumption MFRM, lz asymptotically standard normal. Practical reporting thresholds: |lz| > 1.96 flags person 5% level; |lz| > 2.58 1% level. lz_star_status == "computed_jml_conditional_calibration", lz_star applies Snijders' estimated-ability correction JML person estimates, conditional fitted non-person parameters. propagate non-person calibration uncertainty. MML/EAP person scores, use lz documented caveat rather treating EAP scores satisfied Snijders estimating equation. Note: implementation reads model category probabilities directly diagnostics bundle. Earlier mfrmr releases used Gaussian-residual approximation \(\log P(X = x) \approx -\tfrac{1}{2}(R^2/V) - \tfrac{1}{2}\log(2\pi V)\) stand-\(\log P\), overstated per-item variance \(\log P\) polytomous items, shrinking reported lz toward zero. Numerical lz values therefore directly comparable across mfrmr releases; treat values returned polytomous statistic re-evaluate historical |lz| > 1.96 flagging based earlier approximation.

Psychometrika, 66(3), 331-342. Magis, D., Raiche, G., & Beland, S. (2012). didactic presentation Snijders's lz* index person fit emphasis response model selection ability estimation. Journal Educational Behavioral Statistics, 37(1), 57-81. Sinharay, S. (2016). Asymptotically correct standardization person-fit statistics beyond dichotomous items. Psychometrika, 81(4), 992-1013.

title: Person fit indices: lz and Snijders-corrected lz* — compute_person_fit_indices
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none",                       diagnostic_mode = "legacy") pf <- compute_person_fit_indices(diag, fit = fit) head(pf) summary(pf) # Look for: |lz| > 1.96 (5% level) flags a person whose response #   pattern is statistically inconsistent with the model; > 2.58 is #   a 1% flag. lz_star is populated for JML/fixed-effect person #   estimates and left NA for MML/EAP estimates. Use ReportIndex / #   ReviewStatus for a compact report-ready reading. }

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
text: data data.frame long format (one row per rating event). person Column name person IDs. facets Character vector facet column names. score Column name observed score. weight Optional weight/frequency column name. rating_min Optional minimum category value. Supply rating_max retain unused boundary categories intended score support. rating_max Optional maximum category value. Supply rating_min retain unused boundary categories intended score support. keep_original Keep original category values. Use rating_min / rating_max intended scale unused intermediate categories 1, 2, 4, 5 1-5 scale. missing_codes Optional. NULL (default) -op; TRUE "default" activates FACETS / SPSS / SAS convention (c("99", "999", "-1", "N", "NA", "n/", ".", "")); supply character vector custom code set. Replacement counts returned missing_recoding component supported calling helper. See recode_missing_codes() standalone version. include_person_facet TRUE, include person-level rows facet_level_summary. include_agreement TRUE, include observed-score inter-rater agreement bundle (summary/pairs/settings) output. rater_facet Optional rater facet name used agreement summaries. NULL, inferred facet names. context_facets Optional facets used define matched contexts agreement. NULL, remaining facets (including Person) used. agreement_top_n Optional maximum number agreement pair rows.

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
text: list class mfrm_data_description : overview: one-row run-level summary missing_by_column: missing counts selected input columns missing_rate_summary: per-column missingness rate summary (one row per input column, raw proportion--N columns) score_descriptives: output psych::describe() score weight_descriptives: output psych::describe() weight score_distribution: weighted raw score frequencies prepared score support. Unused boundary categories retained rating range supplied explicitly; unused intermediate categories require keep_original = TRUE. facet_level_summary: per-level usage score summaries facet_crosstabs: pairwise observation-count crosstabs non-person facets (named list keyed "facetA__facetB"); used summary(ds)$design_links flag sparse / disconnected facet-pair coverage linkage_summary: person-facet connectivity diagnostics agreement: observed-score inter-rater agreement bundle row_retention: row counts preparation filters preparation_notes: structured notes row drops, ID trimming, design conditions detected preparation score_support: minimal prepared score-support metadata used summary(ds)$caveats

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
text: function provides compact descriptive bundle similar pre-fit summaries commonly checked TAM workflows: sample size, score distribution, per-facet coverage, linkage counts. psych::describe() used numeric descriptives score weight. Key data-quality checks perform fitting: Sparse categories: score category fewer 10 weighted observations may produce unstable threshold estimates (Linacre, 2002).  Consider collapsing adjacent categories. Unlinked elements: facet level zero overlap one levels another facet, design disconnected parameters placed common scale.  Check linkage_summary low connectivity. Extreme scores: persons facet levels -minimum -maximum scores yield infinite logit estimates JML; handled via Bayesian shrinkage MML.

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
text: Recommended order: overview: confirms sample size, facet count, category span. MinWeightedN column shows smallest weighted observation count across facet levels; values 30 may lead unstable parameter estimates. missing_by_column: identifies immediate data-quality risks. non-zero count warrants investigation fitting. score_distribution: checks sparse/unused score categories. Balanced usage across categories ideal; heavily skewed distributions may compress measurement range. facet_level_summary linkage_summary: checks per-level support person-facet connectivity.  Low linkage ratios indicate sparse disconnected design blocks. agreement: optional observed inter-rater consistency summary (exact agreement, correlation, mean differences per rater pair).

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
text: Run describe_mfrm_data() long-format input. Review summary(ds) plot(ds, ...). Resolve missingness/sparsity issues fit_mfrm().

title: Summarize MFRM input data (TAM-style descriptive snapshot) — describe_mfrm_data
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") ds <- describe_mfrm_data(   data = toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score" ) s_ds <- summary(ds) s_ds$overview #>   Observations TotalWeight Persons Facets Categories RatingMin RatingMax #> 1          768         768      48      2          4         1         4 #>   RatingRangeSource RatingMinSource RatingMaxSource #> 1          observed        observed        observed p_ds <- plot(ds, draw = FALSE) p_ds$data$plot #> [1] "score_distribution"

text: non-reference wave, function extracts facet-level estimates using make_anchor_table() computes element--element difference reference wave.  Standard errors obtained diagnose_mfrm() applied fit.  elements common reference comparison wave included. reporting drift, function removes weighted common-element link offset two waves Drift represents residual instability rather overall shift calibrations. function also records many common elements survive screening step within linking facet treats fewer 5 retained common elements per facet thin support. element flagged either condition met: $$|\Delta_e| > \texttt{drift\_threshold}$$ $$|\Delta_e / SE_{\Delta_e}| > \texttt{flag\_se\_ratio}$$ dual-criterion approach guards flagging elements large imprecise estimates, missing small precisely estimated shifts. facets NULL, non-Person facets compared.  Providing subset (e.g., facets = "Criterion") restricts comparison facets .

title: Detect anchor drift across multiple calibrations — detect_anchor_drift
text: $drift_table: one row per element x wave combination, columns Facet, Level, Wave, Ref_Est, Wave_Est, LinkOffset, Drift, SE_Ref, SE_Wave, SE, Drift_SE_Ratio, LinkSupportAdequate, Flag.  Large drift signals instability alignment common-element link. $summary: aggregated statistics facet wave: number elements, mean/max absolute drift, count flagged elements. $common_elements: pairwise common-element counts tidy table form. Small overlap weakens comparison results interpreted cautiously. $common_by_facet: retained common-element counts linking facet reference-vs-wave comparison. LinkSupportAdequate = FALSE means link rests fewer 5 retained common elements least one facet. $config: records analysis parameters reproducibility. practical reading order summary(drift) first, drift$drift_table, drift$common_by_facet overlap looks thin.

title: Detect anchor drift across multiple calibrations — detect_anchor_drift
text: Fit separate models administration wave. Combine named list: fits <- list(Spring = fit_s, Fall = fit_f). Call drift <- detect_anchor_drift(fits). Review summary(drift) plot_anchor_drift(drift). Flagged elements may need removed anchor sets investigated substantive causes (e.g., rater re-training).

title: Detect anchor drift across multiple calibrations — detect_anchor_drift
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ d1 <- load_mfrmr_data("study1") d2 <- load_mfrmr_data("study2") fit1 <- fit_mfrm(d1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit2 <- fit_mfrm(d2, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) drift <- detect_anchor_drift(list(Wave1 = fit1, Wave2 = fit2)) summary(drift) head(drift$drift_table[, c("Facet", "Level", "Wave", "Drift", "Flag")]) drift$common_elements } # }

what: Detect nesting structure between facets — detect_facet_nesting
title: Detect nesting structure between facets — detect_facet_nesting
text: Classifies every ordered pair facets (optionally including Person) crossed, partially nested, near-perfectly nested, fully nested, based conditional-entropy index: $$\text{nesting\_index}(\B) = 1 - H(B \mid ) / H(B).$$ index near 1 means knowing level essentially determines level B (nested B).

title: Detect nesting structure between facets — detect_facet_nesting
text: "Fully nested": nesting index >= 0.99. "Near-perfectly nested": 0.95 <= index < 0.99. "Partially nested": 0.50 <= index < 0.95. "Crossed": index < 0.50. direction column records facet nested , "crossed" neither direction 0.95.

title: Detect nesting structure between facets — detect_facet_nesting
text: Direction value "Rater nested Region" means every rater appears exactly one region (close ). additive fixed-effects MFRM, concern: severity rater confounded region-level variance model partition. Consider reporting nesting direction explicitly , relevant, refitting without nested facet moving hierarchical estimation tool (e.g. lme4::lmer, brms, TAM) separate variance components. Direction = "crossed" common reading nesting indices 0.5; two facets largely co-occur multiple combinations, setting Linacre (1989) assumed.
