title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
code: diagnose_mfrm(   fit,   interaction_pairs = NULL,   top_n_interactions = 20,   whexact = FALSE,   fit_df_method = c("engine", "facets", "both"),   diagnostic_mode = c("both", "legacy", "marginal_fit"),   residual_pca = c("none", "overall", "facet", "both"),   pca_max_factors = 10L )

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). interaction_pairs Optional list facet pairs. top_n_interactions Number top interactions. whexact Logical controlling ZSTD standardisation mean-square fit statistics. FALSE (default) applies Wilson-Hilferty cube-root transformation \((\mathrm{MnSq}^{1/3} - (1 - 2/(9\,\mathit{df})))/\sqrt{2/(9\,\mathit{df})}\) (recommended; Winsteps/FACETS convention WHEXACT=Y). TRUE uses simpler linear-normal standardisation \((\mathrm{MnSq} - 1)\sqrt{\mathit{df}/2}\), kept backward compatibility earlier mfrmr summaries FACETS' WHEXACT=N mode. fit_df_method Degrees--freedom convention used fit ZSTD. "engine" (default) keeps package-native convention DF_Infit = sum(Var * Weight) DF_Outfit = sum(Weight). "facets" uses FACETS/Wright-Masters fourth-moment approximation df = 2 / q^2 primary InfitZSTD / OutfitZSTD basis caps reported ZSTD values +/-9. "" keeps engine convention primary columns adds *_FACETS companion columns comparison. diagnostic_mode Diagnostic basis compute: "" (current default) computes residual/EAP-based stack strict latent-integrated first-order marginal-fit companion; "legacy" keeps residual/EAP-based stack ; "marginal_fit" returns marginal-fit companion. "" path adds posterior-integrated pass typically doubles quintuples wall-clock time relative "legacy"; pass "legacy" explicitly iterating large designs residual stack needed. Use "" RSM/PCM reporting fits enables plot_marginal_fit() plot_marginal_pairwise() follow-. residual_pca Residual PCA mode: "none", "overall", "facet", "". pca_max_factors Maximum number PCA factors retain per matrix.

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: object class mfrm_diagnostics including: obs: observed/expected/residual-level table measures: facet/person fit table (Infit, Outfit, ZSTD, PTMEA, ModelSE, RealSE, CI_Lower, CI_Upper, CI_Level, CI_Method) overall_fit: overall fit summary fit: element-level fit diagnostics reliability: facet-level model/real separation reliability precision_profile: one-row summary active precision tier recommended use precision_review: package-native checks SE, CI, reliability parameter_uncertainty: MML observed-information uncertainty structural parameters available (steps, bounded-GPCM slopes log positive scales), plus covariance status metadata facet_precision: facet-level precision summary distribution basis SE mode facets_chisq: fixed/random facet variability summary interactions: top interaction diagnostics interrater: inter-rater agreement bundle (summary, pairs) including agreement rater-severity spread indices unexpected: unexpected-response bundle fair_average: adjusted-score reference bundle (reported unavailable bounded GPCM) displacement: displacement diagnostics bundle approximation_notes: method notes SE/CI/reliability summaries diagnostic_basis: guide statistical target diagnostic path fit_standardization: guide df convention behind fit ZSTD values marginal_fit: optional strict marginal-fit companion based posterior-expected first-order category counts residual_pca_overall: optional overall PCA object residual_pca_by_facet: optional facet PCA objects

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: function computes diagnostic bundle used downstream reporting. calculates element-level fit statistics, approximate facet separation/reliability summaries, residual-based QC diagnostics, optionally residual PCA exploratory residual-structure screening. diagnostic_mode keeps legacy residual fit path explicit rather silently replacing . legacy path compatibility-oriented residual/EAP stack, whereas strict marginal path targets latent-integrated first-order category counts. diagnostic_mode = "", output includes diagnostic_basis guide downstream tables summaries can distinguish targets. Choosing diagnostic_mode: "legacy": use continuity historical residual-based workflows priority. "marginal_fit": use want strict latent-integrated screen without extra legacy bundle. "": recommended want continuity legacy residual stack making strict marginal path explicit RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM fits. bounded GPCM, generalized partial credit kernel now drives residual/probability tables strict marginal category-fit companion. Residual-based MnSq summaries still read exploratory screening tools rather strict Rasch-style invariance tests discrimination free, strict marginal companion likewise treated slope-aware screen rather finalized inferential test family. Key fit statistics computed element: Infit MnSq: information-weighted mean-square residual; sensitive -target misfitting patterns.  Expected value = 1.0. Outfit MnSq: unweighted mean-square residual; sensitive -target outliers.  Expected value = 1.0. ZSTD: Wilson-Hilferty cube-root transformation MnSq approximate standard normal deviate. PTMEA: point-measure correlation (item-rest correlation MFRM context); positive values confirm alignment latent trait. MnSq values ZSTD values read separately. mfrmr keeps package-native engine df convention default basis used R/Python/Julia validation engines. FACETS reports closely related MnSq values standardizes Wright-Masters fourth-moment df approximation (df = 2 / q^2) caps reported ZSTD values. Use fit_df_method = "" review two standardization conventions side side without changing primary InfitZSTD / OutfitZSTD columns. Residual basis MML. method = "MML" fits, residuals, MnSq, ZSTD computed EAP person measures marginal model. EAP measures shrunken toward population mean, expected scores – therefore fit statistics – differ systematically JMLE-based engines FACETS, especially persons extreme raw scores. df conventions remove difference: residual-basis difference, standardization difference. Refit method = "JML" external FACETS fit comparison requires JMLE-style residual basis (see facets_fit_review()). Misfit flagging guidelines (Bond & Fox, 2015): MnSq < 0.5: overfit (predictable; may inflate reliability) MnSq 0.5–1.5: productive measurement MnSq > 1.5: underfit (noise degrades measurement) \(|\mathrm{ZSTD}| > 2\): statistically significant misfit (5\ Infit Outfit disagree, Infit generally informative downweights extreme observations.  Large Outfit acceptable Infit typically indicates outlying responses rather systematic misfit. interaction_pairs controls facet interactions summarized. element can : length-2 character vector c("Rater", "Criterion"), omitted (NULL) let function select top interactions automatically. Residual PCA behavior: "none": skip PCA (fastest; recommended initial exploration) "overall": compute overall residual PCA across facets "facet": compute facet-specific residual PCA facet "": compute overall facet-specific PCA Overall PCA examines person \(\times\) combined-facet residual matrix; facet-specific PCA examines person \(\times\) facet-level matrices. summaries exploratory screens residual structure, standalone proofs unidimensionality. Facet-specific PCA can help localise stronger residual signal concentrated. residual-PCA summaries DIMTEST/UNIDIM implementation. DIMTEST-style essential-unidimensionality tests work item-response layer require explicit decision many-facet rating data collapsed, conditioned, adjusted rater/task/facet effects. manuscripts, combine global/element fit, residual PCA, local-dependence screens, use limited wording "evidence consistent essential unidimensionality specified facet structure" rather "unidimensionality established."

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: Practical interpretation often starts : overall_fit: global infit/outfit degrees freedom. reliability: facet-level model/real separation reliability. MML uses model-based ModelSE values available; JML keeps quantities exploratory approximations. fit: element-level misfit scan (Infit, Outfit, ZSTD). unexpected, fair_average, displacement: targeted QC bundles. bounded GPCM, fair_average retained unavailable status compatibility calculation yet validated generalized model. approximation_notes: method notes SE/CI/reliability summaries.

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: Start overall_fit reliability, move element-level diagnostics (fit) targeted bundles (unexpected, displacement, interrater, facets_chisq). Treat fair_average available RSM / PCM branch. Consistent signals across multiple components typically robust single isolated warning.  example, element flagged high Outfit high displacement concerning one flagged single criterion. SE kept compatibility alias ModelSE. RealSE fit-adjusted companion defined ModelSE * sqrt(max(Infit, 1)). Reliability tables report model fit-adjusted bounds observed variance, error variance, true variance; JML entries still treated exploratory. Separation, strata, reliability follow Wright & Masters (1982) conventions: \(G = \mathrm{TrueSD}/\mathrm{RMSE}\), \(R = G^2 / (1 + G^2)\), \(H = (4G + 1) / 3\).

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: Start diagnose_mfrm(fit, diagnostic_mode = "", residual_pca = "none"). Inspect summary(diag) use diagnostic_basis separate legacy residual evidence strict marginal evidence. needed, rerun residual PCA ("overall" "").

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
text: Wright, B. D., & Masters, G. N. (1982). Rating scale analysis. MESA Press. (G/R/H separation, reliability, strata formulas summarized s_diag$reliability follow convention.) Wright, B. D., & Linacre, J. M. (1994). Reasonable mean-square fit values. Rasch Measurement Transactions, 8(3), 370. (Source 0.5-1.5 Infit / Outfit acceptance band s_diag$key_warnings misfit_thresholds apply.) Linacre, J. M. (1989). Many-Facet Rasch Measurement. MESA Press. (FACETS Tables 6 + 7 correspond per-facet element measures, fit, chi-square heterogeneity screen exposed via s_diag$reliability s_diag$facets_chisq.) Bond, T. G., & Fox, C. M. (2015). Applying Rasch model: Fundamental measurement human sciences (3rd ed.). Routledge. (Reference text Rasch-family fit conventions exposed helper.) Linacre, J. M. (2002). Infit Outfit, Mean-square Standardized mean? Rasch Measurement Transactions, 16(2), 878. Linacre, J. M. (2026). user's guide Facets Rasch-model computer programs. Winsteps.com. (WHEXACT / FACETS standardized fit df notes.)

title: Compute diagnostics for an mfrm_fit object — diagnose_mfrm
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Fast smoke run: legacy-only diagnostic mode is enough to confirm # the bundle has the expected slots. ~1 s on example_core. toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit_quick <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                       method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag_quick <- diagnose_mfrm(fit_quick, diagnostic_mode = "legacy",                              residual_pca = "none") summary(diag_quick)$overview[, c("Observations", "Facets", "Categories")]  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, diagnostic_mode = "both", residual_pca = "none") s_diag <- summary(diag) s_diag$overview[, c("Observations", "Facets", "Categories")] s_diag$diagnostic_basis[, c("DiagnosticPath", "Status", "Basis")] s_diag$key_warnings # Look for: "No immediate warnings ..." in `key_warnings` is the #   "all clear" signal. Lines starting with "MnSq misfit:" name the #   element + Infit / Outfit values that fell outside the #   0.5-1.5 acceptance band; review those first. s_diag$facets_chisq # Look for: `FixedProb` < 0.05 means that facet's elements differ #   reliably under the fixed-effect "all elements equal" null. A #   facet with a non-significant chi-square contributes little #   spread to the test scale. s_diag$interrater # Look for: ExactAgreement >= ExpectedExactAgreement and #   AgreementMinusExpected >= 0 indicate raters agree at least as #   often as the model expects. Negative values warrant a closer #   look at `diag$interrater$pairs`. p_qc <- plot_qc_dashboard(fit, diagnostics = diag, draw = FALSE) p_qc$data$plot  # Optional: include residual PCA in the diagnostic bundle diag_pca <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "overall") pca <- analyze_residual_pca(diag_pca, mode = "overall") head(pca$overall_table)  # Reporting route: prec <- precision_review_report(fit, diagnostics = diag) summary(prec) } # } }

what: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table

title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table
code: dif_interaction_table(   fit,   diagnostics,   facet,   group,   data = NULL,   min_obs = 10,   p_adjust = "holm",   abs_t_warn = 2,   abs_bias_warn = 0.5 )

title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Output diagnose_mfrm(). facet Character scalar naming facet. group Character scalar naming grouping column. data Optional data frame group column. NULL (default), data stored fit$prep$data used, must contain group column. min_obs Minimum observations per cell. Cells fewer many observations flagged sparse test statistics set NA. Default 10. p_adjust P-value adjustment method, passed stats::p.adjust(). Default "holm". abs_t_warn Threshold flagging cells absolute t-value. Default 2. abs_bias_warn Threshold flagging cells absolute Obs-Exp average (logits). Default 0.5.

title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table
text: Object class mfrm_dif_interaction : table: tibble per-cell statistics flags. summary: tibble summarizing flagged sparse cell counts. gpcm_boundary: bounded GPCM fits, capability-boundary table. config: list analysis parameters.

title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table
text: function uses fitted model's observation-level residuals (internal compute_obs_table() function) rather re-estimating model. facet-level x group-value cell, computes: N: number observations cell ObsScore: sum observed scores ExpScore: sum expected scores ObsExpAvg: mean observed-minus-expected difference Var_sum: sum model variances StdResidual: (ObsScore - ExpScore) / sqrt(Var_sum) t: approximate t-statistic (equal StdResidual) df: N - 1 p_value: two-tailed p-value t-distribution

what: When to use this instead of analyze_dff()
title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table
text: Use dif_interaction_table() want cell-level screening single facet--group table. Use analyze_dff() want group-pair contrasts summarized differential-functioning effect sizes method-appropriate classifications.

title: Compute interaction table between a facet and a grouping variable — dif_interaction_table

Cells |t| > abs_t_warn |ObsExpAvg| > abs_bias_warn flagged flag_t flag_bias columns. Sparse cells (N < min_obs) sparse = TRUE NA statistics.

title: Estimate bias and interaction screening terms — estimate_bias
text: Bias (interaction) MFRM refers systematic departure additive model: specific rater-criterion (higher-order) combination produces scores consistently higher lower predicted main effects alone.  example, Rater might unexpectedly harsh Criterion 2 despite lenient overall. Mathematically, bias term \(b_{jc}\) rater \(j\) criterion \(c\) modifies linear predictor: $$\eta_{njc} = \theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_c - b_{jc}$$ RSM / PCM, function estimates \(b_{jc}\) residuals fitted additive model using iterative recalibration screen aligned many-facet bias literature (Myford & Wolfe, 2003, 2004): $$b_{jc} = \frac{\sum_n (X_{njc} - E_{njc})}                     {\sum_n \mathrm{Var}_{njc}}$$ iteration updates expected scores using current bias estimates, re-computes bias. Convergence reached maximum absolute change bias estimates falls tol. bounded GPCM, additive-bias idea evaluated slope-aware GPCM kernel conditional profile-likelihood follow-columns; quantities remain screening evidence theta, facet, step, slope estimates held fixed. two-way mode, use facet_a facet_b (interaction_facets length 2). higher-order mode, provide interaction_facets length >= 3.

title: Estimate bias and interaction screening terms — estimate_bias
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) s_bias <- summary(bias) s_bias$overview # Look for: `MaxAbsBias` < ~0.5 logits and `Significant = 0` mean #   no cell exceeded the screen. The `BonferroniSignificant` / #   `HolmSignificant` columns count cells that survive multiple- #   testing correction; both being 0 is a stronger "no bias" #   signal than the raw screen-positive count alone. s_bias$top_rows # Look for: rows with `|t|` > 2 and |Bias Size| > 0.5 logits warrant #   review (large effect AND statistically reliable). Rows with only #   one of those triggered are usually small-cell artefacts. p_bias <- plot_bias_interaction(bias, draw = FALSE) p_bias$data$plot }

text: n_person Vector person counts evaluate. n_rater Vector rater counts evaluate. n_criterion Vector criterion counts evaluate. raters_per_person Vector rater assignments per person. design Optional named design-grid override supplied named list, named vector, one-row data frame. Names may use canonical variables (n_person, n_rater, n_criterion, raters_per_person), current public aliases implied sim_spec (example n_judge, n_task, judge_per_person), role keywords (person, rater, criterion, assignment). Values may vectors. schema-future branch input design$facets = c(person = ..., judge = ..., task = ...) also accepted currently exposed facet keys. specify variable design scalar design-grid arguments. reps Number replications per design condition. score_levels Number ordered score categories. theta_sd Standard deviation simulated person measures. rater_sd Standard deviation simulated rater severities. criterion_sd Standard deviation simulated criterion difficulties. noise_sd Optional observation-level noise added linear predictor. step_span Spread step thresholds logit scale. fit_method Estimation method passed fit_mfrm(). model Measurement model passed fit_mfrm(). RSM PCM use validated Rasch-family design-planning layer. Bounded GPCM available caveated simulation/refit operating-characteristic route. step_facet Step facet passed fit_mfrm() model = "PCM" model = "GPCM". left NULL, function inherits generator step facet sim_spec available otherwise defaults "Criterion". slope_facet Slope facet passed fit_mfrm() model = "GPCM". current bounded branch requires slope_facet == step_facet. slopes Optional bounded-GPCM generator slopes used sim_spec = NULL. See build_mfrm_sim_spec() accepted formats. assignment Optional assignment design used sim_spec = NULL. "sparse_linked" activates planned-missing sparse rating designs; use sparse_controls specify linking set. sparse_controls Optional named list used assignment = "sparse_linked" sim_spec = NULL, retained sparse linked sim_spec. See build_mfrm_sim_spec(). maxit Maximum iterations passed fit_mfrm(). quad_points Quadrature points fit_method = "MML". residual_pca Residual PCA mode passed diagnose_mfrm(). sim_spec Optional output build_mfrm_sim_spec() extract_mfrm_sim_spec() used base data-generating mechanism. supplied, design grid still varies n_person, n_rater, n_criterion, raters_per_person, latent-spread assumptions, thresholds, generator settings come sim_spec. sim_spec contains step-facet-specific thresholds, design grid may vary number levels step facet away specification. sim_spec stores active latent-regression population generator, helper currently requires fit_method = "MML" replication can refit population model. seed Optional seed reproducible replications. progress Logical. Whether show progress bar across design--replication cells. Defaults interactive(), interactive exploratory runs show progress non-interactive tests, scripts, report rendering stay quiet. Set TRUE FALSE explicitly override. parallel Parallelisation strategy rep loop within design row. "" (default) runs serially; "future" uses future.apply::future_lapply respects whatever future::plan() currently active. title: Facet-quality dashboard for facet-level screening — facet_quality_dashboard
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). facet Optional facet name. NULL, function tries infer rater-like facet otherwise falls back first modeled facet. bias_results Optional output estimate_bias() named list outputs. Non-matching bundles skipped quietly. severity_warn Absolute estimate cutoff used flag severity outliers. misfit_warn Mean-square cutoff used flag misfit. Values cutoff reciprocal flagged. central_tendency_max Absolute estimate cutoff used flag central tendency. Levels near zero marked. bias_count_warn Minimum flagged-bias row count required flag level. bias_abs_t_warn Absolute t cutoff used deriving bias-row flags raw bias bundle. bias_abs_size_warn Absolute bias-size cutoff used deriving bias-row flags raw bias bundle. bias_p_max Probability cutoff used deriving bias-row flags raw bias bundle.

title: Facet-quality dashboard for facet-level screening — facet_quality_dashboard
text: object class mfrm_facet_dashboard (also inheriting mfrm_bundle list). object summarizes one target facet: overview reports facet-level screening totals, summary provides aggregate estimates flag counts, detail contains one row per facet level computed screening indicators, ranked orders levels review priority, flagged keeps levels requiring follow-, bias_sources records bias-result bundles contributed counts, settings stores resolved thresholds, notes gives short interpretation messages read dashboard.

title: Facet-quality dashboard for facet-level screening — facet_quality_dashboard
text: dashboard screens individual facet elements across four complementary criteria: Severity: elements \(|\mathrm{Estimate}| >\) severity_warn logits flagged unusually harsh lenient. Misfit: elements Infit Outfit MnSq outside acceptance band flagged. band defaults package pair returned mfrm_misfit_thresholds() (Linacre 0.5-1.5); pass misfit_warn = 1.5 keep older symmetric \([1/\)misfit_warn\(,\;\)misfit_warn\(]\) form (0.67-1.5). Central tendency: elements \(|\mathrm{Estimate}| <\) central_tendency_max logits flagged.  Near-zero estimates may indicate rater avoids extreme categories, producing artificially narrow score ranges. Bias: elements involved \(\ge\) bias_count_warn screen-positive interaction cells (estimate_bias()) flagged. flag density score counts many four criteria element triggers.  Elements flagged multiple criteria warrant priority review (e.g., rater retraining, data exclusion). Default thresholds designed moderate-stakes rating contexts. Adjust application: stricter thresholds high-stakes certification, lenient formative assessment.

title: Facet-quality dashboard for facet-level screening — facet_quality_dashboard

title: Review per-facet-level sample adequacy — facet_small_sample_review
text: mfrmr every facet fixed effect (see ?fit_mfrm, "Fixed effects assumption"), level ratings contributes estimate wide SE shrinkage toward facet mean. helper surfaces levels front users can decide whether drop , pool , move hierarchical model outside mfrmr.

title: Review per-facet-level sample adequacy — facet_small_sample_review
text: "sparse" (n < 10): level-level estimate unstable; SE wide; consider combining adjacent levels treating exploratory . "marginal" (10 <= n < 30): Linacre (1994) 95% CI +-1.0 logit threshold; usable screening . "standard" (30 <= n < 50): meets baseline stability; reasonable publication fit statistics acceptable. "strong" (n >= 50): well-targeted; facet estimate robust. mfrmr shrinkage default, sparse marginal levels "borrow strength" levels. Jones Wind (2018) report rater estimates particularly sensitive thin linking; Facet = "Person" row usually less concern person prior integrates uncertainty.

title: Review per-facet-level sample adequacy — facet_small_sample_review
text: Fit fit_mfrm(); optionally also produce diagnostics diagnose_mfrm() want per-level Infit/Outfit. Call facet_small_sample_review(fit, diagnostics). Read facet_summary first: highlights worst level per facet. summary table gives counts band. facet flagged sparse marginal, discuss Methods section; build_apa_outputs() already adds sentence band fit$summary$FacetSampleSizeFlag set.

title: Review per-facet-level sample adequacy — facet_small_sample_review
text: Linacre, J. M. (2026). User's Guide FACETS, Version 4.5.0. Winsteps.com. https://www.winsteps.com/facets.htm Linacre, J. M. (1994). Sample size item calibration stability. Rasch Measurement Transactions, 7(4), 328. https://www.rasch.org/rmt/rmt74m.htm Jones, E., & Wind, S. . (2018). Using repeated ratings improve measurement precision incomplete rating designs. Journal Applied Measurement, 19(2), 148-161.

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). fixed_p_max Warning cutoff fixed-effect chi-square p-values. random_p_max Warning cutoff random-effect chi-square p-values. top_n Optional maximum number facet rows keep.

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: named list : table: facet-level chi-square diagnostics summary: one-row summary thresholds: applied p-value thresholds

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: helper summarizes facet-level variability fixed random chi-square indices spread heterogeneity checks.

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: table: facet-level fixed/random chi-square p-value flags. summary: number significant facets overall magnitude indicators. thresholds: p-value criteria used flagging. Use table together inter-rater displacement diagnostics distinguish global facet effects local anomalies.

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: Run facets_chisq_table(fit, ...). Inspect summary(chi) facet rows chi$table. Visualize plot_facets_chisq().

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
text: table data.frame contains: Facet Facet name. Levels Number estimated levels facet. MeanMeasure, SD Mean standard deviation level measures. FixedChiSq, FixedDF, FixedProb Fixed-effect chi-square test (null hypothesis: levels equal). Significant result means facet elements differ measurement error alone. RandomChiSq, RandomDF, RandomProb, RandomVar Random-effect test (null hypothesis: variation equals random sample single population). Significant result suggests systematic heterogeneity beyond sampling variation. FixedFlag, RandomFlag Logical flags significance.

title: Build facet variability diagnostics with fixed/random reference tests — facets_chisq_table
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) chi <- facets_chisq_table(fit) summary(chi) p_chi <- plot(chi, draw = FALSE) p_chi$data$plot }

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
text: facets_feature_coverage() summarizes current mfrmr release maps main FACETS output-table, output-file, graph-menu surface package functions. Use helper migration work need public, user-facing answer three questions: FACETS outputs close mfrmr route, outputs partially covered structured R objects, FACETS-specific outputs implemented intentionally outside current package scope.

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
code: facets_feature_coverage(   status = c("all", "implemented", "supported_with_caveat", "partial", "not_implemented",     "not_targeted") )

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
text: status rows return. "" returns full matrix. values filter Status column.

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
text: data.frame columns: FACETSArea FACETSFeature FACETSReference mfrmrRoute Status Scope GapOrBoundary Priority

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
text: matrix based FACETS 64-bit output index, lists output Tables 1–14, DIF/bias plots, R/Web plots, output files, graph-menu curves. mfrmr intentionally prioritizes structured R tables reusable plot data exact FACETS line-printer output. Status meanings: implemented: package-native route covers substantive output. supported_with_caveat: package-native route exists, output must read explicit identification, validation, scope caveats. partial: concept covered, full FACETS formatting, option surface, file type, external integration. not_implemented: FACETS feature direct package-native route current release. not_targeted: feature tied FACETS UI, Web/Excel handoff, another external program format release goal.

title: FACETS Feature Coverage Matrix — facets_feature_coverage
text: Linacre, J. M. (2026). user's guide FACETS, version 4.5.0. Output tables - files - plots - graphs: https://www.winsteps.com/facetman64/outputtableindex.htm.

text: bundle class mfrm_facets_fit_df_guide : summary: one-row scope summary formula_guide: formulas package columns column_guide: engine FACETS-style columns appear decision_guide: recommended comparison steps interpretation_guide: read common difference patterns references: optional source-reference rows settings: guide metadata

title: Guide FACETS-style fit df and ZSTD standardization — facets_fit_df_guide
text: guide separates mean-square size ZSTD standardization. Infit outfit MnSq values answer large residual noise predictability signal . ZSTD values standardize MnSq values using degrees-- freedom convention Wilson-Hilferty-style transformation, ZSTD can differ even underlying MnSq values nearly identical. Two boundaries sit upstream df comparison. First, residual basis: method = "MML" fits evaluate residuals shrunken EAP person measures, whereas FACETS evaluates JMLE estimates, MnSq values can differ standardization applied; refit method = "JML" comparison requires JMLE-style residual basis. Second, small df: mfrmr returns NA ZSTD df < 1 Wilson-Hilferty transformation numerically unstable , FACETS/Winsteps WHEXACT can continue linear approximation, sparse cells can show NA finite external value without indicating fit difference.

#>     2                   Are df values close under the same convention? #>     3                   Do ZSTD values differ after MnSq and df agree? #>     4 Does |ZSTD| > 2 status change only after changing df convention? #>     5                            Is an external FACETS table supplied? #>                                                                                           RecommendedAction #>  If MnSq differs materially, treat this as a fit-statistic or estimation difference before discussing ZSTD. #>                     If df differs, classify the ZSTD gap as a df-convention issue unless MnSq also differs. #>             Check WHEXACT/normalization settings and rounding/truncation before making a substantive claim. #>          Report the flag as convention-sensitive; inspect MnSq and substantive context before acting on it. #>                      Use read_facets_fit_table() or normalize_facets_fit_frame(), then facets_fit_review(). #>  #> Settings #>             Setting Value #>  include_references  TRUE #>  #> Notes #>  - FACETS-style fit df and ZSTD comparison guide. Compare MnSq first, then df and ZSTD. facets_fit_df_guide()$decision_guide #>   Step                                                         Question #> 1    1                                           Are MnSq values close? #> 2    2                   Are df values close under the same convention? #> 3    3                   Do ZSTD values differ after MnSq and df agree? #> 4    4 Does |ZSTD| > 2 status change only after changing df convention? #> 5    5                            Is an external FACETS table supplied? #>                                                                                            RecommendedAction #> 1 If MnSq differs materially, treat this as a fit-statistic or estimation difference before discussing ZSTD. #> 2                    If df differs, classify the ZSTD gap as a df-convention issue unless MnSq also differs. #> 3            Check WHEXACT/normalization settings and rounding/truncation before making a substantive claim. #> 4         Report the flag as convention-sensitive; inspect MnSq and substantive context before acting on it. #> 5                     Use read_facets_fit_table() or normalize_facets_fit_frame(), then facets_fit_review().

FACETS-style names do not mean that FACETS estimated the model. #> 2 FACETS-style wrappers, table labels, and files support transition, handoff, and report organization, not optimizer-level reproduction. #> 3                                           Numerical comparison requires an explicit external FACETS output table supplied by the user. #> 4                              Inference and reporting should be based on native fit, diagnostics, review, table, and plot-data objects. #> 5                           GPCM, D-study, network, and reusable visualization data are extension routes rather than FACETS menu clones. #>                                                                                                              RecommendedWording #> 1                           The model was estimated with mfrmr; diagnose_mfrm(); reporting_checklist() #> 2                 facets_feature_coverage(); run_mfrm_facets(); facets_output_file_bundle() #> 3   read_facets_fit_table(); facets_fit_review(); fit_measures_table(df_sensitivity = TRUE) #> 4                       build_summary_table_bundle(); title: Build an adjusted-score reference table bundle — fair_average_table
text: function wraps package's adjusted-score calculations returns facet-wise stacked tables. Historical display columns Fair(M) Average Fair(Z) Average retained compatibility, package-native aliases AdjustedAverage, StandardizedAdjustedAverage, ModelBasedSE, FitAdjustedSE appended formatted outputs. Rasch-family RSM / PCM branch, tables follow standard FACETS Linacre construction: fair averages Rasch-measure--score transformations evaluated standardized mean/zero-facet environment. Bounded GPCM fits supported slope-aware element-conditional construction. slope-facet element \(j^\star\) per-row fair-average GPCM expected score $$\mathrm{FA}_{p, j^\star} = \sum_k k \cdot P_{GPCM}(X = k \mid \theta_p, a_{j^\star}, \boldsymbol{\delta}_{j^\star})$$ computed element's discrimination \(a_{j^\star}\) threshold structure. Rows non-slope facets (Person, Rater, ...) use geometric-mean-one slope GPCM identification convention, rows remain continuous standard PCM Linacre fair-average reduce exactly slopes equal one. identification-based reporting convention package's bounded GPCM route, unique free-discrimination score-side analogue FACETS fair averages. report FACETS score-side equivalence operational scoring rule unless convention substantively justified. Standard errors fair-average value opt-MML bounded GPCM fits via fair_se = TRUE. original SE, Model S.E., ModelBasedSE, Real S.E., FitAdjustedSE columns retain meaning PCM (scaled facet-measure SEs); fair-average uncertainty reported distinct columns Fair(M) S.E., Fair(M) CI Lower, AdjustedAverageSE.

title: Build an adjusted-score reference table bundle — fair_average_table
text: stacked: cross-facet table global comparison. by_facet: per-facet formatted tables reporting. raw_by_facet: unformatted values custom analyses/plots. settings: scoring-transformation filtering options used. Larger observed-vs-fair gaps can indicate systematic scoring tendencies specific facet levels.

title: Build a FACETS-style fit-measures review table — fit_measures_table
text: x Output fit_mfrm() diagnose_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional diagnostics object. supplied, x may fitted object used provenance. facet Optional facet-name filter, example "Rater". include_person Logical; FALSE (default), excludes Person facet operational facet elements shown first. lower, upper Optional mean-square review band. Defaults mfrm_misfit_thresholds(). zstd_cut Absolute ZSTD cutoff used directional underfit/overfit flags. Default 2. ci_level Confidence level used add approximate Wald intervals facet measures. Default 0.95. threshold_profiles mean-square threshold profiles summarize addition active table band. "literature" (default) returns commonly cited bands Linacre, Bond & Fox, Wright & Linacre; "active" returns active band; "" returns ; "none" suppresses profile summaries. fit_df_method Degrees--freedom convention used diagnostics computed inside helper. "engine" keeps package-native fit df, "facets" makes primary ZSTD columns use FACETS/Wright-Masters fourth-moment df convention, "" keeps engine columns primary adding FACETS-style companion df/ZSTD columns comparison. df_zstd_tolerance Smallest absolute engine-vs-FACETS-style ZSTD difference treated interpretively visible rather rounding noise df_sensitivity. Default 0.05. df_zstd_large_shift Absolute engine-vs-FACETS-style ZSTD difference labeled large_zstd_shift zstd_cut flag status unchanged. Default 0.5. df_ratio_tolerance Relative df-difference threshold used label df_convention_difference; example, 0.05 means 5 percent engine-vs-FACETS-style df difference. Default 0.05. sort_by Sorting rule: "status" prioritizes underfit/overfit rows, "abs_zstd" sorts largest absolute ZSTD, "facet" / "level" sort alphabetically. top_n Optional maximum number rows returned main table.

title: Build a FACETS-style fit-measures review table — fit_measures_table
text: helper gives users direct table route common FACETS-style question: raters, criteria, facet elements show underfit overfit? uses fit statistics already computed diagnose_mfrm(). Directional labels based mean-square ZSTD evidence: high MnSq positive large ZSTD labeled underfit; low MnSq negative large ZSTD labeled overfit. Rows conflicting directions labeled mixed. Treat table review screen inspect substantive context removing raters changing instrument. FACETS-style ZSTD comparison controlled fit_df_method. MnSq values compared first; df ZSTD columns explain MnSq values standardized. Use fit_df_method = "" preparing table FACETS users explaining |ZSTD| flags change across df conventions. df_zstd_tolerance, df_zstd_large_shift, df_ratio_tolerance arguments make df-sensitivity screen explicit table can reproduced stricter permissive review rules.

title: Build a FACETS-style fit-measures review table — fit_measures_table
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) fm <- fit_measures_table(fit, facet = "Rater") fm$facets_table fm$underfit  # Include FACETS-style df/ZSTD companion columns for comparison. fm_facets <- fit_measures_table(fit, facet = "Rater", fit_df_method = "both") fm_facets$df_conversion_guide$decision_guide } # }

Person-involving interactions, higher-order interactions, random-effect interaction terms outside current scope. min_obs_per_interaction Minimum weighted observations recommended interaction cell. Cells value flagged interaction_effect_table() handled according interaction_policy. interaction_policy handle sparse interaction cells: "warn" (default), "error", "silent". anchors Optional anchor table. group_anchors Optional group-anchor table. noncenter_facet One facet leave non-centered. dummy_facets Facets fix zero. positive_facets Facets positive orientation. anchor_policy handle anchor-review issues: "warn" (default), "error", "silent". min_common_anchors Minimum anchored levels per linking facet used anchor-review recommendations. min_obs_per_element Minimum weighted observations per facet level used anchor-review recommendations. min_obs_per_category Minimum weighted observations per score category used anchor-review recommendations. quad_points Integer number Gauss-Hermite quadrature points used MML integration person distribution. Recommended tiers: Internal benchmarks show marginal log-likelihood still drifts ~0.5-1 logit quad_points = 15 quad_points = 61 moderately sized designs, default now sits publication tier; set lower value explicitly exploratory runs. maxit Maximum optimizer iterations. reltol Optimization tolerance. mml_engine MML optimization engine method = "MML": "direct" (default) uses direct BFGS marginal log-likelihood, "em" uses EM loop RSM / PCM population = NULL, "hybrid" uses EM warm start direct optimizer. Unsupported combinations currently fall back "direct" record fallback fit$summary. population_formula Optional one-sided formula person-level latent-regression population model, example ~ grade + ses. current release, latent regression implemented method = "MML" unidimensional conditional-normal population model. person_data Optional one-row-per-person data.frame holding background variables population_formula. Numeric, logical, factor, ordered factor, character predictors expanded stats::model.matrix(); categorical xlevels contrasts stored replay scoring. Required population_formula supplied. person_id Optional person-ID column person_data. Defaults person column exists person_data. population_policy missing background data handled latent-regression fit. "none" (default) keeps 0.1.5 fixed-effects behaviour. "laplace" currently aliases "empirical_bayes" reserved future penalised-MML implementation. facet_prior_sd Optional numeric scalar. supplied, shrinkage prior variance fixed facet_prior_sd^2 instead estimated method moments. Useful eliciting prior domain knowledge previous fit. shrink_person Logical. TRUE facet_shrinkage active, empirical-Bayes shrinkage applied fit$facets$person. Default FALSE, since MML already integrates N(0, 1) prior theta; option mainly benefits JML. attach_diagnostics Logical. TRUE, diagnose_mfrm() run fit residual_pca = "none", per-level SE, Infit, Outfit, InfitZSTD, OutfitZSTD, PtMeaCorr columns diagnostics$measures merged onto fit$facets$others (non-person facets) fit$facets$person (Person rows). convenient downstream code expects FACETS Table 7 style facet table fit statistics one place, lets summary(fit) show per-person fit columns alongside measure. person rows, existing posterior SE (typical method = "MML") preserved diagnostic SE attached existing column empty. EM engine (mml_engine = "em" EM warm-start step mml_engine = "hybrid") honours checkpoint; direct optim() engine ignores . Use make long MML EM fits crash-resilient shared compute environments.

title: Fit many-facet ordered-response models with a flexible number of facets — fit_mfrm
text: fit_mfrm() estimates many-facet ordered-response models. RSM PCM branches follow many-facet Rasch-family tradition (Linacre, 1989); bounded GPCM branch extends partial-credit kernel estimated positive slopes package's documented identification constraints. equal-slope RSM/PCM branch, two-facet design (rater \(j\), criterion \(\)) : $$\ln\frac{P(X_{nij} = k)}{P(X_{nij} = k-1)} =   \theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i - \tau_k$$ \(\theta_n\) person ability, \(\delta_j\) rater severity, \(\beta_i\) criterion difficulty, \(\tau_k\) \(k\)-th Rasch-Andrich threshold.  number facets may specified via facets argument; enters additive term linear predictor \(\eta\). model = "RSM", thresholds \(\tau_k\) shared across levels facets. model = "PCM", level step_facet receives threshold vector \(\tau_{,k}\) package's shared observed score scale. bounded model = "GPCM", adjacent-category kernel multiplied positive slope designated slope-facet level: $$\ln\frac{P(X_{nij} = k)}{P(X_{nij} = k-1)} =   \alpha_g(\eta - \tau_{g,k}),\quad \alpha_g > 0.$$ current implementation requires slope_facet == step_facet identifies slopes sum--zero constraint log slopes, geometric mean 1. two ordered categories (\(K = 1\)), RSM/PCM branch reduces usual binary Rasch logit single category boundary: $$\ln\frac{P(X_{n\cdot} = 1)}{P(X_{n\cdot} = 0)} = \eta - \tau_1$$ Bounded GPCM uses slope-scaled counterpart \(\alpha_g(\eta - \tau_{g,1})\). method = "MML", person parameters integrated using Gauss-Hermite quadrature EAP estimates computed post-hoc. method = "JML", parameters estimated jointly fixed effects. "JMLE" remains accepted compatibility alias, package output now uses "JML" public label. See "Estimation methods" section mfrmr-package details.

title: Fit many-facet ordered-response models with a flexible number of facets — fit_mfrm
text: mfrmr treats RSM / PCM equal-weighting reference route operational many-facet measurement. Rasch-family branch, discrimination fixed, scoring model differentially reweight item-facet combinations estimated slopes. Bounded GPCM supported alternative users explicitly accept discrimination-based reweighting. often improves model fit, package treat better fit alone sufficient reason replace equal-weighting Rasch-family model. weight argument separate modeling choice. supplies observation-weight column; create free-form facet-weighting scheme change fixed-discrimination contract RSM / PCM.

text: fit_mfrm() follows Linacre (1989) many-facet Rasch specification: person ability integrated N(0, 1) prior (N(X\beta, \sigma^2) latent-regression population model population_formula supplied), every facet parameter (Rater, Criterion, Task, ...) estimated fixed effect identified sum--zero constraint. hierarchical prior, shrinkage, variance component facets. text: Joint maximum likelihood (method = "JML" / "JMLE") estimates structural parameters (facets, thresholds, slopes) every person measure fixed parameters one optimization. incidental-parameter problem Neyman & Scott (1948): structural parameter estimates inconsistent number persons grows number items per person held fixed, carrying bias order \(1/L\) (\(L\) number items per person) vanish sample size. Wright & Stone (1979) Wright & Masters (1982, ch. 5) document empirical \((L-1)/L\) correction approximately removes bias dichotomous Rasch model; mfrmr apply correction (bias_correction argument exists). Practical recommendation: Use method = "MML" value reported manuscript operational decision. MML integrates person measures population prior produces consistent structural estimates marginal observed-information SEs. title: Fit many-facet ordered-response models with a flexible number of facets — fit_mfrm
code: # Fast smoke run: a JML fit on the bundled `example_core` toy # dataset finishes in well under a second and returns a populated # `summary` overview ready for inspection. toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit_quick <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                       method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit_quick$summary[, c("Model", "Method", "N", "Converged")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   Model Method     N Converged #>   <chr> <chr>  <int> <lgl>     #> 1 RSM   JML      768 TRUE       if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full run with the package default MML estimator (recommended for # final reporting because person parameters are integrated out under # an N(0, 1) prior). The default `quad_points = 31` is the # publication tier; `quad_points = 7` below is an exploratory speed # setting and should not be used as the final manuscript fit. fit <- fit_mfrm(   data = toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   model = "RSM",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) fit$summary s_fit <- summary(fit) s_fit$overview[, c("Model", "Method", "Converged")] # Look for: Converged = TRUE. If FALSE, raise `maxit`, relax `reltol`, #   or inspect `summary(fit)$key_warnings` for sparse-cell or #   identification flags. s_fit$person_overview # Look for: Mean ~ 0 logits and SD ~ 1 logit are typical when the #   sample is centred on the test difficulty. SD < 0.5 suggests the #   test is too easy / hard for this group; SD > 1.5 suggests strong #   targeting mismatch or extreme-score persons (see `Extreme` flag). s_fit$targeting # Look for: |Targeting| < ~0.5 logits is comfortable; larger absolute #   values mean persons sit systematically above or below the facet #   means under the package's sum-to-zero identification. p_fit <- plot(fit, draw = FALSE) p_fit$wright_map$data$plot  # JML is available for exploratory / fast iteration passes: fit_jml <- fit_mfrm(   data = toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "JML",   model = "RSM",   maxit = 30 ) summary(fit_jml)$overview[, c("Model", "Method", "Converged")]  # Latent regression (MML only) uses person-level background variables: person_tbl <- unique(toy[c("Person")]) person_tbl$Grade <- seq_len(nrow(person_tbl)) person_tbl$Group <- rep(c("A", "B"), length.out = nrow(person_tbl)) fit_pop <- fit_mfrm(   data = toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   population_formula = ~ Grade + Group,   person_data = person_tbl ) summary(fit_pop)$population_overview summary(fit_pop)$population_coding  # Binary responses are supported as ordered two-category scores: set.seed(1) binary_toy <- expand.grid(   Person = paste0("P", 1:30),   Item = paste0("I", 1:4),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) theta <- stats::rnorm(length(unique(binary_toy$Person))) beta <- seq(-0.8, 0.8, length.out = length(unique(binary_toy$Item))) eta <- theta[match(binary_toy$Person, unique(binary_toy$Person))] -   beta[match(binary_toy$Item, unique(binary_toy$Item))] binary_toy$Score <- stats::rbinom(nrow(binary_toy), 1, stats::plogis(eta)) fit_binary <- fit_mfrm(   data = binary_toy,   person = "Person",   facets = "Item",   score = "Score",   model = "RSM",   method = "JML",   maxit = 30 ) fit_binary$summary[, c("Model", "Categories", "Converged")]  # Next steps after fitting: diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) head(chk$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady")]) } # }

title: Bounded GPCM Support Matrix — gpcm_capability_matrix
text: Call gpcm_capability_matrix() using GPCM new workflow. Treat blocked rows explicit non-support, temporary omissions. Treat deferred rows future-extension targets rather part current user-facing support.

It does not establish an operational GPCM linking decision, anchor-drift absence claim, or equating-chain adequacy claim by itself. #> 9                                                                                                                                                                                                                Requires explicit slope-aware specifications and keeps the current bounded branch's facet-role restrictions. Recovery checks are direct simulation/refit summaries, not design-planning or forecasting claims. `assess_mfrm_recovery()` requires user-supplied practical thresholds before RMSE or bias can be interpreted as adequate. #> 10                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Supported with caveat as a partial reporting/export bundle over already-supported GPCM diagnostics, direct tables, plots, manifests, and replay scripts. Full FACETS-style score-side contract review, design forecasting, and automatic operational scoring claims remain outside this route. #> 11                                                                                          Slope-aware element-conditional construction: slope-facet element rows use that level's own slope; non-slope-facet rows (Person, Rater, ...) use the geometric-mean-one slope by identification convention. The historical SE columns in the output are scaled facet-measure SEs, not fair-average SEs. Use `fair_se = TRUE` to request structural delta-method fair-average SEs for non-person rows when the MML observed-information Hessian is available. #> 12                                                                                                    Supported with caveat as a role-based person x rater-like x criterion-like Monte Carlo simulation/refit route. It uses the bounded-GPCM generator and refits bounded GPCM with the supplied or fit-derived step/slope facet contract, but it reports design-level operating characteristics only. Slope-recovery adequacy, diagnostic screening operating characteristics, signal detection, and arbitrary- facet planning remain separate routes. #> 13                                                                                                                                                                               Supported with caveat as slope-aware repeated simulation/refit screening evidence for the current role-based person x rater-like x criterion-like design layer. The summaries are Type I proxy, sensitivity proxy, DIF target-flag, and bias-screening readouts, not calibrated inferential tests, operational screening gates, or arbitrary-facet planning validation. #> 14                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Supported with caveat as direct DFF/DIF screening over the fitted bounded-GPCM expected-score and residual scale. Residual-method contrasts and interaction cells remain screening evidence; refit contrasts must retain explicit linking and precision gates before any stronger subgroup-comparison wording is used. #> 15                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Future extensions, listed for transparency. Out of scope for the current bounded GPCM branch. #> 16 Bias point estimates use the slope-aware GPCM kernel: the bias parameter is the additive shift on the linear predictor that maximises the per-cell GPCM log-likelihood. `LR ChiSq`, `LR Prob.`, and profile-CI columns compare that fitted shift with zero by conditional profile likelihood. SE / t / Prob columns use conditional plug-in information at the bias point estimate. All quantities hold theta, steps, slopes, and other facet estimates fixed, so they support screening and follow-up review rather than standalone fairness claims. #> 17                                                                                                Supported with caveat for package-native scorefile export only. Rows carry fitted expected score, residual, standardized residual, observed-category probability, score slope, native structural expected-score uncertainty, selectable score-side delta SEs, and explicit caveat fields when the required MML diagnostics are available. The route does not export FACETS-equivalent score-side SEs or establish operational score-scale equivalence. #> 18                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Not yet generalized to the full FACETS-style output-contract review. title: Bounded GPCM Score-Side Export Contract — gpcm_score_side_contract
text: helper enable full FACETS-style score-side review. records requirements separate current caveated facets_output_file_bundle(include = "score") route future facets_output_contract_review() route bounded GPCM. Use release-maintenance checklist. Rows marked implemented_with_caveat support current package-native bounded-GPCM scorefile route. Rows marked required_for_full_facets_review still blockers full FACETS-style output-contract review. Rows marked validated_dependency already available package sufficient justify full FACETS score-side equivalence.

title: Import an mirt fit to an mfrmr-compatible bundle — import_mirt_fit

title: Import an mirt fit to an mfrmr-compatible bundle — import_mirt_fit
text: fit object returned mirt::mirt() (SingleGroupClass). model One "RSM", "PCM", "GPCM". importer infer model mirt object; pass model estimated. item_facet Name assign item facet imported bundle (default "Item"). compute_fit Logical. TRUE, run mirt::itemfit() mirt::personfit() populate Infit / Outfit / OutfitZSTD columns returned facet tables, plus build measurement-side mfrm_diagnostics bundle consumable summary(), plot.mfrm_fit(), plot_qc_dashboard(), etc. Default FALSE keeps importer fast (skeleton ).

title: Import an mirt fit to an mfrmr-compatible bundle — import_mirt_fit
text: mfrm_imported_fit object. Slots: summary Model / method / N / LogLik / AIC / BIC. facets$person Person ID, Estimate, SE, Extreme, plus Infit / Outfit / OutfitZSTD / Zh compute_fit = TRUE. facets$others Item-level estimates slopes; compute_fit = TRUE, also Infit / Outfit / S_X2 / RMSEA / df mirt::itemfit(). steps Per-item threshold parameters extracted IRT parameterisation (b1, ..., b(K-1)). config List resolved model item_facet used import; downstream plot table helpers consult dispatch correctly imported bundle. diagnostics mfrm_diagnostics-shape bundle compute_fit = TRUE; NULL otherwise. source Imported-metadata.

title: Import an mirt fit to an mfrmr-compatible bundle — import_mirt_fit
text: Bundles bias / DIF / anchor / replay slots explicitly populated; full bidirectional import / export planned future release.

title: Import an mirt fit to an mfrmr-compatible bundle — import_mirt_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (requireNamespace("mirt", quietly = TRUE)) {   response_matrix <- matrix(sample(0:3, 60, replace = TRUE), nrow = 20)   colnames(response_matrix) <- paste0("Item", seq_len(ncol(response_matrix)))   fit <- mirt::mirt(response_matrix, 1, itemtype = "gpcm", verbose = FALSE)   imported <- import_mirt_fit(fit, model = "GPCM")   imported$summary } } # }

text: named list : summary: one-row inter-rater summary pairs: pair-level agreement table settings: applied options thresholds

title: Build an inter-rater agreement report — interrater_agreement_table
text: helper computes pairwise rater agreement matched contexts returns pair-level table one-row summary. output package-native require knowledge legacy report numbering.

title: Build an inter-rater agreement report — interrater_agreement_table
text: summary: overall agreement level, number/share flagged pairs. pairs: pairwise exact agreement, correlation, direction/size gaps. settings: applied facet matching warning thresholds. Pairs flagged low exact agreement low correlation generally deserve highest calibration priority.

title: Build an inter-rater agreement report — interrater_agreement_table
text: Run explicit rater_facet (context_facets needed). Review summary(ir) top flagged rows ir$pairs. Visualize plot_interrater_agreement().

title: Build an inter-rater agreement report — interrater_agreement_table
text: pairs data.frame contains: Rater1, Rater2 Rater pair identifiers. N Number matched-context observations pair. Exact Proportion exact score agreements. ExpectedExact Expected exact agreement chance. Adjacent Proportion adjacent (+/- 1 category) agreements. MeanDiff Signed mean score difference (Rater1 - Rater2). MAD Mean absolute score difference. Corr Pearson correlation paired scores. Flag Logical; TRUE Exact < exact_warn Corr < corr_warn. OpportunityCount, ExactCount, ExpectedExactCount, AdjacentCount Raw counts behind agreement proportions. summary data.frame contains: RaterFacet Name rater facet analyzed. TotalPairs Number rater pairs evaluated. ExactAgreement Mean exact agreement across pairs. AgreementMinusExpected Observed exact agreement minus expected exact agreement. MeanCorr Mean pairwise correlation. FlaggedPairs, FlaggedShare Count proportion flagged pairs. RaterSeparation, RaterReliability Severity-spread indices rater facet, reported separately agreement.

title: Build an inter-rater agreement report — interrater_agreement_table
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) ir <- interrater_agreement_table(fit, rater_facet = "Rater") # One-row overview: ExactAgreement, ExpectedExactAgreement, MeanCorr, # RaterSeparation, and RaterReliability are the headline reportable # statistics. ir$summary # Per-pair detail (Rater1 vs Rater2 with Exact, Adjacent, Corr, MAD). head(ir$pairs) p_ir <- plot(ir, draw = FALSE) p_ir$data$plot }

ExampleCore   P006   R01   Content     3     A  d <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   data = d,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "JML",   maxit = 30 ) summary(fit) #> Many-Facet Rasch Model Summary #>   Model: RSM | Method: JML | N: 768 | Persons: 48 | Facets: 2 | Categories: 4 #>  #> Status #>  - Overall status: usable_fit #>  - Convergence: converged (severity: pass, sup-norm: 0.033) #>  - Estimation path: RSM / JML #>  - Reporting readiness: exploratory_fit_ready_for_diagnostics #>  #> Key warnings #>  - None. #>  #> Next actions #>  - If formal SE/CI or strict marginal diagnostics are needed, re-fit with `method = "MML"`. #>  - Run `diagnose_mfrm(fit, diagnostic_mode = "both")` for element-level fit review. #>  - Use `plot(fit, type = "wright", preset = "publication")` for targeting and scale review. #>  - After diagnostics, use `reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diagnostics)` for reporting readiness. #>  #> title: Build an anchor table from fitted estimates — make_anchor_table
text: function exports estimated facet parameters anchor table use subsequent calibrations.  standard approach linking across administrations: reference run establishes measurement scale, anchored re-analyses place new data scale. Re-run review_mfrm_anchors() receiving data verify compatibility estimation. digits parameter controls rounding precision. code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) t5 <- measurable_summary_table(fit) summary(t5) p_t5 <- plot(t5, draw = FALSE) p_t5$data$plot }

what: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
text: mfrm_d_study() applies practical D-study projection variance components mfrm_generalizability(). answers questions "happens G Phi use 2, 3, 4 raters?" without re-fitting Rasch/MFRM model.

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
code: mfrm_d_study(   x,   design_grid = NULL,   object_facet = "Person",   random_facets = NULL,   residual_scaling = c("highest_order", "single_condition", "none", "sensitivity"),   ... )

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
text: x Output mfrm_generalizability() mfrm_fit. mfrm_fit supplied, mfrm_generalizability() called first. design_grid Data frame named list giving planned counts random measurement facet. Column names may facet names (example Rater) n_ plus facet name (example n_Rater). NULL, one row using observed number levels returned. object_facet, random_facets Passed mfrm_generalizability() x mfrm_fit. residual_scaling collapsed residual variance scaled planned facet counts increase. "highest_order" treats residual highest-order person---conditions/error variance divides product planned counts. "single_condition" divides smallest planned facet count, conservative sensitivity check unmodeled person--one-facet interactions may dominate. "none" leaves residual unscaled. "sensitivity" returns three assumptions design row. ... Additional arguments passed mfrm_generalizability() x mfrm_fit.

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
text: object class mfrm_d_study, data.frame one row per design scenario columns planned facet counts, variance terms, projected G, projected Phi, interpretation bands, identification status inherited mfrm_generalizability().

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
text: projection uses variance decomposition already estimated mfrm_generalizability(). random measurement facet j, main-effect variance contributes sigma2_j / n_j absolute-error denominator. residual term contains unmodeled person--facet higher-order interaction variance current simplified G-study, selected residual_scaling assumption reported explicitly. relative-decision denominator uses scaled residual term. pragmatic D-study planning layer, full p x r x ANOVA decomposition. person--rater person--item interactions primary estimand, use residual_scaling = "sensitivity" treat output planning evidence; fit fully crossed G-theory model externally interaction components must estimated separately. G Phi values returned belong generalizability-theory metric family. interpreted coefficient alpha, omega, KR-20, IRT marginal/separation reliability, even though summaries may displayed 0–1 scale broader reporting dashboards.

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
text: Cronbach, L. J., Gleser, G. C., Nanda, H., & Rajaratnam, N. (1972). dependability behavioral measurements: Theory generalizability scores profiles. Wiley. Brennan, R. L. (2001). Generalizability theory. Springer.

title: Project G-theory coefficients under alternative D-study designs — mfrm_d_study
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   gt <- mfrm_generalizability(fit)   ds <- mfrm_d_study(gt, data.frame(Rater = c(2, 3, 4), Criterion = 4))   ds[, c("n_Rater", "n_Criterion", "G", "Phi",          "GStatus", "PhiStatus", "IdentificationStatus")]   # If IdentificationStatus is not "identified", even large G/Phi   # values remain identification warnings, not high-stakes evidence. } } # }

what: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: Re-fits rating data underlying mfrm_fit crossed random-effects model Score ~ 1 + (1 | Person) + (1 | Facet1) + ... + Residual via lme4::lmer, returns canonical G-theory variance components plus G / Phi coefficients. Useful reviewers ask generalizability-theory complement Rasch-style separation / reliability statistics diagnose_mfrm() already emits.

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
code: mfrm_generalizability(   fit,   data = NULL,   object_facet = "Person",   random_facets = NULL,   reml = TRUE )

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: fit mfrm_fit fit_mfrm(). data Optional data frame. NULL, rating data stored fit$prep$data used. object_facet Facet plays role "object measurement" – typically "Person" (default). random_facets Character vector non-person facets treat random conditions measurement. Default uses every facet object_facet. reml Logical, passed lme4::lmer() (default TRUE).

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: object class mfrm_generalizability : variance_components One row per random effect plus residual, columns Source, Variance, ProportionVariance. coefficients One-row data frame G (generalizability coefficient, relative decision) Phi (dependability coefficient, absolute decision), coefficient status labels, identification status fitted random-effects model. design Description crossed-random model.

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: G appropriate relative decisions (rank-ordering persons): G = sigma2(p) / (sigma2(p) + sigma2(Residual)). reported Phi appropriate absolute decisions (cut-score classification): Phi = sigma2(p) / (sigma2(p) + sigma2(facet main effects) + sigma2(Residual)), D-study scaling. Use mfrm_d_study() project G / Phi planned numbers raters, items, criteria, random measurement facets. Reporting bands follow Brennan (2001): G / Phi >= 0.8 high-stakes decisions, >= 0.7 routine reporting.

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: helper formulates random-effects model main effects (Score ~ 1 + (1|Person) + (1|Facet1) + ... + Residual); explicit (1 | Person:Rater), (1 | Person:Criterion), (1 | Rater:Criterion) interaction terms estimated. two-way higher interaction variance therefore folded Residual term – standard one-observation-per-cell approximation – can bias G downward person x facet interactions substantively large. function reports one-observation-per-cell baseline. mfrm_d_study() applies D-study scaling, including residual-scaling sensitivity checks, simplified variance-component decomposition. person--facet interaction terms estimated separately, D-study projections remain practical planning evidence rather replacement fully specified G-theory design. Boundary singular lme4 fits retained diagnostic evidence treated high-stakes-ready G/D-study evidence.

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
text: Cronbach, L. J., Gleser, G. C., Nanda, H., & Rajaratnam, N. (1972). dependability behavioral measurements: Theory generalizability scores profiles. Wiley. Brennan, R. L. (2001). Generalizability theory. Springer.

title: Generalizability-theory variance decomposition for an MFRM design — mfrm_generalizability
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   gt <- mfrm_generalizability(fit)   gt$variance_components   # Look for: a Person variance component well above any single   #   non-person facet's variance share. Large rater or criterion   #   variance shares mean those conditions add measurement error   #   relative to person spread.   gt$coefficients   # Look for: G >= 0.7 for routine reporting, >= 0.8 for high-stakes.   #   G < Phi means absolute decisions are noisier than relative   #   decisions; review whether facet main effects need anchoring.   # Always check IdentificationStatus before using the bands:   gt$coefficients[, c("G", "Phi", "GStatus", "PhiStatus",                       "IdentificationStatus")]   gt$design$identification_note   # If IdentificationStatus is not "identified", treat G/Phi as   # design-review evidence rather than high-stakes-ready reliability. } } # }

what: MnSq misfit threshold pair used across mfrmr screening helpers — mfrm_misfit_thresholds

title: MnSq misfit threshold pair used across mfrmr screening helpers — mfrm_misfit_thresholds
text: named numeric vector c(lower = ..., upper = ...) lower < upper.

title: MnSq misfit threshold pair used across mfrmr screening helpers — mfrm_misfit_thresholds
text: Helpers consume band include summary.mfrm_diagnostics() (misfit_flagged block key_warnings auto-flag), build_misfit_casebook() (new element_fit source family), bias / misfit narrative inside build_apa_outputs(), facet_quality_dashboard() misfit_warn = NULL. Setting options top analysis script therefore changes every downstream screen .

title: MnSq misfit threshold pair used across mfrmr screening helpers — mfrm_misfit_thresholds
text: Two scalar R options drive band: mfrmr.misfit_lower Lower acceptance bound. Default 0.5. mfrmr.misfit_upper Upper acceptance bound. Default 1.5. Pass scalar arguments override options single call, e.g. mfrm_misfit_thresholds(lower = 0.7, upper = 1.3) tighter Bond & Fox (2015) reporting band.

title: MnSq misfit threshold pair used across mfrmr screening helpers — mfrm_misfit_thresholds
code: mfrm_misfit_thresholds() #> lower upper  #>   0.5   1.5  old <- options(mfrmr.misfit_lower = 0.7, mfrmr.misfit_upper = 1.3) mfrm_misfit_thresholds() #> lower upper  #>   0.7   1.3  options(old)

title: Analyze the MFRM design network — mfrm_network_analysis
text: mfrm_network_analysis() treats person/facet-level observation design undirected weighted graph. Nodes person facet levels; edges connect levels co-occur least one observed rating; edge weights co-observation counts. resulting network metrics design diagnostics, psychometric measures person ability rater quality. plot(net, type = "centrality"), plot(net, type = "facet_summary"), plot(net, type = "network") provide immediate visual checks; use draw = FALSE extract reusable plot data. useful review columns : Components: one component means design disconnected measurement subsets. IsArticulationPoint: node whose removal increase disconnectedness. IsBridge: edge whose removal increase disconnectedness. Betweenness: routing-dependence indicator; high values identify levels carry many shortest paths design graph. incomplete rater-mediated designs, graph summaries help identify fragile linking structures interpreting facet measures planning additional data collection.

title: Analyze the MFRM design network — mfrm_network_analysis
text: McEwen, M. R. (2015). Development Software Prototype Generating Classifying Incomplete Many-Facet-Rasch Model Rating Designs. Brigham Young University. Csardi, G., Nepusz, T., Traag, V., Horvat, S., Zanini, F., Noom, D., & Muller, K. (2026). igraph: Network Analysis Visualization.

title: Analyze the MFRM design network — mfrm_network_analysis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "JML", maxit = 30 ) if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   net <- mfrm_network_analysis(fit)   net$summary   head(net$node_metrics)   net$cut_nodes   plot(net, type = "centrality", draw = FALSE) } } # }

what: Build report-ready output from mfrm_results() — mfrm_report

Instead, turns comprehensive first-screen result first-screen table, section plan, claim-readiness table, report-gap table, report-index table, template-index table, fit-criteria table, result-specific fit evidence summaries, fit-reporting wording templates, precision/separation reporting templates, bias/DFF reporting templates, misfit/pathway reporting templates, linking/anchor reporting templates, ZSTD-convention table, evidence-boundary table, next-action table, optional Markdown HTML report.
