title: Build report-ready output from mfrm_results() — mfrm_report
text: x mfrm_results() object. style Report emphasis. "qc" default first-screen report. "apa" emphasizes manuscript wording, "validation" emphasizes validity-argument boundary, "reviewer" emphasizes reviewer response preparation, "technical" emphasizes appendix/reproducibility routes. output Return format: "object" mfrm_report object, "markdown" character scalar, "html" temporary HTML file, "tables" report's named data-frame list.

title: Build report-ready output from mfrm_results() — mfrm_report
text: Depending output, mfrm_report object, Markdown character scalar, mfrm_report_html object, named list data frames.

title: Build report-ready output from mfrm_results() — mfrm_report
text: intended workflow : Create res <- mfrm_results(fit, include = ...). Inspect summary(res)$triage summary(res)$next_actions. Create report <- mfrm_report(res, style = "qc"). Read summary(report) report$first_screen opening detailed report tables. Use report$report_index choose next PrimaryTable, TemplateTable, plot route, export route. Use report$template_index copying APA/QC/validation wording. Use style = "apa", "validation", "reviewer", "technical" reporting question needed. Report rows deliberately distinguish evidence claims. first_screen table compact entry point: gives overall row one row per major evidence area status, readiness, main issue, next action, primary route. summary.mfrm_report method summarizes first screen immediate actions, optional -requested sections, claim-readiness counts, report gaps, template-boundary rows without introducing new pass/fail decision. default print method follows short reading order print every detailed evidence table. HTML output places reader guidance report-summary tables full Markdown text browser view starts first-screen route. report_index table detailed evidence-route index: lists major report areas, evidence status, readiness label, review-signal count, primary/template tables, evidence routes, template routes, plot routes, export route, mfrm_results(include = ...) preset inspect next. ordinary use, open detailed tables PrimaryTable TemplateTable columns rather scanning every element report$tables. template_index table stacks fit, precision, bias, misfit, linking wording templates single boundary/claim-strength index users drill area-specific template tables. claim_readiness table marks report claims ready, caveated, unavailable, require additional requested sections. report_gaps table turns statuses follow-actions. fit-specific tables keep multiple MnSq threshold profiles, observed fit-status counts, engine-vs-FACETS-style ZSTD conventions visible, including small-df/capping boundary used FACETS-style ZSTD review. summarize stored fit_measures component mfrm_results(); mfrm_report() recompute diagnostics. fit_reporting_templates table turns counts cautious APA/QC/validation/reviewer wording scaffolds keeping MnSq, ZSTD standardization, df sensitivity, separation/reliability separate sentences. reporting-template tables share EvidenceTable, EvidenceRoute, BoundaryType, ClaimStrength, RecommendedUse columns template can traced back evidence claim boundary. template_index stacks columns across template areas report authors can review unsupported caveated wording opening full template text. precision_reporting_templates table separation, reliability, strata using stored precision review diagnostics$reliability. bias_reporting_templates table available source result built include = "bias" keeps facet-level screens, interaction-bias contrasts, DFF follow-, fairness conclusions separate lanes. misfit_reporting_templates table available source result built include = "misfit_review" keeps unexpected responses, displacement, pathway-map evidence, case-review actions separate. linking_reporting_templates table available source result built include = "linking" keeps anchor readiness, drift review, equating-chain review, GPCM support boundaries separate. example, fit separation collapsed single pass/fail statement; bias screens treated final fairness conclusions; pathway/misfit rows case-review prompts; drift/equating claims require multiple fitted forms waves.

title: Build report-ready output from mfrm_results() — mfrm_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_small <- toy[toy$Person %in% unique(toy$Person)[1:6], , drop = FALSE] fit <- fit_mfrm(toy_small, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) res <- mfrm_results(fit, include = c("fit", "diagnostics", "tables"))  report <- mfrm_report(res, style = "qc") summary(report) report$first_screen report$report_index[, c("Area", "Readiness", "PrimaryTable",                         "TemplateTable", "PlotRoute")] report$template_index[, c("Area", "Topic", "BoundaryType",                           "ClaimStrength", "EvidenceRoute")]  # Open detailed evidence only after the index points to it. fit_primary <- report$report_index$PrimaryTable[   report$report_index$Area == "Fit" ][1] report$tables[[fit_primary]]  mfrm_report(res, output = "markdown") mfrm_report(res, output = "html") } # }

title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: Rasch-family branch used RSM PCM extends basic Rasch model incorporating multiple measurement facets single additive linear predictor log-odds scale. RSM/PCM adjacent-category equation observation person \(n\) ability \(\theta_n\) rated rater \(j\) severity \(\delta_j\) criterion \(\) difficulty \(\beta_i\), probability observing category \(k\) (\(K\) ordered categories) : $$P(X_{nij} = k \mid \theta_n, \delta_j, \beta_i, \tau) =   \frac{\exp\bigl[\sum_{s=1}^{k}(\theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i - \tau_s)\bigr]}        {\sum_{c=0}^{K}\exp\bigl[\sum_{s=1}^{c}(\theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i - \tau_s)\bigr]}$$ \(\tau_s\) Rasch-Andrich threshold (step) parameters RSM reference case \(\sum_{s=1}^{0}(\cdot) \equiv 0\) convention.  Additional facets enter additive terms linear predictor \(\eta = \theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i - \ldots\). additive predictor generalises number facets; facets argument fit_mfrm() accepts arbitrary-length character vector. Rating Scale Model (RSM) RSM (Andrich, 1978), levels step facet share single set threshold parameters \(\tau_1, \ldots, \tau_K\). Partial Credit Model (PCM) PCM (Masters, 1982), level designated step_facet threshold vector package's common observed score scale. current implementation, threshold locations may vary step-facet level, fitted score range defined one global category set taken observed data. Bounded Generalized Partial Credit Model (GPCM) bounded GPCM (Muraki, 1992), adjacent-category partial-credit kernel multiplied positive slope \(\alpha_g\) designated slope-facet level \(g\): $$\ln\frac{P(X_{nij} = k)}{P(X_{nij} = k-1)} =   \alpha_g(\theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i - \tau_{gk}).$$ current implementation requires slope_facet == step_facet identifies slopes log scale geometric mean 1. makes bounded GPCM slope-aware sensitivity/extension route, replacement equal-weighting RSM/PCM interpretation. Ordered-response scope implemented response-model scope ordered categorical . Binary responses \(K = 1\) special case formulation, handled ordinary ordered-score interface. means mfrmr supports ordered binary ordered polytomous data RSM PCM, plus narrow bounded GPCM branch one designated slope_facet currently must equal step_facet. Unordered nominal/multinomial response models yet implemented.

title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) MML integrates person ability distribution using Gauss-Hermite quadrature, broader marginal-likelihood framework introduced Bock & Aitkin (1981) IRT: $$L = \prod_{n} \int P(\mathbf{X}_n \mid \theta, \boldsymbol{\delta})   \, \phi(\theta) \, d\theta   \approx \prod_{n} \sum_{q=1}^{Q} w_q \,   P(\mathbf{X}_n \mid \theta_q, \boldsymbol{\delta})$$ \(\phi(\theta)\) assumed normal prior \((\theta_q, w_q)\) quadrature nodes weights.  Person estimates obtained post-hoc via Expected Posteriori (EAP): $$\hat{\theta}_n^{\mathrm{EAP}} =   \frac{\sum_q \theta_q \, w_q \, L(\mathbf{X}_n \mid \theta_q)}        {\sum_q w_q \, L(\mathbf{X}_n \mid \theta_q)}$$ MML avoids incidental-parameter problem generally preferred smaller samples. Note: Bock & Aitkin (1981) canonical citation Gauss-Hermite-quadrature MML framework. default mfrmr engine (mml_engine = "direct") optimises marginal log-likelihood direct gradient methods (BFGS / L-BFGS-B), Bock & Aitkin's signature EM algorithm. "em" "hybrid" engines follow EM template use BFGS M-step rather B&'s probit IRLS, target polytomous Rasch family rather B&'s 2PL probit model. Joint Maximum Likelihood (JML) JML estimates person facet parameters simultaneously fixed effects maximising joint log-likelihood \(\ell(\boldsymbol{\theta}, \boldsymbol{\delta} \mid \mathbf{X})\) directly.  assume parametric person distribution, can advantageous population shape strongly non-normal, parameter estimates known biased number persons small relative number items (Neyman & Scott, 1948). package still accepts "JMLE" backward-compatible alias, user-facing summaries documentation use "JML" public label. See fit_mfrm() practical guidance choosing two.

title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: RSM / PCM, diagnose_mfrm(..., diagnostic_mode = "") returns two complementary targets: legacy residual / EAP diagnostics marginal_fit layer whose expected counts pairwise summaries integrated posterior quadrature bundle rather plugged EAP point. screen structured limited-information evidence (Orlando & Thissen, 2000; Haberman & Sinharay, 2013; Sinharay & Monroe, 2025), omnibus accept / reject test, complements rather replaces separation / reliability inter-rater agreement summaries. full derivation, notation pairwise local-dependence events, lives vignette("mfrmr-mml--marginal-fit", package = "mfrmr").

title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: Key statistics reported throughout package: Infit (Information-Weighted Mean Square) Weighted average squared standardized residuals, weights model-based variance observation: $$\mathrm{Infit}_j = \frac{\sum_i Z_{ij}^2 \, \mathrm{Var}_i \, w_i}                               {\sum_i \mathrm{Var}_i \, w_i}$$ Expected value 1.0 model fit.  Values 0.5 suggest overfit (Mead-style responses); values 1.5 suggest underfit (noise misfit).  Infit sensitive unexpected patterns among -target observations (Wright & Masters, 1982). Note: 0.5–1.5 range general "productive measurement" band given Linacre (2002, RMT 16(2), 878). Context-specific bands come Wright & Linacre (1994, RMT 8(3), 370): 0.8–1.2 high-stakes MCQ, 0.7–1.3 run---mill MCQ, 0.6–1.4 rating-scale surveys, 0.5–1.7 clinical observation, 0.4–1.2 judged performance. See also Bond & Fox (2015) textbook summaries conventions. Outfit (Unweighted Mean Square) Simple average squared standardized residuals: $$\mathrm{Outfit}_j = \frac{\sum_i Z_{ij}^2 \, w_i}{\sum_i w_i}$$ expected value flagging thresholds Infit, sensitive extreme -target outliers (e.g., high-ability person scoring lowest category). ZSTD (Standardized Fit Statistic) Wilson-Hilferty (1931) cube-root transformation converts mean-square chi-square ratio approximate standard normal deviate: $$\mathrm{ZSTD} = \frac{\mathrm{MnSq}^{1/3} - (1 - 2/(9\,\mathit{df}))}                            {\sqrt{2/(9\,\mathit{df})}}$$ Values near 0 indicate expected fit; \(|\mathrm{ZSTD}| > 2\) flags potential misfit 5\ 1\ ZSTD reported alongside every Infit Outfit value. ZSTD withheld (NA) applicable df falls 1, Wilson-Hilferty transformation numerically unstable; FACETS/Winsteps WHEXACT can continue linear approximation cells. Residual basis MML vs JMLE engines method = "MML" fits, standardized residuals behind Infit, Outfit, ZSTD evaluated EAP person measures, shrunken toward population mean. JMLE engines FACETS evaluate formulas unshrunken JMLE estimates, MnSq ZSTD values numerically interchangeable across two residual bases, visibly extreme-scoring persons. Use method = "JML" external FACETS fit comparison requires JMLE-style residual basis, see facets_fit_df_guide() separate standardization-side df/ZSTD conventions. PTMEA (Point-Measure Correlation) Pearson correlation observed scores estimated person measures within facet level.  Positive values indicate scoring aligns latent trait dimension; negative values suggest reversed orientation scoring errors. Separation Package-reported separation ratio adjusted true standard deviation root-mean-square measurement error: $$G = \frac{\mathrm{SD}_{\mathrm{adj}}}{\mathrm{RMSE}}$$ \(\mathrm{SD}_{\mathrm{adj}} = \sqrt{\mathrm{ObservedVariance} - \mathrm{ErrorVariance}}\). Higher values indicate facet discriminates statistically distinct levels along measured variable. mfrmr, Separation model-based value RealSeparation provides conservative companion based RealSE. Reliability $$R = \frac{G^2}{1 + G^2}$$ Analogous Cronbach's alpha KR-20 reproducibility element ordering. mfrmr, Reliability model-based value RealReliability gives conservative companion based RealSE. MML, anchored observed-information ModelSE estimates non-person facets; JML keeps exploratory summaries. person facet MML, \(G\) \(R\) formulas applied EAP person measures posterior SDs error slot. EAP measures shrunken, observed variance already deflated (approximately true variance times reliability), subtracting mean posterior variance deflates . reported MML person separation/reliability therefore conservative summary: systematically lower IRT empirical-reliability convention \(\mathrm{Var}(\mathrm{EAP}) / (\mathrm{Var}(\mathrm{EAP}) + \overline{\mathrm{PSD}^2})\) numerically comparable JMLE-based person separation reliability FACETS. gap small measurement precise grows precision drops. Person rows can still carry model-based precision tier posterior SDs model-based quantities; tier describes SE source, FACETS comparability. Use method = "JML" FACETS-style person separation table required, treat MML person rows conservative summaries. Rasch/FACETS-style separation reliability fitted logit scale, intra-class correlation. Use compute_facet_icc() want complementary random-effects variance-share view observed-score scale; non-person facets, large ICC values indicate systematic facet variance rather desirable measurement reliability. Strata Number statistically distinguishable groups elements: $$H = \frac{4G + 1}{3}$$ Three strata commonly used practical target (Wright & Masters, 1982), package estimate inherits approximation limits separation index.

title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: Andrich, D. (1978). rating formulation ordered response categories. Psychometrika, 43, 561–573. Bond, T. G., & Fox, C. M. (2015). Applying Rasch model (3rd ed.). Routledge. Bock, R. D., & Aitkin, M. (1981). Marginal maximum likelihood estimation item parameters: Application EM algorithm. Psychometrika, 46, 443–459. Burnham, K. P., & Anderson, D. R. (2002). Model selection multimodel inference: practical information-theoretic approach (2nd ed.). Springer. (AIC / BIC weights Delta-IC bands used compare_mfrm().) Drasgow, F., Levine, M. V., & Williams, E. . (1985). Appropriateness measurement polychotomous item response models standardized indices. British Journal Mathematical Statistical Psychology, 38(1), 67–86. title: mfrmr: Many-Facet Ordered-Response Modeling in R — mfrmr-package
text: RSM vs PCM Rating Scale Model (RSM; Andrich, 1978) assumes levels step facet share identical threshold parameters.  Partial Credit Model (PCM; Masters, 1982) allows level step_facet set thresholds package's shared observed score scale. Use RSM rating rubric identical across items/criteria; use PCM category boundaries expected vary item criterion. current implementation, PCM assumes one common observed score support across fitted data, described fully mixed-category model arbitrary item-specific category counts. MML vs JML Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) integrates person ability distribution using Gauss-Hermite quadrature directly estimate person parameters; person estimates computed post-hoc via Expected Posteriori (EAP).  Joint Maximum Likelihood (JML) estimates person facet parameters simultaneously fixed effects; "JMLE" remains backward-compatible alias. MML generally preferred smaller samples avoids incidental-parameter problem JML.  JML assume normal person distribution can lighter computationally settings, may advantage population shape strongly non-normal. See fit_mfrm() usage. Fixed-calibration scoring fitting predict_mfrm_units() sample_mfrm_plausible_values() score future partially observed persons quadrature grid fitted scoring basis. ordinary MML fits, summaries inherit fitted marginal calibration directly. latent-regression MML fits, use fitted one-dimensional conditional normal population model therefore require one-row-per-person background data scored units fitted population model includes covariates. Intercept-latent-regression fits (population_formula = ~ 1) can reconstruct minimal person table scored person IDs. JML fits, mfrmr uses fitted facet step parameters together standard normal reference prior introduced post hoc scoring layer. useful practical fixed-scale scoring, still described limited approximation rather full ConQuest-style population modeling. Current ConQuest overlap package now includes first-version latent-regression MML branch, overlap ConQuest still described conservatively. documented overlap : ordered-response RSM / PCM, one latent dimension, conditional-normal person population model, person covariates supplied explicit one-row-per-person table expanded package-built model matrix. Categorical person covariates carry fitted levels contrasts scoring. scoped overlap, claim broad ConQuest numerical equivalence arbitrary imported design matrices, multidimensional models, imported design specifications, full plausible-values workflow.

what: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: Package-native guide checking connectedness, building anchor-based links, monitoring drift, screening differential facet functioning (DFF) mfrmr.

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: "design connected enough support common scale?" Use subset_connectivity_report() plot(..., type = "design_matrix"). "elements can export anchors existing fit?" Use make_anchor_table() review_mfrm_anchors(). "anchor new administration baseline?" Use anchor_to_baseline(). "common elements drifted across separately fitted waves?" Use detect_anchor_drift() plot_anchor_drift(). "Can synthesize anchor review, drift, chain evidence one review?" Use build_linking_review(). "specific facet levels function differently across groups?" Use analyze_dff(), plot_dif_heatmap(), plot_dif_summary().

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: Fit fit_mfrm() diagnose diagnose_mfrm(). Check connectedness subset_connectivity_report(). Build review anchors make_anchor_table() review_mfrm_anchors(). Use anchor_to_baseline() need place raw new data onto baseline scale. Use build_equating_chain() screened linking aid across already fitted waves. Use detect_anchor_drift() stability monitoring separately fitted waves. Use build_linking_review() need one operational synthesis object rather separate anchor/drift/chain tables. Run analyze_dff() checking connectivity common-scale evidence.

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: subset_connectivity_report() Summarizes connected subsets, bottleneck facets, design-matrix coverage. make_anchor_table() Extracts reusable anchor candidates fit. anchor_to_baseline() Anchors new raw data baseline fit returns anchored diagnostics plus consistency check baseline scale. detect_anchor_drift() Compares fitted waves directly flag unstable anchor elements. build_equating_chain() Accumulates screened pairwise links across series administrations forms. build_linking_review() Synthesizes anchor review, drift, screened-chain evidence one operational review surface. analyze_dff() Screens differential facet functioning residual refit methods, using screening-language unless linking precision support stronger interpretation.

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: Check connectedness interpreting subgroup wave differences. Use DFF outputs screening results common-scale linking weak. Always name facet, facet level, group pair involved DFF contrast. generic "DIF exists" statement interpretable many-facet design. Residual-method DFF classifications screening labels. ETS /B/C labels require refit output whose ClassificationSystem "ETS". Treat drift flags prompts review, automatic evidence anchor must removed. Treat LinkSupportAdequate = FALSE weak-link warning: least one linking facet retained fewer 5 common elements screening. Rebuild anchors defensible baseline rather chaining unstable links hand.

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: Cross-sectional linkage review: fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> subset_connectivity_report() -> plot(..., type = "design_matrix"). Baseline placement review: make_anchor_table() -> anchor_to_baseline() -> diagnose_mfrm(). Multi-wave drift review: fit wave separately -> detect_anchor_drift() -> build_linking_review() -> plot_anchor_drift(). Group comparison route: subset_connectivity_report() -> analyze_dff() -> dif_report() -> plot_dif_heatmap() / plot_dif_summary().

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
text: visual follow-, see mfrmr_visual_diagnostics. report/table selection, see mfrmr_reports_and_tables. end--end routes, see mfrmr_workflow_methods. longer walkthrough, see vignette("mfrmr-linking--dff", package = "mfrmr").

title: mfrmr Linking and DFF Guide — mfrmr_linking_and_dff
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both")  subsets <- subset_connectivity_report(fit, diagnostics = diag) subsets$summary[, c("Subset", "Observations", "ObservationPercent")]  dff <- analyze_dff(fit, diag, facet = "Rater", group = "Group", data = toy) head(dff$dif_table[, c("Level", "Group1", "Group2",                        "Classification", "ClassificationSystem")]) } # }

what: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: mfrmr_output_guide() returns compact table choosing among main table, report, review, bundle, export, compatibility helpers. user-facing map, analysis result.

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
code: mfrmr_output_guide(   scope = c("all", "public", "entry", "viewer", "binary", "tables", "reports", "reviews",     "bundles", "exports", "compatibility", "gpcm", "simulation", "linking", "network",     "response_time", "facets", "conquest", "r") )

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: scope rows return. "" returns full guide. "public" returns small top-level public surface users. "entry" returns recommended first-screen routes. "viewer" returns local-viewer routes built around mfrm_results(include = ...). "binary" returns two-category person-item Rasch route checks. values filter one output family bounded-GPCM-relevant routes. "linking" returns anchor, drift, equating route rows. "simulation" "network" return advanced design-review rows. "response_time" returns descriptive response-time QC rows. "facets", "conquest", "r" return user-pathway rows people arriving workflows.

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: data.frame one row per recommended route columns: Scope Question OutputFamily Lifecycle UserLevel APILayer ObjectRole DecisionBoundary RecommendedEntry MainFunction UseWhen TypicalInput NextStep GPCMStatus Notes

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: Naming convention used guide: *_table: focused table table-like result one evidence source *_report: multi-table evidence bundle reporting question *_review: status, interpretation, decision-support object *_bundle: reusable collection tables/metadata handoff export_*: writes files appendix artifacts

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: Use mfrmr_output_guide("public") want shortest top-level API map. Use mfrmr_output_guide("entry") specifically want first-screen creation routes. entry guide points new scripts fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> mfrm_results(), existing fits mfrm_results(), exploratory console work mfrm_results_interactive(). creating res, use summary(res)$next_actions choose next purpose-specific helper. Use mfrmr_output_guide("viewer") next step optional local Shiny reader; shows include preset use calling launch_mfrmr_viewer().

title: Choose an mfrmr output helper by user goal — mfrmr_output_guide
text: Treat MainFunction route try next UseWhen guardrail. guide replacement help pages listed functions; namespace map deciding page open. bounded GPCM, use scope = "gpcm" find support matrix table explains --scope routes handled.

title: mfrmr Reporting and APA Guide — mfrmr_reporting_and_apa
text: reporting_checklist() Turns current analysis objects prioritized revision guide DraftReady, Priority, NextAction. DraftReady means "ready draft documented caveats"; ReadyForAPA retained backward-compatible alias, neither field means "formal inference automatically justified". "Visual Displays" rows also mirror public plot family, checklist doubles figure-routing surface. build_apa_outputs() Builds shared-contract prose, table notes, captions, section map current fit diagnostics. build_summary_table_bundle() Turns supported summary() outputs named data.frame tables plus index manuscript appendix handoff, now also supports bundle-level summary() / plot() role coverage numeric QC. export_summary_appendix() Writes validated summary-table bundles CSV optional HTML appendix artifacts without requiring full fit-based export bundle. apa_table() Produces reproducible base-R tables APA-oriented labels, notes, captions. precision_review_report() Summarizes whether precision claims model-based, hybrid, exploratory. facet_statistics_report() Provides facet-level summaries often feed result tables appendix material. build_visual_summaries() Prepares publication-oriented figure data can cited report text. visual_reporting_template() Provides conservative figure placement, caption-starter, results-wording, overclaim-avoidance guidance public visual helpers.

title: mfrmr Reporting and APA Guide — mfrmr_reporting_and_apa
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both")  checklist <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) visual_reporting_template("manuscript")[, c("FigureFamily", "CaptionSkeleton")] head(checklist$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady", "NextAction")]) subset(   checklist$checklist,   Section == "Visual Displays",   c("Item", "Available", "NextAction") )  apa <- build_apa_outputs(fit, diagnostics = diag) apa$section_map[, c("SectionId", "Available")]  tbl <- apa_table(fit, which = "summary") tbl$caption bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(checklist) bundle$table_index apa_from_bundle <- apa_table(bundle, which = "section_summary") apa_from_bundle$caption  report_bundle <- export_mfrm_bundle(   fit,   diagnostics = diag,   output_dir = tempdir(),   prefix = "mfrmr_report_bundle",   include = c("core_tables", "checklist", "apa", "summary_tables", "html"),   overwrite = TRUE ) report_bundle$summary[, c("FilesWritten", "HtmlWritten")] } # }

title: mfrmr Reports and Tables Map — mfrmr_reports_and_tables
text: specifications_report() Documents model type, estimation method, anchors, core run settings. Best method sections reproducibility records. data_quality_report() Summarizes retained dropped rows, missingness, unknown elements. Best data cleaning narratives. estimation_iteration_report() Shows replayed convergence trajectories. Best diagnosing slow unstable estimation. precision_review_report() Summarizes whether SE, CI, reliability indices model-based, hybrid, exploratory. Best deciding strongly phrase inferential claims. facet_statistics_report() Bundles facet summaries, precision summaries, variability tests. Best facet-level reporting. subset_connectivity_report() Summarizes disconnected subsets coverage bottlenecks. Best linking anchor strategy review. rating_scale_table() Gives category counts, average measures, threshold diagnostics. Best first-pass category evaluation. category_structure_report() Adds transition points compact category warnings. Best category-order interpretation. category_curves_report() Returns category-probability, cumulative-probability, expected-ogive, total-information, category-specific information coordinates. Best downstream graphics report drafts. write_mfrm_residual_file() Writes observation-level residual file, optionally modeled category probabilities. Best external case review reproducible handoff. write_mfrm_subset_file() Writes connected-subset summary node-membership files. Best scale-linking review outside R. reporting_checklist() Turns analysis status action list priorities next steps. Best closing reporting gaps. build_apa_outputs() Creates manuscript-draft text, notes, captions, section maps shared reporting contract. build_summary_table_bundle() Converts supported summary() outputs named data.frame tables compact index appendix manuscript handoff, including recovery simulation recovery assessment outputs. also supports bundle-level summary() / plot() QC export. export_summary_appendix() Exports validated summary-table bundles CSV optional HTML appendix artifacts without requiring broader fit-based export bundle. preferred export route recovery simulation evidence. apa_table() Can now take summary-table bundles directly, selected component can move summary() formatted handoff table without rebuilding analysis object path.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_small <- toy[toy$Person %in% unique(toy$Person)[1:12], , drop = FALSE] fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy_small,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both")  spec <- specifications_report(fit) summary(spec)$overview  prec <- precision_review_report(fit, diagnostics = diag) summary(prec)$checks  checklist <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) subset(checklist$checklist, Section == "Visual Displays", c("Item", "NextAction"))  apa <- build_apa_outputs(fit, diagnostics = diag) apa$section_map[, c("Heading", "Available")] bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(checklist) bundle$table_index } # }

text: plot(fit, type = "wright") Shared logit map persons, facet levels, step thresholds. Best targeting spread. plot(fit, type = "pathway") Expected score theta, dominant-category strips. Best scale progression. plot(fit, type = "ccc") Category probability curves. Best checking whether categories peak sequence. plot_unexpected() Observation-level surprises. Best case review local misfit triage. plot_displacement() Level-wise anchor movement. Best anchor robustness residual calibration tension. plot_marginal_fit() Posterior-integrated first-order category residuals. Best seeing facet/category cells drive strict marginal flags. plot_marginal_pairwise() Posterior-integrated exact/adjacent agreement residuals. Best exploratory local-dependence follow-strict marginal flags. plot_interrater_agreement() Exact agreement, expected agreement, pairwise correlation, agreement gaps. Best rater consistency. plot_facets_chisq() Facet variability chi-square summaries. Best checking whether facet contributes meaningful spread. plot_residual_pca() Residual structure Rasch dimension removed. Best exploratory residual-structure review, standalone unidimensionality test. plot_bias_interaction() Interaction-bias screening views cells facet profiles. Best systematic departure additive main-effects model. plot_dif_heatmap() / plot_dif_summary() DFF / DIF screening views facet-level x group contrasts. Best showing facet group pair involved writing substantive interpretations. plot_anchor_drift() Anchor drift screened linking-chain visuals. Best multi-form multi-wave linking review checking retained common-element support. plot_guttman_scalogram() Person x facet-level response matrix unexpected-response overlay. Best teaching-oriented scalogram intuition visual triage data depart expected ordering. plot_residual_qq() Normal Q-Q plot person-level standardized residual aggregates. Best checking tail behavior residuals exploratory follow-fit screen. plot_rater_trajectory() Per-rater severity trajectory across named waves / occasions. Best rater-training drift feedback supplied fits already placed common anchored scale; helper perform linking. plot_rater_agreement_heatmap() Compact pairwise rater x rater heatmap exact agreement (default) Pearson-style correlation. Best rater count makes bar-chart form plot_interrater_agreement() busy. response_time_review() / plot_response_time_review() Descriptive response-time screening person, facet, score category. Best reviewing rapid/slow response patterns alongside MFRM diagnostics; joint speed-accuracy model change fitted measures. plot_local_dependence_heatmap() Yen Q3-style heatmap pairwise residual correlations facet levels. Best exploratory local-dependence screening; pairs strong -diagonal residual correlation merit content-level review. plot_reliability_snapshot() One-figure facet x reliability / separation / strata bar overview built diagnostics$reliability. Best single small figure "facets statistically distinguishable?". plot_residual_matrix() Person x facet-level standardized residual heatmap. Best follow-plot_guttman_scalogram() residual sign magnitude matter, just response code. plot_shrinkage_funnel() Empirical-Bayes shrinkage caterpillar / funnel showing raw versus shrunken facet estimates. Best fits produced via apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage() reviewing much level moved prior.

title: mfrmr Visual Diagnostics Map — mfrmr_visual_diagnostics
text: users coming standard Rasch-measurement software packages, closest mfrmr helper table figure family summarised . mapping approximate; mfrmr designed many-facet workflows, column subsets column names differ. Wright (variable) map plot(fit, type = "wright") plot_wright_unified() correspond FACETS Table 6 / Winsteps "Person-Item map". Pathway / probability curves plot(fit, type = "pathway") plot(fit, type = "ccc") correspond Winsteps Table 21 ("Probability category curves") FACETS category-probability curves. Test / item information compute_information() + plot_information() correspond Winsteps Table 17 ("Test characteristic curve, test information function"). Misfit / Infit / Outfit diagnose_mfrm() Largest |ZSTD| / MnSq misfit blocks summary(diag) correspond Winsteps Table 10/13/14 (Misfit order) FACETS Tables 7/8. Bias / interaction estimate_bias() + plot_bias_interaction() correspond FACETS Table 14 ("Bias / Interaction calibration report"). Differential rater / item functioning analyze_dff() / analyze_dif() + plot_dif_heatmap() / plot_dif_summary() cover FACETS DIF / bias--group route Winsteps DIF (Table 30 group differences) report. Inter-rater agreement interrater_agreement_table() + plot_interrater_agreement() / plot_rater_agreement_heatmap() correspond FACETS Table 7-style observed-vs-expected agreement reports. Anchoring / linking plot_anchor_drift() plot_information() cover FACETS / Winsteps anchored-run review route; full equating-chain helpers exposed via build_equating_chain().

title: mfrmr Visual Diagnostics Map — mfrmr_visual_diagnostics
text: Wright map: look gaps person density facet/step locations; large gaps indicate weaker targeting. Pathway / CCC: look monotone progression clear category dominance bands; flat overlapping curves suggest weak category separation. 3D-ready category surface: use exploratory view category-probability information, replacement 2D pathway/CCC figures reports. Read category_support first retained category zero observed responses. Unexpected / displacement: use screening tools, final evidence . Strict marginal pairwise local-dependence plots exploratory follow-layers diagnostic_mode = "", standalone inferential tests. Inter-rater agreement facet variability address different questions: agreement concerns scoring consistency, whereas variability concerns whether facet elements statistically distinguishable. Residual PCA bias plots interpreted follow-layers main fit screen, first-pass diagnostics. DFF residual-method plots screening visuals. ETS /B/C labels claimed rows whose refit output reports ClassificationSystem == "ETS".

title: mfrmr Visual Diagnostics Map — mfrmr_visual_diagnostics
text: Figure-readiness route: fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> reporting_checklist() -> inspect "Visual Displays" rows -> chosen public plot helper. Quick screening: fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> plot_qc_dashboard(). Strict marginal follow-: diagnose_mfrm() diagnostic_mode = "" -> plot_marginal_fit() -> plot_marginal_pairwise(). Scale targeting review: plot(fit, type = "wright") -> plot(fit, type = "pathway") -> plot(fit, type = "ccc"). Linking review: subset_connectivity_report() -> plot(..., type = "design_matrix") / mfrm_network_analysis() / build_mfrm_network_review() / plot(..., type = "network") -> plot_anchor_drift(). Interaction review: estimate_bias() -> plot_bias_interaction() -> reporting_checklist(). DFF / DIF review: analyze_dff() -> plot_dif_heatmap() / plot_dif_summary() -> inspect explicit facet, level, group-pair columns writing interpretation.

title: mfrmr Visual Diagnostics Map — mfrmr_visual_diagnostics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both") checklist <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) visual_reporting_template("manuscript") subset(   checklist$checklist,   Section == "Visual Displays" & Item %in% c("QC / facet dashboard", "Strict marginal visuals"),   c("Item", "Available", "NextAction") )  qc <- plot_qc_dashboard(fit, diagnostics = diag, draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") qc$data$plot  p_marg <- plot_marginal_fit(diag, draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") p_marg$data$preset  wright <- plot(fit, type = "wright", draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") wright$data$preset  pca <- analyze_residual_pca(diag, mode = "overall") scree <- plot_residual_pca(pca, plot_type = "scree", draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") scree$data$preset } # }

Use evaluate_mfrm_recovery() assess_mfrm_recovery() direct recovery checks plus caveated role-based design evaluation, population forecasting, diagnostic-screening, signal-detection helpers. Choose downstream branch: reporting_checklist() direct report preparation, build_weighting_review() Rasch-versus-bounded-GPCM weighting review, build_misfit_casebook() / build_linking_review() operational case review. bounded GPCM, use build_linking_review() exploratory index direct anchor/drift/chain evidence. Generate reporting bundles: build_summary_table_bundle(), apa_table(), export_summary_appendix(), build_fixed_reports(), build_visual_summaries(). bounded GPCM, use APA, visual, QC, fit-based export bundles caveated sensitivity-reporting surfaces; full score-side FACETS review stays blocked, diagnostic/signal-detection design screening caveated operating-characteristic route. title: mfrmr Workflow and Method Map — mfrmr_workflow_methods
text: Quick first pass: RSM / PCM: fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> plot_qc_dashboard() -> reporting_checklist() want package route next figures. bounded GPCM: fit_mfrm() -> diagnose_mfrm() -> plot_qc_dashboard() / unexpected_response_table() -> rating_scale_table() -> compute_information() -> plot_information() -> plot.mfrm_fit() / category_curves_report() -> fair_average_table() / estimate_bias() screening tables answer question. bounded GPCM, fit-based export family (build_mfrm_manifest(), build_mfrm_replay_script(), export_mfrm_bundle()) available caveated sensitivity-reporting output explicit gpcm_boundary rows. Linking coverage review: subset_connectivity_report() -> plot(..., type = "design_matrix") -> plot_wright_unified(). Manuscript prep: RSM / PCM: reporting_checklist() -> inspect "Visual Displays" "Method Section" rows -> build_apa_outputs() -> build_summary_table_bundle() -> apa_table() export_summary_appendix(). First-release GPCM: reporting_checklist() -> direct table/plot helpers -> build_apa_outputs() / build_visual_summaries() -> export_mfrm_bundle() gpcm_boundary caveats. Weighting-policy review: compare_mfrm() -> build_weighting_review() -> compute_information() / plot_information() want inspect whether bounded GPCM introducing substantively acceptable discrimination-based reweighting relative Rasch-family reference. Design planning forecasting: build_mfrm_sim_spec() extract_mfrm_sim_spec() -> evaluate_mfrm_recovery() -> assess_mfrm_recovery() parameter-recovery checks, evaluate_mfrm_design() -> predict_mfrm_population() -> predict_mfrm_units() sample_mfrm_plausible_values() fitted scoring basis (ordinary MML, latent-regression MML person-level background data, JML documented post hoc EAP approximation). , predict_mfrm_population() scenario-level forecast helper, whereas predict_mfrm_units() / sample_mfrm_plausible_values() scoring layer. Prediction export requires actual prediction objects. First-release GPCM now supports direct data generation via build_mfrm_sim_spec(), extract_mfrm_sim_spec(), simulate_mfrm_data(), evaluate_mfrm_recovery(), assess_mfrm_recovery(), caveated role-based design evaluation population forecasting, diagnostic/signal-detection design screening, residual diagnostics, direct curve/report helpers. current planning layer remains role-based two non-person facets even though estimation supports arbitrary facet counts; future arbitrary-facet planning fields treated design metadata rather finished public behavior.

title: mfrmr Workflow and Method Map — mfrmr_workflow_methods
text: mfrm_fit: summary(fit) plot(fit, ...). mfrm_diagnostics: summary(diag); plotting via dedicated helpers plot_unexpected(), plot_displacement(), plot_qc_dashboard(). mfrm_bias: summary(bias) plot_bias_interaction(). mfrm_data_description: summary(ds) plot(ds, ...). mfrm_anchor_review: summary(review) plot(review, ...). mfrm_misfit_casebook: summary(casebook) print(casebook), grouping views available casebook$group_view_index casebook$group_views, source-specific plotting routed summary(casebook)$plot_routes casebook$plot_map, appendix/report handoff available build_summary_table_bundle() export_summary_appendix(). mfrm_weighting_review: summary(review) print(review), information follow-routed compute_information() plot_information() according review$plot_map, appendix/report handoff available build_summary_table_bundle() export_summary_appendix(). mfrm_linking_review: summary(review) print(review), grouping views available review$group_view_index review$group_views, plotting routed summary(review)$plot_routes, plot_anchor_drift(), plot(anchor_review, ...) according review$plot_map. mfrm_facets_run: summary(run) plot(run, type = c("fit", "qc"), ...). apa_table: summary(tbl) plot(tbl, ...). mfrm_apa_outputs: summary(apa) compact diagnostics report text. mfrm_summary_table_bundle: print(bundle) manuscript-oriented table index plus named tables supported summary() outputs, summary(bundle) table-role/numeric coverage, plot(bundle, ...) table-size numeric-column QC. mfrm_threshold_profiles: summary(profiles) preset threshold grids. mfrm_population_prediction: summary(pred) design-level forecast tables. mfrm_unit_prediction: summary(pred) unit-level posterior summaries fitted scoring basis. mfrm_plausible_values: summary(pv) draw-level uncertainty summaries. mfrm_bundle families: summary() class-aware plot(bundle, ...). Key bundle classes now also use class-aware summary(bundle): mfrm_unexpected, mfrm_fair_average, mfrm_displacement, mfrm_interrater, mfrm_facets_chisq, mfrm_bias_interaction, mfrm_rating_scale, mfrm_category_structure, mfrm_category_curves, mfrm_measurable, mfrm_unexpected_after_bias, mfrm_output_bundle, mfrm_residual_pca, mfrm_specifications, mfrm_data_quality, mfrm_iteration_report, mfrm_subset_connectivity, mfrm_facet_statistics, mfrm_facets_contract_review, mfrm_reference_review, mfrm_reference_benchmark.

title: mfrmr Workflow and Method Map — mfrmr_workflow_methods
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy_full <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") keep_people <- unique(toy_full$Person)[1:12] toy <- toy_full[toy_full$Person %in% keep_people, , drop = FALSE]  fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   method = "MML",   quad_points = 7,   maxit = 30 ) summary(fit)$next_actions  diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none", diagnostic_mode = "both") summary(diag)$next_actions  chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) subset(   chk$checklist,   Section == "Visual Displays",   c("Item", "DraftReady", "NextAction") )  qc <- plot_qc_dashboard(fit, diagnostics = diag, draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") qc$data$preset p_marg <- plot_marginal_fit(diag, draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") p_marg$data$preset  sc <- subset_connectivity_report(fit, diagnostics = diag) p_design <- plot(sc, type = "design_matrix", draw = FALSE, preset = "publication") p_design$data$plot  bundle <- build_summary_table_bundle(chk, appendix_preset = "recommended") summary(bundle)$role_summary plot(bundle, type = "appendix_presets", draw = FALSE)$data$plot } # }

code: normalize_conquest_overlap_files(   population_file,   item_file,   case_file,   population_delimiter = c("auto", "comma", "tab", "semicolon", ",", "\t", ";"),   item_delimiter = c("auto", "comma", "tab", "semicolon", ",", "\t", ";"),   case_delimiter = c("auto", "comma", "tab", "semicolon", ",", "\t", ";"),   conquest_population_term = "auto",   conquest_population_estimate = "auto",   conquest_item_id = "auto",   conquest_item_estimate = "auto",   conquest_case_person = "auto",   conquest_case_estimate = "auto",   keep_extra_columns = TRUE )

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap files to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_files
text: population_file Path extracted ConQuest population-parameter table CSV/TSV/TXT form. item_file Path extracted ConQuest item-estimate table CSV/TSV/TXT form. case_file Path extracted ConQuest case-level EAP table CSV/TSV/TXT form. population_delimiter Delimiter population_file. "auto" chooses comma, tab, semicolon file extension/header line. item_delimiter Delimiter item_file. "auto" chooses file extension/header line. case_delimiter Delimiter case_file. "auto" chooses file extension/header line. conquest_population_term Column population_file stores parameter names. "auto" tries conservative aliases Parameter Term. conquest_population_estimate Column population_file stores parameter estimates. "auto" tries aliases Estimate Est. conquest_item_id Column item_file stores item identifier extracted user. "auto" tries aliases ResponseVar, ItemID, Item, Label. conquest_item_estimate Column item_file stores item estimates. "auto" tries aliases Estimate, Est, Facility. conquest_case_person Column case_file stores person IDs. "auto" tries conservative aliases Person, PID, Sequence ID. conquest_case_estimate Column case_file stores case EAP estimates. "auto" tries conservative aliases Estimate, EAP_1, EAP. keep_extra_columns TRUE, keep remaining columns standardized identifier estimate columns.

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap files to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_files
text: helper thin file-wrapper around normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(). intentionally limited already extracted tabular files parse raw ConQuest report text. recommended workflow : export exact-overlap bundle build_conquest_overlap_bundle(); extract relevant ConQuest tables CSV/TSV/TXT files; call normalize_conquest_overlap_files() files; pass result review_conquest_overlap(). Read summary(normalized)$normalization_scope review confirm files treated extracted tables, raw ConQuest report text, check duplicate-ID / non-numeric-estimate pre-review flags.

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap files to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_files
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bundle <- build_conquest_overlap_bundle() tmp_dir <- tempdir() pop_path <- file.path(tmp_dir, "cq_pop.csv") item_path <- file.path(tmp_dir, "cq_item.tsv") case_path <- file.path(tmp_dir, "cq_case.csv") utils::write.csv(   data.frame(     Term = bundle$mfrmr_population$Parameter,     Est = bundle$mfrmr_population$Estimate   ),   pop_path,   row.names = FALSE ) utils::write.table(   data.frame(     Item = bundle$mfrmr_item_estimates$ResponseVar,     Est = bundle$mfrmr_item_estimates$Estimate   ),   item_path,   sep = "\t",   row.names = FALSE ) utils::write.csv(   data.frame(     PID = bundle$mfrmr_case_eap$Person,     EAP = bundle$mfrmr_case_eap$Estimate   ),   case_path,   row.names = FALSE ) normalized <- normalize_conquest_overlap_files(   population_file = pop_path,   item_file = item_path,   case_file = case_path,   conquest_population_term = "Term",   conquest_population_estimate = "Est",   conquest_item_id = "Item",   conquest_item_estimate = "Est",   conquest_case_person = "PID",   conquest_case_estimate = "EAP" ) summary(normalized)$normalization_scope review <- review_conquest_overlap(bundle, normalized) summary(review)$summary } # }

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap tables to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_tables
text: helper parse raw ConQuest text output. standardizes already extracted tables contract used review_conquest_overlap(): population parameters become columns Parameter, Estimate, EstimateNonNumeric; item estimates become columns ItemID, Estimate, EstimateNonNumeric; case summaries become columns Person, Estimate, EstimateNonNumeric. resulting object intentionally conservative. infer whether item IDs correspond exported response variables original item levels; matching step remains part review_conquest_overlap(), standardized ConQuest tables compared concrete overlap bundle.

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap tables to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_tables
text: returned object class mfrm_conquest_overlap_tables includes: summary: one-row normalization summary conquest_population: standardized population table conquest_item_estimates: standardized item table conquest_case_eap: standardized case table settings: source-column metadata notes: interpretation notes Read summary(normalized)$normalization_scope review confirm object contains extracted tabular inputs, parsed raw ConQuest report text, check duplicate-ID / non-numeric-estimate pre-review flags.

title: Normalize extracted ConQuest overlap tables to the mfrmr review contract — normalize_conquest_overlap_tables
code: normalized <- normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(   conquest_population = data.frame(     Term = c("(Intercept)", "GroupB", "sigma2"),     Est = c(0, 0.2, 1)   ),   conquest_item_estimates = data.frame(     Item = c("I1", "I2"),     Est = c(-0.2, 0.2)   ),   conquest_case_eap = data.frame(     PID = c("P001", "P002"),     EAP = c(-0.1, 0.1)   ),   conquest_population_term = "Term",   conquest_population_estimate = "Est",   conquest_item_id = "Item",   conquest_item_estimate = "Est",   conquest_case_person = "PID",   conquest_case_estimate = "EAP" ) summary(normalized)$normalization_scope #>                            Area             Status #> 1 Extracted table normalization             active #> 2     Raw ConQuest text parsing      not performed #> 3               Bundle matching deferred to review #> 4        Pre-review table check      none detected #>                                                             Evidence #> 1                                              7 standardized row(s) #> 2            already extracted CSV/TSV/TXT or data.frame inputs only #> 3 review_conquest_overlap() matches rows against the exported bundle #> 4                  0 duplicate ID(s); 0 non-numeric estimate cell(s) #>                                                                               Interpretation #> 1        Population, item, and case tables have been converted to the mfrmr review contract. #> 2             This object does not prove that raw ConQuest report text was parsed correctly. #> 3 Identifier matching and numerical comparison are intentionally handled by the review step. #> 4        Resolve duplicate IDs or non-numeric estimates before treating the review as clean.

title: Plot report/table bundles with base R defaults — plot.mfrm_bundle
text: plot() dispatches bundle class: mfrm_unexpected -> plot_unexpected() mfrm_fair_average -> plot_fair_average() mfrm_displacement -> plot_displacement() mfrm_interrater -> plot_interrater_agreement() mfrm_facets_chisq -> plot_facets_chisq() mfrm_bias_interaction -> plot_bias_interaction() mfrm_bias_count -> bias-count plots (cell counts / low-count rates) mfrm_fixed_reports -> pairwise-contrast diagnostics mfrm_visual_summaries -> warning/summary message count plots mfrm_category_structure -> default base-R category plots mfrm_category_curves -> overview (default), ogive, CCC / category probability / conditional probability, cumulative, total-information, category-specific-information plots mfrm_rating_scale -> category-counts/threshold plots mfrm_measurable -> measurable-data coverage/count plots mfrm_unexpected_after_bias -> post-bias unexpected-response plots mfrm_output_bundle -> graph/score output-file diagnostics, including type = "score_se" scorefile SE columns available mfrm_residual_pca -> residual PCA scree, parallel-analysis, loadings views via plot_residual_pca() mfrm_specifications -> facet/anchor/convergence plots mfrm_data_quality -> dashboard, quality-flag, score-map, facet-pattern, row/category/missing-row plots mfrm_facets_fit_review -> FACETS-style df-sensitivity plot mfrm_fit_measures -> fit-status counts, Infit/Outfit scatter, measure intervals, FACETS-style df-sensitivity plots mfrm_iteration_report -> replayed-iteration trajectories mfrm_subset_connectivity -> subset-observation/connectivity plots mfrm_facet_statistics -> facet statistic profile plots mfrm_export_bundle / mfrm_summary_appendix_export -> export handoff plots (formats, artifact_groups, selection_tables, selection_handoff, selection_handoff_bundles, selection_handoff_roles, selection_handoff_role_sections, selection_bundles, selection_roles, selection_sections) class outside families, use dedicated plotting helpers custom base R graphics component tables. mfrm_category_curves, pass preset = "monochrome" grayscale/line-type output. Cumulative .5 boundary lines shown interpretable -range boundaries default; use boundary_status = "" show every finite boundary estimate boundary_status = "none" / show_cumulative_boundaries = FALSE suppress vertical boundary lines. Use plot_data(x, component = "plot_long") category-curve bundle want one ggplot2/plotly-friendly table across curve families.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy_full <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_people <- unique(toy_full$Person)[1:12] toy <- toy_full[toy_full$Person %in% toy_people, , drop = FALSE] fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) ) t4 <- unexpected_response_table(fit, abs_z_min = 1.5, prob_max = 0.4, top_n = 5) p <- plot(t4, draw = FALSE) vis <- build_visual_summaries(fit, diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none")) p_vis <- plot(vis, type = "comparison", draw = FALSE) spec <- specifications_report(fit) p_spec <- plot(spec, type = "facet_elements", draw = FALSE) if (interactive()) {   plot(     t4,     type = "severity",     draw = TRUE,     main = "Unexpected Response Severity (Customized)",     palette = c(higher = "#d95f02", lower = "#1b9e77", bar = "#2b8cbe"),     label_angle = 45   )   plot(     vis,     type = "comparison",     draw = TRUE,     main = "Warning vs Summary Counts (Customized)",     palette = c(warning = "#cb181d", summary = "#3182bd"),     label_angle = 45   ) } } # }

text: method draws quick pre-fit quality views describe_mfrm_data(): score distribution balance facet-level structure size missingness selected columns

title: Plot a data-description object — plot.mfrm_data_description
text: "score_distribution": bar chart weighted observation counts per score category.  Y-axis WeightedN (sum weights category).  Categories observations (< 10) may produce unstable threshold estimates.  roughly uniform unimodal distribution ideal; heavy floor/ceiling effects compress measurement range. "facet_levels": bar chart showing number distinct levels per facet.  Useful verifying design structure matches expectations (e.g., expected number raters criteria).  large numbers levels increase computation time may require higher maxit fit_mfrm(). "missing": bar chart missing-value counts per input column. Columns non-zero counts investigated fitting—rows missing scores, persons, facet IDs dropped estimation.

text: x mfrm_fit object fit_mfrm(). type Plot type. Use NULL, "bundle", "" three-part fit bundle; otherwise choose one "facet", "person", "step", "wright", "pathway", "ccc", "ccc_surface", "category_surface". facet Optional facet name type = "facet". top_n Maximum number facet/step locations retained compact displays. theta_range Numeric length-2 range pathway, CCC, category-surface plot data. theta_points Number theta grid points used pathway, CCC, category-surface plot data. title Optional custom title. palette Optional color overrides. label_angle Rotation angle x-axis labels applicable. show_ci TRUE, add approximate confidence intervals available. ci_level Confidence level used show_ci = TRUE. group Optional grouping type = "wright" overlay per-group person-density curves (DIF / DFF screening view). Either column name (looked first group_data supplied ..., fit$prep$data) vector aligned fit$facets$person. Ignored type values. pass source data alongside, use plot(fit, type = "wright", group = "MyCol", group_data = <df>). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). supplied, pathway plot data reuse fit_measures, fit_status, curve_fit_status instead recomputing diagnostics. include_fit_measures TRUE (default), pathway plot data include tidy fit-measure fit-status tables custom R graphics. Set FALSE curve coordinates needed. draw TRUE, draw plot base graphics. preset Visual preset ("standard", "publication", "compact", "monochrome"). ... Additional arguments ignored S3 compatibility.

title: Plot fitted MFRM results with base R — plot.mfrm_fit
text: Invisibly, mfrm_plot_data object (default single type), mfrm_plot_bundle type = "bundle" / "" / "default".

title: Plot fitted MFRM results with base R — plot.mfrm_fit
text: S3 plotting method provides core fit-family visuals mfrmr. type omitted, returns Wright map alone mfrm_plot_data object (useful single figure first inspection). Pass type = "bundle" ("" / "default") obtain legacy three-plot mfrm_plot_bundle containing Wright map, pathway map, category characteristic curves. returned object always carries machine-readable metadata mfrm_plot_data contract, even plot drawn immediately. type = "wright" shows persons, facet levels, step thresholds shared logit scale. Estimates plotted fitted, sign convention follows fit: higher person values indicate higher ability, higher non-person facet values indicate greater severity/difficulty default negative facet orientation. Facets listed fit_mfrm(positive_facets = ...) reversed (higher values raise expected scores); state active orientation figure captions reporting. type = "pathway" shows expected score traces dominant-category regions across theta. expected-score display distinct Bond--Fox-style measure-versus-fit "pathway" bubble chart used around FACETS/Winsteps output; display, use plot_bubble(). draw-free plot data also includes pathway_long, pathway_annotations, fit_measures, fit_status, curve_fit_status, R users can rebuild pathway map ggplot2, plotly, report pipeline keeping underfit/overfit labels used fit_measures_table(). type = "ccc" shows category response probabilities. type = "ccc_surface" type = "category_surface" returns 3D-ready category-probability surface data external rendering; deliberately add plotly/rgl dependency replace 2D CCC/pathway reporting figures. returned object includes category_support, interpretation_guide, reporting_policy tables retained zero-frequency categories manuscript-use boundaries remain visible beginners. remaining types ("facet", "person", "step", "shrinkage") provide compact location-specific displays.

title: Plot fitted MFRM results with base R — plot.mfrm_fit
text: Fit model fit_mfrm(). Use plot(fit) inspect Wright map glance. Switch type = "pathway", "ccc", "shrinkage" relevant follow-figure, type = "bundle" three-plot overview preparing FACETS-style summary.

title: Plot fitted MFRM results with base R — plot.mfrm_fit
text: plot-selection guide extended examples, see mfrmr_visual_diagnostics vignette("mfrmr-visual-diagnostics", package = "mfrmr").

title: Plot fitted MFRM results with base R — plot.mfrm_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy,   "Person",   c("Rater", "Criterion"),   "Score",   method = "JML",   model = "RSM",   maxit = 30 ) wright <- plot(fit, draw = FALSE) wright$data$plot # Look for: persons clustered against the facet / step rows on the #   shared logit axis. Large gaps between the person density and #   the step / facet rails indicate weak targeting; ceiling / #   floor stripes mean the test is too easy / hard. bundle <- plot(fit, type = "bundle", draw = FALSE) bundle$wright_map$data$plot # Look for: pathway curves rising in the expected order with #   visible dominant-category bands; CCC curves peaking sequentially #   without one category being completely overlapped by neighbours. surface <- plot(fit, type = "ccc_surface", draw = FALSE) head(surface$data$surface) surface$data$category_support # Look for: every retained category having `Observed > 0`; categories #   with zero observations are returned as a zero-observation slice and #   should not be interpreted as a real score region. surface$data$interpretation_guide if (interactive()) {   plot(     fit,     type = "wright",     preset = "publication",     title = "Customized Wright Map",     show_ci = TRUE,     label_angle = 45   )   plot(     fit,     type = "pathway",     title = "Customized Pathway Map",     palette = c("#1f78b4")   )   plot(     fit,     type = "ccc",     title = "Customized Category Characteristic Curves",     palette = c("#1b9e77", "#d95f02", "#7570b3")   ) } }

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data. draw = FALSE, result$data$table contains filtered drift chain table, result$data$matrix contains heatmap matrix requested, returned plot data includes package-native title, subtitle, legend, reference_lines.

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: Three plot types supported: "drift" (mfrm_anchor_drift objects): dot plot element's drift value, grouped facet.  Horizontal reference lines mark drift threshold.  Red points indicate flagged elements. "heatmap" (mfrm_anchor_drift objects): wave--element heat matrix showing drift magnitude.  Darker cells represent larger absolute drift.  Useful spotting systematic patterns (e.g., criteria shifting direction). "chain" (mfrm_equating_chain objects): line plot cumulative offsets across screened linking chain. flatter line indicates smaller -wave shifts; steep segments suggest larger link offsets deserve review.

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: Use type = "drift" mfrm_anchor_drift object review flagged elements directly. Use type = "heatmap" mfrm_anchor_drift object spot wave--element patterns. Use type = "chain" mfrm_equating_chain object build_equating_chain() inspect cumulative offsets across waves.

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: Drift change element's estimated measure calibration waves, accounting screened common-element link offset. element flagged absolute drift exceeds threshold (typically 0.5 logits) drift--SE ratio exceeds secondary criterion (typically 2.0), ensuring practically noticeable relatively precise shifts flagged. drift heatmap plots, red dark-shaded elements exceed thresholds.  Common causes include rater drift time, item exposure effects, curriculum changes. chain plots, uneven spacing waves suggests differential shifts screened linking offsets. \(y\)-axis shows cumulative logit-scale offsets; flatter segments indicate stable adjacent links. Steep segments checked alongside LinkSupportAdequate retained common-element counts making longitudinal claims. drift objects, usually best read summary(x) first use plot see flagged values sit.

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: Build drift screened-linking object detect_anchor_drift() build_equating_chain(). Start draw = FALSE want plotting data custom reporting. Use base-R plot quick screening inspect underlying tables exact values.

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
text: plot-selection guide longer walkthrough, see mfrmr_visual_diagnostics vignette("mfrmr-visual-diagnostics", package = "mfrmr").

title: Plot anchor drift or a screened linking chain — plot_anchor_drift
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") people <- unique(toy$Person) d1 <- toy[toy$Person %in% people[1:12], , drop = FALSE] d2 <- toy[toy$Person %in% people[13:24], , drop = FALSE] fit1 <- fit_mfrm(d1, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit2 <- fit_mfrm(d2, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) drift <- detect_anchor_drift(list(W1 = fit1, W2 = fit2)) drift_plot <- plot_anchor_drift(drift, type = "drift", draw = FALSE) class(drift_plot) names(drift_plot$data) chain <- build_equating_chain(list(F1 = fit1, F2 = fit2)) chain_plot <- plot_anchor_drift(chain, type = "chain", draw = FALSE) head(chain_plot$data$table) if (interactive()) {   plot_anchor_drift(drift, type = "heatmap", preset = "publication") } } # }

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
text: x Output estimate_bias() fit_mfrm(). plot Plot type: "scatter", "ranked", "heatmap", "abs_t_hist", "facet_profile". diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm() (used x fit). facet_a First facet name (required x fit interaction_facets supplied). facet_b Second facet name (required x fit interaction_facets supplied). interaction_facets Character vector two facets. top_n Maximum number ranked rows keep. abs_t_warn Warning cutoff absolute t statistics. abs_bias_warn Warning cutoff absolute bias size. p_max Warning cutoff p-values. sort_by Ranking key: "abs_t", "abs_bias", "prob". show_ci Logical. TRUE plot "scatter" "ranked", draw confidence-interval whiskers Bias Size. Bounded GPCM rows use conditional profile-likelihood limits returned estimate_bias() available; otherwise interval uses per-cell standard error estimate_bias(). Ignored "heatmap", "abs_t_hist", "facet_profile". ci_level Confidence level used show_ci = TRUE; default 0.95. returned plot-data object gains CI_Lower / CI_Upper / CI_Level columns ranked_table scatter_data elements downstream reuse. main Optional plot title override. palette Optional named color overrides (normal, flag, hist, profile). label_angle Label angle hint ranked/profile labels. preset Visual preset ("standard", "publication", "compact", "monochrome"). draw TRUE, draw base graphics.

text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
text: Visualization front-end bias_interaction_report() multiple views. draw = FALSE, returned plot data include plot_long, plot_annotations, flag_summary, plot_settings addition view-specific ranked_table, scatter_data, facet_profile, heatmap components. Use fields rebuilding screening view ggplot2, plotly, Quarto, dashboard.

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
text: "scatter" (default) Scatter plot bias size (x) vs screening t-statistic (y). Points colored flag status. Dashed reference lines abs_bias_warn abs_t_warn.  Use overall triage interaction effects. "ranked" Ranked bar chart top top_n interactions sorted sort_by criterion (absolute t, absolute bias, probability). Bars colored red flagged cells. "heatmap" Facet facet B matrix signed bias size. Cells retain reusable matrix flag tables dashboards. Table 13 follow-display: supports pattern recognition turn screening rows confirmatory tests. "abs_t_hist" Histogram absolute screening t-statistics across interaction cells.  Dashed reference line abs_t_warn.  Use assessing overall distribution interaction effect sizes. "facet_profile" Per-facet-level aggregation showing mean absolute bias flag rate.  Useful identifying individual facet levels drive systematic interaction patterns.

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
text: Start "scatter" "ranked" triage, confirm pattern shape using "abs_t_hist" "facet_profile". Consistent flags across multiple views stronger screening signals systematic interaction bias single extreme row, establish formal inferential evidence.

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
text: Estimate bias estimate_bias() pass mfrm_fit directly. Plot plot = "ranked" top interactions. Cross-check using plot = "scatter" plot = "facet_profile".

title: Plot bias interaction diagnostics (preferred alias) — plot_bias_interaction
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) p <- plot_bias_interaction(   fit,   diagnostics = diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none"),   facet_a = "Rater",   facet_b = "Criterion",   preset = "publication",   draw = FALSE ) }

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
code: plot_bubble(   x,   diagnostics = NULL,   fit_stat = c("Infit", "Outfit"),   view = c("measure", "infit_outfit"),   bubble_size = NULL,   facets = NULL,   fit_range = c(0.5, 1.5),   top_n = 60,   main = NULL,   palette = NULL,   draw = TRUE,   preset = c("standard", "publication", "compact", "monochrome") )

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
text: x Output fit_mfrm diagnose_mfrm. diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm x mfrm_fit object. omitted, diagnostics computed automatically. fit_stat Fit statistic y-axis: "Infit" (default) "Outfit". Ignored view = "infit_outfit" view always plots Infit x Outfit y. view Layout. "measure" (default, historical mfrmr layout) plots Measure (logit) x chosen fit_stat MnSq y. "infit_outfit" plots Infit MnSq x Outfit MnSq y, matching Winsteps Table 30.2 "-misfitting Persons / Items" scatter many MFRM Rasch users expect, defaults bubble_size = "N". bubble_size Variable controlling bubble radius: "SE" (default view = "measure"), "N" (observation count; default view = "infit_outfit"), "equal" (uniform size). facets Character vector facets include. NULL (default) includes non-person facets. fit_range Numeric length-2 vector defining heuristic fit-review band shown shaded region (default c(0.5, 1.5)). top_n Maximum number elements plot (default 60). main Optional custom plot title. palette Optional named colour vector keyed facet name. draw TRUE (default), render plot using base graphics. preset Visual preset ("standard", "publication", "compact", "monochrome").

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
text: Invisibly, object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
text: x mfrm_fit object diagnostics omitted, function computes diagnostics internally via diagnose_mfrm(). repeated plotting workflow, passing precomputed diagnostics object avoids extra work. x-axis shows element measure estimates logit scale (one logit = one unit change log-odds responding higher category).  y-axis shows selected fit mean-square statistic. shaded band fit_range[1] fit_range[2] highlights common heuristic review range. Bubble radius options: "SE": inversely proportional standard error—larger circles indicate precisely estimated elements current SE approximation. "N": proportional observation count—larger circles indicate elements data. "equal": uniform size, useful SE N differences distract fit pattern. Person estimates excluded default typically outnumber facet elements obscure display.

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
text: Points near horizontal reference line 1.0 closer model expectation selected MnSq scale. Points 1.5 suggest underfit relative common review heuristics; elements may inconsistent scoring. Points 0.5 suggest overfit relative common review heuristics; may indicate redundancy restricted range. Points colored facet easy identification.

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
text: Fit model fit_mfrm(). Compute diagnostics diagnose_mfrm(). Call plot_bubble(fit, diagnostics = diag) inspect extreme elements.

title: Bubble chart of measure estimates and fit statistics — plot_bubble
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", model = "RSM", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") p <- plot_bubble(fit, diagnostics = diag, draw = FALSE) head(p$data$table[, c("Facet", "Level", "Estimate", "Infit", "Outfit")]) # Look for (default `view = "measure"`): bubbles inside the shaded #   0.5-1.5 fit-review band. Bubbles above the band are underfit #   (noisy elements); below the band are overfit (overly predictable). # # For the Winsteps Table 30 layout pass `view = "infit_outfit"`: p_io <- plot_bubble(fit, diagnostics = diag, view = "infit_outfit",                      draw = FALSE) p_io$data$view # Look for: bubbles clustered inside the central [0.5, 1.5] x [0.5, 1.5] #   square. Points outside the upper-right corner have both Infit #   AND Outfit > 1.5 (consistent underfit); points outside the #   lower-left have both < 0.5 (consistent overfit). Bubble size in #   this view defaults to N (observation count) so the visual #   weighting matches how seriously the misfit should be taken. }

title: Extract reusable data from an mfrmr plot object — plot_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", maxit = 30)  wright_plot_data <- plot_data(fit, type = "wright") names(wright_plot_data)  wright_table <- plot_data(fit, type = "wright", component = "locations") head(wright_table)  curves <- category_curves_report(fit, theta_points = 51) curve_long <- plot_data(curves, component = "plot_long") head(curve_long[, c("PlotType", "Theta", "Series", "Value")])  pathway_long <- plot_data(fit, type = "pathway", component = "pathway_long") head(pathway_long[, c("Layer", "CurveGroup", "Theta", "Value")]) pathway_fit <- plot_data(fit, type = "pathway", component = "fit_measures") head(pathway_fit[, c("Facet", "Level", "Infit", "Outfit", "FitStatus")])  # Re-render one component with your own styling while keeping the # package-generated data and interpretation metadata. expected <- pathway_long[pathway_long$Layer == "expected_score", , drop = FALSE] plot(expected$Theta, expected$Value, type = "l",      xlab = "Theta", ylab = "Expected score",      main = "Custom expected-score pathway") abline(v = 0, lty = 2, col = "grey60")  info <- compute_information(fit, theta_points = 51) sem_long <- plot_data(   plot_information(info, type = "sem", draw = FALSE),   component = "plot_long" ) head(sem_long[, c("Metric", "Theta", "Value", "DisplayedByDefault")]) } # }
