title: Plot a differential-functioning heatmap — plot_dif_heatmap
text: x Output dif_interaction_table(), analyze_dff(), analyze_dif(). mfrm_dff/mfrm_dif object passed, cell_table element used (requires method = "residual"). metric metric plot: "obs_exp" observed-minus-expected average (default), "t" standardized residual / t-statistic, "contrast" pairwise differential-functioning contrast (mfrm_dff objects dif_table). draw TRUE (default), draw plot. show_values Logical. TRUE (default), print rounded cell values top heatmap. value_digits Non-negative integer number digits decimal point cell labels. flag_threshold Optional non-negative absolute-value threshold. supplied, cells abs(value) >= flag_threshold recorded $data$flag_matrix outlined drawn heatmap. scale_limit Optional positive scalar symmetric color scale -scale_limit +scale_limit. Use make several heatmaps visually comparable. flag_color Border color cells meeting flag_threshold. ... Additional graphical parameters passed graphics::image().

title: Plot a differential-functioning heatmap — plot_dif_heatmap
text: Invisibly, mfrm_plot_data object whose data slot bundles row x column metric matrix ($matrix), source long table ($pairs), metric label. Earlier 0.1.x releases returned bare matrix; consume $data$matrix keep code forward-compatible.

title: Plot a differential-functioning heatmap — plot_dif_heatmap
text: Warm colors (red) indicate positive Obs-Exp values (model underestimates facet level group). Cool colors (blue) indicate negative Obs-Exp values (model overestimates). White/neutral indicates systematic difference. "contrast" view best pairwise differential-functioning summaries, whereas "obs_exp" "t" best cell-level diagnostics.

title: Plot a differential-functioning heatmap — plot_dif_heatmap
text: Compute interaction dif_interaction_table() differential- functioning contrasts analyze_dff(). Plot plot_dif_heatmap(...). Identify extreme cells contrasts follow-.

title: Plot a differential-functioning heatmap — plot_dif_heatmap
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias")  fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", model = "RSM", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") int <- dif_interaction_table(fit, diag, facet = "Rater",                              group = "Group", data = toy, min_obs = 2) heat <- plot_dif_heatmap(int, metric = "obs_exp", draw = FALSE) dim(heat$data$matrix) # Look for (`metric = "obs_exp"`): cells near 0 are aligned with #   model expectation; |Obs - Exp| > 0.5 logits is a substantive #   gap. With `metric = "t"` the cell scale becomes a standardized #   residual where |t| > 2 is a screening flag, not a standalone #   hypothesis test. With `metric = "contrast"` the layout switches #   to Level x GroupPair and reads as the pairwise differential- #   functioning contrast (use `analyze_dff()`). }

Width corresponds effect size contrast's native scale. method = "residual", observed-minus-expected average screening contrast groups. method = "refit", subgroup parameter difference fitted logit scale linking support allows comparable contrast. title: Summary plot of differential functioning effect sizes — plot_dif_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") dff <- analyze_dff(fit, diagnostics = diag,                    facet = "Rater", group = "Group", data = toy) unique(dff$dif_table$ClassificationSystem) p <- plot_dif_summary(dff, draw = FALSE) head(p$data$data) }

title: Plot displacement diagnostics using base R — plot_displacement
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot displacement diagnostics using base R — plot_displacement
text: Displacement quantifies much single element's calibration shift overall model allowed move freely. computed : $$\mathrm{Displacement}_j = \frac{\sum_i (X_{ij} - E_{ij})}                                      {\sum_i \mathrm{Var}_{ij}}$$ sums run observations involving element \(j\). standard error \(1 / \sqrt{\sum_i \mathrm{Var}_{ij}}\), t-statistic \(t = \mathrm{Displacement} / \mathrm{SE}\) flags elements whose observed residual pattern inconsistent current anchor structure. Displacement informative anchoring: large values suggest anchored values may drifting current sample. non-anchored analyses, displacement reflects residual calibration tension.

title: Plot displacement diagnostics using base R — plot_displacement
text: "lollipop" (default) Dot--line chart displacement values. X-axis: displacement (logits).  Y-axis: element labels.  Points colored red flagged (default: \(|\mathrm{Disp.}| > 0.5\) logits).  Dashed lines \(\pm\) threshold.  Ordered absolute displacement. "hist" Histogram displacement values Freedman-Diaconis breaks.  Dashed reference lines \(\pm\) threshold.  Use inspecting overall distribution shape.

title: Plot displacement diagnostics using base R — plot_displacement
text: Lollipop: top absolute displacement levels; flagged points indicate larger movement anchor expectations. Histogram: overall displacement distribution threshold lines. symmetric distribution centred near zero indicates good anchor stability; heavy tails skew suggest systematic drift. Use anchored_only = TRUE main question anchor robustness.

title: Plot displacement diagnostics using base R — plot_displacement
text: Run plot_type = "lollipop" anchored_only = TRUE. Inspect distribution plot_type = "hist". Drill flagged rows via displacement_table().

title: Plot facet-equivalence results — plot_facet_equivalence
text: "forest" places level logit scale confidence interval shades practical-equivalence region around weighted grand mean. "rope" shows percentage level's uncertainty mass falls inside ROPE.

title: Plot facet-equivalence results — plot_facet_equivalence
text: forest plot, shaded band marks ROPE (\(\pm\)equivalence_bound around weighted grand mean). Levels whose entire confidence interval lies inside band close facet grand mean descriptive screen. Levels whose interval extends outside band displaced facet average. Overlapping intervals two elements suggest reliably separable, overlap alone establish formal equivalence—use TOST results . ROPE bar chart, bar shows proportion element's normal-approximation distribution falls inside ROPE-style grand-mean proximity.  Values > 95\ element's normal-approximation uncertainty falls near facet average; 50–95\ meaningfully displaced average.

title: Plot facet-equivalence results — plot_facet_equivalence

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
text: Facet chi-square tests assess whether elements within facet differ significantly. Fixed-effect chi-square tests null hypothesis \(H_0: \delta_1 = \delta_2 = \cdots = \delta_J\) (element measures equal). flagged result (\(p <\) fixed_p_max) suggests detectable -element spread fitted model, interpreted alongside design quality, sample size, diagnostics. Random-effect chi-square tests whether element heterogeneity exceeds expected measurement error alone, treating element measures random draws. flagged result screening evidence facet may exchangeable current model. Random variance estimated -element variance component removing measurement error.  quantifies magnitude true heterogeneity logit scale.

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
text: "fixed" (default) Bar chart fixed-effect chi-square facet. Bars colored red null hypothesis rejected fixed_p_max. flagged (red) bar means facet shows spread worth reviewing fitted model. "random" Bar chart random-effect chi-square facet. Bars colored red rejected random_p_max. "variance" Bar chart estimated random variance (logit\(^2\)) facet.  Reference line 0.  Larger values indicate greater true heterogeneity among elements.

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
text: Colored flags reflect configured p-value thresholds (fixed_p_max, random_p_max). fixed test, flagged (red) result suggests facet spread worth reviewing current model. random test, flagged result screening evidence facet may contribute non-trivial heterogeneity beyond measurement error.

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
text: Review "fixed" "random" panels flagged facets. Check "variance" contextualize heterogeneity. Cross-check inter-rater element-level fit diagnostics.

title: Plot facet variability diagnostics using base R — plot_facets_chisq
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) p <- plot_facets_chisq(fit, draw = FALSE) if (interactive()) {   plot_facets_chisq(     fit,     draw = TRUE,     plot_type = "fixed",     preset = "publication",     main = "Facet Chi-square (Customized)",     palette = c(fixed_ok = "#2b8cbe", fixed_flag = "#cb181d"),     label_angle = 45   ) } }

title: Plot fair-average diagnostics using base R — plot_fair_average
text: Fair-average plots compare observed scoring tendency model-based fair metrics. FairM model-predicted mean score element, adjusting ability distribution persons actually encountered.  answers: "average score rater/criterion produce raters/criteria saw mix persons?" FairZ standardises FairM z-score across elements within facet, making easier compare relative severity across facets different raw-score scales. Use FairM raw-score metric meaningful (e.g., reporting average ratings original 1–4 scale). Use FairZ comparing standardised severity ranks across facets.

text: "difference" (default) Lollipop chart showing gap observed fair-average score element.  X-axis: Observed - Fair metric.  Y-axis: element labels.  Points colored teal (lenient, gap >= 0) orange (severe, gap < 0).  Ordered absolute gap. "scatter" Scatter plot fair metric (x) vs observed average (y) identity line.  Points colored facet.  Useful checking overall alignment observed model-adjusted scores.

title: Plot fair-average diagnostics using base R — plot_fair_average
text: Difference plot: ranked element-level gaps (Observed - Fair), useful triage potentially lenient/severe levels. Scatter plot: global agreement pattern relative identity line. Larger absolute gaps suggest stronger divergence observed model-adjusted scoring.

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information
text: "tif": overall design-weighted precision across theta. "se" / "sem" / "csem": conditional SEM across theta. "": precision conditional SEM together, useful presentations. "iif": facet-level contribution curves one selected facet supported RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM fit.

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information
text: Use "tif" quick overall read precision. Use "sem" "csem" standard-error language easier communicate precision. Use "" want views one figure. Use "iif" want see facet levels shaping total precision curve.

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information
text: total curve peaks realized design precise. Conditional SEM derived 1 / sqrt(precision); lower better. Facet-level curves show facet levels contribute realized precision theta. bounded GPCM, contributions include squared discrimination scaling implied fitted slope_facet. precision peak sits far bulk person measures, realized design may poorly targeted.

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information

title: Plot design-weighted precision curves — plot_information
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", model = "RSM", maxit = 30) info <- compute_information(fit) tif_data <- plot_information(info, type = "tif", draw = FALSE) head(tif_data$data$plot) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   Theta Information    SE #>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 -6           7.61 0.363 #> 2 -5.94        8.07 0.352 #> 3 -5.88        8.56 0.342 #> 4 -5.82        9.09 0.332 #> 5 -5.76        9.64 0.322 #> 6 -5.7        10.2  0.313 iif_data <- plot_information(info, type = "iif", facet = "Rater", draw = FALSE) head(iif_data$data$plot) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   Theta Facet Level Information Exposure #>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 -6    Rater R01          2.23      192 #> 2 -5.94 Rater R01          2.36      192 #> 3 -5.88 Rater R01          2.51      192 #> 4 -5.82 Rater R01          2.66      192 #> 5 -5.76 Rater R01          2.82      192 #> 6 -5.7  Rater R01          3.00      192

title: Plot inter-rater agreement diagnostics using base R — plot_interrater_agreement
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot inter-rater agreement diagnostics using base R — plot_interrater_agreement
text: Inter-rater agreement plots summarize pairwise consistency chosen rater facet.  Agreement statistics computed observations share person context-facet levels, ensuring comparisons reflect identical rating targets. Exact agreement proportion matched observations raters assigned category score.  expected agreement line shows proportion expected chance given rater's marginal category distribution, providing baseline. Pairwise correlation Pearson correlation scores assigned rater pair matched observations. difference plot decomposes disagreement systematic bias (mean signed difference x-axis: positive = Rater 1 severe) total inconsistency (mean absolute difference y-axis).  Points near origin indicate low bias low inconsistency. context_facets parameter specifies facets define "rating target" (e.g., Criterion).  NULL, non-rater facets used context.

title: Plot inter-rater agreement diagnostics using base R — plot_interrater_agreement
text: "exact" (default) Bar chart exact agreement proportion rater pair.  Expected agreement overlaid connected circles. Horizontal reference line exact_warn.  Bars colored red observed agreement falls warning threshold. "corr" Bar chart pairwise Pearson correlation rater pair.  Reference line corr_warn.  Ordered correlation (lowest first).  Low correlations suggest inconsistent rank ordering persons raters. "difference" Scatter plot.  X-axis: mean signed score difference (Rater 1 \(-\) Rater 2); positive values indicate Rater 1 severe.  Y-axis: mean absolute difference (overall disagreement magnitude).  Points colored red flagged.  Vertical reference 0.

title: Plot inter-rater agreement diagnostics using base R — plot_interrater_agreement
text: Pairs exact_warn /corr_warn prioritized rater calibration review.  difference plot, points far origin along x-axis indicate systematic bias; points high y-axis indicate large inconsistency regardless direction.

title: Plot inter-rater agreement diagnostics using base R — plot_interrater_agreement
text: Select rater facet run "exact" view. Confirm "corr" view. Use "difference" inspect directional disagreement.

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
text: dashboard draws nine QC panels 3\(\times\)3 grid: threshold_profile controls warning overlays.  Three built-profiles available: "strict", "standard" (default), "lenient". Use thresholds override profile value named entries. bounded GPCM, dashboard now reuses residual-based diagnostics stack marks fair-average panel unavailable rather silently reusing Rasch-compatibility calculation.

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
text: function draws fixed 3\(\times\)3 panel grid (plot_type argument).  individual panel control, use dedicated helpers: plot_unexpected(), plot_fair_average(), plot_displacement(), plot_interrater_agreement(), plot_facets_chisq().

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
text: Recommended panel order fast review: Category counts + Infit/Outfit (row 1): first-pass model screening. Category bars roughly track expected line; Infit/Outfit points often reviewed heuristic 0.5–1.5 band. Unexpected responses + Displacement (row 2): element-level outliers.  Sparse points small displacements desirable. Inter-rater + Chi-square (row 3): facet-level comparability. Read screening panels: higher agreement suggests stronger scoring consistency, significant fixed chi-square indicates detectable facet spread current model. Separation/Reliability (row 3): approximate screening precision. Higher separation indicates statistically distinct strata current SE approximation. Treat dashboard screening layer; follow dedicated helpers (plot_unexpected(), plot_displacement(), plot_interrater_agreement(), plot_facets_chisq()) detailed diagnosis.

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
text: Fit diagnose model. Run plot_qc_dashboard() one-page triage. Drill flagged panels using dedicated functions.

title: Plot a base-R QC dashboard — plot_qc_dashboard
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Fast smoke run: build the plot data only (no graphics device). toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_small <- toy[toy$Person %in% unique(toy$Person)[1:3], ] fit_quick <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(toy_small, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",            method = "JML", maxit = 3) ) qc_quick <- plot_qc_dashboard(fit_quick, draw = FALSE) names(qc_quick$data)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) qc <- plot_qc_dashboard(fit, draw = FALSE) qc$data$panels$Status # Look for: a row whose `Status` is "OK" for each panel that #   the run should support. "WARN" / "REVIEW" rows tell you which #   downstream helper to run next (e.g. `plot_unexpected()`, #   `plot_residual_pca()`); the dashboard is a triage screen, not #   a publication figure on its own. if (interactive()) {   plot_qc_dashboard(fit, rater_facet = "Rater") } } # } }

title: Plot QC pipeline results — plot_qc_pipeline
text: Invisible verdicts tibble QC pipeline.

title: Plot QC pipeline results — plot_qc_pipeline
text: Two plot types provided visual triage QC results: "traffic_light" (default): horizontal bar chart one row per QC check.  Bars coloured green (Pass), amber (Warn), red (Fail).  Provides --glance summary current QC review state. "detail": panel showing check's observed value pass/warn/fail thresholds.  Useful understanding close borderline result next verdict level.

title: Plot QC pipeline results — plot_qc_pipeline
text: pipeline evaluates 10 checks (depending available diagnostics): Convergence: optimizer converge? Overall Infit: global information-weighted mean-square Overall Outfit: global unweighted mean-square Misfit rate: proportion elements \(|\mathrm{ZSTD}| > 2\) Category usage: minimum observations per score category Disordered steps: whether threshold estimates monotonic Separation (per facet): element discrimination adequacy Residual PCA eigenvalue: first-component eigenvalue (computed) Displacement: maximum absolute displacement across elements Inter-rater agreement: minimum pairwise exact agreement

title: Plot QC pipeline results — plot_qc_pipeline
text: Green (Pass): check meets current threshold-profile criteria. Amber (Warn): borderline—monitor necessarily disqualifying.  Review detail panel see close value fail threshold. Red (Fail): requires investigation strong operational interpretive claims made current run. Common remedies include collapsing categories (disordered steps), removing outlier raters (misfit), increasing sample size (low separation). detail view shows numeric values, making easy communicate exact results stakeholders.

title: Plot QC pipeline results — plot_qc_pipeline

code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) p <- plot_rater_agreement_heatmap(fit, draw = FALSE) dim(p$data$matrix) # Look for (default `metric = "exact"`): # - Off-diagonal cells close to the corresponding entry of #   `summary(diag)$interrater$ExactAgreement` indicate consistent #   pair behaviour; cells well below the average mark a pair #   that disagrees more than the rest. # - With `metric = "correlation"` the colour scale switches to #   `[-1, 1]`; positive cells = pairs agree on relative ordering, #   negative cells = pairs systematically rank persons in opposite #   directions and are the highest-priority review cases. }

Fits assumed placed common scale via anchor-linking equivalent post-hoc transformation (see caveat ). facet Facet whose levels tracked (default "Rater"). ci_level Confidence level per-wave CI ribbons drawn around trajectory (default 0.95). preset Visual preset. draw TRUE, draw base graphics.

title: Rater-severity trajectory across an ordered wave / occasion variable — plot_rater_trajectory
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit_a <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                   method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit_b <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                   method = "JML", maxit = 30) p <- plot_rater_trajectory(list(T1 = fit_a, T2 = fit_b), draw = FALSE) head(p$data$data) # Look for: stable trajectories (small wave-to-wave shifts within #   each rater's CI ribbon) once the waves are anchor-linked. A #   rater whose line drifts >0.5 logits across waves is the typical #   "calibration drift" signal. Without anchor linking the per-wave #   logits are on different scales and the picture cannot be read #   as drift; see the Anchor-linking caveat in the docstring. } # }

text: named list plotting data (class mfrm_plot_data) : plot: "scree", "parallel_scree", "parallel_excess", "loadings" mode: "overall" "facet" facet: facet name (NULL) title: plot title text data: underlying table used plotting

title: Visualize residual PCA results — plot_residual_pca
text: x can either: output analyze_residual_pca(), diagnostics object diagnose_mfrm() (PCA computed internally), fitted object fit_mfrm() (diagnostics PCA computed internally). Plot types: "scree": component vs eigenvalue line plot "parallel_scree": observed eigenvalues residual-permutation parallel-analysis mean upper cutoff "parallel_excess": observed eigenvalue minus parallel-analysis cutoff component "loadings": horizontal bar chart top absolute loadings mode = "facet" facet = NULL, first available facet used.

title: Visualize residual PCA results — plot_residual_pca
text: plot_type = "scree": look dominant early components relative later components unit-eigenvalue reference line. Treat exploratory residual-structure screening, standalone unidimensionality test DIMTEST/UNIDIM substitute. plot_type = "parallel_scree" "parallel_excess": use running analyze_residual_pca() parallel = TRUE. Components residual-permutation cutoff candidates follow-, proof multidimensionality. plot_type = "loadings": identifies variables/elements driving component; inspect sign absolute magnitude. Facet mode (mode = "facet") helps localize residual structure specific facet global PCA review.

title: Visualize residual PCA results — plot_residual_pca
text: Run diagnose_mfrm() residual_pca = "overall" "". Build PCA object via analyze_residual_pca() (pass diagnostics directly). Use scree plot first, loadings plot targeted interpretation.

title: Visualize residual PCA results — plot_residual_pca
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy_full <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_people <- unique(toy_full$Person)[1:24] toy <- toy_full[match(toy_full$Person, toy_people, nomatch = 0L) > 0L, , drop = FALSE] fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) ) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "overall") pca <- analyze_residual_pca(diag, mode = "overall") plt <- plot_residual_pca(pca, mode = "overall", plot_type = "scree", draw = FALSE) head(plt$data) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pca_pa <- analyze_residual_pca(diag, mode = "overall", parallel = TRUE, parallel_reps = 10) pa <- plot_residual_pca(pca_pa, mode = "overall", plot_type = "parallel_scree", draw = FALSE) head(pa$data) } # } plt_load <- plot_residual_pca(   pca, mode = "overall", plot_type = "loadings", component = 1, draw = FALSE ) head(plt_load$data) if (interactive()) {   plot_residual_pca(pca, mode = "overall", plot_type = "scree", preset = "publication") } }

text: Requires fit produced via apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage() fit_mfrm(..., facet_shrinkage = "empirical_bayes") run, fit$facets$others carries Estimate, ShrunkEstimate, ShrinkageFactor columns.

title: Empirical-Bayes shrinkage funnel / caterpillar — plot_shrinkage_funnel
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                  method = "JML", maxit = 30) fit_eb <- apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(fit) p <- plot_shrinkage_funnel(fit_eb, draw = FALSE) head(p$data$table) # Look for: short segments (Raw and Shrunken close together) = #   little pooling. Long segments fanning toward the centre = the #   prior pulled the estimate strongly; this is most pronounced for #   small-N levels. ShrinkageFactor near 1 means most of the #   movement was driven by the prior rather than the data. }

title: Plot unexpected responses using base R — plot_unexpected
text: plotting-data object class mfrm_plot_data.

title: Plot unexpected responses using base R — plot_unexpected
text: helper visualizes flagged observations unexpected_response_table(). observation "unexpected" standardised residual /observed-category probability exceed user-specified cutoffs. severity index composite ranking metric combines absolute standardised residual \(|Z|\) negative log probability \(-\log_{10} P_{\mathrm{obs}}\).  Higher severity indicates responses surprising fitted model. rule parameter controls flagging logic: "either": flag \(|Z| \ge\) abs_z_min \(P_{\mathrm{obs}} \le\) prob_max. "": flag conditions hold simultaneously. common thresholds, many well-behaved runs produce relatively flagged observations, flagged proportion design- model-dependent. Treat output screening display rather calibrated goodness--fit test.

title: Plot unexpected responses using base R — plot_unexpected
text: "scatter" (default) X-axis: standardized residual \(Z\). Y-axis: \(-\log_{10}(P_{\mathrm{obs}})\) (negative log observed-category probability; higher = surprising). Points colored orange observed score higher expected, teal lower.  Dashed lines mark abs_z_min prob_max thresholds.  Clusters points upper corners indicate systematic misfit patterns worth investigating. "severity" Ranked bar chart composite severity index top_n unexpected responses.  Bar length reflects combined unexpectedness; labels identify specific person-facet combination.  Use QC triage case-level prioritization.

title: Plot unexpected responses using base R — plot_unexpected
text: Scatter plot: farther zero x-axis = larger residual mismatch; higher y-axis = lower observed-category probability.  uniform scatter points beyond threshold lines indicates fewer locally surprising responses current thresholds. Severity plot: focuses extreme observations targeted case review.  Look recurring persons facet levels among top entries—repeated appearances may signal rater misuse, scoring errors, model misspecification.

title: Plot unexpected responses using base R — plot_unexpected
text: Fit model run diagnose_mfrm(). Start "scatter" assess global unexpected pattern. Switch "severity" case prioritization.

code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") keep_people <- unique(toy$Person)[1:18] toy_fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(     toy[toy$Person %in% keep_people, , drop = FALSE],     "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",     method = "MML",     quad_points = 5,     maxit = 30   ) ) raters <- unique(toy$Rater)[1:2] criteria <- unique(toy$Criterion)[1:2] new_units <- data.frame(   Person = c("NEW01", "NEW01", "NEW02", "NEW02"),   Rater = c(raters[1], raters[2], raters[1], raters[2]),   Criterion = c(criteria[1], criteria[2], criteria[1], criteria[2]),   Score = c(2, 3, 2, 4) ) pred_units <- predict_mfrm_units(toy_fit, new_units, n_draws = 0) summary(pred_units)$estimates[, c("Person", "Estimate", "Lower", "Upper")] #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   Person Estimate Lower Upper #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 NEW01    -0.149 -1.36  1.36 #> 2 NEW02     0.279 -1.36  1.36

text: Computes Q3-style index inspired Yen (1984) – Pearson correlation standardized residuals every pair levels chosen facet – diagnose_mfrm() bundle. conditional-independence assumption MFRM, |Q3| small every pair; large absolute values flag pairs facet elements (e.g. two raters two items) whose residuals co-move main-effects model expects.

title: Yen-style Q3 local-dependence statistic between facet levels — q3_statistic
text: fit mfrm_fit fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional diagnose_mfrm() output. Computed demand omitted. facet Facet whose levels paired (default "Rater"). min_pairs Minimum number shared response opportunities required retain pair. Pairs threshold drop table (mirrors plot_local_dependence_heatmap()). yen_threshold Community-convention flag threshold (default 0.20). Often attributed Yen (1984), 0.20 cutoff actually Chen & Thissen (1997, p. 284); Yen propose fixed cutoff. cutoff derived raw-residual Q3 3PL - applying standardized-residual Q3 MFRM approximate. marais_threshold Stricter community-convention threshold (default 0.30). Marais (2013, p. 121) reports value "often considered" literature, recommendation; actual recommendation relative comparison implemented relative_offset. relative_offset Screening offset relative-flag rule |Q3 - mean(Q3)| > relative_offset (default 0.20). simplified screening approximation relative comparison advocated Marais (2013) operationalized Christensen et al. (2017) Q3_* = Q3_max - mean(Q3). Christensen et al. (2017) demonstrate empirically single critical value appropriate across designs recommend parametric bootstrap; fixed 0.20 screening default, substitute bootstrap.

title: Yen-style Q3 local-dependence statistic between facet levels — q3_statistic
text: object class mfrm_q3 containing: pairs data frame one row per facet-level pair columns Level1, Level2, Q3, N, AbsQ3, YenFlag, MaraisFlag, RelativeFlag, textual Interpretation summarising thresholds exceeded. summary One-row tibble MeanQ3, MaxAbsQ3, three flagged-pair counts. thresholds thresholds used, reproducibility. facet facet whose levels paired.

title: Yen-style Q3 local-dependence statistic between facet levels — q3_statistic
text: implementation differs Yen's (1984) original definition two respects together affect threshold interpretation. (1) Standardized vs raw residuals. Yen (1984, eqs. 7-8, p. 127) defines Q3 = cor(d_i, d_j) d_{ik} = u_{ik} - P_hat_{ik} raw residual. mfrmr uses standardized residuals Z = (u - P_hat) / sqrt(Var(u)) diagnose_mfrm() stores. Standardization -weights high-variance observations changes sampling distribution resulting correlation; published critical values (Chen & Thissen, 1997; Christensen et al., 2017) derived raw-residual Q3. (2) Mean-aggregation. facet paired (e.g. Rater) multiple residual rows per (Person, Level) cell additional facets design (e.g. multiple Criterion rows per Person-Rater cell), standardized residuals first mean-aggregated one value per (Person, Level) cell, Pearson correlation taken mean-aggregated residuals. Yen's original formulation takes correlation directly per-(Person, Item) residuals, without aggregation. Mean-aggregation reduces noise also shrinks effective sample size can pull correlations toward cell mean. reasons, treat values returned screening summary rather direct substitute published Q3 thresholds. formal local-dependence test raw-residual Q3, use parametric bootstrap recommended Christensen et al. (2017).

title: Yen-style Q3 local-dependence statistic between facet levels — q3_statistic
text: Yen, W. M. (1984). Effects local item dependence fit equating performance three-parameter logistic model. Applied Psychological Measurement, 8(2), 125-145. doi:10.1177/014662168400800201 Chen, W.-H., & Thissen, D. (1997). Local dependence indexes item pairs using item response theory. Journal Educational Behavioral Statistics, 22(3), 265-289. (Origin commonly cited |Q3| > 0.20 cutoff.) Marais, . (2013). Local dependence. K. B. Christensen, S. Kreiner, & M. Mesbah (Eds.), Rasch models health (pp. 111-130). London: ISTE / Wiley. Christensen, K. B., Makransky, G., & Horton, M. (2017). Critical values Yen's Q3: Identification local dependence Rasch model using residual correlations. Applied Psychological Measurement, 41(3), 178-194. doi:10.1177/0146621616677520

title: Yen-style Q3 local-dependence statistic between facet levels — q3_statistic
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) q3 <- q3_statistic(fit) q3$summary # Look for: MaxAbsQ3 < 0.20 (Chen & Thissen 1997 community cutoff) is #   the comfortable regime; values above 0.30 are commonly considered #   strict-flag worthy (Marais, 2013, summarising literature). For a #   formal test, use a parametric bootstrap (Christensen et al., 2017). #   The summary's flag counts give a quick triage; inspect `q3$pairs` #   for the offending level pairs and follow up with content review. head(q3$pairs) }

title: Analyze rater agreement, disagreement, and severity-direction networks — rater_network_analysis
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). rater_facet Name rater-like facet. omitted, mfrmr uses heuristic interrater_agreement_table(). context_facets Facets defining shared scoring contexts. default, person facet non-rater facets used. mode Network definition. "agreement" builds undirected network whose edge weights represent observed agreement. "disagreement" builds undirected network whose edge weights represent observed disagreement. "severity_direction" builds directed network: edge rater rater B means assigned higher scores B shared contexts therefore relatively lenient usual higher-score--better rating convention. weight_metric Pair-level weight used "agreement" "disagreement" networks. Defaults Exact agreement MAD disagreement. Available pair columns include Exact, Adjacent, Corr, MAD, OneMinusExact, AbsMeanDiff. min_pair_n Minimum number shared contexts required rater pair contribute edge. min_weight Minimum edge weight retained graph. score_diff_tolerance Score-difference tolerance directed severity networks. default 0, higher score contributes outgoing leniency edge. Larger values reproduce thresholded disagreement displays "differences greater 3 marks". severity_continuity Continuity constant added incoming outgoing strengths computing finite severity index -log((OutStrength + c) / (InStrength + c)). exact_warn, corr_warn Passed interrater_agreement_table() keep pair flags consistent tabular agreement view. include_graph TRUE, include underlying igraph object returned bundle.

title: Analyze rater agreement, disagreement, and severity-direction networks — rater_network_analysis
text: function implements package-native rater-effect network view complementary MFRM output. follows pairwise-network logic used Lamprianou's rater-effect network work: nodes raters, edges summarize pairwise relationships among raters shared scoring contexts, directed disagreement edges can interpreted relative leniency/severity indicators. network summaries descriptive diagnostics, Rasch logit estimates formal fit statistics. mode = "severity_direction", outgoing strength means rater often assigned higher scores comparison raters; incoming strength means comparison raters often assigned higher scores rater. reported SeverityIndex positive relatively severe raters negative relatively lenient raters, network-analysis scale read MFRM severity logit.

title: Analyze rater agreement, disagreement, and severity-direction networks — rater_network_analysis
text: Lamprianou, . (2018). Investigation rater effects using Social Network Analysis Exponential Random Graph Models. Educational Psychological Measurement, 78(3), 430-459. Lamprianou, . (2025). Network Analysis investigation rater effects language assessment: comparison ChatGPT vs human raters. Research Methods Applied Linguistics, 4, 100205.

title: Analyze rater agreement, disagreement, and severity-direction networks — rater_network_analysis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "JML", maxit = 30 ) if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   rn <- rater_network_analysis(fit, mode = "severity_direction")   rn$summary   head(rn$node_metrics)   plot(rn, type = "severity", draw = FALSE) } } # }

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: named list : category_table: category-level counts, expected counts, fit, ZSTD threshold_table: model step/threshold estimates summary: one-row summary (usage threshold monotonicity) caveats: structured score-support warning/review rows diagnostic_mode: character scalar carried diagnostics$diagnostic_mode ("legacy", "", "marginal_fit"); used downstream reporting helpers pick correct expected-count basis marginal_fit: list bundle diagnostics$marginal_fit strict marginal fit computed, otherwise NULL. Carries raw OverallRMSD / OverallMaxAbsStdResidual / per-cell tables feed MarginalOverallRMSD columns summary.

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: helper provides category usage/fit statistics threshold summaries reviewing score-category functioning. category usage portion global observed-score screen. PCM fits step_facet, threshold diagnostics interpreted within StepFacet rather one pooled whole-scale verdict. Typical checks: sparse category usage (Count, ExpectedCount) category fit (Infit, Outfit, ZStd) threshold ordering within StepFacet (threshold_table$Estimate, GapFromPrev)

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: Start summary: UsedCategories close total Categories suggests score categories represented observed data. small MinCategoryCount indicates potential instability. ThresholdMonotonic = FALSE indicates disordered thresholds within least one threshold set. PCM fits, inspect threshold_table StepFacet drawing scale-wide conclusions. inspect: category_table global category-level misfit/sparsity. threshold_table adjacent-step gaps ordering within StepFacet.

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: Fit model: fit_mfrm(). Build diagnostics: diagnose_mfrm(). Run rating_scale_table() review summary(). Use plot() visualize category profile quickly.

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: plot-selection guide longer walkthrough, see mfrmr_visual_diagnostics vignette("mfrmr-visual-diagnostics", package = "mfrmr").

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: category_table data.frame contains: Category Score category value. Count, Percent Observed count percentage total. AvgPersonMeasure Mean person measure respondents category. Infit, Outfit Category-level fit statistics. InfitZSTD, OutfitZSTD Standardized fit values. ExpectedCount, DiffCount Expected count observed-expected difference. LowCount Logical; TRUE count minimum threshold. InfitFlag, OutfitFlag, ZSTDFlag Fit-based warning flags. ZeroCount, UnusedCategoryType, WeaklyIdentified, CategoryCaveat Structured score-support caveats retained zero-count categories. threshold_table data.frame contains: Step Step label (e.g., "1-2", "2-3"). Estimate Estimated threshold/step difficulty (logits). StepFacet Threshold family identifier fit uses facet-specific threshold sets. GapFromPrev Difference previous threshold within StepFacet thresholds facet-specific. Gaps 1.4 logits may indicate category underuse; gaps 5.0 may indicate wide unused regions (Linacre, 2002). ThresholdMonotonic Logical flag repeated within threshold set. PCM fits, read within StepFacet, pooled item-bank verdict. LowerCategory, UpperCategory, WeaklyIdentified, ThresholdCaveat Adjacent score-category support metadata. Thresholds adjacent retained zero-count categories flagged cautious interpretation.

title: Build a rating-scale diagnostics report — rating_scale_table
text: Andrich, D. (1978). rating formulation ordered response categories. Psychometrika, 43(4), 561-573. doi:10.1007/BF02293814 Masters, G. N. (1982). Rasch model partial credit scoring. Psychometrika, 47(2), 149-174. doi:10.1007/BF02296272 Linacre, J. M. (2002). Infit Outfit, mean-square standardized mean? Rasch Measurement Transactions, 16(2), 878. (Source 0.5-1.5 mean-square acceptance band threshold-gap heuristics used summary(t8)$summary.) Wind, S. . (2023). Detecting rating scale malfunctioning partial credit model generalized partial credit model. Educational Psychological Measurement, 83(5), 953-983. doi:10.1177/00131644221116292  (Recent simulation evidence PCM- GPCM-based rating-scale diagnostics; useful interpreting summary(t8)$summary flags bounded GPCM route.)

Use one-facet CSV exports. facet_map Optional character vector mapping FACETS score-file numbers facet names, example c("1" = "Person", "2" = "Rater"). unnamed, positions used score-file numbers. format File format. "auto" detects FACETS score.N.txt files name/header; "delimited" reads CSV/TSV/semicolon table; "scorefile" reads FACETS score-file table. facet_col, level_col Optional explicit column names delimited tables automatic detection sufficient. score files, level_col can override column immediately F-Number. delimiter Optional delimiter delimited tables. omitted, comma, tab, semicolon detected header line. encoding File encoding passed readLines().

Two common workflows supported: FACETS score file score.2.txt, facet name supplied facet_map inferred Facet2. comma-delimited score files field names fixed-field score files using FACETS manual column positions supported; CSV/TSV table already exported FACETS harmonization script, FACETS-style column names Infit MnSq, Outfit ZStd, T.Count. import, pass table facets_fit_review(). Inspect review$external_table_quality first FACETS export partial, duplicated, missing MnSq/df columns. inspect review$external_comparison supplied FACETS-vs-mfrmr differences review$df_sensitivity / review$df_sensitive engine-vs-FACETS-style df/ZSTD convention sensitivity. Use plot(review, type = "df_sensitivity") quick visual check largest ZSTD shifts caused df convention.

title: Benchmark packaged reference cases — reference_case_benchmark
text: object class mfrm_reference_benchmark.

title: Benchmark packaged reference cases — reference_case_benchmark
text: function checks mfrmr package's curated reference case families: synthetic_truth: checks whether recovered facet measures align known generating values package's synthetic design. synthetic_latent_regression: checks whether first-version latent-regression MML branch recovers known population coefficients, residual latent variance, criterion ordering, posterior-shift direction synthetic overlap case. synthetic_latent_regression_omit: checks whether population-model complete-case omission policy reflected fitted metadata, response-row review, active person estimates, replay provenance. synthetic_conquest_overlap_dry_run: builds narrow ConQuest-overlap bundle latent-regression synthetic case, round-trips package tables normalization/review helpers, confirms package-side workflow without claiming ConQuest executed. synthetic_gpcm: checks whether bounded GPCM branch recovers known criterion-specific slopes, row-centered step parameters, criterion ordering synthetic overlap case. case currently requires model = "GPCM" intended method = "MML". synthetic_bias_contract: checks whether package bias tables pairwise local comparisons satisfy identities documented bias help workflow. *_itercal_pair: compares baseline packaged dataset iterative recalibration counterpart review fit stability, facet-measure alignment, linking coverage together. resulting object intended reference-case check package behavior. establish external validity FACETS, ConQuest, published calibration studies, assume familiarity external table numbering printer layouts. specialized latent-regression omission ConQuest-overlap package-side cases requested, summary(bench) prints preview rows population_policy_checks conquest_overlap_checks alongside reference notes package-versus-external validation boundary remains visible.

title: Benchmark packaged reference cases — reference_case_benchmark
text: overview: one-row reference-case summary. case_summary: pass/warn/fail triage reference case. fit_runs: fitted-run metadata (fit, precision tier, convergence, latent-regression population-model/posterior-basis fields, including categorical-coding details present). design_checks: exact design recovery checks dataset. recovery_checks: known-truth recovery metrics synthetic cases, including latent-regression reference case. bias_checks: source-backed bias/local-measure identity checks. pair_checks: paired-dataset stability screens iterated cases. linking_checks: common-element reviews paired calibration datasets. conquest_overlap_checks: package-side checks ConQuest-overlap bundle/normalization/review workflow; remains package-side check actual ConQuest output tables supplied. population_policy_checks: complete-case omission checks population model benchmark fixtures. source_profile: source-backed rules used reference checks.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: helper ports app-level reporting checklist package-native bundle. try judge substantive reporting quality; instead, checks whether fitted object related diagnostics contain evidence typically reported MFRM write-ups. Checklist items grouped seven core sections: Method section Global fit Facet-level statistics Element-level statistics Rating scale diagnostics Bias/interaction analysis Visual displays fit uses latent-regression population-model branch, checklist also adds Population Model section covering coefficient reporting, categorical model-matrix coding, complete-case omissions, posterior-basis wording, ConQuest scope wording. output designed manuscript preparation, reproducibility records, reproducible reporting workflows.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: reporting_checklist() manuscript-preparation guide. tells reporting elements already present current analysis objects still need generated documented. primary draft-status column DraftReady; ReadyForAPA retained backward-compatible alias.

what: What this checklist does not justify
title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: single run-level pass/fail decision publication. DraftReady = TRUE / ReadyForAPA = TRUE certify formal inferential adequacy. Missing bias rows may simply mean bias_results supplied.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: checklist: one row per reporting item Available = TRUE/FALSE. DraftReady = TRUE means item can drafted report package's documented caveats. ReadyForAPA backward-compatible alias flag; neither field certifies formal inferential adequacy. section_summary: available items section. Global Fit section includes "Fit/separation reporting boundary" row points precision_review_report(), fit_measures_table(), facets_fit_review() users phrase fit, ZSTD, separation, reliability claims. software_scope: external-software relationship summary mfrmr, FACETS, ConQuest, SPSS-style tabular handoffs. facets_positioning: report-ready wording states mfrmr FACETS numerical clone separates native estimation FACETS-style handoff external-table review. visual_scope: plotting-route summary separates report-default 2D figures exploratory surface/3D-ready data handoffs, including short InterpretationCheck main user-facing caveat. references: core background references requested.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: Review rows Available = FALSE DraftReady = FALSE, add missing diagnostics, bias results, narrative context calling build_apa_outputs() draft text generation. RSM / PCM reporting runs, preferred route MML fit plus diagnose_mfrm(..., diagnostic_mode = "") checklist can see legacy strict marginal screens together.

what: How this differs from operational review
title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: reporting_checklist() manuscript/reporting branch package. Use question "still missing report?" rather "observations links need follow-?" operational review: Use build_misfit_casebook() diagnose_mfrm() need ranked misfit cases grouping views local follow-. Use build_linking_review() anchor/drift/chain helpers need operational linking triage rather manuscript-oriented reporting tables.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
text: Fit fit_mfrm(). RSM / PCM reporting runs, prefer method = "MML". Compute diagnostics diagnose_mfrm(). RSM / PCM, prefer diagnostic_mode = "". Run reporting_checklist() see reporting elements already available current analysis objects. issue operational rather manuscript-facing, branch build_misfit_casebook() build_linking_review() instead treating reporting_checklist() single review hub.

title: Build an auto-filled MFRM reporting checklist — reporting_checklist
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Fast smoke run: a JML fit + legacy-only diagnostic produces a # populated checklist in well under a second. toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit_quick <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                       method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag_quick <- diagnose_mfrm(fit_quick, residual_pca = "none",                              diagnostic_mode = "legacy") chk_quick <- reporting_checklist(fit_quick, diagnostics = diag_quick) head(chk_quick$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady")])  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "MML", quad_points = 7, maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "both", diagnostic_mode = "both") chk <- reporting_checklist(fit, diagnostics = diag) summary(chk) # Look for: a high `Ready` / `Total` ratio in the summary block. #   Sections with `Ready = 0` need follow-up before submitting #   (typically diagnostic_mode = "both" or a residual-PCA pass). apa <- build_apa_outputs(fit, diag) head(chk$checklist[, c("Section", "Item", "DraftReady", "NextAction")]) # Look for: every row where `DraftReady = "yes"` is ready to paste #   into the manuscript. `"no"` rows include a concrete `NextAction` #   step (e.g. "run plot_qc_dashboard()") so the gap can be closed #   without re-reading the methodology guide. nchar(apa$report_text) } # } }

text: data data.frame long format one row per observed rating event. person Column name person identifier. facets Optional character vector facet columns summarize. time Column name containing positive response times. score Optional ordered-score column. supplied, score-level response-time summaries returned. time_unit Label response-time unit, "seconds". min_time Minimum valid response time. Values must strictly greater threshold; default 0. rapid_threshold Optional numeric response-time cutoff rapid responses. NULL, estimated rapid_quantile. slow_threshold Optional numeric response-time cutoff slow responses. NULL, estimated slow_quantile. rapid_quantile Quantile used rapid_threshold = NULL. slow_quantile Quantile used slow_threshold = NULL. rapid_rate_warn Group-level rapid-response rate creates descriptive flag; default 0.25. slow_rate_warn Group-level slow-response rate creates descriptive flag; default 0.25. min_n_flag Minimum group size rapid/slow rates flagged; default 3.

code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy$ResponseTime <- 12 + as.numeric(factor(toy$Person)) * 0.4 +   as.numeric(toy$Score) rt <- response_time_review(   toy,   person = "Person",   facets = c("Rater", "Criterion"),   score = "Score",   time = "ResponseTime" ) summary(rt) #> mfrmr response-time review #>  #>  Rows ValidRows DroppedRows Persons Facets   TimeColumn ScoreColumn TimeUnit #>   768       768           0      48      2 ResponseTime       Score  seconds #>  MedianTime MeanLogTime RapidThreshold SlowThreshold  RapidRate  SlowRate #>        24.7     3.16429           16.2          32.6 0.06901042 0.0546875 #>  FlaggedGroups #>             10 #>                                                                               InterpretationBoundary #>  Descriptive response-time screening; not a joint speed-accuracy model and not a fit/pass-fail rule. #>  #> Thresholds: #>  Threshold Value         Basis TimeUnit #>      rapid  16.2 quantile_0.05  seconds #>       slow  32.6 quantile_0.95  seconds #>  #> Flagged groups: #>  Source Group                     Flag   Rate  N ThresholdRate #>  person  P001 high_rapid_response_rate 0.7500 16          0.25 #>  person  P002 high_rapid_response_rate 0.5000 16          0.25 #>  person  P003 high_rapid_response_rate 0.6250 16          0.25 #>  person  P005 high_rapid_response_rate 0.2500 16          0.25 #>  person  P006 high_rapid_response_rate 0.4375 16          0.25 #>  person  P008 high_rapid_response_rate 0.5000 16          0.25 #>  person  P042  high_slow_response_rate 0.2500 16          0.25 #>  person  P044  high_slow_response_rate 0.7500 16          0.25 #>  person  P047  high_slow_response_rate 0.5625 16          0.25 #>  person  P048  high_slow_response_rate 0.8125 16          0.25 #>  #> Notes: #> - Response-time review is descriptive; Leave NULL conquest_population object normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(). conquest_case_eap Normalized ConQuest case-level EAP table data.frame. Leave NULL conquest_population object normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(). conquest_population_term Column conquest_population stores parameter names. "auto" tries conservative aliases Parameter Term. conquest_population_estimate Column conquest_population stores parameter estimates. "auto" tries aliases Estimate Est. conquest_item_id Column conquest_item_estimates stores item identifier. may exported response variable (example I001) original item/facet level. "auto" tries aliases Estimate, Est, Facility. item_id_source conquest_item_id matched. "auto" tries conservative aliases Person, PID, Sequence ID. conquest_case_estimate Column conquest_case_eap stores case EAP estimates. "auto" tries conservative aliases Estimate, EAP_1, EAP.

text: helper compares normalized ConQuest output tables exact- overlap bundle produced build_conquest_overlap_bundle(). intentionally conservative: parse raw ConQuest text output automatically; expects already normalized data frames output normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(); reports numerical differences missing elements without claiming fixed tolerance implies software equivalence. package's external-table review path. distinct reference_case_benchmark(cases = "synthetic_conquest_overlap_dry_run"), round-trips package-native tables normalization review contract without executing ConQuest. intended workflow : export exact-overlap bundle build_conquest_overlap_bundle(); run narrow matching case ConQuest; normalize resulting ConQuest outputs data frames; pass tables inspect direct differences, centered item agreement, case-level EAP agreement.

Item estimates interpreted centering. Case estimates interpreted posterior EAP summaries fitted population model. overall table reports mean maximum absolute differences compared population, centered item, case rows. title: Review an exact-overlap ConQuest comparison against an mfrmr overlap bundle — review_conquest_overlap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bundle <- build_conquest_overlap_bundle() raw_pop <- data.frame(   Term = bundle$mfrmr_population$Parameter,   Est = bundle$mfrmr_population$Estimate ) raw_item <- data.frame(   Item = bundle$mfrmr_item_estimates$ResponseVar,   Est = bundle$mfrmr_item_estimates$Estimate ) raw_case <- data.frame(   PID = bundle$mfrmr_case_eap$Person,   EAP = bundle$mfrmr_case_eap$Estimate ) normalized <- normalize_conquest_overlap_tables(   conquest_population = raw_pop,   conquest_item_estimates = raw_item,   conquest_case_eap = raw_case,   conquest_population_term = "Term",   conquest_population_estimate = "Est",   conquest_item_id = "Item",   conquest_item_estimate = "Est",   conquest_case_person = "PID",   conquest_case_estimate = "EAP" ) review <- review_conquest_overlap(bundle, normalized) summary(review)$summary } # }

text: list class mfrm_anchor_review : anchors: cleaned anchor table used estimation group_anchors: cleaned group-anchor table used estimation facet_summary: counts levels, constrained levels, free levels design_checks: observation-count checks level/category thresholds: active threshold settings used recommendations issue_counts: issue-type counts issues: list issue tables recommendations: package-native anchor guidance strings

title: Review and normalize anchor/group-anchor tables — review_mfrm_anchors
text: Anchoring (also called "fixing" scale linking) constrains selected parameter estimates pre-specified values, placing current analysis previously established scale.  essential comparing results across administrations, linking test forms, monitoring rater drift time. function applies preprocessing key-resolution rules fit_mfrm(), returns review object constraints can checked estimation.  Running review first helps avoid estimation failures caused misspecified data-incompatible anchors. Anchor types: Direct anchors fix individual element measures specific logit values (e.g., Rater R1 anchored 0.35 logits). Group anchors constrain mean set elements target value, allowing individual elements vary freely around mean. types overlap element, direct anchor takes precedence. Design checks verify anchored element least min_obs_per_element weighted observations (default 30) score category least min_obs_per_category (default 10). thresholds follow standard Rasch sample-size recommendations (Linacre, 1994).

title: Review and normalize anchor/group-anchor tables — review_mfrm_anchors
text: issue_counts/issues: concrete data specification problems. facet_summary: constraint coverage facet. design_checks: whether anchor targets enough observations. recommendations: action items estimation.

title: Run a legacy-compatible estimation workflow wrapper — run_mfrm_facets
text: method = "JML" (default): legacy-compatible joint estimation route; default preserves FACETS-style output continuity existing one-shot scripts expect. new analysis scripts, prefer fit_mfrm(..., method = "MML") – MML package-wide recommended route person parameters integrated N(0, 1) prior per-person posterior SEs available. method = "JMLE": explicit JMLE label; internally equivalent JML route. method = "MML": marginal maximum likelihood route using quad_points. Use mml_engine = "em" "hybrid" RSM / PCM fits want staged MML alternatives. model = "PCM" supported; set step_facet facet-specific step structure needed.

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Output diagnose_mfrm(). Computed automatically NULL. threshold_profile Threshold preset: "strict", "standard" (default), "lenient". thresholds Named list override individual thresholds. rater_facet Character name rater facet inter-rater check (auto-detected NULL). include_bias TRUE bias available diagnostics, check DIF/bias. bias_results Optional pre-computed bias results estimate_bias().

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: Object class mfrm_qc_pipeline verdicts, overall status, details, recommendations.

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: pipeline evaluates 10 quality checks assigns verdict (Pass / Warn / Fail) .  overall status severe verdict across checks.  Diagnostics computed automatically via diagnose_mfrm() supplied. Reliability separation used QC signals. mfrmr, Reliability / Separation model-based facet indices RealReliability / RealSeparation provide conservative lower bounds. MML, rely model-based ModelSE values non-person facets; JML, remain exploratory approximations. Three threshold presets available via threshold_profile: Individual thresholds can overridden via thresholds argument (named list keyed internal threshold names shown ). bounded GPCM, pipeline available caveated operational triage supported diagnostics. pass/warn/fail labels remain package QC policy overlays; FACETS score-side equivalence, operational scoring decisions, design-forecasting evidence, automatic fairness / validity decisions.

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: 10 checks : Convergence: model converge? Global fit: Infit/Outfit MnSq within current review band. Reliability: Minimum non-person facet model reliability index. Separation: Minimum non-person facet model separation index. Element misfit: Percentage elements Infit/Outfit outside current review band. Unexpected responses: Percentage observations large standardized residuals. Category structure: Minimum category count threshold ordering. Connectivity: observations single connected subset. Inter-rater agreement: Exact agreement percentage rater facet (applicable). Functioning/Bias screen: Percentage interaction cells cross screening threshold (interaction results available).

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: $overall: character string "Pass", "Warn", "Fail". $verdicts: tibble columns Check, Verdict, Value, Threshold 10 checks. $details: character vector human-readable detail strings. $raw_details: named list per-check numeric details programmatic access. $recommendations: character vector actionable suggestions checks pass. $config: records threshold profile effective thresholds.

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
text: Fit model: fit <- fit_mfrm(...). Optionally compute diagnostics bias: diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit); bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, ...). Run pipeline: qc <- run_qc_pipeline(fit, diag, bias_results = bias). Check qc$overall headline verdict. Review qc$verdicts per-check details. Follow qc$recommendations remediation. Visualize plot_qc_pipeline().

title: Run automated quality control pipeline — run_qc_pipeline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("study1") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "JML", maxit = 30 ) qc <- run_qc_pipeline(fit) qc summary(qc) qc$verdicts } # }

title: Sample approximate plausible values under fitted posterior scoring — sample_mfrm_plausible_values
text: sample_mfrm_plausible_values() thin public wrapper around predict_mfrm_units() exposes fixed-calibration posterior draws standalone object. useful downstream workflows want repeated latent-value imputations rather just one posterior EAP summary. current mfrmr implementation approximate plausible values drawn fitted quadrature-grid posterior scoring basis implied fit. ordinary MML fits fitted marginal calibration; latent-regression MML fits fitted conditional normal population model scored persons; JML fits fixed facet/step calibration together standard normal reference prior quadrature grid. interpreted posterior uncertainty summaries scored persons, deterministic future truth values claim full many-facet plausible-values equivalence population-model software. words, JML path practical scoring approximation layered top fitted joint-likelihood calibration, whereas latent-regression MML path uses fitted one-dimensional conditional normal population model. Neither path described full many-facet plausible-values system ConQuest-style extensions.

title: Sample approximate plausible values under fitted posterior scoring — sample_mfrm_plausible_values
text: underlying posterior scoring follows usual quadrature-based EAP framework Bock Aitkin (1981). interpretation multiple posterior draws plausible-value-style summaries follows general logic discussed Mislevy (1991), current implementation remains practical fixed-calibration approximation rather full published many-facet plausible-values method. JML source fits, quadrature posterior uses package-level standard normal reference prior post hoc scoring layer. Bock, R. D., & Aitkin, M. (1981). Marginal maximum likelihood estimation item parameters: Application EM algorithm. Psychometrika, 46(4), 443-459. Mislevy, R. J. (1991). Randomization-based inference latent variables complex samples. Psychometrika, 56(2), 177-196.

title: Sample approximate plausible values under fitted posterior scoring — sample_mfrm_plausible_values
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") keep_people <- unique(toy$Person)[1:18] toy_fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(     toy[toy$Person %in% keep_people, , drop = FALSE],     "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",     method = "MML",     quad_points = 5,     maxit = 30   ) ) new_units <- data.frame(   Person = c("NEW01", "NEW01"),   Rater = unique(toy$Rater)[1],   Criterion = unique(toy$Criterion)[1:2],   Score = c(2, 3) ) pv <- sample_mfrm_plausible_values(toy_fit, new_units, n_draws = 3, seed = 1) summary(pv)$draw_summary #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   Person Draws MeanValue SDValue LowerValue UpperValue #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 NEW01      3         0       0          0          0

title: Simulate long-format ordered many-facet data for design studies — simulate_mfrm_data
text: n_person Number persons/respondents. n_rater Number rater facet levels. n_criterion Number criterion/item facet levels. raters_per_person Number raters assigned person. design Optional named design override supplied named list, named vector, one-row data frame. sim_spec = NULL, names may use canonical variables (n_person, n_rater, n_criterion, raters_per_person) role keywords (person, rater, criterion, assignment). currently exposed facet keys, schema-future branch input design$facets = c(person = ..., rater = ..., criterion = ...) also accepted. specify variable design scalar count arguments. score_levels Number ordered score categories. theta_sd Standard deviation simulated person measures. rater_sd Standard deviation simulated rater severities. criterion_sd Standard deviation simulated criterion difficulties. noise_sd Optional observation-level noise added linear predictor. step_span Spread step thresholds logit scale. group_levels Optional character vector group labels. supplied, balanced Group column added simulated data. dif_effects Optional data.frame describing true group-linked DIF effects. Must include Group, least one design column Criterion, numeric Effect. interaction_effects Optional data.frame describing true non-group interaction effects. Must include least one design column Rater Criterion, plus numeric Effect. seed Optional random seed. model Measurement model recorded simulation setup. current public generator supports RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM. step_facet Step facet used model = "PCM" threshold values vary across levels. Currently "Criterion" "Rater" supported. slope_facet Slope facet used model = "GPCM". current bounded GPCM branch requires slope_facet == step_facet. thresholds Optional threshold specification. Use numeric vector common thresholds; named list list(C01 = c(-1, 0, 1)); numeric matrix one row per StepFacet one column per step; long data frame columns StepFacet, Step/StepIndex, Estimate. slopes Optional slope specification used model = "GPCM". Use either numeric vector aligned generated slope-facet levels data frame columns SlopeFacet Estimate. Supplied slopes treated relative discriminations normalized package's geometric-mean-one identification convention log scale. omitted, slopes default 1 every slope-facet level, giving exact PCM reduction. assignment Assignment design. "crossed" means every person sees every rater; "rotating" uses balanced rotating subset; "resampled" reuses person-level rater-assignment profiles stored sim_spec; "sparse_linked" uses incomplete rating design optional linking persons; "skeleton" reuses observed response skeleton stored sim_spec, including optional Group/Weight columns available. omitted, function chooses "crossed" raters_per_person == n_rater, otherwise "rotating". sparse_controls Optional named list used assignment = "sparse_linked". Supported entries link_fraction, link_persons, link_raters_per_person, assignment_mode, min_common_persons_per_rater_pair. See build_mfrm_sim_spec() contract. sim_spec Optional output build_mfrm_sim_spec() extract_mfrm_sim_spec(). supplied, defines generator setup; direct scalar arguments treated legacy inputs generally left defaults except seed. custom public two-facet names recorded sim_spec$facet_names also carried simulated output downstream planning helpers. sim_spec stores active latent-regression population generator, returned object also carries generated one-row-per-person background-data table needed refit population model later.

title: Simulate long-format ordered many-facet data for design studies — simulate_mfrm_data
text: long-format data.frame core columns Study, Person, two simulated non-person facet columns, Score. default facet columns Rater Criterion; sim_spec records custom public names, names used instead. group labels simulated reused observed response skeleton, Group column included. weighted response skeleton reused, Weight column also included.

title: Simulate long-format ordered many-facet data for design studies — simulate_mfrm_data
text: function generates synthetic ordered many-facet data RSM, PCM, package's bounded GPCM branch. data-generating process : Draw person abilities: \(\theta_n \sim N(0, \texttt{theta\_sd}^2)\) Draw rater severities: \(\delta_j \sim N(0, \texttt{rater\_sd}^2)\) Draw criterion difficulties: \(\beta_i \sim N(0, \texttt{criterion\_sd}^2)\) Generate evenly-spaced step thresholds spanning \(\pm\)step_span/2 observation, compute linear predictor \(\eta = \theta_n - \delta_j - \beta_i + \epsilon\) \(\epsilon \sim N(0, \texttt{noise\_sd}^2)\) (optional) Compute category probabilities recorded measurement model (RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM) sample response Latent-value generation explicit: latent_distribution = "normal" draws centered normal person/rater/ criterion values using supplied standard deviations latent_distribution = "empirical" resamples centered support values recorded sim_spec$empirical_support sim_spec$population$active = TRUE, person measures generated stored latent-regression population model template person covariates rather theta_sd dif_effects supplied, specified logit shift added \(\eta\) focal group target facet level, creating known DIF signal.  Similarly, interaction_effects injects known bias specific facet-level combinations. generator targets common two-facet rating design (persons \(\times\) raters \(\times\) criteria).  raters_per_person controls incomplete-block structure: less n_rater, person assigned rotating subset raters keep coverage balanced reproducible. Threshold handling intentionally explicit: thresholds = NULL, common equally spaced thresholds generated step_span thresholds numeric vector, used one common threshold set thresholds named list, numeric matrix, data frame, threshold values may vary StepFacet (currently Criterion Rater) bounded GPCM, generator now requires explicit slope contract parallel threshold table. current public branch keeps slope_facet == step_facet, normalizes supplied slopes geometric-mean-one log-slope identification used fit_mfrm(), uses internal category_prob_gpcm() helper response sampling. Broader arbitrary-facet planning remains restricted slope-aware contract generalized beyond current role-based design, population-forecasting, diagnostic-screening, signal-detection helpers. Assignment handling also explicit: "crossed" uses full person x rater x criterion design "rotating" assigns deterministic rotating subset raters per person "sparse_linked" assigns persons incomplete rater subset assigns configurable set linking persons larger rater set "resampled" reuses empirical person-level rater profiles stored sim_spec$assignment_profiles, optionally carrying person-level Group "skeleton" reuses observed person--rater--criterion response skeleton stored sim_spec$design_skeleton, optionally carrying Group Weight Sparse linked simulation intended planned-missing rating designs connectivity maintained common linking persons. returned mfrm_sparse_design attribute summarizes design density, planned missingness, rater coverage, rater-pair common-person counts. summaries design diagnostics, model-fit statistics universal adequacy thresholds. branch follows sparse rater-mediated assessment design work Wind, Jones, Grajeda (2023, doi:10.1177/01466216231182148), Wind Jones (2018, doi:10.1177/0013164417703733), DeMars, Shapovalov, Hathcoat (2023). controlled workflows, build reusable simulation specification first via build_mfrm_sim_spec() derive one observed fit extract_mfrm_sim_spec(), pass sim_spec. Returned data include attributes: mfrm_truth: simulated true parameters (parameter-recovery checks) mfrm_truth$signals: injected DIF interaction signal tables mfrm_truth$slope_table: simulated discrimination table bounded GPCM mfrm_population_data: generated one-row-per-person background data simulation specification stores active latent-regression generator, including model-matrix xlevel contrast provenance categorical covariates mfrm_simulation_spec: generation settings (reproducibility) mfrm_sparse_design: sparse-design diagnostics assignment = "sparse_linked", including design density, planned missing rate, rater coverage, rater-pair common-person counts

title: Simulate long-format ordered many-facet data for design studies — simulate_mfrm_data
text: Higher theta values mfrm_truth$person indicate higher person measures. Higher values mfrm_truth$facets$Rater indicate severe raters. Higher values mfrm_truth$facets$Criterion indicate difficult criteria. mfrm_truth$signals$dif_effects mfrm_truth$signals$interaction_effects record injected detection targets.

code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) out <- subset_connectivity_report(fit) summary(out) p_sub <- plot(out, draw = FALSE) p_design <- plot(out, type = "design_matrix", draw = FALSE) p_net <- plot(out, type = "network", draw = FALSE) p_sub$data$plot p_design$data$plot p_net$data$edges out$summary[, c("Subset", "Observations", "ObservationPercent")] }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy_full <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_people <- unique(toy_full$Person)[1:12] toy <- toy_full[toy_full$Person %in% toy_people, , drop = FALSE] fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) ) t4 <- unexpected_response_table(fit, abs_z_min = 1.5, prob_max = 0.4, top_n = 5) summary(t4) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) t11 <- bias_count_table(bias, branch = "facets") summary(t11) } # }

title: Summarize an mfrm_diagnostics object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_diagnostics

title: Summarize an mfrm_diagnostics object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_diagnostics

title: Summarize an mfrm_diagnostics object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_diagnostics
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy <- toy[toy$Person %in% unique(toy$Person)[1:4], ] fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") s <- summary(diag, top_n = 3) s$key_warnings # Look for: lines beginning with "MnSq misfit:" name the worst #   element + Infit / Outfit values; "Unexpected responses flagged" #   counts how many cell-level surprises the screen returned. s$top_fit # Look for: rows with |InfitZSTD| or |OutfitZSTD| > 2 are misfitting #   at the 5% level; > 3 is misfitting at the 1% level. Investigate #   in order of the AbsZ column. s$facets_chisq # Look for: FixedProb < 0.05 in each non-Person facet means the #   facet contributes meaningful spread; FixedProb >= 0.05 means #   that facet is statistically indistinguishable. }
