title: Summarize an mfrm_fit object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_fit
text: object class summary.mfrm_fit : overview: global model/fit indicators status: concise front-door status block quick review key_warnings: highest-priority warnings review first next_actions: recommended follow-helpers population_overview: current population-model basis, residual variance, omission review population_coefficients: fitted latent-regression coefficients population model active population_design: latent-regression design-matrix column check population model active population_coding: categorical covariate levels contrast provenance population model uses model-matrix coding facet_overview: per-facet estimate distribution summary person_overview: person-measure distribution summary targeting: person-versus-non-person facet targeting overview (Wright-map-style mean/SD comparison) step_overview: threshold/step diagnostics slope_overview: discrimination summary GPCM fits interaction_overview: model-estimated facet-interaction summary fit specified facet_interactions settings_overview: estimation-settings overview pins configuration affects identification/scoring attached_diagnostics: logical flag indicating whether mfrm_fit returned diagnostics already attached attached_diagnostics_cols: character vector diagnostic columns attached fit$facets$person attached_diagnostics = TRUE row_retention: row counts preparation filters preparation_notes: structured preparation notes retained fit$prep reporting_map: routing map showing companion summaries tables used four manuscript-oriented reporting sections (data description, diagnostics, category checks, draft reporting) person_high / person_low: highest lowest person measures facet_extremes: extreme facet-level estimates caveats: structured warning/review rows score-support latent-regression population-model issues notes: short interpretation notes digits: numeric-print precision threaded print.summary.mfrm_fit()

title: Summarize an mfrm_fit object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_fit
text: method provides compact, human-readable summary oriented reporting. returns structured object prints: model fit overview (N, LogLik, AIC/BIC, convergence) estimation settings affect identification/scoring interpretation facet-level estimate distribution (mean/SD/range) person measure distribution step/threshold checks reporting map showing companion summaries/tables used manuscript-oriented data description, diagnostics, category checks, draft reporting high/low person measures extreme facet levels

title: Summarize an mfrm_fit object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_fit
text: overview: convergence information criteria. facet_overview: per-facet spread range estimates. person_overview: distribution person measures. step_overview: threshold spread monotonicity checks. settings_overview: estimation settings affect interpretation. population_coding: fitted categorical levels contrasts must reused scoring new persons population-model posterior. key_warnings / notes: short triage subset retained zero-count score categories latent-regression population-model caveats complete-case omissions, zero-variance design columns, missing coefficients, unstable residual variance present. Incomplete non-finite covariates normally handled fitting input errors complete-case omissions; appear retained population-design check row. caveats: structured rows behind warnings appendix/export use; print(summary(fit)) shows compact Caveats block rows present. reporting_map: get companion outputs manuscript reporting. top_person / top_facet: extreme estimates quick triage.

title: Summarize an mfrm_fit object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_fit
text: Fit model fit_mfrm(). Run summary(fit) first-pass diagnostics. RSM / PCM, continue diagnose_mfrm() element-level fit checks. bounded GPCM, continue compute_information() / plot_information() fixed-calibration posterior scoring helpers.

title: Summarize an mfrm_fit object in a user-friendly format — summary.mfrm_fit
code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(   toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",   method = "MML", quad_points = 5 ) s <- summary(fit) s$overview[, c("Model", "Method", "Converged")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   Model Method Converged #>   <chr> <chr>  <lgl>     #> 1 RSM   MML    TRUE      # Look for: Converged = TRUE. If FALSE the fit is not safe to report; #   raise `maxit`, relax `reltol`, or rerun with `quad_points = 31`. s$person_overview #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   Persons   Mean    SD   Median   Min   Max  Span MeanPosteriorSD #>     <int>  <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>           <dbl> #> 1      48 0.0214  1.04 0.000215 -1.56  2.52  4.08           0.257 # Look for: Mean ~ 0 (logits) and SD ~ 1 are typical when the sample #   is centred on the test difficulty. Min < -3 or Max > 3 with #   `Extreme = "min"/"max"` rows indicates ceiling / floor cases. s$targeting #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   Facet     PersonMean FacetMean Targeting PersonSD FacetSD SpreadRatio #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Criterion     0.0214         0    0.0214     1.04   0.290        3.59 #> 2 Rater         0.0214         0    0.0214     1.04   0.316        3.30 # Look for: |Targeting| < ~0.5 logits across non-person facets is #   comfortable. Larger absolute values mean the test is systematically #   easier or harder than the person sample. SpreadRatio > 2 means #   persons dominate facet variability; < 0.5 means facets dominate.

code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") keep_people <- unique(toy$Person)[1:18] toy_fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(     toy[toy$Person %in% keep_people, , drop = FALSE],     "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",     method = "MML",     quad_points = 5,     maxit = 30   ) ) new_units <- data.frame(   Person = c("NEW01", "NEW01"),   Rater = unique(toy$Rater)[1],   Criterion = unique(toy$Criterion)[1:2],   Score = c(2, 3) ) pv <- sample_mfrm_plausible_values(toy_fit, new_units, n_draws = 3, seed = 1) summary(pv) #> mfrmr Plausible Values Summary #>  #> Draw summary #>  Person Draws MeanValue SDValue LowerValue UpperValue #>   NEW01     3         0       0          0          0 #>  #> Companion estimates #>  Person Estimate    SD  Lower Upper Observations WeightedN #>   NEW01   -0.097 0.648 -1.356 1.356            2         2 #>  #> Row preparation review #>  InputRows KeptRows DroppedRows DroppedMissing DroppedBadScore DroppedBadWeight #>          2        2           0              0               0                0 #>  DroppedNonpositiveWeight #>                         0 #>  #> Settings #>             Setting      Value #>      interval_level       0.95 #>             n_draws          3 #>         quad_points          5 #>                seed          1 #>              method        MML #>      source_columns   <list 4> #>     posterior_basis legacy_mml #>           person_id       NULL #>   population_policy       NULL #>  population_formula       NULL #>  #> Notes #>  - These draws are sampled from the fixed quadrature-grid posterior under the existing MML calibration. #>  - Use them as approximate plausible-value summaries for posterior uncertainty, not as deterministic future truth values.

code: toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") keep_people <- unique(toy$Person)[1:18] toy_fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(     toy[toy$Person %in% keep_people, , drop = FALSE],     "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",     method = "MML",     quad_points = 5,     maxit = 30   ) ) new_units <- data.frame(   Person = c("NEW01", "NEW01"),   Rater = unique(toy$Rater)[1],   Criterion = unique(toy$Criterion)[1:2],   Score = c(2, 3) ) pred_units <- predict_mfrm_units(toy_fit, new_units) summary(pred_units) #> mfrmr Unit Prediction Summary #>  #> Posterior estimates #>  Person Estimate    SD  Lower Upper Observations WeightedN #>   NEW01   -0.097 0.648 -1.356 1.356            2         2 #>  #> Row preparation review #>  InputRows KeptRows DroppedRows DroppedMissing DroppedBadScore DroppedBadWeight #>          2        2           0              0               0                0 #>  DroppedNonpositiveWeight #>                         0 #>  #> Settings #>             Setting      Value #>      interval_level       0.95 #>             n_draws          0 #>         quad_points          5 #>                seed       NULL #>              method        MML #>      source_columns   <list 4> #>     posterior_basis legacy_mml #>           person_id       NULL #>   population_policy       NULL #>  population_formula       NULL #>  #> Notes #>  - Posterior summaries are computed under the fixed fitted MML calibration. #>  - Non-person facets in `new_data` must already exist in the fitted calibration. #>  - Overlapping person IDs are treated as labels in `new_data`; the original fitted person estimates are not updated.

BaselineUnexpectedN Unexpected count bias adjustment. AfterBiasUnexpectedN Unexpected count adjustment. ReducedBy, ReducedPercent Reduction unexpected count.

title: Build an unexpected-after-adjustment screening report — unexpected_after_bias_table
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_bias") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") bias <- estimate_bias(fit, diag, facet_a = "Rater", facet_b = "Criterion", max_iter = 2) t10 <- unexpected_after_bias_table(fit, bias, diagnostics = diag, top_n = 20) summary(t10) p_t10 <- plot(t10, draw = FALSE) p_t10$data$plot }

title: Build an unexpected-response screening report — unexpected_response_table
text: table data.frame contains: Row Original row index prepared data. Person Person identifier (plus one column per facet). Score Observed score category. Observed, Expected Observed model-expected score values. Residual, StdResidual Raw standardized residuals. ObsProb Probability observed category model. MostLikely, MostLikelyProb probable category probability. Severity Composite severity index (higher = unexpected). Direction "Higher expected" "Lower expected". FlagLowProbability, FlagLargeResidual Logical flags criterion. summary data.frame contains: TotalObservations Total observations analyzed. UnexpectedN, UnexpectedPercent Count share flagged rows. AbsZThreshold, ProbThreshold Applied cutoff values. Rule "either" "".

title: Build an unexpected-response screening report — unexpected_response_table
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() toy_full <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") toy_people <- unique(toy_full$Person)[1:12] toy <- toy_full[toy_full$Person %in% toy_people, , drop = FALSE] fit <- suppressWarnings(   fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score", method = "JML", maxit = 30) ) t4 <- unexpected_response_table(fit, abs_z_min = 1.5, prob_max = 0.4, top_n = 5) summary(t4) p_t4 <- plot(t4, draw = FALSE) p_t4$data$plot }

DefaultPlacement #> 1                      Main text when targeting, spread, or shared-logit interpretation is central. #> 2                 Main text or category-functioning subsection for ordered-category interpretation. #> 3                       Main text or appendix; pair with pathway when category behavior is central. #> 4                             Appendix, teaching, review, or downstream interactive rendering only. #> 5                        Main text when precision or targeting across theta is a substantive claim. #> 6  Screening dashboard; usually methods appendix or local triage rather than the final main figure. #> 7                                               Case-review appendix or quality-control supplement. #> 8                                               Diagnostic appendix after diagnostic_mode = "both". #> 9             Main text only if interaction/DFF is a study question; otherwise diagnostic appendix. #> 10                                                   Diagnostic appendix or sensitivity discussion. #> 11                            Teaching material or diagnostic appendix; not a standalone fit claim. #> 12                                            Diagnostic appendix or supplement after a fit screen. #> 13               Diagnostic appendix for rater-training/drift review; requires anchor-linked waves. #> 14                          Diagnostic appendix when rater count makes the bar-chart form too busy. #> 15        Diagnostic appendix or data-quality supplement when response-time metadata are available. #> 16             Appendix or methods supplement when small-N facet estimates were empirically shrunk. #>                                                                                                           WhatToReport #> 1                              Describe whether persons, facet levels, and thresholds overlap on the same logit scale. #> 2                                 Describe expected-score progression and the theta regions where categories dominate. #> 3                         Describe whether categories peak in the intended order and whether adjacent curves separate. #> 4                         Describe it as exploratory category-probability support, not as a default manuscript figure. #> 5                                           Describe where measurement information is highest or weakest across theta. #> 6                           Describe which components triggered follow-up, not a single pass/fail publication verdict. #> 7                                                                Describe which responses or levels need local review. #> 8                                           Describe which facet/category cells or pairwise structures need follow-up. #> 9                   Describe screened interaction or group-by-facet DFF patterns with low-count and threshold caveats. #> 10                                     Describe residual structure as exploratory follow-up after the main fit screen. #> 11 Describe the Guttman-style ordering as a teaching screen and call out where the overlay marks unexpected responses. #> 12          Describe tail behavior of person-level residuals as exploratory follow-up, not as a formal normality test. #> 13   Describe rater-level movement across waves under the stated linking assumption; name the anchor route explicitly. #> 14          Describe pairwise agreement or correlation structure as a compact alternative to the interrater bar chart. #> 15           Describe rapid/slow response-time patterns by person, facet, or score category as descriptive QC context. #> 16       Describe how far raw facet estimates moved toward the facet mean and whether confidence whiskers remain wide. #>                                                                                                                  CaptionSkeleton #> 1            Figure X. Wright map showing person measures, facet-level locations, and step thresholds on the shared logit scale. #> 2                     Figure X. Expected score pathway across theta, with dominant-category regions for the fitted rating scale. #> 3                                   Figure X. Category characteristic curves showing fitted category probabilities across theta. #> 4    Appendix Figure X. Exploratory category-probability surface showing theta, retained category index, and fitted probability. #> 5  Figure X. Test information curve showing where the fitted model provides relatively stronger or weaker measurement precision. #> 6                         Appendix Figure X. Quality-control dashboard summarizing diagnostic components that require follow-up. #> 7               Appendix Figure X. Local response or level-review display for unexpected responses and displacement diagnostics. #> 8              Appendix Figure X. Strict marginal diagnostic display for retained facet/category or pairwise follow-up evidence. #> 9   Figure/Appendix Figure X. Bias or differential-functioning screening display for the specified facet pair or group contrast. #> 10                      Appendix Figure X. Residual PCA scree or loading display used for exploratory residual-structure review. #> 11                       Appendix Figure X. Guttman-style person x facet-level response matrix with unexpected-response overlay. #> 12                                          Appendix Figure X. Normal Q-Q plot of person-level standardized residual aggregates. #> 13                           Appendix Figure X. Rater severity trajectory across waves under the specified anchor-linking route. #> 14                                         Appendix Figure X. Pairwise rater x rater agreement heatmap for the specified metric. #> 15     Appendix Figure X. Descriptive response-time review showing rapid and slow response-time thresholds across rating events. #> 16  Appendix Figure X. Empirical-Bayes shrinkage funnel showing raw and shrunken facet-level estimates with confidence whiskers. #>                                                                                                                             ResultsWording #> 1                 The Wright map was inspected to evaluate targeting and shared-scale overlap among persons, facet levels, and thresholds. #> 2       The pathway plot was inspected to evaluate whether expected scores and dominant-category regions progressed in the intended order. #> 3                 The category characteristic curves were inspected to evaluate the ordering and separation of fitted response categories. #> 4            The category-probability surface was used as exploratory support for understanding the fitted category-probability structure. #> 5                  The information curve was inspected to identify theta regions with relatively stronger or weaker measurement precision. #> 6                        The QC dashboard was used as a triage screen to identify components requiring more specific diagnostic follow-up. #> 7                              Unexpected-response and displacement displays were used to identify local cases or levels requiring review. #> 8                      Strict marginal displays were used as follow-up evidence for facet/category and pairwise local-dependence patterns. #> 9                Bias/DFF displays were used to screen interaction or group-functioning patterns under the documented screening threshold. #> 10                         Residual PCA displays were used as exploratory follow-up for residual structure after the main model dimension. #> 11 The Guttman scalogram was inspected as an exploratory teaching view of person x facet-level response ordering and unexpected responses. #> 12                The residual Q-Q plot was inspected as exploratory follow-up on the distribution of person-level standardized residuals. #> 13                  The rater trajectory plot was inspected, under the stated anchor-linking assumption, to screen for drift across waves. #> 14                   The pairwise agreement heatmap was inspected as a compact alternative to the bar-chart form of the interrater review. #> 15                       Response-time summaries were inspected as descriptive quality-control context outside the fitted MFRM likelihood. #> 16                         The shrinkage funnel was inspected to show which small-N facet levels moved most after empirical-Bayes pooling. #>                                                                                                        WhatNotToClaim #> 1                                                              Do not present targeting as proof of global model fit. #> 2                                   Do not treat smooth category progression as proof that the rating scale is valid. #> 3             Do not overstate overlapping curves as definitive category failure without category counts and context. #> 4  Use the surface as exploratory mfrmr output or downstream renderer input; prefer 2D CCC/pathway plots for reports. #> 5                                Do not ignore the precision tier or approximation caveats used to compute the curve. #> 6                                                            Do not cite the dashboard alone as inferential evidence. #> 7                                                 Do not interpret a single flagged case as final evidence by itself. #> 8                                                Do not treat strict marginal visuals as standalone hypothesis tests. #> 9                               Do not claim formal DIF unless the design and inferential route support that wording. #> 10                                                     Do not treat residual PCA as a standalone dimensionality test. #> 11                         Do not treat the scalogram as a global fit claim; it is a teaching-oriented ordering view. #> 12                                                              Do not treat the Q-Q plot as a formal normality test. #> 13                       Do not claim rater drift without an explicit anchor-linking route across the supplied waves. #> 14                            Do not treat agreement or correlation heatmap cells as formal reliability coefficients. #> 15       Do not treat response-time flags as speed-accuracy parameters, cheating proof, or automatic exclusion rules. #> 16                              Do not treat shrinkage movement as automatic evidence of rater quality or facet bias. #>                                                                                                      BeginnerCheck #> 1                                                   Check gaps between person density and thresholds/facet levels. #> 2                                        Check whether the dominant-category bands progress in the expected order. #> 3 before rendering. #> 5                                          Check whether the information peak covers the theta region of interest. #> 6                                                   Open the component rows or plots behind any dashboard warning. #> 7                                     Sort by magnitude and inspect repeated patterns, not isolated extremes only. #> 8 threshold. #> 10                                   Start with the scree plot, then inspect loadings only for targeted follow-up. #> 11            Check whether the overlay concentrates in a few persons/facet cells rather than spreading uniformly. #> 12           Check whether the tails depart sharply from the identity line before claiming non-Gaussian residuals. #> 13                    Confirm that the waves share an anchor or were post-hoc linked before interpreting movement. #> 14         Switch between metric = "exact" and metric = "correlation" and check that both tell a consistent story. #> 15 Start with the distribution plot, then inspect whether rapid/slow rates concentrate in persons or facet levels. #> 16            Start with the longest raw-to-shrunken segments and compare their CI width before and after pooling. #>                                                           ThreeDPolicy #> 1                      2D recommended; 3D Wright maps are discouraged. title: Write a standalone residual file — write_mfrm_residual_file
text: fit Output fit_mfrm(). diagnostics Optional output diagnose_mfrm(). Supplying avoids recomputing observation diagnostics. path Output file path. format File format: "csv" "tsv". omitted, inferred path extension .csv .tsv, otherwise "csv". digits Rounding digits numeric columns. overwrite FALSE, existing files overwritten. include_probabilities TRUE, append model probabilities response categories PrCategory_* columns.

title: Write a standalone residual file — write_mfrm_residual_file

title: Write a standalone residual file — write_mfrm_residual_file
text: exported table observation-level model-native. includes observed score, expected score, residual, standardized residual, variance, score information, observed-category probability, modeled person measure quantities available. writer separate facets_output_file_bundle() direct analysis handoff rather legacy graph/score bundle.

title: Write a standalone residual file — write_mfrm_residual_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ toy <- load_mfrmr_data("example_core") fit <- fit_mfrm(toy, "Person", c("Rater", "Criterion"), "Score",                 method = "JML", maxit = 30) diag <- diagnose_mfrm(fit, residual_pca = "none") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") out <- write_mfrm_residual_file(fit, diag, path, overwrite = TRUE) out$written_files } # }

title: mfrmr 0.2.2
text: example-execution policy now keeps R CMD check time within CRAN incoming budget (0.2.1 submission passed content checks Windows Debian tripped overall-checktime limit Windows incoming host accepted review): long-running illustrations previously wrapped \donttest now \dontrun, example pages whose executed examples measurably exceeded 0.25 seconds gated @examplesIf interactive(), keeping 129 lightweight pages plus core fit_mfrm() example executed surface. illustrations remain help pages run interactive sessions. Release metadata reviewer-facing documentation now consistently use 0.2.2 maintenance-candidate boundary: CITATION.cff, README routing, validation evidence-map references, bounded-GPCM roadmap point current 0.2.2 surface rather previous CRAN 0.2.1 baseline. README now starts public user workflow keeps reproducibility notes journal-neutral, fit_mfrm() -> mfrm_results() -> mfrm_report() -> export_mfrm_results() first-contact route. workflow vignette now uses small generated CSV artifacts representative output CRAN-style builds. Heavy fitting simulation chunks remain disabled unless NOT_CRAN=true, default vignette still shows result tables first-contact route. Reporting visualization documentation now makes publication boundary explicit: APA/report helpers provide conservative drafting scaffolds, claim-readiness caveat-routing tables, reproducible handoff files, high-stakes journal claims still require study-specific design, literature, sensitivity, substantive interpretation decisions. visual diagnostics vignette plot_data() help now show use draw = FALSE, plot_data_components(), plot_data() build custom figures retaining reference lines, guidance, interpretation metadata package-native plot. Reporting visual help now make fit-level HTML bundle route explicit: export_mfrm_bundle(fit, include = c(..., "html")) writes local HTML/CSV/replay bundle without requiring prior mfrm_results() object, preset = "monochrome" documented grayscale / print-friendly plot route. Model-comparison reporting now routed appendix/export contract: build_summary_table_bundle() accepts build_model_choice_review() objects summaries, README / reporting-vignette guidance now separates -data fit comparison, equal-weighting versus bounded-GPCM model roles, cautious wording, latent-regression reporting boundaries. pkgdown configuration now groups reference topics primary workflow, bounded-GPCM boundary, diagnostics, reporting/export, simulation/recovery, linking/bias/DFF, FACETS migration, advanced review surfaces. cran-comments.md rewritten CRAN maintenance-release note rather journal-preparation note; remaining pkgdown URL NOTE must resolved release publishing prepared pkgdown route revising release URLs.

title: mfrmr 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-12 release focuses clearer public workflow, readable reporting surface, source-aware review outputs. main user-facing entry points mfrm_results(), mfrm_report(), export_mfrm_results(), launch_mfrmr_viewer(), mfrmr_output_guide("public"). Highlights: shorter public workflow now starts fit_mfrm() -> mfrm_results() -> mfrm_report() -> export_mfrm_results(). mfrm_report() summary(mfrm_report(...)) provide first-screen report readiness tables, report routes, cautious interpretation boundaries. Simulation recovery summaries expose operating-characteristic, sparse-design, peer-review, recovery-review tables appendix export workflows. Linking, anchor, bias, misfit/pathway, network review outputs routed explicit result components scoped follow-helpers. Release-review artifacts now distinguish CRAN-time lightweight checks full non-CRAN regression evidence, readiness gate checks local R CMD check log matches target package version. Detailed changes: generated APA narrative longer labels overall fit “acceptable”/“elevated”. now reports whether mean-square fit fell within outside active screening band, names band, states band position screening evidence rather model-validity decision. Element-level wording uses screening-band language. APA narrative now separates separation reliability observed inter-rater agreement explicitly: reliability sentences state separation indices inter-rater agreement, agreement sentences introduced separate quantity. APA Methods text MML fits now states estimation basis: person measures EAP estimates, residual-based fit statistics evaluated EAP measures rather JMLE estimates. matching fit-basis caution routes external FACETS comparisons method = "JML". APA narrative Table 1 note now report measure-level confidence intervals available: Results fit/precision section states CI level, method, CIEligible counts, facet-summary table note instructs reporting CI_Lower / CI_Upper eligible rows. APA Wright-map facet-summary notes now state fitted sign convention explicitly (higher person values = higher ability; higher non-person facet values = greater severity/difficulty default negative orientation), including positive_facets exceptions, instead previous “depending facet orientation” wording. pathway-map note plot.mfrm_fit() help now state expected-score pathway display distinct Bond--Fox-style measure-versus-fit bubble chart available via plot_bubble(). MML-vs-FACETS residual basis now documented across fit surfaces: diagnose_mfrm(), facets_fit_df_guide(), facets_fit_review() (including new residual-basis guidance row interpretation-guide rows), package statistical-background help, FACETS migration vignette state MML fit statistics computed shrunken EAP person measures route JMLE-style comparisons method = "JML". Small-df ZSTD availability now documented explicit boundary: mfrmr withholds ZSTD NA applicable df falls 1 (Wilson-Hilferty instability), FACETS/Winsteps WHEXACT can report value sparse cell. fit guides facets_fit_review() now describe NA-vs-finite ZSTD pairs availability differences rather fit differences. MML person-row separation/reliability convention now documented: person rows apply separation formulas EAP measures posterior SDs, yields conservative summary lower IRT empirical-reliability convention numerically comparable JMLE-based FACETS person reliability. APA reliability sentence carries note Person row present MML. preset = "monochrome" now accepted preset-based plot helpers, including plot_fair_average(), plot_displacement(), plot_unexpected(), plot_interrater_agreement(), plot_marginal_fit(), plot_marginal_pairwise(), plot_facets_chisq(), plot_qc_dashboard(), plot_threshold_ladder(), network/secondary plot methods, fit-family plots. assess_mfrm_recovery() now reports uncertainty evidence separately recovery metrics. makes unavailable SE/coverage evidence visible without turning implied RMSE bias failure. inst/validation/release-readiness.R now follows target package version DESCRIPTION, selects versioned evidence-map/checklist files available, reports stale R CMD check logs package-check review items check-log package version match target release. release-readiness GPCM scope review now also works installed package: R/ capability-matrix source file present (example inside R CMD check runs CI artifact reviews), reads gpcm_capability_matrix() gpcm_runtime_guard_coverage() installed namespace instead reporting missing-source concern. precision_review_report() now includes source-grounded fit/separation basis table. keeps mean-square fit, ZSTD standardization, Rasch/FACETS-style separation, package QC thresholds separate reporting lanes. New mfrm_results() gives users FACETS-style first-screen entry point: accepts existing fit, run_mfrm_facets() object, standard long-format data frame, runs diagnostics automatically, gathers available tables/reviews/plot routes, can emit lightweight temporary HTML report. summary includes next-action routes replay-code scaffold. Existing table, report, analysis, review helpers remain supported detailed components. mfrm_results_interactive() adds explicit opt-column-selection wizard interactive sessions . summary(mfrm_results(...)) now includes triage table orders unavailable, review, information, OK signals across diagnostics, plots, tables, precision/reliability, reporting, model scope, network review surfaces. next_actions uses triage layer first-screen routing. mfrmr_output_guide("public") now gives shortest top-level public API map: explicit fit, comprehensive results, report readiness, optional viewer, download export, scoped guide, opt-interactive routes. guide also carries APILayer, ObjectRole, DecisionBoundary columns top-level public surfaces, specialist follow-ups, advanced design-review rows, migration/integration routes separated users scan broader namespace, users can see whether route estimates, summarizes, displays, exports, points next helper. mfrmr_output_guide("entry") still gives first-screen creation routes, including explicit-fit, comprehensive-results, purpose-specific guide, opt-interactive routes. guide labels lifecycle, user level, recommended-entry status, includes advanced simulation/network route rows. README now starts shorter public-surface workflow: fit_mfrm() -> mfrm_results() -> mfrm_report() -> export_mfrm_results(). Detailed table, report, review, export helpers presented scoped follow-ups rather functions memorize first analysis. mfrmr_output_guide("viewer") now maps local Shiny viewer workflows back mfrm_results(include = ...) object created first, including publication, validation, bias-screen, pathway/misfit, combined review routes. plot.mfrm_diagnostic_screening() now gives diagnostic-screening simulation output integrated visualization route. default overview combines legacy ZSTD, strict marginal, strict pairwise, strict combined, optional report-review rates; focused views cover report signals, scenario contrasts, runtime summaries. draw-free return mfrm_plot_data object, plot_data(diag_eval, type = "overview", component = "plot_long") can used directly ggplot2, plotly, Quarto, custom export workflows. Draw-free diagnostic-screening plot objects also carry overview, reading_order, next_actions, reporting_notes, figure_recipes, keeping custom figure/report handoffs aligned interpretation boundaries used summary(diag_eval). summary(evaluate_mfrm_diagnostic_screening(...)) now provides explicit diagnostic-screening report surface, build_summary_table_bundle() / export_summary_appendix() can export scenario, performance, report-signal, contrast, draw-free plot-data tables. Diagnostic-screening summaries now include reading_order, next_actions, reporting_notes, figure_recipes tables users can separate first-read tables, follow-actions, figure/caption planning, appendix-tables, operating-characteristic claims, validation-gate boundaries writing report text. mfrmr_output_guide("simulation") now routes users diagnostic-screening evaluation appendix export alongside data generation, design/recovery evaluation, network review, peer-review design review. mfrm_results(include = ...) now accepts purpose presets: "publication", "validation", "facets", "bias", "misfit_review", "network", "gpcm_review" addition "standard" "". bias preset surfaces facet-level bias-screen guidance leaving interaction-bias facet-pair selection explicit; misfit preset bundles unexpected responses, displacement review, pathway-map fit annotations. mfrmr_output_guide("binary") README now make ordinary person-item dichotomous route explicit: pass item column single non-person facet use model = "RSM"; exactly two ordered categories usual binary Rasch logit centering threshold-identification conventions. New launch_mfrmr_viewer() provides optional local Shiny reader existing mfrm_results objects. estimate models, read external web applications, change diagnostics; displays already-built overview, triage, status, QC evidence, APA-style report text available, bias screens, pathway/misfit review, tables, plots, replay code. QC, Report, Bias, Pathway/Misfit tabs now include local section-status tables unavailable -requested sections visible point inspection. New export_mfrm_results() writes lightweight download folder existing mfrm_results object: summary CSVs, collected tables, HTML, RDS, replay code, written-files manifest, optional PNG plot export best-effort zip creation. compact result-object handoff route; export_mfrm_bundle() remains broader fit-centered analysis archive. export_mfrm_results() now accepts include = "report" write mfrm_report() artifacts download folder: report-table CSVs report_index, evidence summaries, reporting templates, plus report Markdown report HTML. New mfrm_report() turns existing mfrm_results object report-ready QC, APA, validation, reviewer, technical section plans claim-readiness, report-gap, evidence-boundary, next-action tables. reporting layer existing results, new estimator, diagnostic, acceptance rule. mfrm_report() now includes first_screen, FACETS-like entry table overall row one row per major evidence area. reports status, report summary: overview status, first-screen rows, immediate actions, optional -requested sections, claim-readiness counts, report gaps, boundary rows, standard routes. keeps comprehensive report tables inspect next. keeps expanded report surface navigable without hiding detailed tables. mfrm_report() report_index now also includes explicit evidence, template, plot, export, EvidenceRoute, BoundaryType, ClaimStrength, RecommendedUse. makes template wording easier trace back source table helps keep descriptive, caveated, follow--claims separate. mfrm_report() now includes template_index, stacked template index fit, precision, bias, misfit/pathway, linking/anchor reporting templates. lets users review boundary type, claim strength, recommended use, evidence route reliability, strata, precision tier, review/warn checks keeping Rasch/FACETS-style separation reliability distinct inter-rater agreement, model fit, standalone validity evidence. mfrm_report() now fairness conclusions separated screen-positive rows reported final fairness invariance decisions. mfrm_report() now includes misfit/pathway reporting surfaces source result built include = "misfit_review": misfit_evidence_summary misfit_reporting_templates. keep unexpected-response rows, displacement review, pathway-map evidence, case-review wording separate local misfit prompts reported automatic exclusion, fairness, validity decisions. mfrm_report() now includes linking/anchor reporting surfaces source result built include = "linking": linking_evidence_summary linking_reporting_templates. keep anchor readiness, drift review, screened equating-chain review, GPCM support boundaries separate anchor evidence reported automatic drift absence, completed equating, DFF support, validity proof. mfrm_results(include = "linking") now adds fitted object’s stored anchor-review evidence operational linking-readiness surface comprehensive results object. exposes plot(res, type = "anchors") anchor-readiness visualization routes drift/equating follow-explicit multi-fit calls detect_anchor_drift() build_equating_chain(). Recovery review can now retain fit/separation operating characteristics diagnostic context. summaries help users inspect MnSq, ZSTD, separation, reliability behavior without making top-line recovery gates. Recovery assessment validation summaries also expose diagnostic_reporting_notes zero separation/reliability ZSTD sensitivity routed report caveats rather recovery release decisions. assess_mfrm_recovery() now includes condition_review condition_reporting_notes recovery simulations. bounded GPCM, tables separate slope-regime context generated score-category support recovery metrics users interpret stress cases. Bounded-GPCM simulation specifications now carry slope_regime metadata. labels documented package recovery-review labels, literature-derived fit adequacy cut points. optional recovery-validation summary now separates core release recovery decision extended sensitivity evidence. keeps stress cases visible without treating top-line release failures default. build_summary_table_bundle() can now convert recovery-validation summaries appendix-ready tables, including top-line decision, case, condition, diagnostic reporting tables. export_summary_appendix() accepts recovery-validation summaries CSV/HTML appendix handoff, export_mfrm_bundle(summary_tables = ...) can co-locate tables fit-based export bundle. export_summary_appendix() export_mfrm_bundle(summary_tables = ...) now accept person-fit summary objects consistently build_summary_table_bundle(). precision_review_report() can now sent build_summary_table_bundle() appendix/export helpers. fit_separation_basis table remains precision-review surface fit, ZSTD, separation/reliability/strata, QC thresholds mistaken release recovery success gates. fit_measures_table() facets_fit_review() summaries can now sent appendix/export helpers. df/ZSTD sensitivity optional external FACETS matching tables stay separate MnSq fit status top-line validation decisions. reporting_checklist() now includes Global Fit row fit/separation reporting boundary, pointing users precision_review_report(), fit_measures_table(), facets_fit_review() drafting fit, ZSTD, separation, reliability claims. New observed-data resampling helpers, build_mfrm_resampling_spec() draw_mfrm_resamples(), create person-clustered stratified subsample bootstrap inputs manifest tables stratum representation rater/facet coverage. helpers explicitly framed stability reproducibility inputs full-data reference, true-parameter recovery evidence. build_mfrm_sim_spec() simulate_mfrm_data() now support assignment = "sparse_linked" planned-missing sparse rating designs. generated data retain sparse-design metadata design density, planned missingness, rater coverage, rater-pair common-person links. evaluate_mfrm_design() evaluate_mfrm_recovery() now carry sparse linked generators directly, including run-level summary columns planned missingness rater-pair linking diagnostics. build_summary_table_bundle() now separates sparse linked diagnostics appendix-ready sparse_design tables design-evaluation recovery-simulation outputs, planned missingness rater linkage buried inside performance recovery metrics. table also labels zero common-person rater pairs requested-link target shortfalls design-review issues, recovery failures. summary(evaluate_mfrm_design(...)), summary(evaluate_mfrm_recovery(...)), build_summary_table_bundle() now include compact sparse_review table sparse linked designs active. plot.mfrm_design_evaluation() also accepts sparse-design metrics plannedmissingrate, mincommonpersons, zerocommonpairs, pairsshorttarget. New build_mfrm_network_review() synthesizes existing design-network analysis reportable review surface: connectedness, articulation points, bridge edges, facet-level vulnerability, optional sparse-linking diagnostics, reporting map keeps network evidence separate MFRM estimates, fit, separation, recovery gates. Generated data carry peer-review design metadata assignment density, reviewer load, reciprocal review pairs, common submissions per reviewer pair. build_peer_review_design_review() turns metadata appendix-ready assignment diagnostics, build_mfrm_network_review() can include metadata alongside graph connectedness review. Recovery assessment validation summaries now expose reading_order table users can find next table inspect without opening plot data.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-16 Documentation accuracy pass plus research-grounded visualization GPCM bias-inference refinements. Documentation, citations, band attributions corrected primary sources, mathematical screening-SE corrections, Snijders-corrected person-fit reporting assumptions met, clearer plot data review. release keeps 0.1.6 defaults, infrastructure polish release. Public review helpers consolidated *_review* names documented , former *_audit* public spellings, S3 compatibility classes, duplicate top-level fields removed deliberate breaking cleanup.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: users, main changes 0.2.0 : defensible mathematics inference: RSM/PCM/GPCM reductions, GPCM slope handling, fit df/ZSTD conventions, Snijders-corrected person fit, information curves, recovery simulations, score-support edge cases now covered explicit regression tests. Clearer FACETS relationship: mfrmr positioned package-native MFRM workflow FACETS-style tables, review helpers, migration routes, promise numerically reproduce every FACETS estimate. Better user-facing diagnostics: fit-measure tables, data-quality reports, category curves, residual-PCA follow-, person-fit summaries, recovery checks, reporting bundles now expose structured tables asking users interpret plots console output. R-first visualization access: plot helpers increasingly return reusable draw = FALSE data, long-form plot tables, annotations, style metadata users can rebuild figures ggplot2, plotly, Quarto, reporting workflows. Quieter, reproducible workflows: routine preparation rating-range messages stored fit/data objects opt-console; long-running design evaluation shows progress interactive runs default. Breaking changes 0.2.0 intentional concentrated around public naming clarity: former exported *_audit* helper names compatibility S3 classes removed favour canonical *_review* surface. Model defaults 0.1.6 retained. detailed notes organized follows: user pathways, output contracts, visualization, naming changes; mathematical/statistical corrections regression-test coverage; recovery simulation validation workflow; citation/documentation corrections; smaller feature additions, bug fixes, build hygiene, deferred work.

what: User pathways, output contracts, and terminology
title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: FACETS positioning guide: new facets_positioning_guide() makes package boundary explicit reports migration notes. mfrmr presented FACETS numerical clone: estimates remain package-native unless external FACETS output supplied comparison, FACETS-style wrappers, coverage tables, output files serve transition, handoff, report-organization purposes. Report-ready FACETS relationship wording: reporting_checklist() summary(reporting_checklist(...)) now carry facets_positioning table Quarto, appendix, handoff workflows can quote boundary language used facets_positioning_guide(). build_summary_table_bundle() includes table facets_relationship_wording. FACETS output-contract review naming: facets_output_contract_review() now sole public helper FACETS-style output-contract review. returned bundle class mfrm_facets_contract_review; helper checks package output columns derived metric consistency FACETS-style contract, claim numerical FACETS equivalence. Public result components use column_review metric_checks, older bookkeeping labels. FACETS pathway bias Wright maps: mfrmr_output_guide("facets") now explicitly routes FACETS users Table 14-style bias/interactions via estimate_bias(), bias_interaction_report(), bias_pairwise_report(), plot_bias_interaction(), variable-map review via plot(fit, type = "wright"), plot_wright_unified(), plot_data(type = "wright"). FACETS pathway anchors category outputs: mfrmr_output_guide("facets") now also exposes direct/group anchor routes review_mfrm_anchors(), make_anchor_table(), fit_mfrm(anchors = ..., group_anchors = ...), drift/linking review detect_anchor_drift() plot_anchor_drift(), FACETS-style category fair-average routes rating_scale_table(), category_structure_report(), category_curves_report(), fair_average_table(). Standalone residual subset file writers: new write_mfrm_residual_file() writes observation-level observed, expected, residual, standardized residual, score-information, optional category probability columns CSV/TSV. New write_mfrm_subset_file() writes connected-subset summaries plus node-membership files external linking review, without forcing users legacy graph/score output bundle. Category-specific information curves: category_curves_report() now includes category_information table plot(..., type = "category_information"). Category contributions use ^2 P_k(theta) (k - E[X | theta])^2 sum total curve information theta value; PCM/RSM reduces unit-slope form. Cumulative probability curves: category_curves_report() now also includes cumulative_probabilities cumulative_boundaries, plot(..., type = "cumulative") FACETS/Winsteps-style accumulated category-probability view. P(X <= k) flipped P(X >= k) directions returned; boundary rows report approximate theta values P(X <= k) = .5, crossing status columns --range multiple-crossing boundaries -interpreted. Category-curve overview plot: plot(category_curves_report(...)) now defaults overview panel shows category probabilities, cumulative probabilities, total information, category-specific information together. Existing focused views remain available type = "ogive", "ccc", "cumulative", "information", "category_information". plot now also supports preset = "monochrome" line-type separation explicit cumulative .5 boundary-line control boundary_status = "in_range", "", "none". plot_data(..., component = "plot_long") returns ggplot2/plotly-friendly long table spanning ogive, category-probability, cumulative-probability, total-information, category-specific-information series, curve_style carrying resolved color/line-type mapping. Quieter rating-range provenance: fit_mfrm() longer emits informational message routine observed-score range inference default. provenance retained fit$prep, summary(fit)$settings_overview, describe_mfrm_data() users can still tell whether rating_min / rating_max declared inferred. Set options(mfrmr.show_inferred_rating_range = TRUE) restore one-time message interactive checks. Structured data-preparation notes: prepare_mfrm_data() now stores row retention preparation notes fit$prep$row_retention fit$prep$preparation_notes. Row drops, whitespace trimming, duplicate person--facet cells, single-level facets therefore available summary(fit) summary(describe_mfrm_data(...)) instead existing transient console messages. Routine row-drop, trim, single-level-facet messages quiet default; set options(mfrmr.show_preparation_messages = TRUE) show interactive checks. Fit-annotated pathway plot data: plot(fit, type = "pathway", draw = FALSE) now returns R-friendly pathway_long pathway_annotations tables alongside fit_measures, fit_status, curve_fit_status, fit_measure_status. lets users rebuild FACETS-style pathway maps ggplot2, plotly, Quarto retaining underfit/overfit labels used fit_measures_table(). R-first plot-data contracts bias information plots: plot_bias_interaction(..., draw = FALSE) now exposes plot_long, plot_annotations, flag_summary, plot_settings across scatter, ranked, heatmap, facet-profile views. compute_information() now stores conditional_sem, information_long, precision/SEM summary, plot_information(type = "sem") / "csem" supported aliases conditional standard-error--measurement curve. Category-probability plot aliases annotations: plot(category_curves_report(...), type = "category_probability") type = "conditional_probability" now route category-probability curves type = "ccc", matching FACETS/Winsteps terminology without changing underlying probability data. Draw-free plot data now include plot_annotations curve_summary alongside plot_long, curve_style, boundary_lines, plot_settings. FACETS feature coverage matrix: new facets_feature_coverage() gives public, release-scoped map FACETS 64-bit output index current mfrmr routes, separating implemented, partial, not_implemented, not_targeted surfaces. makes unsupported FACETS-specific outputs Winsteps control-file export, raw FACETS report parsing, arbitrary Web/Excel menu plots explicit rather implicit. G-study / D-study planning route: new mfrm_d_study() extends mfrm_generalizability() observed variance-component review planned design comparison. reports projected G Phi alternative numbers raters, criteria, random measurement facets, exposes residual-scaling sensitivity assumptions simplified G-study residuals silently -interpreted. plot(mfrm_d_study(...), draw = FALSE) plot_data() expose reusable coefficient/error-variance series custom design-planning visuals. plot.mfrm_d_study() now supports line plots group_var, ggplot2-like panel_by / panel_grid small multiples, two-axis heatmap / contour views rater--task design grids. optional surface3d view available exploration, heatmap/contour remain recommended reporting displays. Plot data labels coefficients MetricFamily = "G-theory" confused IRT classical-test-theory reliability coefficients. Connectivity network visualization: subset_connectivity_report() now includes reusable node/edge tables, plot(..., type = "network") provides igraph-based co-observation graph igraph installed. draw = FALSE, returned plot data supports custom R visualization without depending base plotting default. MFRM design-network analysis: new mfrm_network_analysis() treats person/facet-level observation design undirected weighted co-observation graph returns graph-level connectedness, node degree strength, betweenness/closeness, articulation points, bridge edges, facet-level vulnerability summaries. explicitly framed design linking diagnostics, person ability, rater quality, model-fit statistics. plot(..., type = "centrality"), plot(..., type = "facet_summary"), plot(..., type = "network") provide immediate visual checks draw-free plot data. Rater-effect network analysis: new rater_network_analysis() adds Lamprianou-style pairwise rater network route separate design connectedness. supports agreement, disagreement, directed severity-direction networks, returns rater-level /strength, betweenness/closeness, finite network severity index, retained edge tables, pairwise metrics used thresholding. help page states indices descriptive network diagnostics rather Rasch logit estimates formal fit statistics. plot(..., type = "network"), "severity", "centrality", "matrix" provide immediate visual checks reusable plot data. Halo-effect network screening: new rater_halo_network_analysis() reshapes observed ratings rater--criterion nodes, computes Spearman/Pearson/Kendall node-pair correlations, labels -rater cross-criterion edges halo, contrasts halo-edge weights non-halo edges. default Bonferroni-adjusted edge filter follows conservative network-screening strategy used Lamprianou’s halo example. returned bundle includes summary, node_metrics, edge_metrics, pair_metrics, halo_summary_by_rater, caveats Welch halo/non-halo comparison descriptive network edges dependent. halo_summary_by_rater now includes ReviewStatus ReviewReason based -rater cross-criterion mean weight, incident non-halo comparison weight, retained halo-edge count, labels framed screening priorities rather causal halo diagnoses. plot(..., type = "edge_distribution"), "halo_summary", "matrix", "network" provide immediate visual review draw-free plot data. Fit-measures review table: new fit_measures_table() gives direct FACETS-style fit-measure view raters, criteria, facet elements. returns R-friendly columns facets_table labels Infit MnSq, Outfit ZStd, Fit Status, Review Reason; underfit, overfit, mixed subsets included immediate review. Fit-threshold sensitivity summaries: fit_measures_table() now returns profile_summary_by_facet profile_summary_overall, reporting underfit, overfit, mixed, -flag rates multiple literature-based MnSq bands Linacre, Bond & Fox, Wright & Linacre. main table still uses active review band, threshold_profiles controls whether literature, active, , profile summaries returned. Fit-measure CI display: fit_measures_table(ci_level = ...) now adds approximate measure confidence intervals R-friendly table facets_table. plot(fit_measures, type = "measure_ci", ci_level = ..., preset = "monochrome") draws interval plot requested confidence level. FACETS df/ZSTD guide: new facets_fit_df_guide() documents engine-vs-FACETS-style degrees--freedom distinction MnSq--ZSTD transformation workflow. fit_measures_table(fit_df_method = "") now exposes primary df plus FACETS-style companion df/ZSTD columns, adds df_sensitivity, df_sensitive, df_sensitivity_summary users can identify rows whose |ZSTD| flag status interpretation convention-sensitive. df-sensitivity screen exposes explicit df_zstd_tolerance, df_zstd_large_shift, df_ratio_tolerance settings FACETS-style reviews can reproduced stricter permissive rules. plot(fit_measures, type = "df_sensitivity") visualizes largest engine-vs-FACETS-style ZSTD shifts. facets_fit_review() now uses row-level df-sensitivity engine returns df_sensitivity, df_sensitive, df_sensitivity_summary; former internal_comparison component removed keep public output vocabulary consistent. External FACETS ZSTD tolerance now named external_zstd_tolerance, separating external-table comparison engine-vs-FACETS-style df sensitivity. plot(..., type = "df_sensitivity") route available facets_fit_review() bundles. Data quality score-support review: data_quality_report() now keeps full fitted score support category_counts, adds score_support_review, surfaces caveats zero-frequency categories. Intermediate gaps declared 1-5 scale observed 1, 2, 4, 5 flagged either retained zero-count categories (keep_original = TRUE) original-label gaps hidden internal recoding (keep_original = FALSE). plot(data_quality_report(...), type = "score_support") highlights categories, preset = "monochrome" available. Facet-level category-usage QC: data_quality_report() now adds category_usage_by_facet category_usage_summary, covering every fitted facet level crossed retained score support. flags local zero sparse category use, rater never uses middle category even category appears elsewhere data. plot(data_quality_report(...), type = "facet_category_usage") provides quick view affected facet levels. Data quality dashboard: plot(data_quality_report(...), type = "dashboard") now combines row review, score-support category use, facet-level category-use issues, missing/invalid row counts one base-R view. draw = FALSE, returned plot data contains four panel tables report handoff. Data quality flags: data_quality_report() now includes quality_flags, prioritized QC table summarizes row exclusions, unknown design levels, score-support gaps, facet-level category-use cautions, restricted facet response patterns counts next actions. summary(data_quality_report(...)) previews table priority flag present. Facet response-pattern QC: data_quality_report() now adds facet_response_patterns, flags facet levels use one score category, assign one category least configured dominant-category cutoff, use boundary categories. catches cases rater assigning score 1 responses 1-5 scale. Data quality overview score map plots: data_quality_report() now adds quality_overview, compact area-level status table rows, score support, facet-level category use, facet response patterns, design matching. plot(..., type = "quality_flags") summarizes priority QC flags area, plot(..., type = "facet_response_patterns") shows dominant local category use facet level, plot(..., type = "score_map") shows original--internal score mappings labels recoded. User-pathway plot-data access: mfrmr_output_guide("facets"), mfrmr_output_guide("conquest"), mfrmr_output_guide("r") now give focused starting points users arriving FACETS, ConQuest, R-first visualization workflows. New plot_data() extracts full reusable plot-data list, one named component, mfrm_plot_data object mfrmr plot helper supports draw = FALSE. New plot_data_components() lists reusable plot-data component, shape, role, accessor call, custom-graphics notes users can discover plot_long, annotation, settings, style, review tables without reading underlying list structure. Monochrome plot preset: plot helpers use package visual preset system now accept preset = "monochrome". Color remains default ("standard"), monochrome supports print-oriented figures color-independent review. Interval-aware visualization guide: new mfrmr_interval_guide() maps public 95% CI / uncertainty routes across fit-measure tables, Wright maps, fair averages, bias screens, displacement, DFF/DIF summaries, anchor drift, rater severity profiles, rater trajectories, manuscript Figure 1 composites, shrinkage, ICC review. guide records interval basis interpretation boundary CI displays remain precision screening evidence rather automatic fit, fairness, validity decisions. Searchable help concepts: high-level route guides CI-capable plot / table helpers now carry Rd concept tags confidence intervals, visual diagnostics, reporting workflow, route selection, GPCM boundaries. makes help.search() useful finding right help page users know function name. Shrinkage CI plot data: plot_shrinkage_funnel(show_ci = TRUE, ci_level = ...) now draws approximate raw shrunken estimate whiskers returns RawCI_Lower, RawCI_Upper, ShrunkCI_Lower, ShrunkCI_Upper, CI_Level downstream graphics. plot(fit, type = "shrinkage") now uses requested ci_level CI whiskers instead fixed 95% level. Shrinkage figure guidance: visual_reporting_template() visual diagnostics vignette now include empirical-Bayes shrinkage funnel route, caption skeleton, beginner check, interpretation guardrails users can report shrinkage movement without treating automatic rater-quality, bias, validity evidence. Response-time diagnostic layer: new response_time_review() plot_response_time_review() summarize response-time metadata outside fitted MFRM likelihood. review returns rapid/slow thresholds, event-level flags, person/facet/score summaries, grouped plot data timing patterns can reviewed descriptive QC rather joint speed-accuracy parameters automatic exclusion rules. mfrmr_output_guide("response_time") R-user pathway now expose route alongside review reusable plot-data helpers. mfrm_results(include = "response_time", response_time = ..., response_time_data = ...) can now carry descriptive review first-screen result object, summary(res)$next_actions, plot(res, type = "response_time"), local viewer payload, export_mfrm_results() table exports without changing fitted MFRM estimates. Bounded GPCM boundary text: README, vignettes, help pages, unsupported-path messages now state current GPCM scope consistently: direct data generation, parameter recovery, fair averages, bias screening, summary-table bundles, appendix export, caveated APA/QC/export bundles, exploratory linking review available within documented caveats. Role-based design evaluation population forecasting also available caveated bounded-GPCM sensitivity evidence requested design preserves simulation specification’s slope structure. Role-based diagnostic signal-detection design screening available caveated slope-aware operating-characteristic evidence gpcm_boundary output. Full FACETS-style score-side contract review score-side equivalence, posterior predictive checks, heavy backend routes remain outside validated route. validation artifacts now include bounded-GPCM roadmap supported_with_caveat, blocked, deferred rows tracked explicit release-scope decisions instead implicit support gaps. GPCM score-side boundary checks: blocked bounded-GPCM score-side helpers now stop producing partial outputs. facets_output_file_bundle() facets_output_contract_review() help pages also state graph package-native scorefile output caveated bounded-GPCM routes full FACETS output-contract review remains outside bounded-GPCM boundary. gpcm_score_side_contract() records estimand, uncertainty, reduction-test, schema, guard, release-wording requirements separate caveated scorefile output full FACETS-style score-side review. GPCM route guidance: gpcm_capability_matrix() now includes RecommendedRoute NextValidationStep columns, supported, caveated, blocked, deferred helper family states current substitute workflow validation evidence needed boundary can move. GPCM --scope route guidance: blocked deferred bounded-GPCM routes now report matching capability-matrix row, recommended substitute route, next validation step returning error. errors carry class mfrmr_gpcm_scope_error helper, area, status, recommended-route, next-validation-step fields programmatic handling. GPCM route-boundary coverage: capability-matrix tests validation review now verify every blocked deferred bounded-GPCM row represented structured stop condition explicit future-scope entry, messages stay synchronized matrix’s area, status, route, next-validation-step fields. gpcm_runtime_guard_coverage() exported public table route-boundary check. mfrmr_output_guide("gpcm") now points users gpcm_capability_matrix() coverage table. GPCM release-readiness alignment: inst/validation/release-readiness.R now checks blocked deferred gpcm_capability_matrix() rows non-empty route guidance, represented installed bounded-GPCM scope notes, covered future-scope rows release-evidence checklist. User-facing wording: public visualization reporting documentation now uses “plot data”, “surface data”, “data handoff” instead implementation terminology possible, retaining actual field names plot_payloads users need access . RSM/PCM wording review: package-level, fitting, information, bias, export docs now distinguish equal-weighting RSM / PCM reference route broader ordered-response model surface. Stale “Rasch-” “legacy-compatible” labels narrowed described helpers now also serve bounded GPCM direct summary-table workflows. Model-choice user guide: README GPCM/MML vignettes now give user-facing guidance choosing RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM, including report wording templates warning better GPCM fit sensitivity evidence rather automatic operational-scoring decision. documentation terminology regression test now guards reintroducing stale Rasch-phrasing removed pass. Fit-level model-choice review: new build_model_choice_review() bundles compare_mfrm(), model-role guidance, downstream route availability, report wording templates, bounded-GPCM support matrix, optional build_weighting_review() run users can review RSM / PCM / bounded-GPCM candidates actual fitted objects. Review-name migration completed breaking cleanup: review_mfrm_anchors(), precision_review_report(), review_conquest_overlap(), facet_small_sample_review(), build_weighting_review() now exported review-name implementations. former public *_audit* function spellings, S3 compatibility classes, old public component names orientation_audit, nesting_audit, hierarchical_audit, shrinkage_audit removed renamed rather shown user-facing migration artifacts. Compatibility registry narrowed: compatibility_alias_table() now lists retained compatibility names remain part public package surface, mfrmRFacets, analyze_dif, JMLE, long-standing output column aliases. longer advertises removed review-name migration artifacts. Public output review wording: linking-review tables plot routes now use anchor_review labels user-facing source metadata, model-choice raw objects summaries expose weighting_review_status / weighting_review, diagnostics summary-table bundles export precision_review without old duplicate table. Bias reports now expose orientation_review; model-comparison output uses nesting_review; reproducibility manifests use hierarchical_review shrinkage_review. DFF subgroup refit rows now record anchor-review notes LinkingReview. Review component accessors: new anchor_review() precision_review() helpers provide stable route package-native review components. intentionally read canonical *_review fields. Reference review naming: reference_case_review() now canonical package-native report-completeness helper, reporting-checklist / cheatsheet wording now uses review labels hierarchical complete-case follow-items. Review-wording guardrail: current public guides generated help now avoid exposing audit user-facing package concept. Data-quality row-status output uses row_review; prediction provenance uses row_review / population_review; bias, model-comparison, manifest components use *_review names; ordinary user-facing guidance uses review/check/traceability terminology. Output helper guide: new mfrmr_output_guide() gives users compact purpose--helper map choosing among *_table, *_report, *_review, *_bundle, export_*, compatibility routes. compatibility guide now also states old *_audit helper component names part 0.2.0 public surface.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: Identified step/threshold parameterization: RSM, PCM, bounded GPCM now optimize step/threshold profiles correct sum--zero degrees freedom (steps - 1 per profile). Earlier pre-release implementations centered step vector optimization still left centered-away null direction optimizer, AIC/BIC parameter count, Hessian. point-estimate scale unchanged; likelihood parameter count observed-information basis now aligned stated identification constraint. MML joint covariance layer structural parameters: diagnose_mfrm() now reuses one observed-information covariance non-person facet SEs exposes covariance basis step/threshold bounded-GPCM slope uncertainty diagnostics$parameter_uncertainty. Step rows get SE, normal CI_Lower / CI_Upper, covariance status metadata. GPCM slope rows get log-slope SEs plus positive-scale delta-method SEs log-normal confidence limits. fit_mfrm(..., attach_diagnostics = TRUE) attaches structural SE columns fit$steps fit$slopes MML Hessian available. Measure-level CI contract: diagnose_mfrm()$measures now records CI_Level = 0.95 CI_Method = "Normal approximation" alongside CI_Lower / CI_Upper. interval calculation uses qnorm(0.975) rather rounded multiplier, row-level CIEligible, CIBasis, CIUse continue distinguish primary reporting intervals review screening approximations. Weighted BIC transparency: compare_mfrm() now reports WeightedN, ICSampleSize, ICSampleSizeBasis comparison table. makes BIC penalty basis explicit: ordinary fits use row count, weighted fits use sum weights already used fitted model summary. Residual-PCA boundary handling: exploratory residual-PCA helpers now capture non-fatal PCA-engine warnings inside returned PCA bundle instead emitting loose warnings diagnostics plotting. Degenerate residual-correlation conditions therefore remain visible review without looking like confirmatory test failures. Residual-PCA parallel analysis: analyze_residual_pca() now supports parallel = TRUE residual-permutation parallel analysis. null comparison permutes standardized residuals within residual columns, preserving column distributions missingness breaking residual association. PCA tables gain ParallelMean, ParallelCutoff, ExcessOverParallelCutoff, ExceedsParallelCutoff, parallel_status records availability successful permutation counts. plot_residual_pca() adds parallel_scree parallel_excess views. reported exploratory follow-evidence dimensionality review, standalone proof unidimensionality multidimensionality. GPCM fair-average structural SEs: fair_average_table(fair_se = TRUE) now adds opt-structural delta-method SE CI columns bounded GPCM fair averages MML observed-information covariance available. original SE / Model S.E. / Real S.E. columns keep measure-SE meaning; fair-average uncertainty exposed distinct columns Fair(M) S.E., AdjustedAverageSE, AdjustedAverageCI_Lower / AdjustedAverageCI_Upper. Person rows remain unavailable MML person EAP estimates conditioned rather included structural Hessian. summary(fair_average_table(...)) print method now surface whether fair-average SEs requested, many rows available, resulting status mix. plot_fair_average(show_ci = TRUE) uses columns automatically bounded-GPCM fit objects. GPCM expected-score consistency: internal expected_score_table() route now uses response-probability bundle diagnostics category-count calculations, bounded GPCM expected scores respect fitted slope parameters instead falling PCM kernel. Fair-average documentation now also states non-slope-facet rows use identification-based reporting convention, FACETS score-side equivalence claim. GPCM invalid-slope guard: low-level GPCM expected-score helper longer treats non-finite, zero, negative slopes slope = 1. returns unavailable expected scores instead, malformed bounded-GPCM object silently become PCM-style calculation fair-average internals. internal iteration-state replay helper also now explicit GPCM probability-kernel branch, avoiding latent PCM fallback route later moved inside supported GPCM boundary. RSM--PCM reduction checks: test suite now pins RSM special case common-threshold PCM. identical common thresholds, RSM PCM must agree category probabilities, unweighted weighted log-likelihoods, response-bundle diagnostics, generated simulation data, reconstructed simulation probabilities. public compare_mfrm(..., nested = TRUE) path now also regression check reported LRT degrees freedom equal identified RSM--PCM step-structure difference. Boundary-safe LRT reporting: compare_mfrm(..., nested = TRUE) now records comparison_basis$lrt_status comparison_basis$lrt_reason. Non-finite log-likelihoods, equal parameter counts, unsupported nesting, negative likelihood-ratio statistics longer fail silently imply model-choice conclusion; LRT withheld print/summary path states reported. GPCM slope-scale consistency: GPCM simulation specifications now treat supplied slopes relative discriminations normalize geometric-mean-one log-slope identification used fit_mfrm(). Recovery summaries compare identified log slopes without additional mean-alignment step, absolute slope-scale bias hidden recovery table. PCM reduction check GPCM simulation: simulation tests now pin special case bounded GPCM unit slopes. simulation specification, seed, step-facet thresholds, generated visible data reconstructed category-probability matrix must match PCM numerical tolerance. guards intended mathematical reduction without implying freely estimated GPCM fit equal PCM fit. PCM reduction check downstream diagnostics: unit-slope GPCM reduction now also tested response-probability bundle layer. bundle used expected scores, variance-based fit diagnostics, information calculations must match PCM bundle probabilities, expected category scores, score variances, fourth central moments, score information. companion sensitivity check verifies non-unit slopes move quantities away PCM values, GPCM path neither hidden PCM fallback unconstrained divergence. Mathematical consistency regression tests: test suite now pins probability, expectation, variance, fourth-moment, information, conditional-SEM identities across low-level bounded-GPCM response bundle, RSM / PCM / bounded-GPCM category-curve reports, draw-free CCC/pathway plot data, compute_information(). checks also require facet-level information-contribution curves aggregate back total information curve. guards user-visible visualization reporting tables drifting away probability kernels. Fit-measure consistency regression tests: test suite now verifies fit_measures_table() preserves documented df/ZSTD formulas, confidence-interval formulas, active fit-status labels, threshold-profile counts rates, df-sensitivity status taxonomy, draw-free measure_ci / df_sensitivity plot data. pins FACETS-style reporting surface row-level calculations users see returned tables. Data-quality consistency regression tests: test suite now verifies data_quality_report() summary counts recomputable returned detail tables, quality_flags quality_overview summarize QC evidence, draw-free quality_flags, facet_category_usage, facet_response_patterns, score_map plot data preserve score-support gaps, facet-level category-use issues, restricted rater response patterns, original-label gaps hidden score recoding. GPCM bias SE (estimate_bias()): conditional plug-SE additive bias shift now uses correct GPCM information (_i a_i^2 (X_i)). previous pre-release implementation optimized point estimate slope-aware GPCM kernel used PCM information (_i (X_i)) S.E. / t / Prob.. review label remains "screening". FACETS-style fit ZSTD df layer: diagnose_mfrm() now accepts fit_df_method = "engine", "facets", "". default keeps existing package-native df convention (DF_Infit = sum(Var * Weight), DF_Outfit = sum(Weight)). FACETS path adds Wright-Masters/FACETS fourth-moment df approximation (df = 2 / q^2) caps FACETS-style ZSTD values +/-9. Use fit_df_method = "" comparing mfrmr fit flags FACETS output: preserves engine ZSTD columns adds DF_Infit_FACETS, DF_Outfit_FACETS, InfitZSTD_FACETS, OutfitZSTD_FACETS. FACETS fit review helper: new facets_fit_review() separates engine-level fit-standardization differences optional external FACETS table comparisons. engine-vs-FACETS review compares engine FACETS-style df/ZSTD values row--row flags cases df convention changes usual |ZSTD| >= 2 screen. FACETS-like table supplied, external review matches rows Facet / Level (person labels person-tables) classifies differences , rounding, df_or_whexact_difference, mnsq_or_measure_difference, needs_review. makes FACETS comparisons reproducible without treating mfrmr’s package-native df convention error. FACETS fit table import: new read_facets_fit_table() / import_facets_fit_table() reads existing FACETS output Facet / Level / Infit / Outfit / ZSTD / df schema expected facets_fit_review(). supports already harmonized CSV/TSV-style tables, partial FACETS extracts ZSTD TCount MnSq/df columns, FACETS score.N.txt files, including fixed-field score files using FACETS manual column positions, optional facet_map assigning score-file numbers user-facing facet names. facets_fit_review() now returns external_table_quality users can see duplicate Facet x Level rows whether MnSq, ZSTD, df, count columns available supplied external table. GPCM fair-average CI display: plot_fair_average(show_ci = TRUE) longer fabricates CIs measure-level SEs bounded GPCM fits. now uses opt-structural fair-average SE columns fit object supplied records unavailable-CI note precomputed fair-average bundles lack columns. GPCM bias likelihood checks: estimate_bias() now adds conditional profile-likelihood columns bounded GPCM rows: LR ChiSq, LR d.f., LR Prob., Profile CI Lower, Profile CI Upper, Profile CI Level, Profile CI Status. compare fitted additive bias shift zero holding theta, steps, slopes, facet estimates fixed. strengthen GPCM screening evidence without turning standalone confirmatory fairness inference. summary(estimate_bias(...)) now surfaces profile-LR screen-positive count carries columns top-row review table available.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: Category-curve information output: category_curves_report() now carries per-curve ScoreVariance, Slope, Information columns. GPCM, Information computed (^2 (X )), matching Muraki/Samejima polytomous information identity; RSM / PCM, reduces usual score variance. plot(category_curves_report(fit), type = "information") returns corresponding curve-level information plot data. Bias heatmap review data: plot_bias_interaction(plot = "heatmap", draw = FALSE) now returns heatmap_cells, heatmap_matrix, flag/count matrices, interpretation guidance, reference notes. display documented FACETS Table 13-style screening follow-, confirmatory evidence.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: evaluate_mfrm_recovery() adds dedicated parameter-recovery simulation route. repeatedly simulates known MFRM generating setup, refits requested model, returns row-level truth/estimate comparisons plus summaries parameter type.
