Location-like parameters mean-aligned within replication reporting recovery Bias, RMSE, MAE, correlation, 95% coverage standard errors available; bounded-GPCM slopes compared identified log-slope scale generator fitter imposed geometric-mean-one slope convention. output also carries ADEMP-style simulation-study metadata recovery checks separated broader design-evaluation claims. plot(evaluate_mfrm_recovery(...)) adds review plots recovery summaries, coverage, row-level error distributions, truth-estimate scatter, replication status. draw = FALSE returns mfrm_plot_data object reusable plot tables notes. assess_mfrm_recovery() adds user-facing adequacy checklist recovery simulations. separates run completion, convergence, uncertainty availability, coverage, Monte Carlo precision, optional practical RMSE/Bias thresholds ok / review / concern style statuses next-action text. plot(assess_mfrm_recovery(...)) now provides checklist status-count parameter-metric review plots users can see part assessment needs attention reading full tables. draw = FALSE plot data include reading_order, guidance, user-facing handoff tables section_status follow-starts review/concern rows rather raw row-level output. Simulation refit score support: simulation-based refit helpers now pass generator’s declared 1:score_levels score support fit_mfrm(). keeps zero-count boundary categories fitted support recovery, design-evaluation, diagnostic-screening, bias-screening runs, avoids repeated rating-range inference messages release-validation logs. Compact step-threshold specifications: build_mfrm_sim_spec() simulate_mfrm_data() now accept step-facet-specific thresholds named list row-named numeric matrix, addition existing long StepFacet / StepIndex / Estimate table. Design-evaluation progress control: evaluate_mfrm_design(progress = interactive()) now shows progress bar interactive sessions default. Non-interactive tests, Quarto rendering, batch scripts stay quiet unless users set progress = TRUE; users can also set progress = FALSE fully silent exploratory runs. Release recovery-validation protocol: inst/validation/recovery-validation.R provides optional long-run validation script release review. defines structured review steps, core RSM / PCM / bounded-GPCM recovery cases, extended latent-regression case, practical thresholds, summary writer produces top-line release-decision, case-level release-decision, review-step, case-plan, case-summary, metric-summary, overall decision-table, domain decision-table, run-note, RDS, Markdown outputs without adding heavy Monte Carlo runs routine package tests. release decision uses recovery metrics, convergence, Monte Carlo precision primary evidence; domain decision table separately reports uncertainty status missing JML coverage columns mistaken recovery failure. Printing validation object calling summary(validation) now shows release-level decision case-level statuses full tables. Recovery reporting handoff: build_summary_table_bundle() now accepts evaluate_mfrm_recovery() assess_mfrm_recovery() outputs directly, including ADEMP-style methods metadata, replication status, checklist rows, metric review rows, thresholds, notes, appendix-preset roles. Recovery appendix export: export_summary_appendix() now recognizes recovery simulation recovery assessment objects direct inputs. workflow vignette shows full sequence simulation specification recovery plots, adequacy assessment, summary-table bundle, appendix export. Research-grounded release evidence map: inst/validation/release-evidence-map-0.2.0.md gives source-based review plan 0.2.0. links release checks Andrich’s RSM, Masters’ PCM, Muraki’s GPCM information-function work, FACETS/Winsteps fit conventions, ADEMP-style simulation-study reporting, separates release-gate checks future-scope items. companion release-evidence-checklist-0.2.0.csv provides structured required / caveat / future-scope checklist release review. external-parameter-recovery-simulation-0.2.0.md summarizes separate common-data recovery cross-engine agreement workflow limits without bundling generated simulation datasets; validation bundle also includes sourceable helper re-reading local external-output directory workflow refreshed. Release-readiness protocol: inst/validation/release-readiness.R turns evidence map reproducible review object. records eight review steps, parses R CMD check log, checks 0.2.0 version contract, verifies CI workflow contract warning failures retained check artifacts, scans public docs disallowed removed-helper wording, confirms evidence artifacts, reports top-line ok / review / concern gate summary without adding exported user-facing API.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: Muraki DOI consistency: DESCRIPTION now cites Muraki (1992) using GPCM article DOI, 10.1177/014662169201600206. Muraki (1993) Applied Psychological Measurement reference GPCM information help pages now uses 10.1177/014662169301700403. Wright (1998) page: R/api-shrinkage.R references corrected Rasch Measurement Transactions, 12(2), 638 632-633 (page 638 RMT issue different paper; verified https://www.rasch.org/rmt/rmt122.htm). Linacre (1989, “2004”) reporting_checklist(): bare “Linacre (1989, 2004)” tag R/api-reporting-checklist.R now Linacre (1989, 2002). 2002 paper “Optimizing rating scale category effectiveness,” JAM, 3(1), 85-106 – canonical Linacre reference rating-scale guidance. (bibliographic entry existed “Linacre (2004)”.) Eckes (2011) full reference: inline (cf. Eckes, 2011; ...) caveat dif_report() now complete @references entry pointing Introduction Many-Facet Rasch Measurement (1st ed., Peter Lang). McNamara & Knoch (2012) also fully cited. Mean-square fit ranges ?mfrmr-package previously attributed context-specific bands (high-stakes / clinical / survey) Linacre (2002). actual source Wright & Linacre (1994), RMT 8(3), 370. band assignments also swapped: high-stakes MCQ 0.8-1.2 (0.6-1.4), survey 0.6-1.4, clinical observation 0.5-1.7. Corrected. Yen Q3 (q3_statistic()): previously stated mfrmr’s Q3 uses standardized residuals matching Yen (1984). Yen’s eq. 7 (p. 127) uses raw residuals; mfrmr’s standardized-residual choice now documented deliberate departure. |Q3| > 0.20 cutoff attributed Yen Chen & Thissen (1997), JEBS, 22(3), 265-289. Re-attributed. Christensen et al. (2017) q3_statistic(): central finding Christensen et al. single critical value appropriate across designs parametric bootstrap used. Documentation now states clearly; fixed relative_offset = 0.20 described screening default rather re-implementation Q3_*. Morris (1983) posterior-SE correction formula ?apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage dimensionally wrong: previously written 2 B^2 (tau^2 + SE^2)^2 / (K - 3), SE^4-units. actual Morris (1983, eq. 4.1-4.2, p. 51) correction (2 / (K - r - 2)) * B^2 * delta^2. Corrected, re-derived magnitude examples (SE understated ~73% K=3, ~29% K=5, ~7% K=15). Koo & Li (2016) ICC band boundary: compute_facet_icc() previously placed ICC = 0.9 Excellent (>= 0.9). Koo & Li (2016, p. 161) write “values greater 0.90 indicate excellent reliability” – strict >. Code R/api-hierarchical-audit.R now uses > 0.9 Excellent; ICC = 0.9 reads Good.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: Linacre FACETS / Winsteps manuals: cited years updated 2023 / 2024 2026 (current FACETS 4.5.0 = April 2026, Winsteps 5.11.0 = March 2026 per https://www.winsteps.com/index.htm). Bock & Aitkin (1981) clarification: ?mfrmr-package now notes default mml_engine = "direct" optimises marginal log-likelihood gradient methods (BFGS / L-BFGS-B), Bock & Aitkin’s signature EM. "em" "hybrid" engines follow EM template BFGS M-step (rather B&’s probit IRLS), target polytomous Rasch family rather 2PL. Linacre (1994) sample-size bands: mfrm_core.R reporting.R now describe bands “adapted Linacre (1994)” rather “follow Linacre (1994)”. 30-examinee floor Linacre’s; < 10 sparse < 50 standard watermarks mfrmr-specific screening choices. Snijders (2001) lz\* correction: compute_person_fit_indices() now computes Snijders weight-projection correction JML/fixed-effect person estimates, conditional fitted non-person calibration. implementation uses polytomous form w_tilde_k = log(P_k) - c_n d log(P_k) / d theta, c_n = Cov(log P, score) / (theta). MML/EAP person scores keep lz_star = NA lz_star_status = "not_applicable_eap" EAP satisfy ML/MAP/WLE estimating-equation setup. Report-ready person-fit output: compute_person_fit_indices() now adds practical 5% / 1% flag columns compact ReportIndex, ReportValue, ReviewStatus, ReviewReason, ReportCaveat columns. ReportIndex uses lz_star Snijders correction actually computed; otherwise falls back uncorrected lz status caveat left visible. plot_person_fit() now carries person-fit indices draw-free plot data, adds reusable plot_long flag_summary tables, supports fit_index = "loglik" plus preset = "monochrome" report-focused person-fit displays. Person-fit summary table-bundle handoff: compute_person_fit_indices() now returns mfrm_person_fit_indices data-frame subclass. summary(person_fit) gives overview counts, ReviewStatus / ReportIndex / lz_star_status summaries, top review rows, thresholds, caveats, reporting map. summary now accepted build_summary_table_bundle(), person-fit review rows Snijders-availability caveats can move appendix/report workflows without custom table wrangling. Marais (2013) |Q3| > 0.30: documented community convention Marais cites, recommendation; actual recommendation relative--mean comparison.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: defaults change 0.1.6 0.2.0. 0.1.6 defaults (quad_points = 31, diagnostic_mode = "", plot.mfrm_fit(type = "wright"), keep_original = FALSE) retained. Note users upgrading directly CRAN 0.1.5 0.2.0 (skipping intermediate 0.1.6 builds): three defaults flipped 0.1.6 remain values 0.2.0 – diagnose_mfrm(diagnostic_mode) went "legacy" "", plot(fit) returns Wright map alone instead three-plot overview (overview still available via plot(fit, type = "bundle")), fit_mfrm(quad_points) went 15 31. See “mfrmr 0.1.6” section full description revert paths.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: New GitHub Actions workflows added alongside existing pkgdown.yaml: R-CMD-check.yaml runs matrix Ubuntu (release / devel / oldrel-1) plus macos-latest windows-latest (release), test-coverage.yaml runs covr artifact upload (external service contacted).

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: plot_dif_heatmap() gains display controls cell labels (show_values, value_digits), absolute flag thresholds (flag_threshold, flag_color), shared symmetric color limits (scale_limit) several heatmaps can drawn comparable scale. plot_dif_summary() gains optional normal-approximation confidence intervals, effect-threshold guide lines, method-aware axis labels, interpretation-guide data downstream code can render alongside figure.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: print.mfrm_plot_data() now defined, headline draw = FALSE return value renders compact summary (name, title, reusable data shapes, legend / reference-line counts) instead raw list dump.

what: Bounded GPCM fair-average and bias unblock (slope-aware)
title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: fair_average_table() estimate_bias() longer hard-stop GPCM fits. helpers now use slope-aware element-conditional GPCM construction: fair_average_table(): slope-facet element rows, fair-average uses element’s discrimination a_{j*} threshold structure: FA_{p,j*} = sum_k k * P_GPCM(X = k | theta_p, a_{j*}, delta_{j*}). non-slope facets (Person, Rater, …), fair-average uses geometric-mean-one slope GPCM identification, construction continuous PCM Linacre fair-average reduces exactly slopes equal one (regression-tested machine precision). estimate_bias(): per-cell bias parameter additive shift linear predictor maximises per-cell GPCM log-likelihood. dispatch routes inner nll per-iteration category_prob calls GPCM kernel instead PCM kernel; SE / t / Prob columns retain screening-tier semantics documented ?estimate_bias. helpers gain method = "GPCM-slope-aware" caveat field names slope convention. fair averages, original SE columns remain measure-level SEs, fair_se = TRUE adds structural delta-method fair-average SEs non-person rows MML Hessian available. bias values, SE / t / Prob columns retain conditional screening interpretation. See ?fair_average_table, ?estimate_bias, gpcm_capability_matrix() full support contract. build_apa_outputs(), facets_output_contract_review(), facets_output_file_bundle(include = "score") remain blocked GPCM 0.2.0; require SE infrastructure ship publication-quality outputs.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: compute_person_fit_indices() now computes lz model category probability observed category directly (true Drasgow, Levine & Williams (1985) polytomous form), via three new intermediate columns PrObserved, ItemEntropy, ItemVarLogP compute_obs_table(). previous Gaussian-residual approximation overstated Var[log P] roughly factor five 4-category fixture pulled lz toward zero. weight-projection correction estimating-equation assumptions met, otherwise stays NA explicit status. displacement_table()$summary now returns NA_real_ MaxAbsDisplacement MaxAbsDisplacementT every flagged level zero information (every Displacement NA). Previously helper called max(..., na.rm = TRUE) -NA vector, returned -Inf emitted “non-missing arguments max; returning -Inf” warning. guarded version regression-tested test-core-coverage-gaps.R. analyze_dff() dif_interaction_table() now reject invalid p_adjust, non-integer min_obs, invalid focal groups, -missing group columns front, instead failing later inside contrast computation. Missing empty group rows dropped message().

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: ?analyze_dff, ?plot_dif_summary, ?mfrmr_linking_and_dff, ?mfrmr_visual_diagnostics now distinguish residual-method screening labels refit-method ETS /B/C classifications explicitly route users plot_dif_heatmap() plot_dif_summary(). ?compute_person_fit_indices now describes lz_star computed intentionally left NA: JML/fixed-effect person scores receive Snijders (2001) correction, whereas MML/EAP scores remain uncorrected EAP outside Snijders estimating-equation setup. ?mfrm_generalizability now discloses lme4 random-effects model main-effects (Score ~ 1 + (1|Person) + (1|Facet) + ... + Residual, explicit (1|Person:Facet) interaction terms), folds two-way interaction variance Residual can bias G downward. companion mfrm_d_study() projects G Phi planned facet counts, reports residual-scaling assumption explicitly; users need full p x r x decomposition treat projections planning evidence, substitute separately estimated interaction components. ?q3_statistic now discloses , chosen facet multiple residual rows per (Person, Level) cell additional facets design, standardized residuals mean-aggregated one value per cell Pearson correlation. Yen’s (1984) original definition takes correlation per-(Person, Item) residuals without aggregation, published |Q3| > 0.20 threshold Christensen et al. (2017) critical values derived original formulation; values returned treated screening summary rather direct substitute thresholds. ?bias_pairwise_report now discloses contrast SE uses independence approximation sqrt(SE_i^2 + SE_j^2). -facet bias values share sum--zero identification true Cov(b_i, b_j) < 0, reported SE -estimate t-statistic / p-value conservative (true significance higher reported). across-facet contrasts covariance term approximately zero approximation appropriate. Two new vignettes ship Migration Scope section pkgdown article navigation: vignette("mfrmr-facets-migration") walks Facets users corresponding mfrmr workflow numeric contract checks, vignette("mfrmr-gpcm-scope") documents downstream helpers bounded GPCM route currently supports versus restricts use substitute helper restricted.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: .Rbuildignore tightened inst/references/ source-package boundary. two runtime / user-facing files directory – facets_column_contract.csv (read runtime facets_output_contract_review()) FACETS_manual_mapping.md (FACETS Table mfrmr helper mapping cited README) – preserved.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: cpp11 MML backend (src/mml_backend.cpp, RSM PCM ) opt-via options(mfrmr.use_cpp11_backend = TRUE) release. validated pure-R reference tolerance = 1e-12 fixed regression fixture. default flip planned follow-release cycle community testing.

title: mfrmr 0.2.0
text: Considered 0.2.0 shipped 0.2.0; carried later release: User-facing GPCM unblock build_apa_outputs(), facets_output_contract_review(), facets_output_file_bundle(include = "score"). (fair_average_table() estimate_bias() unblocked .) classical-DIF helper (working title analyze_dif_classical()) covering Mantel-Haenszel, logistic regression, SIBTEST. Five additional Rasch / IRT classic plots (KIDMAP, TCC, expected score curve, cumulative ICC, information surface). native classical-DIF vignette (migration bounded-GPCM-scope vignettes ship release; see Documentation section ). scheduled follow-release.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: release adds empirical-Bayes shrinkage small-N facets, hierarchical-structure sample-adequacy review layer, integrated missing-code pre-processing, APA output adapters Word / HTML, model-estimated two-way non-person facet interactions, confidence-interval propagation plot surface ICC reporting family, expanded reproducibility manifests. Six bug fixes close issues affected bias statistics, ZSTD sign, input validation, graphical state hygiene.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Three default values change release. Scripts explicitly pass old value unaffected; scripts rely default reviewed. diagnose_mfrm(diagnostic_mode = ...) default flips "legacy" "". Strict marginal screens produced automatically RSM / PCM fits without caller request . Pass diagnostic_mode = "legacy" restore earlier behaviour. plot(fit) default output now Wright map alone, returned mfrm_plot_data object. previous three-plot overview (Wright + pathway + CCC) remains available via plot(fit, type = "bundle"), returns mfrm_plot_bundle three slots. fit_mfrm(quad_points = ...) default increases 15 31 default MML fit stable enough direct manuscript reporting. Pass quad_points = 15 (7) restore earlier iteration speed exploratory scans.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm() gains facet_interactions confirmatory two-way interactions non-person facets RSM PCM fits, example facet_interactions = "Rater:Criterion". terms estimated simultaneously main MFRM parameters fixed effects zero marginal-sum constraints, contributing (- 1) * (B - 1) free parameters x B interaction block. New supporting pieces: interaction_effect_table(fit) returns one row per interaction cell, estimates, weighted counts, sparse-cell flags, identification note. summary(fit) reports compact interaction overview interaction terms present. compare_mfrm(..., nested = TRUE) now recognizes -family additive-vs- interaction comparisons nested structural settings match smaller model’s interaction set subset larger model’s set. feature intentionally narrow initial CRAN-facing release: person-involving interactions, higher-order interactions, GPCM interactions, random-effect facet interactions deferred. Residual bias screening via estimate_bias() estimate_all_bias() remains separate model-estimated fixed effects.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm(..., facet_shrinkage = "empirical_bayes") applies James-Stein / empirical-Bayes shrinkage non-person facet’s fixed-effect estimates. fit$facets$others gains ShrunkEstimate, ShrunkSE, ShrinkageFactor columns, fit$shrinkage_report summarises per-facet prior variance, mean shrinkage, effective degrees freedom. estimator classical method--moments form (Efron & Morris, 1973): tau_hat^2 = max(0, mean(delta_hat_j^2) - mean(SE_j^2)), using raw second moment mfrmr’s sum--zero identification (facet mean exactly 0 construction, degree freedom consumed). B_j = SE_j^2 / (tau_hat^2 + SE_j^2) (shrinkage factor). correction tau_hat^2 uncertainty). Two post-hoc helpers make shrinkage available existing fits: apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(fit, facet_prior_sd = NULL, shrink_person = FALSE) augments existing mfrm_fit. shrinkage_report(fit) returns per-facet summary table. "laplace" alias currently routes empirical-Bayes path reserved future penalised-MML implementation. Integration: summary(fit) exposes FacetShrinkage FacetShrinkageTau2Mean; build_apa_outputs() adds Method-section sentence naming mode, mean tau_hat^2, mean shrinkage Efron & Morris (1973) citation; build_mfrm_manifest() gains shrinkage_audit table; reporting_checklist() gains “Empirical-Bayes shrinkage” item.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Five new exported functions describe observed design, flag small-N facet levels, quantify ICC / design effect. Estimation remains fixed-effects MFRM; helpers purely descriptive alter fit. detect_facet_nesting(data, facets, person) classifies every ordered pair facets (plus Person, optionally) Fully nested, Near-perfectly nested, Partially nested, Crossed using conditional-entropy index 1 - H(B|)/H(B). facet_small_sample_review(fit) returns per-level N / Estimate / SE / Infit / Outfit / SampleCategory every facet. SampleCategory one "sparse" (< 10), "marginal" (< 30), "standard" (< 50), "strong" (>= 50). Thresholds follow Linacre (1994) configurable. reliability bands; facets use “variance share” label (Trivial / Small / Moderate / Large). compute_facet_design_effect(data, facets, icc_table) computes Kish (1965) Deff = 1 + (m - 1) * rho effective N per facet. analyze_hierarchical_structure(data, facets, ...) bundles four helpers (igraph available) bipartite connectivity summary Person * facet-level edges. Fit- reporting-stack integration: fit$summary carries FacetSampleSizeFlag, FacetMinLevelN, FacetSparseCount. reporting_checklist() gains two items: “Facet sample-size adequacy” (auto-ready flag "standard" / "strong") “Hierarchical structure review” (ready user passes hierarchical_structure = analyze_hierarchical_structure(...)). build_apa_outputs() adds Method sentence naming sample-adequacy band linking facet_small_sample_review(). build_mfrm_manifest() gains hierarchical_audit table. recommend_mfrm_design()$caveats now points users three post-fit audit functions. Optional dependencies igraph lme4 move Suggests; either absent relevant report omitted clear message().

what: Missing-code pre-processing in the fit call
title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm() now accepts missing_codes = NULL | TRUE | "default" | <character vector>, forwarded prepare_mfrm_data(), review_mfrm_anchors(), describe_mfrm_data(). active, standard FACETS / SPSS / SAS sentinels ("99", "999", "-1", "N", "NA", "n/", ".", "" default, caller- supplied set) converted NA person, facets, score columns downstream processing. Replacement counts recorded fit$prep$missing_recoding surfaced build_mfrm_manifest()$missing_recoding. default (missing_codes = NULL) strictly backward-compatible. standalone recode_missing_codes() helper also exported users prefer recode calling fit_mfrm().

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: as_kable.apa_table() converts apa_table knitr::kable() object caption note . kableExtra installed note becomes proper table footnote; otherwise appended Markdown. as_flextable.apa_table() produces flextable::flextable() caption note pre-wired, suitable officer / Word / PowerPoint exports. Two generics, as_kable() as_flextable(), exported mfrmr classes (third-party wrappers) can register compatible methods. build_apa_outputs(..., context = list(output_mode = "reflow")) now returns Method / Results paragraphs single long lines per sentence-joined paragraph, format Word / Quarto / RMarkdown prefer. default "wrapped" keeps 92-column layout console readability. kableExtra flextable join Suggests.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: plot(fit, type = "shrinkage") renders horizontal forest-style dotplot original shrunk facet-level estimates, arrows indicating shrinkage direction, optional 95 % CI error bars (show_ci = TRUE), reference line zero. shrinkage applied plot shows unavailable-state message inviting user re-fit facet_shrinkage = "empirical_bayes". plot.mfrm_facet_sample_review() draws horizontal bar chart per-level observation counts coloured Linacre band, dashed vertical lines thresholds. plot.mfrm_facet_nesting() renders pairwise nesting index heatmap numeric cell labels. three methods follow existing preset = c("standard", "publication", "compact") convention use base-R graphics.

what: Confidence intervals across the plot surface
title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: plot_bias_interaction(show_ci = TRUE, ci_level = 0.95) draws BiasSize +/- z * SE whiskers scatter ranked views. plot_displacement(show_ci = TRUE) draws Displacement +/- z * DisplacementSE whiskers lollipop view. plot_fair_average(show_ci = TRUE) draws fair-average CI whiskers observed-score scale using delta-method propagation SE_fair = Var(X | Measure) * ModelSE logit Measure error. Rows near rating boundary (implied score variance effectively zero) excluded whiskers, drawn open circles, counted subtitle. compute_facet_icc(ci_method = "profile" | "boot") returns ICC confidence intervals new ICC_CI_Lower / ICC_CI_Upper / ICC_CI_Level / ICC_CI_Method columns, propagated analyze_hierarchical_structure() drawn whiskers plot.mfrm_hierarchical_structure(type = "icc"). default ci_method = "none" keeps point-estimate-behaviour.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Fourteen additions across plot surface, base-R / additive (default behaviours unchanged): plot_threshold_ladder() (new) — vertical ladder Rasch-Andrich thresholds RSM PCM, disordered-step crossings highlighted preset’s fail colour. returned object includes per-step Group / Step / Threshold / Disordered rows. plot(fit, type = "ccc_overlay") (new branch plot.mfrm_fit) — observed category proportions binned person measure overlaid model CCC curves, --glance model-data fit visual. plot_person_fit() (new) — FACETS Table 6 style per-person Infit / Outfit bubble standard 0.5-1.5 acceptance band (Linacre, 2002). plot_bias_interaction(plot = "heatmap") (new mode) — diverging Rater x Criterion grid coloured bias size, flagged cells outlined emphasis. plot(fit, type = "wright", group = ..., group_data = ...) (new option) — overlays per-group person-density curves Wright map’s left density column, useful DIF / DFF screening. plot_rater_severity_profile() (new) — per-rater severity ranking CI whiskers optional +/-0.5 (gentle) +/-1.0 (strict) guidance bands rater-training feedback. plot_anchor_drift(type = "forest") (new mode) — per-wave anchor-element CI forest point estimate + z * SE whiskers. plot.mfrm_equating_chain() (new S3 method) — type = "common_anchors" (default bar chart pairwise common-anchor counts) type = "graph" (bipartite Wave x anchor element graph via igraph). plot_apa_figure_one() (new) — 2x2 publication composite bundling Wright map, rater severity profile, threshold ladder, one-panel summary block. plot_dif_summary() (new) — compact effect-size summary [analyze_dff()] / [analyze_dif()] ETS / B / C colour coding. plot_guttman_scalogram() (new) — Person x facet-level observed-category matrix, ordered person measure location measure, unexpected cells highlighted. plot_residual_qq() (new) — normal Q-Q plot person-level standardized residuals distributional misfit diagnostics. plot_rater_trajectory() (new) — per-rater severity trajectory across ordered wave / session variable CI whiskers. Accepts named list fits. plot_rater_agreement_heatmap() (new) — symmetric rater x rater agreement matrix colored exact agreement (default) Pearson-style Corr column interrater_agreement_table(). Quadratic-weighted kappa currently computed helper therefore exposed metric option. igraph already Suggests; equating-graph view falls back bar chart igraph installed.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Direct regression tests 0.1.6 additions: test-attach-diagnostics.R — 18 assertions covering attach_diagnostics = TRUE merge, type validation, idempotence, MML / JML agreement checks. test-icc-ci-method.R — 25 assertions covering compute_facet_icc(ci_method = "profile" / "boot"), bootstrap seed reproducibility, range validation, deprecated icc_ci_method alias, plot.mfrm_hierarchical_structure(type = "icc") integration. test-ci-api-consistency.R — 21 assertions covering lifecycle::deprecate_warn() path conf_level, show_ci / ci_level plot_fair_average / plot_displacement / plot_bias_interaction, CI column schema. test-messaging--guards.R — assertions covering quiet--default inferred rating-range message, opt-one-time message, analyze_dff(method = "refit") missing(diagnostics) guard, missing_codes integration. test-lme4-confint-helper.R — 17 assertions covering .lme4_confint_components() across terse verbose lme4 row-name conventions. test-plotting-extras.R + test-plotting-screening.R — 78 assertions covering 14 new plot helpers.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: row_max_fast() three category_prob_* polytomous-response kernels now R/core-category-probabilities.R instead inline R/mfrm_core.R. Pure file-level reorganization; behaviour change. remaining structural split mfrm_core.R (likelihood / optimizer / EM / gradients / prep / report tables) scheduled future release.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: mfrm_misfit_thresholds() returns lower / upper Linacre acceptance band mfrmr screens use flagging element-level Infit / Outfit MnSq misfit. Defaults c(lower = 0.5, upper = 1.5) can overridden globally via R options: options(mfrmr.misfit_lower = 0.7) options(mfrmr.misfit_upper = 1.3) Helpers consume band include summary(diagnose_mfrm(...)) (misfit_flagged block + key_warnings auto-flag), build_misfit_casebook() (new element_fit source family), bias / misfit narrative inside build_apa_outputs(), facet_quality_dashboard() misfit_warn = NULL. Setting options top analysis script therefore changes every downstream screen .

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Four new public helpers extend diagnostic plot family: plot_local_dependence_heatmap(fit) – N x N Q3-style pairwise residual correlation heatmap facet levels. Complements plot_marginal_pairwise() showing every pair shared color scale rather top-N bar list. plot_reliability_snapshot(fit) – compact facet x reliability / separation / strata bar overview built diagnostics$reliability. Useful single small figure “persons / raters / criteria distinguishable?”. plot_residual_matrix(fit) – person x facet-level standardized residual heatmap. Complements plot_guttman_scalogram() showing residual sign magnitude rather raw response code. plot_shrinkage_funnel(fit) – empirical-Bayes shrinkage caterpillar / funnel fits augmented via apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage(). plot_bubble() gains view = c("measure", "infit_outfit") argument. default "measure" keeps historical Measure (logit) x MnSq bubble layout; view = "infit_outfit" switches Winsteps Table 30 layout (Infit MnSq x, Outfit MnSq y, bubble size defaults N). views return mfrm_plot_data contract. plot_dif_heatmap(draw = FALSE) now returns mfrm_plot_data object whose data$matrix metric matrix (previously bare matrix ). plot_information(..., draw = FALSE) outputs now include series field listing curves legend describes ("Information", "SE", type = ""), downstream ggplot2 re-renderers can map right column without inspecting type manually.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: summary(diagnose_mfrm(...)) now prints fixed-effect chi-square block (“elements equal?”) directly diag$facets_chisq (Facet, Levels, MeanMeasure, SD, FixedChiSq, FixedDF, FixedProb, plus random-effect counterparts present) inter-rater agreement summary (Exact / Expected / Adjacent agreement, MeanAbsDiff, MeanCorr, RaterSeparation, RaterReliability) instead leaving diagnostics object . new summary(diag)$facets_chisq summary(diag)$interrater slots also expose tables programmatic use. summary(diagnose_mfrm(...))$key_warnings now names worst MnSq-misfit elements (e.g. MnSq misfit: Person:P023 (Infit=1.70, Outfit=2.40; outside 0.5-1.5).) prints dedicated MnSq misfit block showing every flagged element. Threshold pair exposed summary(diag)$misfit_thresholds steered mfrm_misfit_thresholds() (see ). summary(diagnose_mfrm(...)) now prints category usage block (one row per observed score Count, AvgMeasure, Disordering flag average measure decreases across adjacent categories). Exposed programmatically summary(diag)$category_usage. summary(fit_mfrm(...)) now prints targeting block (Person mean - Facet mean, plus PersonSD / FacetSD / SpreadRatio) every non-person facet. package’s sum--zero identification collapses person mean construction; row labels make explicit spread ratio surfaces whether persons facets dominate test scale. summary(estimate_bias(...)) now reports Bonferroni Holm significant-cell counts alongside raw screen-positive count. exposed summary(bias)$overview BonferroniSignificant HolmSignificant. print(fit) print(summary(fit)) now show “Attached diagnostics” line fit_mfrm(..., attach_diagnostics = TRUE) merged per-element fit columns onto fit$facets. attach-diagnostics path now extends person-facet table, per-person Infit, Outfit, InfitZSTD, OutfitZSTD, PtMeaCorr columns visible summary(fit)$person_high summary(fit)$person_low.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: improve navigability core estimation engine, four self-contained sections moved R/mfrm_core.R focused files. functions remain internal public API unchanged. R/core-likelihood.R – polytomous Rasch likelihoods cumulative response-probability helpers. R/core-data-prep.R – data validation, indexing, small formatting utilities. R/core-anchor-review.R – anchor-table reading, normalization, connectivity / overlap audit. R/core-optimizer.R – optim() / EM dispatch MML-EM scaffolding. R/api-simulation.R similarly grew R/api-simulation-future-branch.R companion file holding future-branch design-schema layer. Public simulation entry points (simulate_mfrm_data, evaluate_mfrm_design, evaluate_mfrm_diagnostic_screening, evaluate_mfrm_signal_detection) remain R/api-simulation.R. - evaluate_mfrm_diagnostic_screening(include_report = TRUE) can now retain mfrm_results() / mfrm_report() report_index signals replicate level summarize report_signal_summary, keeping report-layer readiness separate diagnostic-screening Type sensitivity proxies. - plot.mfrm_diagnostic_screening() can now turn diagnostic-screening summaries integrated plot-data bundles overview rates, report signals, scenario contrasts, runtime checks. draw-free return follows mfrm_plot_data / plot_data() contract. R/api-plotting-extras2.R renamed R/api-plotting-screening.R drop numerical suffix favour functional name; tests follow rename. new tests/testthat/helper-fixtures.R exposes make_toy_fit() / make_toy_diagnostics() / local_toy_fit() helpers future tests can reuse standard example_core fit without retyping load_mfrmr_data() + fit_mfrm() + diagnose_mfrm() chain.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: export_mfrm_bundle() build_mfrm_replay_script() now write self-contained replay package: generated replay.R includes every argument affected original fit_mfrm() call. Earlier 0.1.x scripts silently dropped missing_codes, mml_engine, slope_facet, anchor_policy, min_common_anchors, min_obs_per_*, facet_shrinkage, facet_prior_sd, shrink_person, attach_diagnostics, fits depended arguments actually replay. fit_mfrm() now records inputs fit$config$replay_inputs (post match.arg) bundle generator single source truth. replay script begins utils::packageVersion("mfrmr") guard warns installed version differs recorded one. export_mfrm_bundle(..., data = ...) accepts original analysis data; supplied, data written bundle <prefix>_replay_data.csv replay script reads co-located file. recorded input hash now computed user’s original data (package’s internal prep$data, carries synthesised columns), users can verify CSV matches recorded fingerprint. new tests/testthat/test-replay-roundtrip.R actually sources generated replay script fresh environment compares reproduced log-likelihood person estimates original.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: calc_interrater_agreement() (inter-rater agreement helper diagnose_mfrm() calls Person part facet_cols) previously used list() per-context probability lookup c(exp_vals, ...) accumulation inside per-row loop. gave near-quadratic scaling: 6,400 observations took ~2 s, 72,000 observations took ~141 s. lookup now environment (hash-backed character keys) exp_vals preallocated filled index, helper now scales linearly number observations. 72,000-observation benchmark review, diagnose_mfrm() drops ~141 s ~15 s. make_union_find() helper used connectivity audit also rewritten iterative find_root (path compression) instead previous recursive form. Designs whose union chain depth exceeded options(expressions) (default 5,000) longer error “evaluation deeply nested”.

what: Input validation: degenerate inputs surface earlier
title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: prepare_mfrm_data() now: records many rows dropped due missing values non-positive weights, instead dropping silently; trims leading/trailing whitespace Person facet IDs records row count ” P01 ” “P01” silently become two persons; warning()s input contains duplicate Person x facet rows (violate MFRM’s conditional-independence assumption) lets fit continue rather refusing outright. fit_mfrm() now treats NaN / Inf maxit, reltol, quad_points invalid input localised English error, instead falling R’s locale-dependent “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed” message.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: two-page landscape cheatsheet now ships pre-rendered form system.file("cheatsheet", "mfrmr-cheatsheet.pdf", package = "mfrmr") alongside existing .Rmd source. Users without working LaTeX toolchain can open PDF directly; users want customize can still knit .Rmd rmarkdown::render(). README ?mfrmr package help now point files.

what: Help-page examples: “what to look for” annotations
title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: -visited help pages now embed concrete interpretation comments inside @examples blocks. shipped example shows value ranges patterns indicate “good”, threshold rule thumb applies, follow-run value . Coverage 0.1.6 includes: ?fit_mfrm (convergence, person SD, targeting bands). ?diagnose_mfrm (key_warnings, MnSq misfit lines, facets_chisq, inter-rater agreement minus expected). ?summary.mfrm_fit ?summary.mfrm_diagnostics (overview, person distribution, top_fit ZSTD bands, facets_chisq, targeting). ?estimate_bias, ?analyze_dff, ?compute_facet_icc, ?apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage (effect-size bands, Penfield classification, Koo & Li 2016 reliability bands, shrinkage factor interpretation). ?build_apa_outputs, ?reporting_checklist, ?plot_qc_dashboard, ?plot.mfrm_fit (manuscript-readiness signals, dashboard panel status, Wright / pathway / CCC interpretation). ?plot_bubble, ?plot_dif_heatmap, ?plot_local_dependence_heatmap, ?plot_reliability_snapshot, ?plot_residual_matrix, ?plot_shrinkage_funnel, ?plot_guttman_scalogram, ?plot_residual_qq, ?plot_rater_trajectory, ?plot_rater_agreement_heatmap (cell / band thresholds, reference-line interpretation).

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Several main entry points now expose small fast-path block (JML fit example_core plus single diagnostic / plot call) heavier \donttest{} block. fast path R CMD check’s example-time budget provides regression net runs every check, full \donttest{} block continues showcase larger MML / publication-route examples. final CRAN-timing pass keeps active examples lightweight maxit = 30 smoke fits printed examples demonstrate converged objects without returning original long-running example surface. Affected pages: ?fit_mfrm, ?diagnose_mfrm, ?plot_qc_dashboard, ?reporting_checklist, ?build_apa_outputs.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: ?mfrmr_visual_diagnostics adds “Cross-reference FACETS / Winsteps tables” section lists closest mfrmr helper canonical Rasch / MFRM table figure family (Wright map, pathway / probability curves, test information, misfit, bias / interaction, DIF / DRF, inter-rater agreement, anchoring / linking). ?mfrmr_visual_diagnostics visual reporting template now enumerate 4 secondary plot helpers 4 screening helpers added 0.1.6. ?diagnose_mfrm cites Wright & Masters (1982) separation / strata / reliability section reproduces formulae (G = TrueSD / RMSE, R = G^2 / (1 + G^2), H = (4G + 1) / 3) reliability outputs traceable source. ?fit_mfrm example block now flags quad_points = 7 opening fit exploratory speed setting. README ?mfrmr package help now point public cheatsheet (system.file("cheatsheet", "mfrmr-cheatsheet.Rmd", package = "mfrmr")). bias / misfit APA narrative now spells |ZSTD| (|MnSq - 1| ZSTD unavailable) instead generic |metric| label. build_misfit_casebook() now also draws element-level Infit / Outfit MnSq misfit cases diagnostics$fit (addition marginal cells, pairwise screens, unexpected responses, displacement). casebook therefore matches name implies.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: q3_statistic(fit, diagnostics) returns Yen (1984) Q3 index every facet-level pair, three published reporting thresholds (Yen 0.20, Marais 0.30, Christensen et al. relative 0.20) textual Interpretation column names flag(s) pair triggered. helper reuses standardized- residual pivot plot_local_dependence_heatmap() already draws, table heatmap stay numerically consistent.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: compute_person_fit_indices(diagnostics, fit) adds person-level fit detail top Infit / Outfit / ZSTD columns diagnose_mfrm() already exposes: lz (Drasgow, Levine & Williams, 1985): standardized log-likelihood fitted model. lz\* (Snijders, 2001): estimated-ability correction computed JML/fixed-effect person estimates conditional fitted non-person calibration; returned NA MML/EAP scores explanatory status. reported lz statistic asymptotically standard normal conditional-independence assumption; |lz| > 1.96 / 2.58 5% / 1% reporting flags.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: mfrm_generalizability(fit) re-fits rating data crossed random-effects model Score ~ 1 + (1 | Person) + (1 | Facet1) + ... via lme4::lmer returns canonical G / Phi coefficients plus per-source variance components. Useful reviewer asks generalizability-theory complement Rasch-style separation / reliability statistics diagnose_mfrm() already emits.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Three thin importers expose external fit objects via mfrm_fit interface mfrmr plot table helpers consume: import_mirt_fit(fit, model) accepts mirt::mirt() result. import_tam_fit(fit, model) accepts TAM::tam.mml() / TAM::tam.jml(). import_erm_fit(fit, model) accepts eRm::PCM() / eRm::RM() / eRm::RSM(). imported objects carry mfrm_imported_fit class populate measurement-side slots (facets$person, facets$others, steps, summary) . Bias / DIF / anchor / replay slots explicitly populated; full bundle import planned future release.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: compute_facet_icc(boot = "boot") gains ci_boot_parallel ("" / "multicore" / "snow") ci_boot_ncpus arguments forwarded lme4::bootMer(). per-replicate cli progress bar suppressed parallel execution worker processes hold copy progress state.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: evaluate_mfrm_design() accepts parallel = c("", "future") argument. "future" requested future.apply Suggests package installed, rep loop within design row honours whatever future::plan() currently active; cross-design-row parallelism planned future release. Without future.apply call falls back serial execution explicit message.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm() accepts checkpoint = list(file = ..., every_iter = ...) argument. supplied mml_engine = "em" (hybrid) fit, EM scaffolding writes state file every every_iter outer iterations using saveRDS(). file exists subsequent call starts, engine resumes recorded iteration. direct optim() engine ignores checkpoint; non-EM fits run unaffected.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: new tests/testthat/test-gpcm-verification.R exercises every "supported" "supported_with_caveat" row gpcm_capability_matrix() toy dataset asserts documented helper returns expected shape. "blocked" "deferred" rows negative tests confirm helper either refuses run returns explicit caveat. tests make GPCM scope contract future commits silently shrink.

what: Optional FACETS Table 7 style fit output on fitfacetsfacetsothers
title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm(attach_diagnostics = TRUE) runs diagnose_mfrm() fit merges per-level SE, Infit, Outfit, PtMeaCorr columns onto fit$facets$others. makes facet table look like FACETS Table 7 summary without separate call. default FALSE preserves minimal Facet / Level / Estimate layout 0.1.5.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: build_mfrm_manifest() gains several new tables replay bundles carry everything deterministic re-run needs: environment now records RNGKind, RNGSeedDigest, Locale, UTC ISO-8601 timestamp addition existing package platform fields. dependencies (new) records installed version every Imports Suggests dependency, Role column. input_hash (new) hashes input data, anchors, group anchors, score_map SHA-256 (via digest, now Suggests) MD5--RDS fallback. hash deterministic across sessions. session_info (new) unrolls utils::sessionInfo() long data frame (Scope / Package / Version). hierarchical_audit, missing_recoding, shrinkage_audit (new) surface three new audit layers one place. digest added Suggests.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: Bias / interaction NA. estimate_bias() estimate_all_bias() previously returned NA every cell’s S.E., t, Prob., Obs-Exp Average, Infit, Outfit, Significant counts collapsed zero. Root cause nzchar(NA_character_) call (returns TRUE) internal predicate. Downstream helpers bias_interaction_report() plot_bias_interaction() now populated . estimate_bias() silent failure typo’d facet names. mis-spelled facet_a / facet_b (e.g. "Raters" trailing s) previously returned empty list() warning. now raises informative error naming available facets. Missing diagnostics argument likewise raises explicit mfrmr error rather falling R’s locale-dependent missing- argument message. ZSTD sign. zstd_from_mnsq() numerically unstable small degrees freedom return large positive ZSTD MnSq close zero, flipping sign relative companion Outfit ZSTD element. df >= 1 guard returns NA degenerate cells. Score range. prepare_mfrm_data() now stops observed Score falls outside declared [rating_min, rating_max] range. Previously negative score_k values passed m[cbind(, 0)], silently dropping rows likelihood n_obs kept original value. Silent facet-name mismatches. sanitize_noncenter_facet(), sanitize_dummy_facets(), build_facet_signs() now emit warning supplied facet names part fitted model. Previously typos positive_facets = "rater" (lowercase) noncenter_facet = "Raters" silently flip sign convention facet measures. Graphical state hygiene. apply_plot_preset() .draw_shrinkage_plot() now restore user’s par() exit, per “Writing R Extensions” 2.1. plot methods relied apply_plot_preset() inherit automatically. DFF contrast sign flip. analyze_dff() adds ContrastDirection column residual refit branches. two methods use opposite sign conventions design, new column spells interpretation applies. compute_facet_icc() singular fit. Total variance sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) now reported ICC = NA Interpretation = "Non-identifiable" instead falsely meaningful value. first lme4 convergence diagnostic surfaces message() rather silently suppressed. Extreme-person flag persistence. .data.frame.mfrm_fit() now carries new Extreme column ggplot2 / CSV pipelines instead dropping . as_kable(format = "pipe") output. Previously silently returned HTML kableExtra installed apa_table carried non-empty note. "pipe" now consistently returns Markdown table appended Note. line. review_mfrm_anchors() false positives. Overlap-adequacy risk flags skipped anchors group anchors supplied, single-wave analyses longer emit “high severity” warnings OverlapLevels == 0 everywhere. Fractional-score tolerance. Tightened sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) (~1.5e-8) 1e-6, integer codes like 1.0000001 round-trip CSV floats now accepted. Genuinely fractional scores (1.5, 2.75) still caught. Rating-range inference output. rating_min / rating_max inferred observed scores, provenance now retained fit summaries data-description output rather emitted routine message. Users prefer interactive reminder can set options(mfrmr.show_inferred_rating_range = TRUE); fit_mfrm() still limits opt-message one per fit. Locale-independent error plot(fit, type = ...). Passing unknown type previously raised R’s locale-dependent match.arg() error. now raises English mfrmr-style error listing valid choices. plot_dif_heatmap(draw = FALSE) return contract. helper documented mfrm_plot_data object invisibly returned bare matrix, breaking documented contract used sibling plot_* helpers. now returns mfrm_plot_data whose data slot bundles matrix, pairs, metric, value_column. Code relied old shape switch dim(heat) dim(heat$data$matrix). Approximate 95% CI whiskers bias displacement plots. plot_bias_interaction(show_ci = TRUE, ci_level = 0.95) (scatter ranked modes) now draws BiasSize \u00b1 z \u00b7 SE whiskers using per-cell SE [estimate_bias()]. plot_displacement( show_ci = TRUE) (lollipop mode) draws Displacement \u00b1 z \u00b7 DisplacementSE whiskers audit-table standard error. functions now populate CI_Lower / CI_Upper / CI_Level columns returned plot-data element downstream pipelines can reuse bounds. plot_fair_average() CI support (now implemented later release) uses delta-method propagation fair-average SE lives logit scale plot uses observed-score scale, requires delta-method transformation.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit_mfrm() emits one-time message() called anchor_policy = "silent" anchor review flags issues. prepare_mfrm_data() records whether rating_min / rating_max inferred observed scores supplied explicitly, fit/data summaries surface provenance. former informational message opt-options(mfrmr.show_inferred_rating_range = TRUE). Row drops, ID trimming, facets one observed level recorded preparation_notes; routine preparation messages opt-options(mfrmr.show_preparation_messages = TRUE). Non-numeric score labels ("low", "medium", "high") now raise targeted error front instead surfacing opaque “valid observations remain” message. detect_anchor_drift() build_equating_chain() thin-linking warnings now list per-facet retained-vs-threshold counts (e.g. "Rater (3/5)"). bias_interaction_report()$summary carries FlagStatus column empty ranked_table rows longer ambiguous “nothing flagged” “nothing computed”. Latent-regression fits warn design matrix near-singular (rcond(mm) < 1e-8), catching numerically collinear covariates rather exact rank deficiency.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage() docstring R comments now document shrinkage-variance formula max(0, K^{-1} * sum(delta^2) - mean(SE^2)), matching implementation sum--zero identification. ?fit_mfrm “Input requirements” now states MFRM conditional independence assumption (Linacre, 1989) points diagnose_mfrm(..., diagnostic_mode = "") / strict_pairwise_local_dependence exploratory follow-. ?fit_mfrm example block now flags quad_points = 7 opening fit exploratory speed setting reminds readers package default quad_points = 31 publication tier, example longer reads recommendation new default. ?fit_mfrm now presents recommended quad_points tiers \tabular{} block (7 fast scan, 15 default, 31+ publication) readers re-extract recommendation prose. “adapted Linacre (1994)” wording sample-size bands wall-clock cost diagnostic_mode = "" also spelled . ?fit_mfrm missing_codes docstring now itemised list three branches (NULL / TRUE / custom vector) instead dense prose. ?run_mfrm_facets now notes method = "JML" default legacy FACETS-style output continuity points users fit_mfrm(..., method = "MML") new analysis scripts. ?apply_empirical_bayes_shrinkage now states EffectiveDF = Σ(1 − B_j) matches “effective number parameters” Efron & Morris (1973). ?compute_facet_icc notes Koo & Li (2016) recommend applying reliability bands 95% confidence interval ICC, current implementation bands point estimate . ?analyze_facet_equivalence adds Kass & Raftery (1995) reference BIC-based Bayes-factor approximation BF_{01} ≈ exp((BIC_{H1} − BIC_{H0}) / 2). explicitly alongside Wright & Linacre (1994). calc_displacement_table() now cites Winsteps user guide combined |Displacement| > 0.5 logit |t| > 2 flagging rule. analyze_facet_equivalence() docstring frames equivalence_bound = 0.5 default starting point. Added print() S3 methods 13 classes previously fell back default list printer (mfrm_apa_outputs, mfrm_bias, mfrm_bundle, mfrm_design_evaluation, mfrm_diagnostics, mfrm_facet_dashboard, mfrm_future_branch_active_branch, mfrm_plausible_values, mfrm_population_prediction, mfrm_reporting_checklist, mfrm_signal_detection, mfrm_threshold_profiles, mfrm_unit_prediction). delegates existing summary() method. Reference citations corrected: Efron & Morris (1973) page range 379-402 (379-421). McEwen (2018) BYU dissertation year (2017). Wright (1998) RMT 12(2) extreme scores (Wright 1988, exist). Jones & Wind (2018) JAM 19(2), 148-161 (“Wind & Jones, 2018, JAM 19(1), 1-19”, exist). Linacre (2023) User’s Guide Facets, Version 4.5 applied uniformly comments used 2024.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: plot_qc_dashboard() plots signed ZSTD distribution combining Infit Outfit ZSTD, reference lines -3 / -2 / 0 / 2 / 3. previous absolute-value histogram collapsed -fit -fit tails. plot_residual_pca(..., plot_type = "scree") draws Rasch secondary-dimension reference lines 1.0 / 1.4 / 2.0 / 3.0, consistent Winsteps user guide. legend returned reference_lines record new entries. Residual-PCA content_checks entry uses case-insensitive regex check passes whether APA contract uses "Residual PCA" longer "Exploratory residual PCA".

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: fit$facets$person exposes PosteriorSD SE aliases alongside legacy SD. MML fits populate three posterior SD Gauss-Hermite prior; JML fits set SE = NA_real_ note per-person SEs pulled diagnose_mfrm()$measures. analyze_dff(method = "refit") subgroup fits return LinkingReview column captures anchor-review messages emitted refit, replacing previous hard-coded anchor_policy = "silent" silence. detect_anchor_drift() returns common_vs_reference n_common_all_waves alongside existing pairwise common_elements table, 3+ wave linking reviews. analyze_residual_pca(..., pca_max_factors = "auto") caps factor count min(10, ncol - 1, nrow - 1) per matrix; value previously silently coerced NA. describe_mfrm_data() returns two new components: missing_rate_summary (per-column missing / non-missing counts) facet_crosstabs (long-format pairwise observation-count tables, suitable heatmap plotting). DESCRIPTION removes duplicated Author / Maintainer fields auto-generated CRAN; Authors@R remains single source truth. Description: field now three sentences (10 lines prose), improving CRAN web readability retaining two rating-scale / partial-credit DOI references. inst/CITATION now tracks meta$Version meta$Title dynamically, citation("mfrmr") prints current installed version rather hard-coded string.

title: mfrmr 0.1.6
text: 6,380+ tests pass (6,343 0.1.5), 0 failures 0 errors. New test files: test-shrinkage.R (40 tests) covers closed-form math, edge cases (K < 3, tau^2 <= 0, user-supplied prior), fit_mfrm integration, reporting manifest trails, three new plot methods. test-missing-codes-integration.R (17 tests) covers fit_mfrm, describe_mfrm_data, review_mfrm_anchors, manifest paths. test-hierarchical-audit.R (10 tests) covers five new hierarchical-audit helpers integration points. Pre-existing test-harness errors unrelated 0.1.5 behaviour also cleaned (S3 dispatch, GPCM scope wording, internal-helper prefixing mfrmr:::).

title: mfrmr 0.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-12

title: mfrmr 0.1.5
text: Reworked print(fit), summary(fit), summary(diagnose_mfrm(...)) results start Status, Key warnings, Next actions. Added clearer recommended workflow README help pages: fit MML, review diagnostics diagnostic_mode = "", move reporting helpers. Improved ordered-score handling guidance, including binary two-category use, rejection fractional score values, non-consecutive score-code mapping score_map, clearer warnings retained zero-count categories.

title: mfrmr 0.1.5
text: Added first public latent-regression MML branch ordered RSM / PCM fits person covariates, including simulation scoring support fitted population model. Added bounded GPCM support documented direct route, including core summaries, diagnostics, plots, posterior scoring, information checks, keeping unsupported downstream routes explicit. Extended ordered-response support documentation binary RSM / PCM use, fixed-calibration scoring JML, PCM information curves.

title: mfrmr 0.1.5
text: Added strict marginal follow-plots plot_marginal_fit() plot_marginal_pairwise(). Strengthened reporting surface reporting_checklist(), build_summary_table_bundle(), export_summary_appendix(), visual_reporting_template() manuscript-oriented tables, appendix artifacts, figure-placement guidance. Added structured caveats summaries appendix tables retained zero-count score categories latent-regression population-model omission/design issues. Added exploratory plot(fit, type = "ccc_surface", draw = FALSE) output advanced visualization keeping 2D Wright/pathway/category plots default reporting route.

title: mfrmr 0.1.5
text: Added scoped ConQuest overlap helpers concise software-scope summaries FACETS, ConQuest, SPSS handoffs. Clarified latent-regression reporting outputs coefficient reporting kept separate post hoc score regression.

title: mfrmr 0.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-30

title: mfrmr 0.1.4
text: Replaced misencoded author name documentation references PDF manual builds cleanly CRAN’s LaTeX checks. Revised DESCRIPTION references avoid incoming spell-check notes preserving requested author-year-doi citation format.

title: mfrmr 0.1.3
text: Revised DESCRIPTION references use requested authors (year) <doi:...> format. Added documented return-value section facet_quality_dashboard(), including output class, structure, interpretation. Replaced \dontrun{} \donttest{} executable examples CRAN can exercise examples checks.

title: mfrmr 0.1.2
text: reduced CRAN check time trimming CRAN-test subset lightweight smoke tests incoming pretest still reported Windows overall-checktime NOTE version 0.1.1.

title: mfrmr 0.1.1
text: Revised DESCRIPTION metadata avoid CRAN incoming spell-check notes cited proper names. Reduced CRAN check time skipping long integration coverage-expansion test files CRAN checks keeping full local test suite.

title: mfrmr 0.1.0
text: Native R implementation many-facet Rasch model (MFRM) estimation without TAM/sirt backends. Supports arbitrary facet counts fit_mfrm() method selection (MML default, JML). Includes FACETS-style bias/interaction iterative estimation via estimate_bias(). Provides fixed-width report helpers (build_fixed_reports()). Adds APA-style narrative output (build_apa_outputs()). Adds visual warning summaries (build_visual_summaries()) configurable threshold profiles. Implements residual PCA diagnostics visualization (analyze_residual_pca(), plot_residual_pca()). Bundles Eckes & Jin (2021)-inspired synthetic Study 1/2 datasets data/ inst/extdata/.

title: mfrmr 0.1.0
text: Added GitHub Actions CI cross-platform R CMD check. Added CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md. Added citation metadata (inst/CITATION, CITATION.cff). Expanded README explicit installation citation instructions.
